gmw_00012138.name: IRAS F17044+6720
gmw_00012138.overview: IRAS F17044+6720 is a highly disturbed merging galaxy system that features either a double nucleus or a single nucleus bisected by a dust lane [arXiv:1805.00514]. The system hosts a 14-kiloparsec tidal tail containing several bright, ionized gas-emitting clumps [arXiv:1805.00514].

sources.summary: The paper describes IRAS F17044+6720 as a highly disturbed merger-like system with a long tidal tail, spectacular [OIII] emission, and a nuclear broad outflow component.

quotes.quote: IRAS F17044+6720 The HST continuum image of this source shows a highly disturbed morphology. Two condensations of enhanced continuum emission, separated by 0.39 arcsec ( 0.90 kpc), are visible in the central region of the source, which might correspond to the two nuclei of the merging galaxies, or a single nucleus bisected by a dust lane. A series of dust features is also observed through almost the entire extent of the system, although they are more prominent as they cross the ``circular'' halo of diffuse continuum emission in the nuclear region. In addition, a tidal tail of length 6 arcsec ( 14 kpc) emerges from the nuclear region to the north, and then bends to the west. Several clumps are visible within this tail structure.

In the case of IRAS F17044+6720, the HST [ OIII ] image is truly spectacular. Regions of enhanced ionized gas emission are observed in the nuclear region, and then extended 4.0arcsec ( 9.2 kpc) in the direction of the tidal tail, along PA 155; this association with the continuum tidal tail suggests that the general morphology of emission line gas is tidal in origin, and is not shaped by other mechanisms such as outflows origination from the nucleus, or the anisotropy of the AGN radiation field. Three bright, [ OIII ] emitting clumps are visible in the tail structure (see Figure HST_frames ) that we label clumps A, B, C at distances 1.8, 2.8, 3.7 arcseconds ( 4.2, 6.4, 8.5 kpc) from the nucleus respectively.

gmw_00012139.name: cD galaxy in the dumbbell pair
aliases_local: cD galaxy in the dumbbell pair
aliases_external: A3266 central cD galaxy in dumbbell pair
gmw_00012139.overview: The cD galaxy in the dumbbell pair is located in the A3266 cluster and features a faint secondary nucleus [arXiv:astro-ph/9909243]. This system exhibits an asymmetric rise in stellar velocity dispersion, which is likely a sign of tidal interaction with an infalling subcluster [arXiv:astro-ph/9909243].

sources.original_name: cD galaxy in the dumbbell pair
sources.summary: The paper describes the A3266 cD galaxy as a dumbbell system with a faint secondary nucleus, velocity offset, asymmetric dispersion rise, and tidal interaction from the merger.

quotes.quote: A3266 also has a central cD galaxy with a faint secondary nucleus in a dumbbell morphology. The right panel of Figure fig:fig2 shows the smoothed, flatfielded ROSAT PSPC image overlayed on the Digitized Sky Survey image of the central 8.5x8.5' region of the A3266 cluster. While the cD galaxy in the dumbbell pair, located at 4:31:12.16, -61:27:15.3) appears slightly offset (30") from the X-ray center, this may be due to a combination of smoothing and the ROSAT pointing error, 15". The faint nucleus and the cD have a relative velocity of 400 39 km s ^ 1 , somewhat larger than the velocity dispersion of the nucleus of the cD (327 34 km s ^ 1 ). Carter et al. (1985) found that the cD shows a rise in stellar velocity dispersion with radius to a maximum of 700 km sec ^ 1 .

This rise is asymmetric and can be understood in context of the subcluster merger. We propose that it is a sign of a tidal interaction. The interaction appears to be with the infalling subcluster rather than the faint nucleus.

gmw_00012140.name: PG 1126-041
gmw_00012140.overview: PG 1126-041 is a low optical luminosity quasar host galaxy that exhibits spiral structure or tidal tails [arXiv:astro-ph/0610719]. The system is currently interacting with a neighboring galaxy located at a projected distance of 6.6 kpc [arXiv:astro-ph/0610719].

sources.summary: The paper treats PG 1126-041 as a QSO host showing spiral or tidal structure, interaction with another galaxy at 6.6 kpc projected distance, and relatively low optical luminosity.

quotes.quote: Most of the sources of Table tab:masses with < 200 show spiral structure or tidal tails (PG 0050+124, PG 1119+120, PG 1126-041, PG 1229+204, PG1426+015, PG 2130+099) in their NIR images ( surace01 ; veilleux06 ).
According to and , PG 1126-041 also interacts with a galaxy at a projected distance of 6.6 kpc.

Examples include some PG QSOs in our sample (PG 1126-041, PG 1229+204, and PG 2130+099) that are amongst the sources with the lowest optical luminosity and that have signatures of spiral structure ( surace01 ; veilleux06 ).

gmw_00012141.name: suspected host of GRB 061201
gmw_00012141.overview: The suspected host galaxy of GRB 061201 is a bulge-dominated galaxy located at a redshift of z=0.111 [arXiv:1910.14505]. It exhibits emission lines and has a star formation rate of 0.14 solar masses per year based on its H-alpha flux [arXiv:1910.14505].

sources.summary: The paper describes the suspected GRB 061201 host G1 as a bulge-dominated galaxy 17 arcsec northwest of the afterglow, with z=0.111 emission lines and H-based SFR of 0.14 solar masses per year.

quotes.quote: On VLT/FORS2 images the suspected host is a bulge-dominated galaxy 17 arcsec NW from the afterglow in the following G1; see figure 5 in Stratta2007 . Long-slit spectroscopy revealed emission lines from and H at a common redshift of z=0.111 . Similar results were reported based on Magellan observations BergerGCN5952 . The SFR based on the H flux is 0.14 M _ yr ^ 1 Stratta2007 .

gmw_00012142.name: host galaxy of GRB 070724A
gmw_00012142.overview: The host galaxy of GRB 070724A is a dust-rich galaxy located at a redshift of z = 0.457 [arXiv:1910.14505]. Optical spectra of the galaxy reveal a global star formation rate estimated between 1 and 100 solar masses per year [arXiv:1910.14505].

sources.summary: The paper describes the GRB 070724A host as a z=0.457 dust-rich galaxy whose optical spectra imply star formation, though internal extinction makes the global SFR difficult to measure robustly.

quotes.quote: These authors also studied the host galaxy which lies at a redshift of z =0.457 see also their figure 3 Kocevski2010MNRAS.404 . The optical/NIR afterglow was affected by about 2 mag host-galaxy visual extinction along the line of sight Berger2009ApJ...704..877B , indicating that this is a dust-rich host. Optical spectra revealed a global SFR between about 1 and 100 M _ yr ^ 1 ; internal host-galaxy extinction made a more robust measurement difficult Kocevski2010MNRAS.404 . According to , the SFR is 2.5 .

gmw_00012143.name: GRB 080123 host-galaxy candidate G1
gmw_00012143.overview: GRB 080123 host-galaxy candidate G1 is an inclined, barred spiral galaxy located at a redshift of z = 0.496 [arXiv:1910.14505]. The galaxy features two symmetric spiral arms and has an estimated star formation rate of 0.7 solar masses per year [arXiv:1910.14505].

sources.summary: The paper identifies G1 as the likely GRB 080123 host: a z=0.496 inclined barred spiral with symmetric arms, emission-line spectroscopy, and H-based SFR of 0.7 solar masses per year.

quotes.quote: The most prominent object in the field is a galaxy (G1, z =0.496; R_C =19.6 mag) 8 arcsec north-east of the center of the XRT error circle Leibler2010 . It is the most likely host-galaxy candidate. The FORS2 R_C band image reveals that this is an inclined, barred spiral with two symmetric spiral arms.
The spectrum shows emission lines of H , H , and at a common redshift of z =0.496, in agreement with what was reported by .
The (not extinction-corrected) SFR based on the measured H line flux in the FORS2 spectrum is 0.7 M _ yr ^ 1 .

gmw_00012144.name: host of GRB 100206A
gmw_00012144.overview: The host galaxy of GRB 100206A is a starburst galaxy located at a redshift of z = 0.407 [arXiv:1910.14505]. Radio observations of the galaxy reveal a star formation rate of approximately 60 solar masses per year [arXiv:1910.14505].

sources.summary: The paper reports that the only radio-detected short-GRB host in the sample is GRB 100206A at z=0.407, already known as a starburst; its radio flux implies SFR about 60.

quotes.quote: Only one host galaxy was detected, the one of GRB 100206A at z =0.407. However, its starburst nature was already known from optical/IR data. Its measured radio flux can be interpreted as being due to a star formation rate (SFR) of about 60 . This is in good agreement with earlier expectations based on the observed broad-band spectral energy distribution of this galaxy.
Using the imfit task under CASA, at 5.5 GHz we measure a flux density of F_ = 65 11 Jy, centered at R.A., Decl. (J2000) = 03:08:39.148 0.017 s, 13:09:29.28 0.15 arcsec.

gmw_00012145.name: host galaxy of GRB 100816A
gmw_00012145.overview: The host galaxy of GRB 100816A is a massive, bulge-dominated galaxy at a redshift of z=0.8049 [arXiv:1910.14505]. Viewed nearly face-on, it exhibits a morphologically disturbed structure, high dust content, and a starburst-like spectral energy distribution [arXiv:1910.14505].

sources.summary: The paper describes the GRB 100816A host as a face-on, disturbed, bulge-dominated dusty galaxy at z=0.8049; spectra and photometry indicate high SFR, high metallicity, large stellar mass, and starburst-like SED.

quotes.quote: The afterglow was situated in the northern region of its host, about 1.3 '' away from the host galaxy center (for the given redshift this corresponds to about 10 kpc projected distance). In the combined GROND white-band the galaxy appears morphologically disturbed in north-east direction. It is a bulge-dominated galaxy seen nearly face-on (G1; Fig. fig:100816 ). The host galaxy of GRB 100816A was also detected by in the W1 and W2 bands, but not in W3 and W4. Based on VLT/X-Shooter spectra, Kruhler2015 find that the host is a dusty galaxy ( E(B-V)_ host = 1.32 (+0.24, 0.22) mag) with an SFR of 58^ +51 _ 26 M _ yr ^ 1 and a metallicity of 12+log(O/H)=8.75 ^ +0.16 _ 0.18 .

The galaxy was also studied by Perez2013EAS61 using the Spanish 10m Gran Telescopio CANARIAS (GTC) and the telescopes at Calar Alto. These authors found that the SED is best fit by a starburst galaxy. According to our GROND photometry, this is a luminous (M _B=- 21.7 mag), dusty ( E(B-V)_ host = 0.4 mag), massive (log M/M _ = 10.4 0.4) galaxy with an SFR between 40 and 400 M _ yr ^ 1 (Fig. fig:100816 ).

gmw_00012146.name: host galaxy of GRB 130603B
gmw_00012146.overview: The host galaxy of GRB 130603B is a very dusty star-forming galaxy located at a redshift of z = 0.3565 [arXiv:1910.14505]. It has a global visual extinction of approximately 1.3 mag and an extinction-corrected star formation rate of 2 to 5 solar masses per year [arXiv:1910.14505].

sources.summary: The paper describes the GRB 130603B host as a dusty star-forming galaxy at z=0.3565 with significant line-of-sight and global extinction, extinction-corrected SFR of 2-5, and no detected 5.5 GHz host flux.

quotes.quote: The X-ray/optical afterglow and the host galaxy has been studied in detail by several groups Cucchiara2013ApJ,Fong2014ApJ...780,Ugarte2014 . These authors found that the afterglow was located in a star-forming galaxy at z =0.3565. The galaxy is very dusty. The line-of-sight extinction to the optical afterglow was about A_V (host)=0.9 mag, while the global visual extinction of this galaxy is about 1.3 mag. Its extinction-corrected SFR is about 2 to 5 M _ yr ^ 1 .
Our non-detection of this galaxy at 5.5 GHz is in agreement with the predicted radio flux of the host in model 'MS5' discussed by her figure 4 Contini2018 .

gmw_00012147.name: GRB 150424A host-galaxy candidate G1
gmw_00012147.overview: GRB 150424A host-galaxy candidate G1 is a star-forming spiral galaxy located at a redshift of z = 0.2981 [arXiv:1910.14505]. It has an estimated stellar mass of approximately 10^10 solar masses and exhibits internal reddening [arXiv:1910.14505].

sources.summary: The paper identifies G1 as a spiral galaxy near the GRB 150424A transient at z=0.2981; spectroscopy and SED fitting indicate star formation, internal reddening, and stellar mass near 10^10 solar masses.

quotes.quote: Follow-up observations performed with the Spanish GTC 10m telescope (programme ID GTC72-15A; PI: A. Castro-Tirado) revealed the presence of a spiral galaxy 2 arcsec south-west to the optical transient G1; see figure 1 in Jin2018ApJ...857..128J and figure 2 in Knust2017A&A...607A..84K . Castro-Tirado2015GCN17758 reported a redshift of z = 0.3 for G1. In a reanalysis of this spectrum we found emission from H , as well as the doublet at a common redshift of z = 0.2981 0.0001 . The flux in the H line is more prominent in the western part than in the eastern part of the galaxy (28 10^ 17 erg s ^ 1 cm ^ 2 vs. 13 10^ 17 erg s ^ 1 cm ^ 2 ). In total its flux corresponds to a SFR of 0.15 .
Fixing the redshift to z =0.3, Le Phare finds that this is a star-forming galaxy (SFR 7 M _ yr ^ 1 ) with an internal reddening of E(B-V)_ host = 0.25 mag. Its mass in stars is about 10 ^ 10 M _ .

gmw_00012148.name: J132416.13+274411.6
gmw_00012148.overview: J132416.13+274411.6 is a Lyman-alpha emitting galaxy located at a high redshift of z=5.70 [arXiv:1612.00902]. Narrowband imaging of the galaxy has revealed a tentative 2-sigma flux excess in the CIII] emission line [arXiv:1612.00902].

sources.summary: The paper targets the z=5.70 LAE J132416.13+274411.6 with HST narrowband imaging, finding a tentative CIII] flux excess and setting upper limits on CIII] flux and equivalent width.

quotes.quote: To detect the CIII ] line for brightest LAE at z=5.7 , J132416.13+274411.6 (source 1, see Table tab:sxdf ), we use six-orbits ( 16000 seconds integration time) F128N imaging in the SDF field. Deep HST F110W and F160W broadband imaging of the brightest galaxy (source 1) have already been conducted in , and we use the F110W and F160W imaging to determine the continuum level and continuum slope of source 1.

After subtracting the continuum, we obtain the residual flux of the galaxy F_ CIII ] = 3.34 1.81 10^ 18 \ erg \ s^ 1 \ cm^ 2 . The residual flux is calculated by F_ CIII ] = (F_ F128N F_ F125W )* FWHM_ F128N . There is a tentative 2- excess of flux measured in F128N compared to the continuum level. We put a 3- upper limit of F_ CIII ] 5.43 10^ 18 \ erg \ s^ 1 \ cm^ 2 and we place a 3- upper limit for EW of 6.57 \ .

gmw_00012149.name: NGC 5557-E2
gmw_00012149.overview: NGC 5557-E2 is a likely old tidal dwarf galaxy candidate located along a tidal tail [arXiv:1403.0626]. It is characterized by a low surface brightness, an exponential light profile, a large effective radius of 1.8 kpc, and a lack of ionized gas [arXiv:1403.0626].

sources.summary: NGC 5557-E2 is treated as a likely old tidal dwarf candidate in the same tail as E1, with an exponential profile, large radius, low surface brightness, and retained gas.

quotes.quote: As examples, the light profiles of NGC 5557-E1 and NGC 5557-E2 are shown in Fig. lprof . They are well fitted by close-to-exponential S ersic profiles Sersic68 , with an index n of resp. 1.3 and 0.6.

With an effective radius as large as resp. 2.3 and 1.8 kpc, NGC 5557-E1/E2 occupy, like the young TDGs, the upper locus of the size--mass relation. Besides, the two dwarfs also stand out in the central surface brightness versus stellar mass relation, lying on the low-surface-brightness edge of the relation.

We could not estimate the metallicity of its companion galaxy, NGC 5557-E2, because of its very low surface brightness and lack of ionized gas. However, given its location, further out along the same tidal tail, its \ velocity close to that of E1, it has most likely the same tidal origin. This might also be the case for NGC 5557-E3, located at the apparent tip of the tidal tail; however, its very perturbed, elongated morphology raise doubts on whether this condensation is gravitationally bound -- another characteristic to warrant a TDG status. Thus, in the following for simplicity, we will call E1 and E2 confirmed old TDG candidates, although only the metallicity of E1 could be derived.

gmw_00012150.name: NGC 5557-E3
gmw_00012150.overview: NGC 5557-E3 is a faint, blue object located at the apparent tip of the eastern tidal tail of the galaxy NGC 5557 [arXiv:1403.0626]. It exhibits a highly perturbed, elongated morphology, raising doubts about whether the structure is gravitationally bound [arXiv:1403.0626].

sources.summary: NGC 5557-E3 is a faint object in NGC 5557 tidal debris; the paper notes no extracted spectrum, disturbed morphology, and uncertain bound tidal-dwarf status.

quotes.quote: Its eastern tidal tail hosts three blue objects, and associated with them, three isolated \ clouds, referred later as NGC 5557-E1, E2 and E3.

The absence of emission lines in NGC 5557-E2 and NGC 5557-E3 (despite the presence of \ gas) did not allow us to determine their optical redshift. Furthermore due to the low surface brightness of these galaxies, no stellar continuum could be extracted. Note however that the \ velocities of E2 and E3 also match that of E1, suggesting that they all belong to the same structure Duc11 .

This might also be the case for NGC 5557-E3, located at the apparent tip of the tidal tail; however, its very perturbed, elongated morphology raise doubts on whether this condensation is gravitationally bound -- another characteristic to warrant a TDG status.

gmw_00012151.name: SDSS0119+1452
gmw_00012151.overview: SDSS0119+1452 is an asymmetric galaxy classified as S0-Sa with a blue, clumpy center and no discernable spiral arms [arXiv:1412.4739]. It exhibits rotation and features an excess H I clump off its southwest corner, as well as an optical companion that is undetected in H I [arXiv:1412.4739].

sources.summary: The paper describes SDSS0119+1452 as a rotating, asymmetric LCBG with a southwest H I clump and an optical companion undetected in H I.

quotes.quote: SDSS0119+1452 (NGC 469) is a galaxy that shows rotation in its Moment 1 map (though it has a narrow linewidth), but also has an excess clump of H I off of its southwest corner (no similar feature appears in the SDSS optical images). Its axis of rotation in the Moment 1 map coincides with what appears to be the optical minor axis. Its optical morphology looks asymmetric, with a blue, clumpy center and no discernable spiral arms. Hyperleda classifies it as S0-Sa. NED identifies a companion (NGC 471) about 8 ( 90 R_ eff (B) ) away that is visible in SDSS images but is not detected in our H I observations.

gmw_00012152.name: SDSS0125+0110
gmw_00012152.overview: SDSS0125+0110 is an isolated rotating Sb-type disk galaxy located within the NGC 533 group [arXiv:1412.4739]. It exhibits a relatively undisturbed morphology with a redder center and has no nearby companion galaxies [arXiv:1412.4739].

sources.summary: The paper presents SDSS0125+0110 as an isolated rotating galaxy in the NGC 533 group, relatively undisturbed but slightly outside the strict LCBG cuts.

quotes.quote: SDSS0125+0110 (ARK 044) is a rotating galaxy in the NGC 533 group. It has no nearby companions. It has the least-disturbed morphology of all of the galaxies in our sample, and a redder center than most of the other LCBGs discussed here. This galaxy was not in the original CG04 sample, and following the SDSS DR7 photometry, it does not fall within the LCBG criteria defined by W04 ; it is slightly redder and less compact. Hyperleda classifies this galaxy as Sb.

gmw_00012153.name: SDSS0728+3532
gmw_00012153.overview: SDSS0728+3532 is a rotating Sbc disk galaxy [arXiv:1412.4739]. It features a large, low-column-density neutral hydrogen tidal tail extending to the south, which likely results from an interaction with a nearby blue galaxy [arXiv:1412.4739].

sources.summary: The paper describes SDSS0728+3532 as a rotating Sbc LCBG with an extended low-column H I tidal tail and nearby blue galaxies linked to the H I emission.

quotes.quote: SDSS0728+3532 (ARK 134) is a rotating galaxy with a large, low column density H I tidal tail extending to the south. The tail appears to be caused by an interaction with at least one other blue galaxy (SDSS0728+3531), which optically coincides with the location of the H I emission of the tail (though it does not have an obvious velocity component in the Moment 1 map distinct from the tidal tail, so we cannot confirm that it is the cause of the tidal tail). More H I emission is detected to the west of SDSS0728+3532 in our observations, which coincides with another blue galaxy in SDSS images. There is no optical emission corresponding to the the tidal tail visible in SDSS images. Hyperleda classifies SDSS0728+3532 as an Sbc.

gmw_00012154.name: SDSS0934+0014
gmw_00012154.overview: SDSS0934+0014 is a disturbed, possibly face-on Sa galaxy [arXiv:1412.4739]. It has a companion to the southeast and a blue companion to the north, with velocity gradients suggesting that an interaction may be taking place [arXiv:1412.4739].

sources.summary: The paper describes SDSS0934+0014 as a disturbed, possibly face-on Sa galaxy with nearby companions, disturbed rotation, and evidence that interaction may be occurring.

quotes.quote: SDSS0934+0014 (UGC 05097) has a disturbed morphology and may be face-on, as its rotation curve is difficult to fit (though Hyperleda gives its optical inclination as 68 ^ ). It has a companion about 11 ( 123 R_ eff (B) ) to the southeast, and also has a blue companion 1 ( 11 R_ eff (B) ) to the north (UGC 05099) that is not detected in either the low-resolution or high-resolution maps. SDSS0934+0014 and its northern companion are not obviously interacting in the SDSS optical image, but SDSS0934+0014 shows much more disturbed rotation than its H I profile indicates, so interaction is possible. There is a steeper gradient in both velocity contours and velocity dispersion contours near the border of the two galaxies, which could also indicate that some interaction is taking place. The H I profile at the location of the interaction does appear to have multiple components.

gmw_00012155.name: SDSS0936+0106
gmw_00012155.overview: SDSS0936+0106 is a non-disturbed, rotating disk galaxy classified as Sb [arXiv:1412.4739]. Although it has a blue companion galaxy to the northwest, there is no H I evidence of interaction between the two systems [arXiv:1412.4739].

sources.summary: The paper presents SDSS0936+0106 as an ordered, centrally peaked rotating galaxy with a nearby blue companion but no H I evidence of interaction.

quotes.quote: SDSS0936+0106 (CGCG 007-009), like SDSS0125+0110, is a non-disturbed, non-interacting rotating galaxy. Unlike SDSS0125+0110, SDSS0936+0106 does have a blue companion galaxy about 5 ( 44 R_ eff (B) ) to the northwest. There does not seem to be any evidence of the two galaxies interacting from the H I moment maps. Its H I emission is centrally peaked, its rotation is ordered, and its velocity dispersion is higher at the center and lower at the edges. Hyperleda does not have a classification for SDSS0936+0106, though NED classifies it as Sb.

gmw_00012156.name: SDSS1319+5203
gmw_00012156.overview: SDSS1319+5203 is a luminous compact blue galaxy classified as an Sb-Sbc spiral [arXiv:1412.4739]. It belongs to a trio of blue galaxies that share a common neutral hydrogen envelope, and its velocity field indicates an ongoing interaction with its dominant companion [arXiv:1412.4739].

sources.summary: The paper describes SDSS1319+5203 as the LCBG in a three-galaxy H I envelope where the velocity field indicates interaction with the dominant companion.

quotes.quote: SDSS1319+5203 (SBS 1317+523B) is one of a trio of blue galaxies sharing a common H I envelope as seen at low resolution. The other two galaxies are not LCBGs. One, Mrk 251, is blue and compact, though it is too faint to be classified as an LCBG. The other, SBS 1317+523A, is blue and bright, but its surface brightness is too low to qualify for LCBG status.

SBS 1317+523B is included in the CG04 sample, but all three galaxies are well within the 9 GBT beam, so contamination from both galaxies likely affects their measurements. Hyperleda classifies SBS 1317+523B as Sb-Sbc, and Mrk 251 as Sab. The galaxy group is dominated by SBS 1317+523A and the velocity field for the system shows interaction between it and SBS 1317+523B.

gmw_00012157.name: SDSS1402+0955
gmw_00012157.overview: SDSS1402+0955 is a bright, blue galaxy classified as an elliptical galaxy in a multiple-galaxy system [arXiv:1412.4739]. Although it exhibits regular rotation and has no detected H I companions, its asymmetrical optical light and diffuse stellar halo suggest a possible past interaction or merger [arXiv:1412.4739].

sources.summary: The paper describes SDSS1402+0955 as a bright blue galaxy with regular rotation, no H I companion, asymmetric optical light, and possible past interaction.

quotes.quote: SDSS1402+0955 (NGC 5414) is a bright, blue galaxy that does not appear to have any companions detected in H I , though in an SDSS image there appear to be several nearby galaxies in the plane of the sky. Its velocity field shows that it appears to be regularly rotating. Hyperleda classifies it as an elliptical galaxy in a multiple-galaxy system, though NED does not have a galaxy with velocity information at a similar velocity to SDSS1402+0955 within 5 of the LCBG.

The SDSS optical image does appear asymmetrical, with a diffuse stellar halo that may show signatures of past interaction. The H I image shows a bit of asymmetry on the receding edge, so it is possible that a past interaction or merger could have upset the galaxy's rotation enough to make rotation curve fitting difficult, even if position-velocity plots look relatively undisturbed.

gmw_00012158.name: SDSS1507+5511
gmw_00012158.overview: SDSS1507+5511 is classified as a rotating compact Sbc disk galaxy [arXiv:1412.4739]. It features centrally peaked H I emission and an excess of gas above its rotation plane, which points toward a faint blue companion galaxy to the northeast [arXiv:1412.4739].

sources.summary: The paper presents SDSS1507+5511 as a rotating compact Sbc galaxy with central H I, excess gas above the rotation plane, and an H I-detected blue companion.

quotes.quote: SDSS1507+5511 (UGC 09737) is a rotating galaxy with an excess of gas above its plane of rotation. It has a faint blue companion about 9 ( 69 R_ eff (B) ) to the northeast (in the direction of the excess gas) that is detected in our H I observations. Hyperleda classifies UGC 09737 as a compact Sbc galaxy. Its H I emission is centrally peaked, with its peak coinciding with the optical emission of the galaxy. While its velocity field shows rotation, it is difficult to fit a rotation curve to this galaxy without large errors given the spatial resolution of our observations.

gmw_00012159.name: OQQ J1051+3241
gmw_00012159.overview: OQQ J1051+3241 is an active galactic nucleus classified as an optically quiescent quasar [arXiv:2401.01937]. X-ray modeling reveals that the source is obscured, lacks a detected Fe K emission line, and displays an optical spectrum with a slight rise toward the blue end [arXiv:2401.01937].

sources.summary: The paper models OQQ J1051+3241 as a more obscured OQQ than J0751, lacking significant Fe K emission and showing a slightly bluer optical spectrum.

quotes.quote: One serendipitous OQQ had sufficient \ counts across two observations (0781410101 and 0781410201; both only observed the source with the PN detector) to be analysed further with . We fit this source (OQQ J1051+3241) with several models, as was done with the targeted observations of OQQ J0751+4028 see Section sec:J0751xray and greenwell_xmm_2022 . We find the best fit model to be borus02 balokovic_new_2018 , at fixed angle of inclination assuming viewing through the torus ( =0.05 ). We do not find a significant Fe K emission line, and allowing the angle of inclination to vary does not favour any angle strongly. This object is more obscured and has a higher than J0751: log ( \ / cm ^ 2 ) = 23.26 0.06 , =2.05 0.2 (see Figure fig:xrayobscomp , top panel).

The \ spectrum of OQQ J1051+3241 is shown in Figure fig:xrayobscomp_optspec (top spectrum). In this case it shows a slight rise towards the blue end, implying that this is not as strictly typical \ of an OQQ as OQQ J0751+4028.

gmw_00012160.name: OQQ J1208+1159
gmw_00012160.overview: OQQ J1208+1159 is an optically quiescent quasar that exhibits high mid-infrared variability [arXiv:2401.01937]. It is flagged as a BZB blazar candidate and is detected as a compact radio source [arXiv:2401.01937].

sources.summary: The paper identifies OQQ J1208+1159 as the highest-variability OQQ, flagged as a BZB and radio-detected, making a blazar interpretation plausible.

quotes.quote: Figure fig:wisevarhigh shows the fitting with the OQQ showing highest variability, OQQ J1208+1159. This source is flagged as a WGS BZB (see Figure fig:blazarstripbzb ), and is detected in \ becker_first_1995 as a compact source with integrated flux 67.31 mJy . This combined with the MIR variability suggests that it may be a blazar.

gmw_00012161.name: C153
gmw_00012161.overview: C153 is a fast-moving, comet-like galaxy located in the core of the galaxy cluster A2125 [arXiv:astro-ph/0412359]. It features a distinct, cool X-ray emitting tail on one side, which was formed by ram pressure stripping as the galaxy crossed the central region of the cluster [arXiv:astro-ph/0412359].

sources.summary: The paper describes C153 as a fast-moving comet-like galaxy in A2125 with a cool X-ray tail probably formed by ram pressure stripping, modeled as a magnetized moving subclump.

quotes.quote: High resolution observations of cluster of galaxies by Chandra have revealed the existence of an X-ray emitting comet-like galaxy C153 in the core of cluster of galaxies A2125. The galaxy C153 moving fast in the cluster core has a distinct X-ray tail on one side, obviously due to ram pressure stripping, since the galaxy C153 crossed the central region of A2125. The X-ray emitting plasma in the tail is substantially cooler than the ambient plasma. We present results of two-dimensional magnetohydrodynamic simulations of the time evolution of a subclump like C153 moving in magnetized intergalactic matter.

gmw_00012162.overview: The active galactic nucleus 2047+098 has no known optical counterpart [arXiv:1603.03882]. Also identified as 3FGL J2049.7+1002, it is classified as a BL Lacertae object with a tentative redshift of 0.226 [arXiv:1701.06363], while its jet displays two inward-moving features [arXiv:1603.03882].

sources.summary: The paper reports SAAO spectroscopy of 3FGL J2049.7+1002, notes tentative Ca II absorption and z=0.226, and uses the featureless spectrum to classify the source as a BL Lac.

quotes.quote: 3FGL J2049.7+1002 3FGL J2049.7+1002 was observed with the SAAO 1.9-m. The averaged spectrum displayed in Fig. fig:J2049 yields potential Ca ii H K absorption lines at 4824 and 4859 . This is, however, tentative given the low signal to noise. If these line identifications are correct the redshift is z=0.226 0.001 . Based on the featureless spectrum we suggest that 2FGL J2049.8+1001 is a BL Lac.

sources.summary: The paper notes that 2047+098 is an AGN without a known optical counterpart, has an unreferenced 2LAC redshift, and shows two inward-moving jet features.

quotes.quote: 2047+098 (PKS 2047+098): This AGN has no known optical counterpart. The value z = 0.01513 listed in the 2LAC 2LAC data table is unreferenced. Its jet has two inward moving features (id = 2 and 3).

gmw_00012163.name: J0921
gmw_00012163.overview: J0921 is a distant starburst dwarf galaxy that contains a complex structure of multiple scattered H II regions [arXiv:2402.12497]. Direct-method oxygen abundance measurements across these regions show a large spread, indicating that the galaxy may be chemically inhomogeneous [arXiv:2402.12497].

sources.summary: The paper analyzes J0921's complex HII-region structure, selecting 23 regions despite resolution limits and comparing direct-method abundances that show a large spread, suggesting chemical inhomogeneity.

quotes.quote: The emission maps of J0921 reveal a more complex structure of multiple scattered HII regions that are less well defined by eye, likely due to it being the most distant galaxy selected. For an initial estimate of the number of regions within this galaxy, we consider the analysis of J0921 carried out by J20 (hereafter referred to as J20 ), who found 30 HII regions with the same MUSE data.
This results in 23 HII regions for J0921, though we note that four regions are smaller than the spatial resolution of the observations, increasing the uncertainty in the properties we have derived.

For J0921, there are 10 HII regions cross-matched to those in J20 with direct method abundances, and for Leo P we take the value given by McQuinn_2015 . In Figure fig:cdf o abundance , we plot the oxygen abundances determined from the different calibrations against the direct method abundances for the 10 corresponding regions in J0921 and the single region in Leo P. Here we see that the S calibration produces values of 12+log(O/H) (abbreviated to (O/H) _ S cal in Table tab:results ), closest to those available that are determined via the direct method. The direct method and S calibration abundance values also show a similarly large spread for J0921 ( 0.6 dex), indicating that this galaxy may be particularly chemically inhomogeneous, as discussed in J20 .

gmw_00012164.name: KKH046
gmw_00012164.overview: KKH046 is a dwarf starburst galaxy that hosts active star formation [arXiv:2402.12497]. Observations of the galaxy have identified six distinct H II regions, which surround visible ionized gas bubbles [arXiv:2402.12497].

sources.summary: The paper analyzes KKH046 with improved MUSE AO seeing, tuned HII-region thresholds, and tests against contaminated low-threshold structures, yielding reasonable ionized bubbles and six selected HII regions.

quotes.quote: We note that the seeing for the observations of KKH046 were considerably improved by the use of the VLT/MUSE Adaptive Optics (AO) system.

For KKH046 and Leo P, we set the minimum number of pixels within a region ( N_ min ) to be comparable to the size of the seeing disc for the reported seeing of each observation, and we set the minimum flux threshold ( F_ min ) to twice the standard deviation of the H flux within each galaxy. These F_ min values correspond to emission measures of 120 pc cm ^ 6 , which are greater than the cutoff values used in McLeod2021 for NGC 300 ( 60 pc cm ^ 6 ) and in Della_Bruna_2021 for NGC 7793 ( 82 pc cm ^ 6 ). However, a test of lower thresholds for KKH046 produced large, irregularly shaped structures and multiple small leaves found to have negative electron densities, which could indicate potential contamination from DIG. We find that the criteria stated above produce regions of reasonable size around each visibly distinguishable ionised gas bubble within KKH046 and Leo P, that is, greater than the spatial resolution of the observations and comparable to similar studies of extragalactic HII regions.
Overall, our selection strategy produces 23 HII regions within J0921, six within KKH046 and one in Leo P, with sizes ranging from 22 to 270 pc.

gmw_00012165.name: Leo P
gmw_00012165.overview: Leo P is a dwarf galaxy that contains a single identified H II region [arXiv:2402.12497]. This low-metallicity region is powered by a single late O-type star and exhibits high direct radiation pressure [arXiv:2402.12497].

sources.summary: The paper analyzes one HII region in Leo P, using SLUG modeling tied to a known late O-type star and finding unusually high direct radiation pressure compared with the sample.

quotes.quote: We plot models for 12+log(O/H) ranging from 7.0 to 8.0, although we note that the models in this database have a gap between 7.0-7.4, which is the metallicity range that covers Leo P. In general, however, we find that the flux ratios of the regions studied in this work appear to be comparable to low-metallicity photoionisation models, supporting their classification as HII regions. Overall, our selection strategy produces 23 HII regions within J0921, six within KKH046 and one in Leo P, with sizes ranging from 22 to 270 pc.

This parameter is of particular interest for our investigation of Leo P, since pre-existing studies ( Evans2019 ; Telford2021, Telford2023 ) have identified a single O-type star within this HII region of mass 22^ +7 _ 5 M_ and age 7^ +4 _ 3 Myr ( Telford2021 ). To make an estimate from SLUG , we create 20 libraries each of 1000 simulations and find the best photometric match for each library to the star cluster in the Leo P HII region.
We find that the HII region SMC N76 and the region within Leo P show a significantly higher P_ dir than the other regions in this sample.

gmw_00012166.name: LEDA 41136
gmw_00012166.overview: LEDA 41136 is a dwarf elliptical galaxy [arXiv:2308.01957]. Its field of view contains many stars projected onto the same line of sight [arXiv:2308.01957].

sources.summary: LEDA 41136 is used as a dwarf elliptical test case with many projected stars; segmentation finds 54 objects, builds model windows, initializes parameters, and fits the image.

quotes.quote: segmapfit demonstrates this process with LEDA 41136, a dwarf elliptical galaxy with many stars projected onto the same line of sight. The figure shows the process of creating a segmentation map, here using SourceExtractor Bertin1996 with default parameters (except the bright star on the left which was manually fixed due to shredding). In total, 54 objects are detected in the image and added to the model seven times each (once for each band) for the various instruments/telescopes. This segmentation map serves as input to which then constructs windows minimally enclosing each segment. These windows are then expanded by a factor of two and enlarged with a border of 20 more pixels to ensure all light is included in the window into the LSB regime.

automatically initializes every object in the field with reasonable starting position, ellipticity, position angle (PA), and other parameters. then fits the parameters and produces the resulting image (see examplemultiband ). Note that we fit an image with a single prominent galaxy here for demonstration purposes, can operate on an arbitrary number of galaxies and stars.

gmw_00012167.name: ESO 479-G1
gmw_00012167.overview: ESO 479-G1 is a nearby spiral galaxy [arXiv:2308.01957]. Photometric modeling of the galaxy reveals the presence of both a central bar and inner spiral arms [arXiv:2308.01957].

sources.summary: ESO 479-G1 is a nearby spiral used for a spline-warp galaxy fit; the model reveals a bar and inner spiral arms while optimizing 115 parameters.

quotes.quote: spline demonstrates an example spline warp galaxy model fit to ESO 479-G1, a nearby spiral galaxy. Clearly visible in the model are a bar and inner spiral arms, demonstrating the expressive power of this numerical representation. The SB profile is quite deep, much like that shown in Figure 6 of Stone2021b using the previous version of . However, unlike the previous version, can model the other objects in the image simultaneously to achieve greater depth on the image for the main galaxy. Within this framework, one can also fit detailed spline models for overlapping galaxies.

It took approximately 5 minutes for the model to converge for spline on an NVIDIA A100 GPU. Since this is a spline warp model, the light profile, PA and axis ratio all are represented by splines meaning the number of parameters grows quickly. In this case there are 115 parameters in total which are optimized on 490,000 pixels (700 by 700), including 25 parameters for each spline, one for the sky, and the parameters for the interloping S e rsic galaxies.

gmw_00012168.name: GLEAM J033023--074052
gmw_00012168.overview: GLEAM J033023--074052 is an active galactic nucleus with a compact radio source that is unresolved on milliarcsecond scales [arXiv:2012.01842]. Its changing radio spectral shape and compact linear size of less than 45 parsecs suggest it is likely a blazar with jets oriented toward the observer [arXiv:2012.01842].

sources.summary: The paper classifies GLEAM J033023--074052 as changing spectral shape, notes VLBI compactness below 45 pc, and interprets its compact variable radio source as a likely blazar.

quotes.quote: For instance, GLEAM J033023--074052 was classified peaked-spectrum by Callingham_2017 but is classified as changing spectral shape between epochs. VLBI observations of GLEAM J033023--074052 found it was unresolved on milliarcssecond-scales, and constrained its projected linear size to be <45 pc keim2019extragalactic .

The compact linear size and changing spectral shape over a year-long period is consistent with the jets from the AGN being oriented towards the observer (i.e. a blazar), as opposed to a small double source that would be expected for a young AGN. The radio SED for GLEAM J033023--074052 is shown in Figure fig:J033023 .

gmw_00012169.name: GLEAM J013243--165444
gmw_00012169.overview: GLEAM J013243--165444 is a known blazar and active galactic nucleus that is variable in both GLEAM surveys [arXiv:2012.01842]. Although it exhibits a change in its spectral shape, the source maintains a peaked spectrum with a peak at 150 MHz [arXiv:2012.01842].

sources.summary: The paper identifies GLEAM J013243--165444 as a known blazar in the master sample, variable in both surveys, with significant spectral-shape change and a persistent 150 MHz peaked spectrum.

quotes.quote: The one source identified as variable, GLEAM J013243--165444, is also in our master population. We also classify GLEAM J013243--165444 as variable source with significant change in spectral shape with a VIP of 122 and a MOSS parameter of 85 . Despite its blazar identification and variability in both surveys, GLEAM J013243--165444 maintains a peaked spectrum in both years of GLEAM observations with a peak at 150 MHz.

gmw_00012170.name: Bo o tes V
gmw_00012170.overview: Boötes V is an ultra-faint dwarf galaxy candidate located at a heliocentric distance of approximately 100 kpc [arXiv:2209.08242]. It has a physical half-light radius of 26.9 pc and contains highly metal-poor stars [arXiv:2209.08242].

sources.summary: The paper presents Bo o tes V as a new UNIONS ultra-faint dwarf galaxy candidate, a resolved stellar overdensity at about 100 kpc with Gaia-supported membership and metal-poor spectroscopy.
sources.paper_coordinate_text: Right Ascension (RA) of 14h 15m 38.6s and a Declination (Dec) of +32 \ 54 \ 40

quotes.quote: We present the discovery of , a new ultra-faint dwarf galaxy candidate. This satellite is detected as a resolved overdensity of stars during an ongoing search for new Local Group dwarf galaxy candidates in the UNIONS photometric dataset. It has a physical half-light radius of 26.9 ^ +7.5 _ 5.4 pc, a V band magnitude of 4.5 0.4 mag, and resides at a heliocentric distance of approximately 100 kpc. We use Gaia DR3 astrometry to identify member stars, characterize the systemic proper motion, and confirm the reality of this faint stellar system.

The brightest star in this system was followed up using Gemini GMOS-N long-slit spectroscopy and is measured to have a metallicity of [ Fe/H ] = 2.85 0.10 dex and a heliocentric radial velocity of v_r = 5.1 13.4 . \ is larger (in terms of scale radius), more distant, and more metal-poor than the vast majority of globular clusters. It is likely that \ is an ultra-faint dwarf galaxy, though future spectroscopic studies will be necessary to definitively classify this object.

gmw_00012171.name: Sextans B
gmw_00012171.overview: Sextans B is an irregular dwarf galaxy with a stellar mass roughly comparable to that of the Small Magellanic Cloud [arXiv:2103.07476]. It is located in the NGC 3109 association, which is a very loose group of dwarf galaxies [arXiv:2103.07476].

sources.summary: Sextans B is selected as an SMC-mass irregular dwarf Magellanic analog in the NGC 3109 association; DELVE-DEEP completed imaging with reduced 3-degree coverage because of its proximity.

quotes.quote: DELVE-DEEP targets four isolated dwarf galaxies at 1.4--2.1 ( lumdist and deep_targets ). These galaxies were chosen as MC analogs: NGC 55 and NGC 300 have stellar masses roughly comparable to the LMC, while Sextans B and IC 5152 are within an order of magnitude of the SMC ( 0.1-0.8 M_ , SMC ). NGC 55 is an irregular, barred spiral galaxy deVaucouleurs:1991 ; therefore, it closely resembles the LMC not only in stellar mass but also in morphology. Unlike the LMC, NGC 55 is almost edge-on, which makes it well suited for stellar content studies. NGC 300 is an almost pure disk galaxy and is similar to M33, an LMC-mass satellite of M31. Both NGC 55 and NGC 300 belong to the nearby Sculptor Group, which is actually an unbound filamentary structure extended along our line of sight Karachentsev:2003 . Both Sextans B and IC 5152 are irregular dwarf galaxies. While Sextans B is a member of a very loose group of dwarf galaxies (the NGC 3109 association; Tully:2006 ), IC 5152 is an exceptionally isolated object, with the nearest neighbor being NGC 55 at a distance of 800 kpc Karachentsev:2002 .

Covering this large of a region around Sextans B is prohibitive given the DELVE-DEEP time allocation, and the coverage around Sextans B was reduced to 3 . Observations of each target start with the central pointing and progress outward. Small dithers of 2 are applied in a hexagonal pattern to each tiling to cover the gaps between CCDs. Star--galaxy separation is critical to the DELVE-DEEP science, and observations are performed only when the i band zenith PSF is estimated to have FWHM < 0 9 . DELVE imaging for Sextans B was completed in 2019A, and imaging of NGC 55 is 78 complete as of 2020B.

gmw_00012172.name: target 4
gmw_00012172.overview: The high-redshift quasar target 4 is surrounded by extended emission that exhibits a distinct east-west velocity split [arXiv:1911.10740]. This surrounding nebula is among the largest in its sample, showing high projected specific angular momentum and strong evidence of a sheared velocity map [arXiv:1911.10740].

sources.summary: Target 4, identified with SDSS1011+2941, has extended emission with an east-west velocity split, significant projected angular momentum, and strong evidence for a sheared velocity map.

quotes.quote: The vast majority of the targets do not exhibit any clear kinematic structure. However, two targets (ID 4 and ID 7) stand out from the rest of the sample in this regard. The Eastern side of the extended emission around target 4 appears to be mostly blue-shifted, while the Western side appears to be mostly red-shifted. For target 7, the South/South-East side of the nebula appears to be broadly red-shifted while the North/North-West side is mostly blue-shifted.

However, for targets 4 and 7, there is a vanishing probability that the flat model is better ( p_ flat 10^ 15 and p_ flat 10^ 21 ). Figure fig:angmom shows the detected nebulae in the parameter space of R_ eff versus | j _ f | . The size of the markers is indicative of the likelihood of a shear model being correct ( size=-Log_ 10 (p_ flat ) ). From this combined perspective, it is clear that targets 4 and 7 represent two targets which are (i) among the largest detections, (ii) have significant projected specific angular momentum, and (iii) have strong indications from the BIC values that the velocity map is sheared rather than flat.

gmw_00012173.name: target 7
gmw_00012173.overview: The high-redshift quasar target 7 is surrounded by a large extended nebula [arXiv:1911.10740]. This nebula exhibits a north-south velocity split, providing strong evidence for coherent, sheared kinematics in its circumgalactic medium [arXiv:1911.10740].

sources.summary: Target 7, identified with SDSS0958+4703, shows a north-south velocity split in its nebula and strong evidence for coherent, sheared CGM kinematics.

quotes.quote: The vast majority of the targets do not exhibit any clear kinematic structure. However, two targets (ID 4 and ID 7) stand out from the rest of the sample in this regard. The Eastern side of the extended emission around target 4 appears to be mostly blue-shifted, while the Western side appears to be mostly red-shifted. For target 7, the South/South-East side of the nebula appears to be broadly red-shifted while the North/North-West side is mostly blue-shifted.

However, for targets 4 and 7, there is a vanishing probability that the flat model is better ( p_ flat 10^ 15 and p_ flat 10^ 21 ). Figure fig:angmom shows the detected nebulae in the parameter space of R_ eff versus | j _ f | . The size of the markers is indicative of the likelihood of a shear model being correct ( size=-Log_ 10 (p_ flat ) ). From this combined perspective, it is clear that targets 4 and 7 represent two targets which are (i) among the largest detections, (ii) have significant projected specific angular momentum, and (iii) have strong indications from the BIC values that the velocity map is sheared rather than flat.

gmw_00012174.name: REBELS-15
gmw_00012174.overview: REBELS-15 is an extremely UV-luminous galaxy located at a redshift of z = 6.875 [arXiv:2202.01219]. It is a relatively weak Lyman-alpha emitter with a very low total escape fraction of approximately 0.4 percent [arXiv:2202.01219].

sources.summary: REBELS-15 is an extremely UV-luminous z=6.875 galaxy with strong inferred optical lines but weak observed Ly emission and a very low total escape fraction.

quotes.quote: REBELS-15 REBELS-15 is an extremely UV-luminous ( = -22.6 ) galaxy in the XMM3 field at = 6.875 (Schouws et al. in prep) with an inferred stellar mass of = 9.1. This source was identified as XMM3-504799 in Endsley2021_LyA and exhibits a very blue IRAC color ( [ 3.6 ] [ 4.5 ] = 1.16^ +0.32 _ 0.43 ) suggesting an EW = 4570 . At this redshift, [ OIII ] 5007 only contributes slightly to the 3.6 m excess, requiring extremely strong H and [ OIII ] 4959 emission to produce the observed IRAC color. Our Binospec data reveal a 7.5 detection with a peak wavelength at 9583.2 corresponding to = 6.883 (Fig. fig:LyA_Detections ).

The total flux is measured to be (5.5 1.0) 10 ^ 18 erg/s/cm ^2 indicating an EW of 3.7 0.8 using the VIRCam Y band photometry for the continuum flux density (0.58 0.09 Jy). REBELS-15 is thus a relatively weak Ly emitter among the UV-bright z 7 population, and is particularly weak with respect to the sub-population with strong emission (EW > 800 ; Endsley2021_LyA ). Consistent with this result, we estimate a very low total escape fraction of 0.4^ +0.2 _ 0.1 from REBELS-15.

gmw_00012175.name: REBELS-03
gmw_00012175.overview: REBELS-03 is an ultraviolet-bright galaxy located in the XMM1 field at a redshift of z = 6.969 [arXiv:2202.01219]. It has an inferred stellar mass of 10^8.9 solar masses and shows no indication of emission above 5 sigma in its Binospec spectrum [arXiv:2202.01219].

sources.summary: REBELS-03 is a UV-bright z=6.969 XMM1 galaxy with relatively weak inferred EW; Binospec finds no significant Ly emission and only poor EW limits.

quotes.quote: REBELS-03 REBELS-03 is a UV-bright ( = -21.8 ) galaxy situated in the XMM1 field at = 6.969 ( Bouwens2021_REBELS ; Schouws et al. in prep). This galaxy has an inferred stellar mass of = 8.9 and a relatively weak EW of 510 given its flat IRAC color ( [ 3.6 ] [ 4.5 ] = 0.01^ +0.22 _ 0.23 ). We have observed REBELS-03 for 3.75 hours with MMT/Binospec with clear conditions and relatively good seeing (0.73 arcsec). We find no indication of significant ( > 5 ) emission from this source after searching our Binospec spectrum in conservative wavelength range corresponding to velocity offsets betweeen 500 to 1000 km s ^ 1 (Fig. fig:LyA_nonDetections ).
Due to the presence of a strong skylines around the expected wavelength of , the 5 EW limit for REBELS-03 is quite poor ranging from 14.7--35.5 for the various assumed velocity offsets and FWHMs.

gmw_00012176.name: REBELS-05
gmw_00012176.overview: REBELS-05 is a ultraviolet-bright galaxy located at a redshift of 6.496 [arXiv:2202.01219]. Spectroscopic observations of the galaxy show no evidence of Lyman-alpha emission, providing stringent limits on its equivalent width [arXiv:2202.01219].

sources.summary: REBELS-05 is a z=6.496 UV-bright XMM1 galaxy with degenerate stellar-mass and EW estimates; Binospec finds no significant Ly emission and stringent EW limits.

quotes.quote: REBELS-05 REBELS-05 is another UV-bright ( = -21.6 ) galaxy in the XMM1 field that lies at = 6.496 ( Bouwens2021_REBELS ; Schouws et al. in prep) and was first identified as ID=118717 in Bowler2014 . Because this source lies at z<6.6 where both IRAC bands are contaminated by strong nebular emission lines (i.e. in [ 3.6 ] and H in [ 4.5 ] ), there is significant degeneracy between the inferred stellar mass and EW (e.g. Schaerer2010 ). The median posterior values from beagle suggest a stellar mass of = 8.9 and an EW of 1060 given its moderately blue IRAC color ( [ 3.6 ] [ 4.5 ] = 0.47^ +0.29 _ 0.35 ). REBELS-05 was observed using the same Binospec mask as REBELS-03 with a total exposure time of 3.75 hours under clear conditions and relatively good seeing (0.73 arcsec). We again search for at wavelengths corresponding to = 500 to 1000 km s ^ 1 and find no evidence of significant emission (Fig. fig:LyA_nonDetections ).

This results in 5 limiting EWs ranging between < 3.8--4.5 . Such stringent EW constraints are enabled by the fact that no strong skylines exist in the wavelength regime where is expected for REBELS-05. The upper limits on flux translate to <0.9-1.1 for REBELS-05.

gmw_00012177.name: REBELS-26
gmw_00012177.overview: REBELS-26 is a ultraviolet-bright galaxy located in the COSMOS field at a redshift of z = 6.598 [arXiv:2202.01219]. Spectroscopic observations spanning nine hours with Binospec detected no Lyman-alpha emission from the system [arXiv:2202.01219].

sources.summary: REBELS-26 is a z=6.598 UV-bright COSMOS galaxy with degenerate stellar-mass and EW estimates; nine hours of Binospec data yield no significant Ly emission.

quotes.quote: REBELS-26 REBELS-26 is a UV-bright ( = -21.8 ) galaxy in the wide-area COSMOS field at = 6.598 (Schouws et al. in prep) and was first identified as ID=104600 in Bowler2014 . Due to this redshift, the inferred stellar mass and EW of REBELS-26 are quite degenerate similar to REBELS-05. The median posterior values from beagle suggest = 9.1 and EW = 800 for REBELS-26 given its moderate IRAC color ( [ 3.6 ] [ 4.5 ] = 0.42^ +0.16 _ 0.17 ). We have observed this galaxy for 9.0 hours with Binospec under largely clear conditions and moderate seeing on average (1.09 arcsec). We find no indication of significant emission in our Binospec spectra for REBELS-26 at wavelengths corresponding to = 500 to 1000 km s ^ 1 (Fig. fig:LyA_nonDetections ).

Because no strong skylines exist around the expected wavelength of , we derive stringent 5 EW upper limits between 5.4--6.1 . Here we have used the VIRCam Y band photometry for the continuum flux density ( m = 24.98 ). The total escape fraction of this system is inferred to be <1.9-2.2 using the range of 5 upper limits on the line flux.

gmw_00012178.name: REBELS-27
gmw_00012178.overview: REBELS-27 is a ultraviolet-luminous galaxy located at a redshift of z = 7.090 in the COSMOS field [arXiv:2202.01219]. It has a relatively flat IRAC color suggesting weak emission, and observations find no indication of Lyman-alpha line emission [arXiv:2202.01219].

sources.summary: REBELS-27 is a UV-luminous z=7.090 COSMOS galaxy with weak inferred emission; Binospec finds no significant Ly line and implies low escape-fraction limits.

quotes.quote: REBELS-27 REBELS-27 is another UV-luminous ( = -21.9 ) galaxy identified across the wide-area COSMOS field located at = 7.090 ( Bouwens2021_REBELS ; Schouws et al. in prep). This source was referred to as UVISTA-Y-004 in Stefanon2017_Brightestz89,Stefanon2019 and UVISTA-301 in Bowler2020 . It has an inferred stellar mass of = 9.5 and its flat IRAC color ( [ 3.6 ] [ 4.5 ] = 0.10^ +0.20 _ 0.22 ) suggests relatively weak emission (EW = 310 ).

After searching for at wavelengths corresponding to = 500 to 1000 km s ^ 1 , we find no indication of significant line emission (Fig. fig:LyA_nonDetections ). The 5 upper limiting EW ranges between 5.6--13.9 given that a moderate-strength skyline impacts part of the relevant wavelength regime. For the continuum flux density, we use the VIRCam J band photometry ( m = 24.73 ). The total escape fraction from REBELS-27 is inferred to be <3.2-8.0 .

gmw_00012179.name: B0850+054
gmw_00012179.overview: B0850+054 is a gigahertz-peaked spectrum radio source that acts as a gravitational lens system [arXiv:astro-ph/0210504]. It consists of two compact radio images separated by 0.7 arcseconds, with a lens galaxy redshift measured at 0.58 [arXiv:astro-ph/0210504].

sources.summary: The paper presents B0850+054 as a new CLASS gravitational lens: two compact, same-spectrum radio images separated by 0.7 arcsec, with VLBA jet structure and a measured lens-galaxy redshift.

quotes.quote: Radio observations with the VLA, the WSRT and MERLIN show that the radio source B0850+054 is comprised of two compact components with identical spectra, a separation of 0.7 arcsec and a flux density ratio of 6:1. VLBA observations at 5 GHz reveal structures that are consistent with the gravitational lens hypothesis. The brighter of the two images is resolved into a linear string of at least six sub-components whilst the weaker image is radially stretched towards the lens galaxy.

We have discovered a new lens system during the course of the CLASS survey, B0850+054. It consists of two images of a GPS radio source with a separation of 0.7 arcsec and a flux density ratio of 6:1. UKIRT K band imaging does not resolve the lens galaxy and the lensed images, but a lens galaxy redshift of 0.58 has been measured with the W. M. Keck telescope.

gmw_00012180.name: J1706 + 4340
gmw_00012180.overview: J1706 + 4340 is a double-double radio galaxy with large-scale outer lobes estimated to be 260 to 300 million years old [arXiv:1608.07437]. Following a quiescent period of about 27 million years, a renewal of jet activity occurred approximately 12 million years ago [arXiv:1608.07437].

sources.summary: The paper presents multifrequency radio observations of J1706 + 4340, dating old outer lobes, renewed jet activity after quiescence, similar inner and outer jet powers, and a black-hole-spin interpretation.

quotes.quote: We report the outcome of multifrequency radio observations of a double--double radio source J1706 + 4340 carried out with the Very Large Array and Giant Metrewave Radio Telescope. After supplementing our own data with those available in the literature, we collected a considerable set of radio measurements covering the range from 74 to 8460 MHz. This has enabled us to perform a comprehensive review of physical properties of the source and its dynamical evolution analysis. In particular, we found that, while the age of the large-scale outer lobes is in the range 260 300 Myr, the renewal of the jet activity, which is directly responsible for the double--double structure, took place only about 12 Myr ago after about 27-Myr long period of quiescence. Another important property of J1706 + 4340 we found is that the injection spectral indices and the jet powers for the inner and the outer doubles are very similar.
This implies that it is the spin of the supermassive black hole rather than e.g. an instability of the accretion disc that is likely responsible for the jet production and its properties.

gmw_00012181.name: 1REXJ134105+3959.8
gmw_00012181.overview: 1REXJ134105+3959.8 is a known high-frequency peaked BL Lacertae object and blazar [arXiv:1912.07613]. Although predicted to be detectable by major Cherenkov telescopes, a brief observation by VERITAS yielded only a flux upper limit [arXiv:1912.07613].

sources.summary: The paper identifies 1REXJ134105+3959.8 as a known HBL/BL Lac Te-REX source predicted to be detectable by CTA or MAGIC; a short VERITAS exposure produced only an upper limit.

quotes.quote: Furthermore, according to this analysis we expect that three Te-REX (1REXJ121510+0732.0, 1REXJ134105+3959.8, 1REXJ141756+2543.3) could be visible even with a relatively shallow observation (5h) of CTA or 50h of MAGIC. These are three known HBL sources, also detected by Fermi-LAT and present in the BZCAT and 2WHSP catalogues, with a clear BL Lac spectrum (break below 40 ).

To the best of our knowledge, none of the Te-REX sources considered here have been detected at high energies by HESS, VERITAS or Magic (mostly because they have not been yet observed): only 1REXJ134105+3959.8, which is one of the sources that we predict to be detectable with current Cherenkov telescopes, has been observed by VERITAS for 2.7 hours, providing a flux upper limit in the VERITAS energy band archambault16 . Given the very low exposure time, the non-detection is not in contrast with our predictions, that were based on 50h of exposure time.

gmw_00012182.name: 1REXJ141756+2543.3
gmw_00012182.overview: 1REXJ141756+2543.3 is a Fermi-detected blazar classified as a BL Lacertae object [arXiv:1912.07613]. It is categorized as an extreme high-energy peaked BL Lac object with a synchrotron emission peak at 1 keV [arXiv:1912.07613].

sources.summary: The paper treats 1REXJ141756+2543.3 as a known Fermi-detected HBL with a clear BL Lac spectrum, identifying it as an extreme HBL expected to be detectable by current Cherenkov telescopes or CTA.

quotes.quote: Furthermore, according to this analysis we expect that three Te-REX (1REXJ121510+0732.0, 1REXJ134105+3959.8, 1REXJ141756+2543.3) could be visible even with a relatively shallow observation (5h) of CTA or 50h of MAGIC. These are three known HBL sources, also detected by Fermi-LAT and present in the BZCAT and 2WHSP catalogues, with a clear BL Lac spectrum (break below 40 ). 1REXJ141756+2543.3 is an extreme HBL (EHBL, costamante01 ) since the Synchrotron bump peaks at 1keV (see Fig. ehbl ).

In their sample there is also an other EHBL in common with the Te-REX sources (the previously discussed 1REXJ141756+2543.3), that we predict should be detectable by current Cherenkov telescopes in 50 hours of observations or by CTA in 5 hours.

gmw_00012183.name: 1REXJ154946-3045.0
gmw_00012183.overview: 1REXJ154946-3045.0 is an active galactic nucleus identified as a BL Lacertae object [arXiv:1912.07613]. It is a candidate for detection at TeV energies and has been associated with a Fermi gamma-ray source [arXiv:1912.07613].

sources.summary: The paper presents 1REXJ154946-3045.0 as a CTA TeV candidate and new Fermi-source identification, with radio and X-ray error circles, an optical BL Lac counterpart, and consistent high-energy positions.

quotes.quote: The Te-REX source 1REXJ154946-3045.0 is a good example of a candidate for the detection at TeV energies by CTA and, at the same time, it represents a new identification of the FERMI source 3FGL J1549.9-3044. The finding chart in Fig. astri shows the radio (NVSS) and X-ray (ROSAT PSPC) error circles that define the source as REX. The accurate radio position pin-points the optical counterpart that has been then observed at ESO 3.6m and identified as a BL Lac with a tentative redshift of z=0.25 (see the optical spectrum in Appendix spectraall ). The finding chart reports also the position of the 3FHL and 4FGL source which is fully consistent with the REX object making the association certain. In a forthcoming paper, we will evaluate the possible detectability of this interesting source at energies above 10 TeV, with the ASTRI mini-array (Saturni et al., in preparation).

gmw_00012184.name: Transient T7
gmw_00012184.overview: Transient T7 is an isolated, point-like source that lacks a nearby potential host galaxy [arXiv:2401.04944]. It is detected in both optical and X-ray bands, and its persistent detection over a two-year span suggests it is a variable quasar rather than a supernova [arXiv:2401.04944].

sources.summary: The paper describes Transient T7 as an isolated point-like source detected in optical bands and X-rays, still visible in a second visit, and therefore likely a variable quasar rather than a supernova.

quotes.quote: Transient T7 is detected in both F606W and F435W, appears point-like and isolated. It lacks any nearby potential host galaxy, although there may be a hint of signal 0 59 due E and due W, and 0 23 due S, as well as some ``fuzz'' 0 42 to the NW in both Visits that could be due to a very faint potential host or galactic companions. Unlike most transients reported here, T7 is also clearly detected in Visit 2 (2.24 yr later) and has significant X-ray detections by both \ and XMM-Newton (see Table tab:xray ). This transient is thus unlikely to be a SN, but rather a quasar that exhibits significant variation in brightness.

gmw_00012185.name: Transient T10
gmw_00012185.overview: Transient T10 is an isolated, point-like source that lacks a nearby potential host galaxy [arXiv:2401.04944]. It is detected in both the F606W and F435W bands, and its X-ray detections suggest it is a variable quasar rather than a supernova [arXiv:2401.04944].

sources.summary: The paper describes Transient T10 as an isolated point-like source seen in two optical bands, re-detected in another visit, and supported by X-ray detections as a likely variable quasar.

quotes.quote: Transient T10 is detected in both F606W and F435W, appears point-like and is isolated, lacking any nearby potential host galaxy. Unlike most transients reported here, T10 is also clearly detected in Visit 2 (0.30 yr earlier) and has significant X-ray detections by both \ and XMM-Newton (Table tab:xray ). This transient is thus unlikely to be a SNe, but rather a quasar that exhibits significant variation in brightness.

gmw_00012186.name: IRAS 20551 4250
gmw_00012186.overview: IRAS 20551 4250 is a luminous infrared galaxy located at a redshift of z=0.043 [arXiv:1308.4414]. The galaxy hosts an obscured active galactic nucleus and exhibits strong emission lines from HCN, HCO+, HNC, H2S, and CH3CN [arXiv:1308.4414].

sources.summary: ALMA observations of IRAS 20551 4250 detect HCN/HCO+/HNC J=4-3 plus H2S and CH3CN/CCH, find an elevated HCN/HCO+ ratio, and tentatively detect vibrationally excited HCN in its obscured-AGN host.

quotes.quote: We present results from our ALMA Cycle 0 observations, at the frequencies around the HCN, HCO ^ + , and HNC J=4--3 transition lines, of the luminous infrared galaxy IRAS 20551 4250 at z=0.043, which is known to host an energetically important obscured AGN. In addition to the targeted HCN, HCO ^ + , and HNC J=4--3 emission lines, two additional strong emission lines are seen, which we attribute to H _ 2 S and CH _ 3 CN (+ CCH). The HCN-to-HCO ^ + J=4--3 flux ratio ( 0.7) is higher than in the other starburst-dominated galaxy ( 0.2) observed in our ALMA Cycle 0 program. We tentatively ( 5 ) detected the vibrationally excited (v _ 2 =1) HCN J=4--3 (l=1f) emission line, which is important for testing an infrared radiative pumping scenario for HCN.

gmw_00012187.name: J0316-2953
gmw_00012187.overview: The galaxy J0316-2953 has been the subject of updated stellar content analyses [arXiv:2309.12966]. Its newly estimated age is approximately 1 Gyr older than previous determinations, while its inferred metallicity has shifted from slightly super-solar to below solar [arXiv:2309.12966].

sources.summary: The appendix notes that J0316-2953's new ages agree with DR1 within 1.5 sigma, while its metallicity shifts from slightly super-solar to below solar, with smaller rmax disagreement.

quotes.quote: J0316-2953 . For this galaxy, the age estimates agree within 1.5 (with the ones estimated here being 1 Gyr older), but the inferred [ M/H ] values differ by 0.2 dex. In DR1 J0316-2953 resulted to be slightly super-solar, while here it has [ M/H ] values below solar now with the new analysis. The disagreement is however smaller when considering the rmax case.

gmw_00012188.name: NGC 1452
gmw_00012188.overview: NGC 1452 features a broad, double-peaked A_2 amplitude profile [arXiv:astro-ph/0702434]. The outer peak of this profile is associated with a galaxy bar, while the stronger inner peak corresponds to a bright oval structure [arXiv:astro-ph/0702434].

sources.summary: NGC 1452 has a broad double-peaked A_2 profile; the outer peak is linked to the bar, while the stronger inner A_2 maximum is identified as a bright oval.

quotes.quote: NGC 1452 has a broad double peaked A_2 amplitude profile, but in this case the higher Fourier modes ( A_4 , A_6 and A_8 ) appear clearly only at 35'', which is the more distant component of the A_2 double peak. Also, the Q_T profile does not completely follow the A_2 amplitude profile: the peak in the Q_T profile is fairly sharp and coincides with the radial distance where the higher Fourier modes are also significant. This maximum evidently corresponds to a bar, a component which appears also in the surface brightness profile at a similar radial distance.

The strongest A_2 maximum appears at 20'', where the higher Fourier modes are weak or absent. At this radial distance there is only a weak shoulder in the Q_T profile. Most probably this A_2 maximum corresponds to a bright oval, identified also in the structural decomposition. This example shows that although an oval might have a fairly high relative mass, it does not necessarily induce strong tangential forces.

gmw_00012189.name: NGC 4625A
gmw_00012189.overview: NGC 4625A is a putative dwarf companion galaxy to NGC 4625 [arXiv:1206.4104]. It is devoid of neutral gas within the velocity range of NGC 4625, though it may potentially be a dwarf elliptical or spheroidal companion [arXiv:1206.4104].

sources.summary: NGC 4625A is discussed as a putative dwarf companion: the paper finds no neutral gas at its velocity range but cannot exclude a dwarf elliptical or spheroidal companion.

quotes.quote: However, aside from the hint of a dwarf companion to NGC 4625 mentioned in Gil de Paz et al. (2005) we see no such companion near NGC 4618. At this point too little is known about this putative companion, NGC 4625A to say whether or not it is or could have interacted with either NGC 4625 or NGC 4618.

The H I disk of NGC 4625 is remarkably quiescent with a nearly uniform velocity dispersion and if the proposed companion galaxy NGC 4625A exists, it is devoid of neutral gas within our velocity range. Our one channel 5 detection for NGC 4625A is 1.26 10 ^ 4 M _ . This strongly suggests a lack of neutral gas or, if there is gas present, it is not in our velocity range and therefore not a likely companion of NGC 4625, nor NGC 4618. We cannot rule out the possibilty that NGC 4625A is a dwarf elliptical or spheroidal companion to NGC 4625.

gmw_00012190.name: J0109--3047
gmw_00012190.overview: J0109--3047 is a high-redshift quasar host galaxy located at a redshift of approximately 6.79 [arXiv:1707.05238]. The galaxy has been detected in [C II] and CO(7--6) emission, though it exhibits a faint 1 mm rest-frame continuum and remains undetected in the 3 mm continuum [arXiv:1707.05238].

sources.summary: The paper presents J0109--3047 as the faintest-continuum VIKING host, with CII detection, marginal CO(6--5), strong CO(7--6), no detected carbon line, and no 3 mm continuum detection.

quotes.quote: J0109--3047 ( z_ [ CII ] =6.7909 ) The host galaxy of quasar J0109--3047 was detected in \ with z_ [ CII ] =6.7909 0.0004 , F_ [ CII ] =2.04 0.20 Jy , and a width of FWHM =340 36 ven16 . The continuum of the host was the faintest of the three quasars considered here with S_ 1 mm =0.56 0.11 mJy at 158 m in the rest-frame. The CO(6--5) line is only marginally detected in this quasar host with a S/N 2.3 (Figures fig:spectra and fig:maps ). From a fit to the spectrum, we derive a line flux of F_ CO(6-5) =0.11 0.05 \ and a luminosity of =(4.4 1.9) 10^7 . In contrast, the CO(7--6) line was detected with a S/N 6 at the same spatial position as the \ line in the line map (Figure fig:maps ).

Fitting a Gaussian to the spectrum while fixing the width and redshift to that of the \ line gives a line strength of F_ CO(7-6) =0.24 0.04 ( =(1.2 0.2) 10^8 ). We do not detect the \ line and place 3 limits on the line flux of F_ [ CI ] <0.15 \ ( L_ [ CI ] <7.4 10^7 ). The host galaxy of J0109--3047 was not detected in the 3 mm continuum image (Figure fig:maps ).

gmw_00012191.name: UGC 6283
gmw_00012191.overview: UGC 6283 is a galaxy that is not completely edge-on [arXiv:astro-ph/0207112]. It features a warp curve where the disk bends slightly in one direction, turns back, and then bends in the opposite direction, creating a kink that may be influenced by spiral arms [arXiv:astro-ph/0207112].

sources.summary: The paper explains UGC 6283's optical and H I warp discrepancy as a warp curve that bends, reverses, and may be affected by spiral arms.

quotes.quote: In the other case, UGC 6283, the difference is probably due to the shape of the warp curve itself. The disk bends first slightly in one direction, turns back and then bends in the opposite direction. The warp seen in the optical is probably tracing that first small 'kink' of the warp shape. So in this case the warp is correctly detected, but the general sense of the warp is not, because the optical disk ends before the HI warp changes direction. This galaxy is not completely edge-on and it is possible that this 'kink' could be due to the presence of spiral arms.

gmw_00012192.name: Abell 2218b
gmw_00012192.overview: Abell 2218b is a gravitationally lensed galaxy that exhibits highly asymmetric H-alpha and [NII] spectral line profiles [arXiv:1206.4047]. Its combined line profile is well fitted by a double Gaussian, and the galaxy displays strong neon emission alongside a detected Brackett-alpha line [arXiv:1206.4047].

sources.summary: The paper finds Abell 2218b has asymmetric H and [NII] profiles, a double-Gaussian velocity dispersion, strong neon emission, and a detected Br line.

quotes.quote: The spectra from both slit positions (Section sec:obs_LBT ) of Abell 2218b show highly asymmetric line profiles. The profile at the central position is double-peaked in both H and [ NII ] while the central+ 0 9 spectrum shows a single-sided profile with stronger emission at the red side of the line. The combined line profile from both slit positions is well fitted with a double Gaussian, whose deconvolved spectral width (corrected for instrumental resolution) indicates a velocity dispersion of 163 3 km/s.

The IRS spectrum for Abell 2218b shows a strong [ Ne III ] 15.56 line as well as the [ Ne II ] line at 12.81 , apart from other PAH features. The Br line is detected at 4.8 with line flux of 4.3 0.9 10 ^ 16 erg/s/cm ^2 .

gmw_00012193.name: Abell 2667a
gmw_00012193.overview: Abell 2667a is a gravitationally lensed infrared galaxy [arXiv:1206.4047]. Its spectra, extracted from two apertures, reveal a main arc north-northeastern of the brightest cluster galaxy and a smaller arc to the east that both display the same profile for H-alpha and [NII] lines [arXiv:1206.4047].

sources.summary: The paper extracts Abell 2667a spectra from two H-bright slit apertures, identifying main and smaller lensed arcs and finding the same H/[NII] profile in both.

quotes.quote: The spectra of Abell 2667a were extracted from two apertures in the slit. Both of these positions are very bright in H . Referring to Figure fig_slitpos , these apertures are at (1) the main arc north-northeastern of the brightest cluster galaxy (BCG) (identified as `A1' by Sand05 , and in Figure fig_slitpos ); and (2) the smaller arc east of the BCG (the `A2' arc in the Sand et al. study). Although it appears that a part of the smaller arc is outside the slit in the optical image, the H line is clearly detected (the light grey spectrum in Figure fig_plotall3x top row, left column). Both apertures show the same profile for both H and [ NII ] lines.

gmw_00012194.name: The Clone
gmw_00012194.overview: The Clone is a high-redshift galaxy with a spectroscopic redshift of 2.0026 [arXiv:1206.4047]. Infrared observations of the galaxy show polycyclic aromatic hydrocarbon (PAH) emission features, though different continuum fitting methods yield varying flux measurements [arXiv:1206.4047].

sources.summary: The paper compares its reduction of the Clone with prior IRS work, notes differing PAH fluxes, and treats the apparent Pa feature as inconsistent with the optical redshift.

quotes.quote: For the two IRS spectra from the literature, the Clone and cB58, our reduction generally agrees with those published previously by Fadely10 and Siana08 , respectively. Small differences are noted in the PAH line fluxes due to the way that the continuum for the fit is defined. PAHFIT uses multi-component dust features to represent the continuum, while a single-component power-law is used by Fadely et al. and Siana et al. Their approach resulted in a similarly good fit to the data compared to ours, although the measured PAH flux could differ, as observed in the Clone's PAH fluxes from the 6.2-11.3 features where the Fadely10 values are a factor of 2.6-3.7 larger than ours.

The formal fit to Pa line flux of the Clone yields (0.3 1.3) 10^ 16 erg/s/cm ^2 , from which we report an upper limit similar to that of cB58 above. In the panel showing the Pa wavelength region for the Clone in Figure fig_litirs , we note a spectral feature at 5.9 (1.96 rest-frame) that resembles a Pa line at z 2.1 . However, the spectroscopic redshift of 2.0026 was measured with multiple optical lines Hainline09 and thus precludes the possibility of the feature being interpreted as the Pa line.

gmw_00012195.name: Abell 2218a
gmw_00012195.overview: Abell 2218a is a high-redshift, star-forming infrared galaxy [arXiv:1206.4047]. Its hydrogen-alpha emission line exhibits a double-peaked profile, and its paschen-alpha emission line has been detected at a 4.8-sigma significance level [arXiv:1206.4047].

sources.summary: The paper reports a double-peaked H line for Abell 2218a, compares its slit orientation to earlier spectroscopy, and detects Pa at 4.8 sigma.

quotes.quote: The H line of Abell 2218a also shows a double-peaked profile indicating a velocity dispersion of 188 5 km/s. The galaxy has been a subject of a previous near-IR spectroscopic study by Richard11 . Our slit orientation, however, is different (see their Figure 1); they excluded the and components (the former being a major component) in the nomenclature of Kneib04 , shown in Figure fig_slitpos , while our slit orientation covers all the main components ( , and ). The Pa observation, which targets the component, detected Pa line at 4.8 with line flux of 5.7 1.2 10 ^ 16 erg/s/cm ^2 . This measurement is consistent with the previously published reduction of the same data by Papovich09 who measured the line flux to be 8.5 1.4 10 ^ 16 erg/s/cm ^2 .

gmw_00012196.name: 8 O'clock arc
gmw_00012196.overview: The 8 O'clock arc is a high-redshift galaxy that exhibits a bright Paschen-alpha emission line [arXiv:1206.4047]. Its dust distribution is characterized by an inhomogeneous mixture of dust, which causes varying extinction measurements [arXiv:1206.4047].
