sources.summary: The paper finds the 8 O'clock arc has the sample's brightest Pa line, continuum complications from a nearby IR-bright galaxy, and inhomogeneous dust extinction.

quotes.quote: Abell 1835a shows a faint H line, too faint to measure asymmetry in the line profile, but a bright Pa line with a flux of 7.9 1.5 10 ^ 16 erg/s/cm ^2 . This is the second brightest Pa detection in our sample, after the 8 O'clock arc, whose Pa flux is 11.5 2.2 10 ^ 16 erg/s/cm ^2 . The LL1 PAH spectrum of the 8 O'clock arc does not have sufficient S/N to use PAHFIT to construct a noise free spectrum as a template for the Pa line flux measurement (Section sec:obs_IRS ) due to the contamination from the nearby IR-bright dust-obscured galaxy, thus instead the continuum of the arc for the measurement is defined by interpolating between the IRAC 5.0 and 8.0 photometric points. These IRAC observations yield fluxes of 74 3 and 64 4 Jy for the 5.8 and 8.0 bands, respectively. We present the Pa spectrum of the 8 O'clock arc in Figure fig_8oclock .

The other object with a well-detected Pa (the 8 O'clock arc), however, shows a 1.8 mag difference between A _ v ^ H / Spitzer and A _ v ^ IR . The latter disagreement indicates an inhomogeneous mixture of dust in the 8 O'clock arc and highlights the diversity of the dust distribution scenarios at redshift 1 < z < 3 .

gmw_00012197.name: IRAS 11058-1131
gmw_00012197.overview: IRAS 11058-1131 is a Seyfert 2 active galaxy [arXiv:1308.4129]. It features an S-shaped narrow-line region and illuminated spiral arms that exhibit roughly rotational kinematics [arXiv:1308.4129].

sources.summary: The paper describes IRAS 11058-1131 as a Seyfert 2 with an S-shaped NLR, complex roughly rotational kinematics, and illuminated spiral-arm material.

quotes.quote: IRAS 11058-1131 This is a Seyfert 2 that shows a likely intersection between the NLR and host disk in the visible S-shaped NLR seen in Figure iras11058 . The kinematics of this target have been deemed 'complex' as no individual kinematic components can be seen in the radial velocity data. Velocities appear to be roughly rotational, as velocities further from the nucleus do not decelerate back toward systemic, but end with blueshifted values to the northeast and redshifted values to the southwest, reaching maximum velocities of 200 km s ^ 1 . This implies a counter-clockwise rotation, which agrees with the apparent winding-up of the spiral arms that are illuminated. Velocity gradients responsible for the knotty, 'complex' kinematics observed may be due to in situ acceleration of ambient material similar to those seen in dust lanes of Mrk 573 Fis10 , though these gradients fail to align with specific flux peaks in the data.

gmw_00012198.name: VCC 1528
gmw_00012198.overview: VCC 1528 is a dwarf elliptical galaxy located in the Virgo cluster [arXiv:1205.3176]. The rotational support exhibited by its globular cluster system suggests a discy origin, though its high blue globular cluster frequency challenges simple harassment-driven mass-loss scenarios [arXiv:1205.3176].

sources.summary: The paper discusses VCC 1528 as one of three Virgo dEs whose rotating globular-cluster systems suggest a discy origin, while its blue-cluster frequency T_N=23 challenges harassment-driven mass-loss scenarios.

quotes.quote: For instance, Beasley2009 show that the GCSs of three Virgo dEs (VCC 1261, VCC 1087 and VCC 1528) exhibit significant rotational support, possibly indicating a discy origin for these systems. They propose that a combination of mass loss due to harassment and subsequent stellar population aging can explain the high (v/ ) of their GCSs, and their compatibility with the Tully-Fisher relation of late-type galaxies. Data from the ACSVCS however indicate that VCC 1261, VCC 1087 and VCC 1528 have T_ N,blue = 6, 19 and 23, respectively. These GC frequencies are 2-7 times larger than the typical values found in field disc galaxies (see Fig. fig:harass_lsb ), so any mass loss scenario would require the progenitors to preferentially lose disc mass (with respect to GCs) by these factors in order to produce comparable remnants -- an unfeasible scenario according to our analysis.

gmw_00012199.name: ID 160133
gmw_00012199.overview: ID 160133 is a high-redshift galaxy located at a redshift of 4.02 [arXiv:2402.17829]. High-resolution imaging reveals clumpy, low surface brightness structures around its main core, while spectroscopic data indicates a broad Balmer-line component interpreted as a galactic outflow [arXiv:2402.17829].

sources.summary: The paper identifies ID 160133 as a z=4.02 galaxy needing a broad Balmer-line component, interpreted primarily as an outflow with width 250 4 km/s, modest SFR surface density, and clumpy structure around its core.

quotes.quote: With the above criteria, we find that a broad component is required to fit \ and of two galaxies: ID 160133 and ID 110000 (Fig. Fig:outflow_individual ). We primarily interpret these components as an outflow, with _ broad = 250 4 km/s and 200 10 km/s, respectively. We measure a broad to narrow line flux ratio of 0.16 ( 0.3 ) and a broad to narrow ratio of 3.4 ( 2.8 ). These galaxies lie at redshifts 4.02 and 5.76 , respectively, and both have a modest /( yr ^ 1 kpc ^ 2 ) of 0.6 0.2 ( 0.4 0.2 ), comparable to the average galaxy population at their redshifts, and sSFR /(yr ^ 1 ) of 7.6 0.2 ( 8.5 0.3 ). We also notice that both galaxies with outflow signatures show low surface brigthness clumpy structures around the main cores from high-resolution JWST images, which makes the scenario of gas accretion an interesting, alternative possibility that cannot be excluded with our spectroscopic data.

gmw_00012200.name: ID 110000
gmw_00012200.overview: ID 110000 is a galaxy at a redshift of 5.76 [arXiv:2402.17829]. High-resolution imaging reveals low surface brightness clumpy structures around its main core, and the galaxy hosts a gas outflow with a velocity of 200 km/s [arXiv:2402.17829].

sources.summary: The paper identifies ID 110000 as a z=5.76 galaxy needing a broad Balmer-line component, interpreted primarily as an outflow with width 200 10 km/s, modest SFR surface density, and clumpy structure around its core.

quotes.quote: With the above criteria, we find that a broad component is required to fit \ and of two galaxies: ID 160133 and ID 110000 (Fig. Fig:outflow_individual ). We primarily interpret these components as an outflow, with _ broad = 250 4 km/s and 200 10 km/s, respectively. We measure a broad to narrow line flux ratio of 0.16 ( 0.3 ) and a broad to narrow ratio of 3.4 ( 2.8 ). These galaxies lie at redshifts 4.02 and 5.76 , respectively, and both have a modest /( yr ^ 1 kpc ^ 2 ) of 0.6 0.2 ( 0.4 0.2 ), comparable to the average galaxy population at their redshifts, and sSFR /(yr ^ 1 ) of 7.6 0.2 ( 8.5 0.3 ). We also notice that both galaxies with outflow signatures show low surface brigthness clumpy structures around the main cores from high-resolution JWST images, which makes the scenario of gas accretion an interesting, alternative possibility that cannot be excluded with our spectroscopic data.

gmw_00012201.name: DC 396844
gmw_00012201.overview: DC 396844 is a high-redshift galaxy [arXiv:2212.08083]. Observations of this system reveal a circumgalactic medium with a line emission profile that can be modeled with both extended and compact halo solutions [arXiv:2212.08083].

sources.summary: The paper uses DC 396844 as the worked ALPINE halo example, fitting posterior profiles, explaining a bimodality between extended and compact halo solutions, and reusing its observing setup for sensitivity comparisons.

quotes.quote: As an illustration, the corner plot in Fig. fig:corner_1 shows the 1D and 2D marginalized posterior for one of the galaxies in the sample, DC 396844 .

The origin of the bimodality can be understood focusing on the emission profiles for DC 396844 (right panel of Fig. fig:corner_1 ): the profiles for low- V_ halos are even more extended than the observed ones; on the contrary, higher V_ values result in a more compact emission that is barely compatible with the observed ones. Given the large relative uncertainty on data points at large impact parameter, both behaviors are consistent with the data.

We have assumed the beam size, redshift, and FWHM of the system to be equal to those of DC 396844 ; such choice does not affect our conclusions in any relevant way. As a reference, we also plot the data for DC 396844 (red points), as well as the associated line sensitivity (gray shaded region) taken from 20 corresponding to an integration time of 20 min , bethermin:2019 .

gmw_00012202.name: SDSS J004846.45-004611.9
gmw_00012202.overview: SDSS J004846.45-004611.9 is a z=0.94 core Extremely Red Quasar candidate that exhibits exceptionally strong, high-ionization outflows [arXiv:2203.05558]. Its host galaxy is dominated by a 400 Myr post-starburst stellar population that formed during a brief, intense starburst [arXiv:2203.05558].

sources.summary: The paper identifies SDSS J004846.45-004611.9 as a z=0.94 core ERQ candidate with exceptionally strong high-ionization outflows and a host dominated by a 400 Myr post-starburst population after a brief starburst.

quotes.quote: We present a z = 0.94 quasar, SDSS J004846.45-004611.9, discovered in the SDSS-III BOSS survey. A visual analysis of this spectrum reveals highly broadened and blueshifted narrow emission lines, in particular [ Ne V ] 3426 and [ O III ] 5007 , with outflow velocities of 4000 km s ^ 1 , along with unusually large [ Ne V ] 3426 / [ Ne III ] 3869 ratios. The gas shows higher ionization at higher outflow velocities, indicating a connection between the powerful outflow and the unusual strength of the high ionization lines. The SED and the i - W3 color of the source reveal that it is likely a ``core'' Extremely Red Quasar (core ERQ); a candidate population of young AGN that are violently ``blowing out'' gas and dust from their centers.

The stellar population is entirely dominated by a single SSP with an age of 400 Myr, suggesting the host galaxy is a `post-starburst' .f., Wild_2009 . Given that our code does not favor the inclusion of 300 or 500 Myr old templates in the fit, we can conclude that the burst had a short duration. If we assume that the stellar mass ascribed to the 400 Myr old population formed at a constant rate over 100 Myr, it implies a star formation rate of 560 M _ yr ^ 1 , consistent with values found for sub-mm galaxies .f., Dudzevivciute_2020 .

gmw_00012203.name: J 145722.70-010800.9
gmw_00012203.overview: J 145722.70-010800.9 is a radio-quiet mini-broad absorption line quasar [arXiv:0902.0951]. Its spectrum features doublet absorption lines [arXiv:0902.0951], and historical observations suggest the source may have been brighter during the ROSAT epoch [arXiv:0902.0951].

sources.summary: The paper finds J 145722.70-010800.9 is not unusually X-ray bright for a radio-quiet mini-BAL QSO, notes its doublet absorption, and suggests it may have been brighter in the ROSAT epoch.

quotes.quote: The mini-BAL in J 145722.70-010800.9 may have strengthened slightly, but otherwise there is no evidence for variation in the spectra over the 1.5 and 1.8 yr (QSO frame) times between observations. The mini-BAL in J 145722.70-010800.9 has a doublet structure, while that of J 111914.32+600457.2 resides in the center of a broad but very shallow trough (from approximately 12,000 to 22,000 km s ^ 1 ) and has no visible doublet structure. In light of our observation that mini-BAL QSOs are relatively X-ray bright compared to BAL QSOs, our XMM-Newton observations (Table makeXMMObsTableTab ) indicate that these sources are not highly atypical for radio-quiet (J 145722.70-010800.9 ) or radio-loud (J 111914.32+600457.2 ) mini-BAL QSOs.

The doublet absorption features in the SDSS spectrum of J 145722.70-010800.9 do not match lines expected for an intervening absorber at z 0.44 . The source 145723-010723 is about 24 , or 2 R_ from the ROSAT catalog source identification, where R_ = 12 is the ROSAT 1 positional error (including 6 of systematic error). J 145722.70-010800.9 is somewhat closer ( 1.2 R_ ) to the ROSAT source identification vabbbbdeghhhpppstz00 . The 60 radius region centered on J 145722.70-010800.9 in the ROSAT image blends the two sources and accounts for about half of the count rate reported in the Faint Source Catalog based on that catalog's extraction radius of 300 . The ROSAT count rate is 3--4 times higher than expected from estimates based on the two XMM-Newton sources combined, suggesting that some source in this region was brighter in the earlier epoch.

gmw_00012204.name: J 111914.32+600457.2
gmw_00012204.overview: J 111914.32+600457.2 is a radio-loud mini-broad absorption line (mini-BAL) quasar [arXiv:0902.0951]. Its mini-BAL feature is located within a broad, shallow trough, and the source exhibits ordinary X-ray brightness for its class [arXiv:0902.0951].

sources.summary: The paper describes J 111914.32+600457.2 as a radio-loud mini-BAL QSO with a shallow trough, ordinary mini-BAL X-ray brightness, and uncertain dimming relative to ROSAT.

quotes.quote: The mini-BAL in J 145722.70-010800.9 has a doublet structure, while that of J 111914.32+600457.2 resides in the center of a broad but very shallow trough (from approximately 12,000 to 22,000 km s ^ 1 ) and has no visible doublet structure. In light of our observation that mini-BAL QSOs are relatively X-ray bright compared to BAL QSOs, our XMM-Newton observations (Table makeXMMObsTableTab ) indicate that these sources are not highly atypical for radio-quiet (J 145722.70-010800.9 ) or radio-loud (J 111914.32+600457.2 ) mini-BAL QSOs.

The situation is more complicated for J 111914.32+600457.2 , as the excess ROSAT counts over the background are not as centrally peaked as was the case for J 145722.70-010800.9 , making the exact count rate estimate highly dependent on aperture size. Using a 60 radius aperture, we estimate that the source J 111914.32+600457.2 has dimmed by 50 , but this factor could be up to 90 with an aperture as large as 300 in radius. Our XMM-Newton images show two somewhat fainter sources 200 distant from J 111914.32+600457.2 , but these sources do not coincide with the brightest regions of the 300 ROSAT aperture. As for J 145722.70-010800.9 , we conclude that some variable source contributed to the excess of counts in the ROSAT epoch, but we cannot be certain that the variable source was the QSO 111914.32+600457.2 .

gmw_00012205.name: J 121205.30+152005.8
gmw_00012205.overview: J 121205.30+152005.8 is a high-ionization broad-absorption line quasar [arXiv:0902.0951]. The active galactic nucleus is relatively bright in X-rays, though its Chandra X-ray spectrum contains too few counts for detailed spectral analysis [arXiv:0902.0951].

sources.summary: The paper identifies J 121205.30+152005.8 as a relatively X-ray-bright HiBAL source observed for 5 ks with Chandra, but with too few counts for detailed spectral analysis.

quotes.quote: Our current study identifies two additional HiBAL sources as being relatively X-ray bright (for BAL QSOs): J 121205.30+152005.8 and J 143031.78+322145.9 (with _ OX > 0.1 ). Both sources were observed for 5 ks by Chandra (observation id numbers 2104 and 4279, respectively), yielding X-ray spectra with 30 counts each. These spectra are insufficient for detailed spectral analysis.

gmw_00012206.name: J 143031.78+322145.9
gmw_00012206.overview: J 143031.78+322145.9 is a high-ionization broad-absorption line quasar [arXiv:0902.0951]. It is relatively bright in X-rays for a broad-absorption line source, though its Chandra observation yielded too few counts for detailed spectral analysis [arXiv:0902.0951].

sources.summary: The paper identifies J 143031.78+322145.9 as a relatively X-ray-bright HiBAL source observed for 5 ks with Chandra, but with too few counts for detailed spectral analysis.

quotes.quote: Our current study identifies two additional HiBAL sources as being relatively X-ray bright (for BAL QSOs): J 121205.30+152005.8 and J 143031.78+322145.9 (with _ OX > 0.1 ). Both sources were observed for 5 ks by Chandra (observation id numbers 2104 and 4279, respectively), yielding X-ray spectra with 30 counts each. These spectra are insufficient for detailed spectral analysis.

gmw_00012207.name: UGC07125
gmw_00012207.overview: UGC07125 is a spiral galaxy [arXiv:2109.02159] classified as a dwarf galaxy [arXiv:1908.06489] located at a distance of approximately 15.6 Mpc [arXiv:2109.02159]. The galaxy hosted the Type IIP core-collapse supernova SN 2020qmp, which was detected on one of its spiral arms [arXiv:2109.02159].

sources.summary: The paper identifies UGC07125 as the 15.6 Mpc host of SN 2020qmp, places the transient on its spiral arm, and uses velocity and Tully-Fischer distances to choose the adopted distance.

quotes.quote: We present a detailed analysis of SN 2020qmp, a nearby Type IIP core-collapse supernova (CCSN) that was discovered by the Palomar Gattini-IR survey in the galaxy UGC07125 (distance of 15.6 4 Mpc).

The transient was detected on the spiral arm of galaxy UGC07125. There are eight different Tully-Fischer distances TullyFischerPaper provided by the Nasa Extragalatic Database (NED). Using Tully's Cosmic Flow Calculator Shaya2017, Graziani2019 , the galactocentric velocity of UGC07125 is 1089 2 km/s. This galactocentric speed makes distances of less than 10 Mpc and greater than 25 Mpc unlikely; five of the distances from NED are outside this 10 Mpc to 25 Mpc range.
Therefore, out of the two Tully-Fischer distances left, we chose 15.6^ +4.4 _ 3.4 Mpc Tullydistance , which is still is within the error range of the other distance, 17.5^ +3.9 _ 3.1 2014distance .

sources.summary: The BEC dark matter fit gives UGC7125 the largest static halo, smallest central density, lower particle-mass limits, and longest observed rotation curve in the modeled dwarf sample.

quotes.quote: The lower limit is given by galaxy UGC7125 having the largest static BEC halo ( R =14.381 kpc), and the upper limit from UGC7603 having the smallest one ( R =3.793 kpc). It is worth to note, that UGC7125 also has the smallest ( _ =0.105 10^ 24 g/cm ^3 ), while UGC7603 the largest central density ( _ =2.692 10^ 24 g/cm ^3 ) among these galaxies.

Again, the two limits are constrained by the galaxies UGC7125 (from below, R' =15.36 kpc) and UGC7603 (from above, R' =3.794 kpc). We also note that UGC7125 possesses the longest, while UGC7603 the shortest observed rotation curve in the sample, hence the size of the static BEC halo seems to correlate with the length of the rotation curves.

gmw_00012208.name: SDSS J222428.53+261423.2
gmw_00012208.overview: SDSS J222428.53+261423.2 is a sub-kiloparsec dual active galactic nucleus candidate [arXiv:2404.12911]. Its optical spectrum displays double-peaked narrow emission lines with velocity separations of approximately 930 km/s, which may indicate two cores separated by about 500 pc [arXiv:2404.12911].

sources.summary: The paper presents SDSS J2224 as a sub-kpc dual AGN candidate whose double-peaked narrow emission lines have about 930 km/s separations and would imply about 500 pc core separation if due to two AGNs.

quotes.quote: In this paper, we presented a detailed analysis of the Sloan Digital Sky Survey optical spectrum of a new sub-kpc scale dual AGN candidate SDSS J222428.53+261423.2 (=SDSS J2224). The target is one of the few AGNs with all the optical narrow emission lines characterized by double-peaked profiles and with peak separations in velocity units of about 930 km/s. If the double-peaked narrow emission lines (DPNELs) are due to a dual AGN in , the estimated physical separation between the two cores is about 500 pc. Meanwhile, three alternative explanations are also discussed in this paper, however, we can not find solid evidence to completely rule them out. Our results support the presence of a sub-kpc dual AGN with DPNELs in all lines, indicating a key episode of galaxy merging evolution at sub-kpc scale.

gmw_00012209.name: NGC 2820
gmw_00012209.overview: NGC 2820 is a galaxy with a heliocentric systemic velocity of 1577 km/s and a rotation velocity of 175 km/s [arXiv:astro-ph/0502036]. Its neutral hydrogen disk spans approximately 48 kpc, featuring a gigantic northern loop that contains an HI mass of 600 million solar masses [arXiv:astro-ph/0502036].

sources.summary: The paper finds NGC 2820 has a 48 kpc HI disk, a radio halo, a sharp southern HI cutoff, and a giant northern HI loop likely shaped by ram pressure plus tides.

quotes.quote: The heliocentric systemic velocity of NGC 2820 is 1577 km s ^ 1 and its rotation velocity is 175 km s ^ 1 . The linear extent of the HI disk of NGC 2820 is about 48 kpc and its HI mass is 6.6 10^ 9 M _ .

We report detection of a gigantic HI loop arising to the north of NGC 2820. The loop is 17.5 kpc across and rises up to 4.9 kpc. It encompasses an HI mass of 6 10^8 M _ . No radio continuum or H emission is associated with this loop. We present possible origin scenarios which include a central starburst, ram pressure stripping and tidal stripping.

gmw_00012210.name: NGC 2814
gmw_00012210.overview: NGC 2814 is a tidally disturbed galaxy that features a radio halo [arXiv:astro-ph/0502036]. Its radio continuum and neutral hydrogen disks are tilted relative to its optical disk, and it possesses a kinematically distinct southern neutral hydrogen streamer alongside a radio tail [arXiv:astro-ph/0502036].

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 2814 as tidally disturbed, with a radio halo, tilted HI and radio disks, a kinematically distinct southern HI streamer, and an associated radio tail.

quotes.quote: A radio halo is also detected around NGC 2814.

We detect various tidal features close to NGC 2814. The radio continuum disk and HI disk of NGC 2814 are tilted with respect to the optical disk. A HI streamer is seen to emerge from the south of NGC 2814 but the two are are kinematically distinct. The velocity field of the streamer is similar to parts of NGC 2820 close to it. The streamer has a sky plane extent of 12.6 kpc and encompasses an HI mass of 1.3 10^8 M _ .
A tail emerging from the south of NGC 2814 and extending westwards is detected in the radio continuum. The tail has a spectral index of 1.6 .

gmw_00012211.name: UGC 12060
gmw_00012211.overview: UGC 12060 is a galaxy with an optical morphology that is consistent with a low inclination of 20 degrees [arXiv:1005.5456]. However, this low inclination appears to be inconsistent with expectations from the Tully-Fisher relation [arXiv:1005.5456].

sources.summary: The paper finds MOND falls below UGC 12060's inner rotation curve and above its outer curve; a 20 degree inclination would fit MOND but conflicts with Tully-Fisher expectations.

quotes.quote: UGC 12060. -- The rotation curve as predicted by MOND falls below the observed one in the inner parts of the rotation curve, and is above the observed one in the outer parts. An inclination of 20^ in stead of 40^ is required for agreement between MOND and the observations. Such a low inclination is consistent with the optical morphology of this galaxy, but appears inconsistent with the Tully-Fisher relation.

gmw_00012212.name: source B
gmw_00012212.overview: Source B is a high-redshift galaxy located at a redshift of z=2.32 [arXiv:1609.04021]. It exhibits strong line emission, including Lyman-alpha, and is positioned at the flux peak of the MAMMOTH-1 nebula, which it may dominantly power [arXiv:1609.04021].

sources.summary: The paper identifies source B as a z=2.32 object at MAMMOTH-1's flux peak, with strong line emission, faint broadband photometry, coordinates, and a possible dominant powering role.
sources.paper_coordinate_text: We use source B's position as the center of the MAMMOTH-1: = 14:41:27.62 = +40:03:31.44.

quotes.quote: Source B resides in the flux peak of the broad-band subtracted narrowband image. Our LBT/MODS spectroscopy shows that source A is a low-redshift AGN at z=0.16 , while source B is an object at z=2.32 . In Figure 3, we present the 1-D spectrum of source B which has strong emission in Ly , , , and . Using LBT/LBC imaging, we find that source B has a brightness of U_ AB =25.77 0.07 , V_ AB =24.37 0.03 , i_ AB =24.30 0.03 . Although it is difficult to identify all the possible powering sources associated with MAMMOTH-1, source B's location and redshift suggest that it could be the dominant powering source of MAMMOTH-1.
We use source B's position as the center of the MAMMOTH-1: = 14:41:27.62 = +40:03:31.44.

gmw_00012213.name: GB3 0212+704
gmw_00012213.overview: GB3 0212+704 is a tailed radio galaxy located near a hot spot [arXiv:2206.09228]. High-resolution LOFAR observations reveal a tail morphology with a steepening spectral index behind the tail, which may contribute to local inverse Compton emission [arXiv:2206.09228].

sources.summary: The paper identifies GB3 0212+704 as a tailed radio galaxy near a hot spot, measuring its fluxes, spectral index, and possible contribution to local IC emission.

quotes.quote: In the vicinity ( 0.5 ) of the ``hot spot" (Fig. fig:TempMap , turquoise circle), there is a radio source GB3 0212+704 https://vizier.cds.unistra.fr/viz-bin/VizieR-5?-ref=VIZ620b3ce430f570&-out.add=.&-source=VIII/53/gb1&recno=3648 maslowski72 (Fig. fig:TempMap , blue circle) and LOFAR high resolution study shows a tail morphology. The spectral index map between LOFAR 145 MHz and VLA 1.5 GHz also shows the steepening of the spectrum behind the tail. The tail radio galaxy has flux density of S (141 MHz) = 12.1 1.2 mJy, S (1.4 GHz)= 2.9 +/- 0.2 mJy, S (1.5 GHz)= 2.7 +/- 0.2 mJy. The average spectral index is = -0.62 0.01.

Furthermore, the vicinity of the radio galaxy may indeed be causing the IC emission due to the reacceleration of relativistic particles injected from the site, or by adiabatic compression, the merger shock may be ``re-energizing" the radio plasma. The radio galaxy spectral index found by LOFAR is within 1 of the IC emission photon index we found.

gmw_00012214.name: SDSS J164+43
gmw_00012214.overview: SDSS J164+43 is an active galactic nucleus [arXiv:2306.12500] that exhibits silicate absorption [arXiv:2306.12500]. Modeling suggests the system is observed at a grazing viewing angle that allows a view of the central source [arXiv:2306.12500].

sources.summary: The paper reports silicate absorption in SDSS J164+43 and finds skirtor torus parameters indicating unusual inclination, radius ratio, and grazing torus view.

quotes.quote: It also shows the presence of silicate emission except in SDSS J164+43, where it is in absorption as suggested by skirtor model (Figure fig:sed2 ).

The main results of the comparison between each parameter using the gonzalez/2019 as a reference, can be summarized as follows: The viewing angle, i : The five objects best fitted in this work with skirtor display torus inclination angle i between 10 and 50 deg. In the gonzalez/2019 distribution the least frequent values of i are 20 and 30 deg, which are the values obtained for SDSS J164+43 and III ZW 77, respectively. Moreover, the AGN that is best-fit with clumpy (ESO 138 G1), has an i value of 0 deg. It is located in the second largest peak of the distribution in gonzalez/2019 . The number of dusty clouds in the direction of the line of sight, N_ 0 : In the only AGN that was fit with clumpy (ESO 138 G1) the value of N_ 0 =14 is close to the highest peak of the gonzalez/2019 distribution. The ratio between the outer and inner radius of the torus, ( Y ): The values of 10 and 30 obtained here with skirtor coincide with the two peaks of the distribution in gonzalez/2019 . In SDSS J164+43 and III ZW 77, the Y of 10 and 30, respectively, is not present in their distribution.
In SDSS J164+43, 2MASX J113+16, and NGC 424 the results suggest a viewing angle that is grazing the torus, allowing the view of the central source.

gmw_00012215.name: 2MASX J113+16
gmw_00012215.overview: 2MASX J113+16 is an active galactic nucleus with an edge-on optical depth of 7 at 9.7 microns [arXiv:2306.12500]. Its spectral energy distribution differs from typical Type I active galaxies, suggesting a viewing angle that grazes the torus [arXiv:2306.12500].

sources.summary: The paper finds 2MASX J113+16 differs from the Type I comparison SED, has optical depth 7, and is viewed grazing the torus.

quotes.quote: A close inspection of the SED shapes in figures fig:sedst1e2skir and fig:sedst1e2cat obtained for NGC 1068 and NGC 4151, respectively, with the best fitted SED shapes of the CLiF AGNs (blue panels in figures fig:sed1 and fig:sed2 ) suggests that all but 2MASX J113+16 and NGC 424 have a SED that is very close to that of NGC 4151, this latter object assumed as representative of Type I AGN.

The edge-on optical depth at 9.7 microns, _ 9.7 m : The values of 5, 5 and 7 found for III ZW 77, MRK 1388 and 2MASX J113+16, respectively, coincide with the peaks of the distribution in gonzalez/2019 .
In SDSS J164+43, 2MASX J113+16, and NGC 424 the results suggest a viewing angle that is grazing the torus, allowing the view of the central source.

gmw_00012216.name: foreground galaxy
gmw_00012216.overview: The foreground galaxy is a spiral system located at a redshift of approximately 0.06 [arXiv:0810.2646]. It features a dusty disk that extends well beyond its starlight, with structured spiral-arm extinction detectable out to 1.5 R25 [arXiv:0810.2646].

sources.summary: The paper describes the foreground spiral as having a dusty disk far beyond its starlight, with spiral-arm extinction measurable to 1.5 R25 and optical depths around 0.25 in blue filters.

quotes.quote: The foreground disk system (at z 0.06 ) shows a dusty disk much more extended than the starlight, with spiral lanes seen in extinction out to 1.5 R_ 25 , approximately six half-light radii. This pair is the first where extinction can be mapped reliably out to this distance from the center. The spiral arms of the extended dust disk show typical extinction values of A_ F475W 0.25 , A_ F606W 0.25 , and A_ F814W 0.15 . The extinction law inferred from these measures is similar to the local Milky Way one, and we show that the smoothing effects of sampling at limited spatial resolution ( <57 pc, in these data) flattens the observed function through mixing of regions with different extinction. This galaxy illustrates the diversity of dust distributions in spirals, and the limitations of adopting a single dust model for optically similar galaxies.

The radial extent of the dust in the foreground disk is the most striking feature of the pair. In a part of the disk where there is barely any light from the foreground galaxy, there is significant structured extinction evident in the spiral arms, readily visible in the color image (Figure f:rgb ). At the corresponding radius on the other side of the disk, even these long exposures do not detect starlight, indicating that the cold dense ISM extends well past the optical limit of the disk.

gmw_00012217.name: 2MASXJ00482185-2507365
gmw_00012217.overview: 2MASXJ00482185-2507365 is a background spiral galaxy located at a redshift of z=0.06 [arXiv:0810.2646]. It features a regular, smooth bulge that is partially occulted by a foreground spiral galaxy [arXiv:0810.2646].

sources.summary: The paper identifies 2MASXJ00482185-2507365 as the z=0.06 background spiral whose smooth bulge backlights the foreground dust lanes and enables isophotal modeling of the overlap geometry.

quotes.quote: The background galaxy ( z=0.06 ) is a spiral with a prominent, regular and smooth bulge, which is partially occulted by a foreground spiral.

The image shows clear dust structures associated with the foreground spiral, extending to (and slightly beyond) the center of the bulge of the background galaxy. Figure f:map shows the F475W band grayscale image in which the dust lanes of the foreground galaxy are neatly visible against the background galaxy's bulge. The background galaxy was catalogued as 2MASXJ00482185-2507365 by 2MASS. give its redshift as z=0.06 .

First, the areas affected by the foreground galaxy were masked, and a set of elliptical isophotes were fit to the background galaxy. We then subtracted a noiseless realization of this mean profile, and modeled the foreground galaxy after masking off areas where absorption might be important, notably the overlap region and dark lanes in the background galaxy. The masking typically left more than 60 of each isophote. Subsequently, we subtracted the model of the foreground galaxy from the data, and refit the background galaxy.

gmw_00012218.name: PSO J093.2268 + 04.6703
gmw_00012218.overview: PSO J093.2268 + 04.6703 is a faint galaxy located at a redshift of z = 0.3304 [arXiv:2108.01282]. It is identified as the host galaxy of the fast radio burst FRB20180301A with an association probability of 99.9% [arXiv:2108.01282].

sources.summary: The paper identifies PSO J093.2268 + 04.6703 as the faint putative host of FRB20180301A, measures z=0.3304, models its stellar mass from photometry, and finds a 99.9 PATH association probability.

quotes.quote: In archival Pan-STARRS images we identify a faint galaxy, PSO J093.2268 + 04.6703, as the putative host of FRB20180301A.

Observations of the host with the Keck/DEIMOS spectrograph on UT 2020 September 17 determined the galaxy's redshift z= 0.3304 based on nebular emission lines such as [ N ii ] and H . The measured redshift was found to be consistent with the redshift range (z=0.13-0.35) estimated by extragalactic DM in . We also performed NIR observations of the galaxy in JHK- bands using the MMT telescope on UT 2021 February 27 28. The photometric measurements derived from MMT imaging, along with those from NOT, were used by CIGALE cigale for SED fitting to estimate the stellar mass of the galaxy (see Appendix for details).
We find a Probabilistic Association of Transients to their Hosts (PATH) PATH probability of 99.9 for the association of FRB20180301A with the given galaxy.

gmw_00012219.name: host of FRB20191228A
gmw_00012219.overview: The host of FRB20191228A is a galaxy located at a redshift of z=0.243, which was determined using nebular emission lines [arXiv:2108.01282]. Radio observations of the galaxy show no compact, persistent radio emission above the detection limit of 22 microjanskys per beam [arXiv:2108.01282].

sources.summary: The paper identifies the host of FRB20191228A with 100 PATH probability, measures z=0.243 from nebular emission, performs photometry, and finds no compact persistent radio emission above its ATCA limit.

quotes.quote: A candidate host galaxy was identified with centroid approximately 1.5 from the burst coordinates with a 100 PATH association probability (see Fig. fig:host ). Photometry of the identified galaxy was performed with Source-Extractor SExtractor using a circular aperture with a 5 diameter.

On UT 2020 November 08, the Keck/LRIS spectrograph was used to perform spectroscopic observations in order to determine the galaxy redshift. The nebular emissions such as H , [ O iii ] established the redshift of the host to be z=0.243 . We also performed observations of the host of FRB20191228A using the ATCA at center frequencies of 5.5 and 7.5 GHz to search for a compact and persistent radio emission.
We did not detect any radio emission above 22 Jy beam ^ 1 (3 ) constraining the luminosity of the source to be < 3.4 10^ 21 W Hz ^ 1 at 6.5 GHz.

gmw_00012220.name: DES J033358.99 140459.2
gmw_00012220.overview: DES J033358.99 140459.2 is a galaxy located at a redshift of z=0.3688 [arXiv:2108.01282]. It is identified as the host galaxy of the fast radio burst FRB20200906A, though observations show no radio emission from the galaxy itself above a 3-sigma flux density limit [arXiv:2108.01282].

sources.summary: The paper identifies DES J033358.99 140459.2 as the FRB20200906A host with 100 PATH probability, measures z=0.3688, models its SED, and reports no VLA radio detection.

quotes.quote: A candidate host galaxy for FRB20200906A was identified in the DES, Pan-STARRS and AllWISE database as DES J033358.99 140459.2, PSO J033358.994 140459.287 and J033358.99 140459.1 respectively, with 100 PATH association probability (see Fig. fig:host ).

On UT 2020 September 17, we also triggered Keck/DEIMOS for spectroscopic observations and found the redshift of the putative host to be z=0.3688 . The photometric measurements from Pan-STARRS and those from the VLT were used to model the SED of the host galaxy. The derived properties are presented in Table tab:measurements . We triggered the VLA (project code: VLA/20A-157) on UT 2020 September 22 to observe the host galaxy of FRB20200906A in the frequency range 4-8 GHz. We found no radio emission from the host galaxy above a 3 flux density of 12 Jy beam ^ 1 , implying an upper-limit on the source's luminosity to be < 4.3 10^ 21 W Hz ^ 1 at 6 GHz.

gmw_00012221.name: SDSS J133356.02+001229.1
gmw_00012221.overview: SDSS J133356.02+001229.1 is a broad-absorption line quasar located at a redshift of approximately 0.9197 [arXiv:1809.10155]. The galaxy hosts a high-velocity outflow that has exhibited multiple cycles of emergence, strengthening, and near disappearance over timescales of days to years [arXiv:1809.10155].

sources.summary: The paper presents SDSS J133356.02+001229.1 as a LoBAL quasar with rapidly varying high-velocity BAL absorption, showing repeated emergence, strengthening, and near disappearance of the blue outflow component over days-to-years timescales.

quotes.quote: We report the discovery of rapid variations of a high-velocity \ broad absorption line (BAL) trough in the quasar SDSS J133356.02+001229.1 (z _ em 0.9197). vivek12 revealed the emergence and subsequent near disappearance of a BAL component in this source having an ejection velocity of 28000 . Our further follow up studies with South African Large Telescope (SALT) reveal the dramatic nature of the absorption line variability in this source. The absorption line emerged again at the same velocity and nearly disappeared within the SALT observations. Our observations allow us to probe variability over timescales of the order of few days to 4.2 years in the QSO rest-frame.

In this paper, we report two cycles of appearance and near disappearance in the \ broad absorption line outflow in SDSS J133356.02+001229.1. The blue component which appeared in 2001, is observed to first increase in absorption line strength in 2008. Reaching a maximum strength in 2009, it continued to decrease in strength and almost vanished in 2011.

gmw_00012222.name: SDSS J1630+3119
gmw_00012222.overview: SDSS J1630+3119 is an infrared-luminous quasar at a redshift of 1.996 that exhibits extremely faint ultraviolet emission [arXiv:2203.03789]. Its optical and near-infrared photometric data join smoothly, suggesting that the quasar did not vary between the SDSS and 2MASS observations [arXiv:2203.03789].

sources.summary: The paper identifies SDSS J1630+3119 as an infrared-luminous FeLoBAL quasar at z=1.996 with faint UV emission, extensive multiwavelength data, a complete 2175 A bump profile, and no significant SDSS-to-2MASS variability.

quotes.quote: We finally identified 3 credible candidates, i.e., J102036.10+602339.0 (referred to as SDSS J1020+6023 hereafter), SDSS J134951.93+382334.1 (referred to as SDSS J1349+3823 hereafter), and SDSS J163004.29+311957.6 (referred to as SDSS J1630+3119 hereafter). Their emission-line redshifts given by the SDSS DR7 quasar catalogue (Schneider et al. 2010) are 0.9940 0.0006 , 1.0943 0.0009 , and 1.9960 0.0007 , respectively. Since SDSS J1630+3119 has the most observational data and its spectrum shows the entire bump profile, it is analysed and reported in the text in detail.

SDSS J1630+3119 is an infrared-luminous quasar with an extremely faint UV emission. The broadband SED for SDSS J1630+3119 from UV to near-IR is presented by the photometric images taken with the SDSS (York et al. 2000) at the u' , g' , ' , i' , and z' bands, the Two Micron All Sky Survey (2MASS; Skrutskie et al. 2006) at the J , H , and Ks bands, and the Wide-field Infrared Survey Explorer (WISE; Wright et al. 2010) at the W1 , W2 , W3 , and W4 bands. The optical and near-IR photometric data of SDSS J1630+3119 join smoothly, suggesting that the quasar did not vary significantly between the SDSS and 2MASS observations.

gmw_00012223.name: SDSS J1349+3823
gmw_00012223.overview: SDSS J1349+3823 is an analogue iron low-ionization broad-absorption line quasar [arXiv:2203.03789]. Its optical spectrum displays broad absorption troughs of magnesium II and iron II multiplets, alongside a strong intrinsic 2175 Å bump feature [arXiv:2203.03789].

sources.summary: The paper treats SDSS J1349+3823 as an analogue FeLoBAL quasar with GALEX/SDSS/2MASS data, an intrinsic 2175 A bump, Mg II and Fe II absorption troughs, and a strong fitted bump amplitude.

quotes.quote: SDSS J1349+3823 is another analogue of SDSS J1630+3119. The archives provide multiwavelength photometric magnitudes from the GALEX, SDSS and 2MASS surveys and spectroscopic fluxes from the SDSS survey. Its 1D spectrum from 3800 to 9200 was accessed from the SDSS DR7.

The optical spectrum simultaneously shows a remarkable intrinsic 2175 bump feature and broad absorption troughs of Mg II and Fe II multiplets UV 1, UV 2, UV 3 and UV 62,63. The extremely low flux at the u' band implies that there are possible strong absorption troughs of Al III and even Fe III UV 34 1895.46, 1914.06, and 1926.30, and UV 48 2062.21, 2068.90, and 2079.62. The same fitting process was used to explore the 2175 bump feature in SDSS J1020+6023. The best-fitting model yields c_ 1 =-0.73 0.02 , c_ 2 =0.37 0.01 , c_ 3 =2.02 0.13 , x_ 0 =4.67 0.01 m ^ 1 , and =1.18 0.08 m ^ 1 , where the bump strength is A _ bump =2.68 0.25 m ^ 1 .

gmw_00012224.name: H02
gmw_00012224.overview: H02 is a post-starburst galaxy [arXiv:2204.07465]. It exhibits a high CO(2-1) velocity dispersion and a small size, which suggest a high turbulent pressure within the system [arXiv:2204.07465].

sources.summary: The paper reports H02 as a post-starburst galaxy with discrepant ALMA and IRAM CO(2-1) fluxes, compact consistent CO sizes, and high velocity dispersion suggesting turbulent pressure.

quotes.quote: For H02 and H03, the ALMA and IRAM CO (2--1) measurements differ by 37 . For H02, the IRAM flux is larger than the ALMA flux; for H03 the ALMA flux is larger than the IRAM flux.

Two galaxies (H02 and H03) have both CO (3--2) and CO (2--1) observations; these galaxies have sizes consistent between the two tracers, although differences would be difficult to determine with these observations, as >50 of the flux is contained within a central unresolved beamsize in both tracers for both galaxies.
The high CO(2--1) velocity dispersion and small size measured by Smercina2021 for H02 and H03, which led them to infer a high turbulent pressure, is also observed here.

gmw_00012225.name: S02
gmw_00012225.overview: S02 is a post-starburst galaxy that exhibits a blueshifted molecular gas outflow to the southeast of its CO(3-2) centroid [arXiv:2204.07465]. The galaxy's stellar velocity field is aligned with the bulk of its molecular gas velocity, though dense gas tracers remain undetected [arXiv:2204.07465].

sources.summary: The paper reports no dense-gas tracer detections for S02, then uses CO(3-2), MaNGA, and HST data to identify and compare a blueshifted molecular-gas outflow.

quotes.quote: In Figure fig:radex_co_dens , we show the parameter likelihood corner plots considering CO (1--0), CO (2--1), CO (3--2) and either HCO ^+ (1--0) or HCN (1--0) for the two post-starburst galaxies (H03, S02) with both datasets. For S02, neither dense gas tracer is detected, so we assume the dense gas flux ratios are just below the detection threshold.

In the case of one galaxy, S02, we see evidence for a blueshifted outflow, which we explore further in the next section. Blueshifted Outflow S02 has a blueshifted component to the south east of the CO (3--2) centroid.

We compare the CO (3--2) observations for S02 with archival HST imaging (PI: Zabludoff, ID 11643) and MaNGA manga IFU data in Figure fig:s02 . The stellar velocity field from MaNGA is aligned with the bulk of the molecular gas velocity.

gmw_00012226.name: S05
gmw_00012226.overview: S05 is a post-starburst galaxy characterized by a low star formation rate [arXiv:2204.07465]. Comparisons of Neon-based and H-alpha star formation rates indicate that the galaxy lacks obscured star formation [arXiv:2204.07465].

sources.summary: The paper includes S05 as a post-starburst galaxy with prior ALMA dense-gas observations, low dense-gas limits consistent with low SFRs, and no evidence for significant obscured star formation.

quotes.quote: From the French2015 sample, the galaxies H02 and S05 were previously observed with ALMA in various dense gas tracers, as reported in French2018b .
Our previous study of the dense gas in two post-starburst galaxies H02, S05 French2018b found low limits on and , consistent with their low SFRs.

Smercina2018 measured Neon-based SFRs for a sample of 15 post-starburst galaxies, two of which (S02 and S05) are studied in this work. We compare the Neon-based SFRs to H based SFRs in Figure fig:sfr_comp . No systematic bias is observed; of the two galaxies considered here, the measurements of S02 agree well, and the Neon-based SFR for S05 is lower than the H SFR. This indicates that neither galaxy has significant obscured star formation.

gmw_00012227.name: R02
gmw_00012227.overview: R02 is a young post-starburst galaxy [arXiv:2204.07465]. Unlike most similar galaxies, it exhibits a high dense-gas fraction, which may indicate a rapid decline in dense gas following its starburst phase [arXiv:2204.07465].

sources.summary: The paper treats R02 as a young Rowlands2015 post-starburst whose dense-gas fraction is the sample exception, consistent with rapid early decline in dense gas after the starburst.

quotes.quote: The galaxies R02 and R05 are the second and fifth galaxies in the Rowlands2015 sample, respectively.

Most (5/6) of the post-starburst galaxies have low ( / <0.04 ) fractions, except for R02, which is the youngest post-starburst galaxy in our ALMA-targeted sample. This may indicate a rapid ( <<100 Myr) decline in the dense gas fraction at the start of the starburst, but a larger sample will be required to determine whether this trend is significant, as it is driven by the observations for a single galaxy.

The two highest SFR post-starbursts shown are R02 and R05, from the Rowlands2015 sample, which uses a different selection method (see sec:sample ). These are also the youngest post-starburst galaxies considered here and have values consistent with the SFRs. The location of these young post-starbursts on the -SFR relation is consistent with the evolution of the star formation efficiency ( SFR/ ) seen by Li2019 .

gmw_00012228.name: R05
gmw_00012228.overview: R05 is a young post-starburst galaxy selected from the Rowlands2015 sample [arXiv:2204.07465]. It represents one of the youngest post-starbursts in its class and exhibits a high star formation rate [arXiv:2204.07465].

sources.summary: The paper treats R05 as a young Rowlands2015 post-starburst; HNC remains undetected after re-imaging, and with R02 it forms the highest-SFR, youngest subset.

quotes.quote: The galaxies R02 and R05 are the second and fifth galaxies in the Rowlands2015 sample, respectively.

For the cases in which no lines are detected (HNC in R05 and all lines in S02), we manually re-image the data using updated continuum regions and natural weighting (robust = 2), but are unable to detect any additional emission. We also re-imaged these datasets using a UV taper of 3 arcseconds and are still unable to detect any additional emission.

The two highest SFR post-starbursts shown are R02 and R05, from the Rowlands2015 sample, which uses a different selection method (see sec:sample ). These are also the youngest post-starburst galaxies considered here and have values consistent with the SFRs. The location of these young post-starbursts on the -SFR relation is consistent with the evolution of the star formation efficiency ( SFR/ ) seen by Li2019 .

gmw_00012229.overview: The galaxy 260583 is a bright Magellanic-type irregular galaxy located at a distance of approximately 26 Mpc [arXiv:1805.07407]. It has a spectroscopic redshift of 1816 km/s and is part of a dwarf-dominated group in the COSMOS field [arXiv:1805.07407].

sources.summary: The paper identifies 260583 as a bright Magellanic-type irregular in a dwarf-dominated COSMOS group, with redshift 1816 km/s and many fragmented COSMOS catalog components.

quotes.quote: 260583 (LSBC L1-099) This is a bright Magellanic-type irregular, first catalogued by , and detected in H i by . It has a spectroscopic redshift of 1816 km/s, and is part of a dwarf-dominated group in the COSMOS field at a distance of roughly 26 Mpc. This galaxy is highly fragmented in the COSMOS 2015 catalogue; as many as 18 separate catalog entries may correspond to star-forming regions or nebulosity associated with this galaxy.

gmw_00012230.overview: The irregular galaxy 279307 may be a superposition or a merger of two or more objects [arXiv:1805.07407]. It is split into two separate components in the COSMOS 2015 catalogue and exhibits multiple faint, marginally resolved point sources [arXiv:1805.07407].

sources.summary: The paper describes 279307 as an irregular galaxy, possibly a superposition or merger, split into two COSMOS components and marginally resolved with faint point-source structure.

quotes.quote: 279307 This irregular galaxy may be a superposition or merger between two or more objects. In the COSMOS 2015 catalogue, it is split into two separate components. It appears to have multiple faint/marginal point sources, so we have placed it in Class 2 (marginally resolved), although there is also a single, much brighter point source towards the edge of the object that could be a contaminating foreground star.

gmw_00012231.overview: 549719 is a low-surface-brightness galaxy that is resolved into several dozen faint point sources [arXiv:1805.07407]. These point sources exhibit a broad range of colors, suggesting they are the brightest supergiant stars in an actively star-forming system [arXiv:1805.07407].

sources.summary: The paper describes 549719 as a low-surface-brightness object resolved by HST into many faint point sources, likely an actively star-forming faint member of a nearby COSMOS group.

quotes.quote: 549719 This low surface-brightness object, close to a bright star, is resolved into several dozen faint point sources in HST images (see Figure fig:DG1 ). Unusually, it has imaging in three separate ACS filters, F814W and F475W from the COSMOS survey, and F606W (as well as F814W) from the CANDELS survey CANDELS . A comparison of the different HST images shows that the point sources have a broad range of colours, suggesting that they may be the brightest (supergiant) stars in an actively star-forming system. The object also appears bright in GALEX images of the COSMOS field. The photometric redshift puts this object at a rough distance of 21.5 34 Mpc, but given many COSMOS galaxies in this distance range are part of the previously mentioned group at 26 Mpc, it seems likely that this object is another faint member of the group.

gmw_00012232.overview: 677414 is a bright Magellanic-type irregular galaxy with a spectroscopic redshift of 1729 km/s [arXiv:1805.07407]. It is likely part of the same group as the galaxy 260583 and is fragmented into at least four separate components in the COSMOS 2015 catalogue [arXiv:1805.07407].

sources.summary: The paper identifies 677414 as another bright Magellanic-type irregular, with redshift 1729 km/s, likely associated with 260583, and fragmented into several COSMOS components.

quotes.quote: 677414 (LSBC L1-100) This is another bright, Magellanic-type irregular, originally catalogued by . It has a spectroscopic redshift of 1729 km/s, and is likely part of the same group as 260583. It is fragmented into at least four separate components in the COSMOS 2015 catalogue.

gmw_00012233.name: J100222.70+022520.3
gmw_00012233.overview: J100222.70+022520.3 is a large, diffuse galaxy with a photometric redshift of approximately 0.09 [arXiv:1805.07407]. Deep ground-based imaging reveals that the object contains a central bar and twisting isophotes, making it a candidate field ultra-diffuse galaxy [arXiv:1805.07407].

sources.summary: The paper describes J100222.70+022520.3 as a large diffuse object near a bright star, with possible bar structure, photo-z 0.09, uncertain resolved sources, and candidate field UDG status.

quotes.quote: J100222.70+022520.3 This object is large and relatively diffuse, but is also located very close to a bright star. In the deeper ground-based COSMOS images it appears to have a central bar and twisting isophotes. It was masked out of the COSMOS 2015 catalogue, although it appears in earlier versions of the COSMOS photometric and photo- z catalogues Capak2007, Mobasher07 , where it has a photo- z of 0.09 (i.e. a distance of D 400 Mpc) . It is not clear whether it contains resolved point sources; the one or two in this area may be foreground stars seen in projection. Given its unusual size and surface brightness, we have included it in Class 7, although it is also another plausible candidate field UDG .

gmw_00012234.name: RCP 28
gmw_00012234.overview: RCP 28 is a compact, dwarf irregular galaxy [arXiv:2302.04888]. Hubble Space Telescope observations revealed a thin, linear feature pointing directly to the center of the galaxy that shares its redshift and color [arXiv:2302.04888].

sources.summary: The paper presents RCP 28 as a dwarf, compact irregular galaxy observed with HST, sharing redshift with a thin linear feature that points to its center and has similar color.

quotes.quote: We serendipitously identified a thin, linear feature in HST ACS images of the nearby dwarf galaxy RCP 28 roman:21,dokkum:22 , as shown in Fig. \ overview.fig . RCP 28 was observed September 5 2022 for one orbit in F606W and one orbit in F814W, in the context of mid-cycle program GO-16912.

The streak points to the center of a somewhat irregular-looking galaxy, at =2^ h 41^ m 45 43 ; =-8 20 55 4 (J2000). The galaxy has F814W =21.86 0.10 and F606W F814W = 0.84 0.05 ; that is, the brightness of the streak is 40 of the brightness of the galaxy and both objects have the same color within the errors.

The same emission lines are detected in the galaxy, confirming that it is at the same redshift as the linear feature (see Fig. \ overview.fig ). The galaxy is compact and somewhat irregular, as shown in Fig. \ overview.fig and by the contours in Fig. \ morph.fig .

gmw_00012235.name: IC 1952
gmw_00012235.overview: IC 1952 is classified as a highly inclined, S-shaped barred spiral galaxy [arXiv:2303.00626]. It exhibits localized star formation within its disk, extraplanar gas plumes, and a deficiency in neutral hydrogen [arXiv:2303.00626].

sources.summary: IC 1952 is described as a highly inclined barred spiral with HI deficiency, extraplanar gas, localized disk star formation, and a separable compact background radio source.

quotes.quote: IC 1952 IC 1952 is loosely classified as a highly inclined S-shaped barred spiral galaxy. Due to a contamination of the R band image by a foreground star, measurement of the expected HI mass is not possible hence could not determine whether IC 1952 is HI deficient or not; however, Murugeshan2021 do determine IC 1952 to be HI deficient as compared to its integrated HI disk stability. There is evidence of extraplanar gas with plumes of HI extending out of the disk as well as a slight extension towards the south-east.

Due to the high angular resolution of the WALLABY continuum observations, we can easily distinguish the compact background radio source that lies behind IC 1952. We also observe localised SF occurring within the disk of IC 1952.

gmw_00012236.name: NGC 1422
gmw_00012236.overview: NGC 1422 is a faint, low-mass edge-on barred spiral galaxy located in the Eridanus group [arXiv:2303.00626]. It exhibits a truncated neutral hydrogen disk [arXiv:2108.04414, arXiv:2303.00626] and is strongly perturbed by tidal interactions [arXiv:2303.00626].

sources.summary: NGC 1422 is the sample's faint, low-mass edge-on barred spiral, with HI deficiency, truncated HI/W3PAH disks suggesting ram pressure stripping, strong tidal perturbation, and weak core radio emission.

quotes.quote: NGC 1422 NGC 1422 is classified as an edge-on barred spiral galaxy and is the lowest stellar mass and faintest galaxy in our sample. It is located in the north-eastern region of the Eridanus group and is measured to be HI deficient. The HI disk for NGC 1422 is truncated compared to the stellar disk, and extends beyond the galactic plane suggesting ram-pressure stripping is occurring within this galaxy Murugeshan2021 . Murugeshan2021 also find this galaxy to be HI deficient with respect to its HI disk stability parameter. Wangs2022 find this galaxy to be strongly perturbed by tidal interactions and is the only galaxy in our sample with this classification.

NGC 1422 has the lowest measured SFR and no AGN activity leading to extremely low radio continuum emission that only shows up at the 3 detection limit in the core of the galaxy and is undetected in NVSS. Similar to the HI distribution, we see a truncation of the W3PAH emission at the edge of the stellar disk and an extension beyond the galactic plane, which suggests the heated dust and ISM is also being affected by ram-pressure stripping. The q_ 12 map for this galaxy does not provide evidence for environmental effects due to the extent of radio emission being limited to the core of the galaxy, however we can likely rule out AGN related radio emission due to the values being consistent with SF activity.

sources.summary: The paper highlights NGC 1422 as an Eridanus galaxy with a truncated H I distribution relative to its stellar disk, suggesting environmental processing.

quotes.quote: A few galaxies in the Eridanus group show asymmetric 1 morphologies (for example ESO482-G027, IC 1952, ESO482-G013) and/or exhibit truncated 1 discs as in the case of NGC 1422, ESO548-G070 and IC 1953, without exhibiting any visible perturbations in their stellar discs. In Fig. Fig.5 we show the 1 contours of NGC 1422 and ESO482-G027 overlaid on top of their composite DSS optical images. It is clearly seen that NGC 1422 exhibits a truncated 1 distribution compared to its stellar disc.

gmw_00012237.name: UGC06667
gmw_00012237.overview: UGC06667 is a galaxy characterized by a downward-hook structure [arXiv:2307.09507]. It exhibits double-valued acceleration profiles that turn over at different radii, causing its radial acceleration relation slope to transition from positive to negative as it is tracked inward [arXiv:2307.09507].

sources.summary: UGC06667 is described as a more complicated downward-hook galaxy whose double-valued acceleration profiles turn over at different radii, driving the RAR slope from positive to negative inward.

quotes.quote: A slightly more complicated example of a downward hook is UGC06667 (yellow squares). This galaxy has double-valued acceleration profiles for both \ and , but the turnover points occur at different radii. Specifically, \ peaks and begins to decline at a larger radius than . This means that as we track the RAR profile from the outer part of UGC06667 inward (from low \ to high ), the slope will transition from positive, m >0 , to negative, m<0 , as we cross the radius where \ peaks (where p_ tot first becomes > 2 ).

gmw_00012238.name: COSMOS-22402
gmw_00012238.overview: COSMOS-22402 is a high-redshift star-forming galaxy [arXiv:2007.12197]. It exhibits blended C IV doublet emission [arXiv:2007.12197], high-quality C III] and O III] measurements [arXiv:2007.12197], and a sub-solar C/O abundance ratio [arXiv:2007.12197].

sources.summary: The paper reports COSMOS-22402 has C IV emission, high-quality C III] and O III] measurements, and a sub-solar C/O abundance consistent with the BEAGLE model results.

quotes.quote: We detect the blended C IV 1549 doublet with S/N >3 in three galaxies (COSMOS-22402, UDS-07665, and UDS-08735; Figure fig:uv_spec ). The data imply large C IV EWs in COSMOS-22402 ( 4.7 0.9 ) and UDS-07665 ( 8.6 2.8 ), with C IV /C III ] ratios of 0.46-0.66 .

With our current dataset, this limits us to COSMOS-11530 and COSMOS-22402, each of which have high quality (S/N >7 ) measurements of both lines. To infer the C/O ratios, we follow a similar procedure to other recent analyses e.g. Erb2010,Stark2014,Mainali2020 . We first compute the ratio of doubly ionized carbon and oxygen with PyNeb Luridiana2015 , using the flux ratios of C III ] and O III ] together with an estimate of the electron temperature derived from the dust-corrected O III ] 1666 / [ O III ] 5007 flux ratio. We then apply a small ionization correction factor (ICF) to account for the fact that the fraction of carbon that is in the doubly-ionized state is not necessarily the same as that of oxygen. Following Mainali2020 , we estimate the ICFs using scaling relations derived in Berg2019 . If we conservatively adopt a metallicity range between 0.05 \ Z_ and 0.2 \ Z_ , we find ICF values of 1.0 for COSMOS-11530 and 1.4 for COSMOS-22402. Applying these values, we compute C/O ratios of C/O =-0.95 0.11 ( 0.21 0.05 C/O _ ) for COSMOS-11530 and C/O =-0.48 0.13 ( 0.60 0.18 C/O _ ) for COSMOS-22402. Both values are sub-solar, consistent with the range implied by the BEAGLE models.

gmw_00012239.name: COSMOS-04432
gmw_00012239.overview: COSMOS-04432 is a galaxy characterized by a very red rest-frame ultraviolet continuum, which likely implies a large dust covering fraction [arXiv:2007.12197]. It is one of the strongest C III] emitters in its sample, displaying nebular line properties that suggest active galactic nucleus ionization may be present [arXiv:2007.12197].

sources.summary: The paper identifies COSMOS-04432 as one of the strongest C III] emitters, near the AGN diagnostic boundary, with red UV continuum suggesting dust and a possible power-law AGN spectrum.

quotes.quote: However we note that the two strongest C III ] emitters in our sample (EW _ CIII ] =18.7 \ for COSMOS-04432 and EW _ CIII ] =17.8 \ for UDS-07665) are situated very close to the AGN boundary of the Nakajima2018 diagnostic. For both sources, the [ O III ] line is also extremely prominent, with EW _ [ OIII ] +H =1608 \ (COSMOS-04432) and EW _ [ OIII ] +H =1800 \ (UDS-07665). These objects thus have nebular line properties that are very similar to what has been seen at z>6 , but their position in Figure fig:uv_ratio suggests that AGN ionization might be required to explain the line ratios.

The SED of COSMOS-04432 (top panel of Figure fig:agn_sed ) appears very different. While the flux excess from the rest-frame optical nebular lines are present, the rest-frame UV continuum appears very red ( =-0.90 ), likely implying a significant dust covering fraction. Such red UV slopes are very similar to what is often seen in UV-selected z 3 galaxies with narrow-lined AGN Hainline2011,LeFevre2019 . While rare at z>6 , red UV colors are present in a subset of the population with intense [ O III ] emission Smit2018 . COSMOS-04432 appears to be an analog of these reddened reionization-era sources.

gmw_00012240.name: UDS-08735
gmw_00012240.overview: UDS-08735 is a high-redshift emission line galaxy [arXiv:2007.12197]. Its rest-frame ultraviolet spectrum exhibits a particularly large C IV equivalent width and a low O III]/C IV ratio, which are difficult to explain using stellar ionization alone [arXiv:2007.12197].

sources.summary: The paper finds UDS-08735 has the largest C IV equivalent width in the sample, a low O III]/C IV ratio, red UV slope, and spectra difficult to explain by stars alone.

quotes.quote: We detect the blended C IV 1549 doublet with S/N >3 in three galaxies (COSMOS-22402, UDS-07665, and UDS-08735; Figure fig:uv_spec ). The data imply large C IV EWs in COSMOS-22402 ( 4.7 0.9 ) and UDS-07665 ( 8.6 2.8 ), with C IV /C III ] ratios of 0.46-0.66 . For UDS-08735, we see even stronger C IV (EW =20.4 4.7 ), with a very large C IV /C III ] ratio ( 2.8 ). We do not see any interstellar or stellar absorption in the vicinity of these lines, but we cannot rule out a modest level of emission line filling (and hence larger C IV line strengths). Higher resolution spectra will provide a more robust measure of the total C IV EWs in these systems.

The rest-frame UV spectrum of UDS-08735 is more challenging to reproduce with stars as the primary ionizing source. The C IV EW is the largest in our sample ( 20.4 4.7 ), one of the only measurements known with similar values to those seen at z>6 . As with the sources described above, no X-ray source is present in the vicinity of the galaxy. However the ( O III ] /C IV ) ratio ( <-0.67 at 3 ) is much lower than in the systems described above, similar instead to the values observed in z 2-4 type II quasars ( <-0.4 , Hainline2011, Alexandroff2013 ).

gmw_00012241.name: COSMOS-11530
gmw_00012241.overview: COSMOS-11530 is a star-forming galaxy that exhibits confident He II, O III], and C III] emission lines [arXiv:2007.12197]. The broad profile of its He II emission suggests a contribution from stellar winds, and the galaxy possesses a sub-solar C/O abundance [arXiv:2007.12197].

sources.summary: The paper reports COSMOS-11530 has confident He II, O III], and C III] emission, broad He II likely involving stellar winds, and a sub-solar C/O abundance.

quotes.quote: We detect confident He II 1640 emission ( 8.5 ) in only one galaxy (COSMOS-11530) in our sample. The spectrum of this galaxy also shows O III ] 1666 and C III ] 1908 (Figure fig:uv_spec ). The He II EW is 2.2 0.3 , comparable to the EWs derived for metal-poor dwarf galaxies at z 0-2 e.g. Erb2010,Senchyna2017,Senchyna2019b,Berg2019 . However, the full width at half maximum (FWHM) of He II ( 760 km/s; uncorrected for spectral resolution) is nearly twice that of the O III ] 1666 emission line ( 390 km/s at 5100 , i.e., close to the spectral resolution). The broad profile suggests a significant contribution from stellar winds, as is commonly seen in star-forming galaxies.

With our current dataset, this limits us to COSMOS-11530 and COSMOS-22402, each of which have high quality (S/N >7 ) measurements of both lines. To infer the C/O ratios, we follow a similar procedure to other recent analyses e.g. Erb2010,Stark2014,Mainali2020 . We first compute the ratio of doubly ionized carbon and oxygen with PyNeb Luridiana2015 , using the flux ratios of C III ] and O III ] together with an estimate of the electron temperature derived from the dust-corrected O III ] 1666 / [ O III ] 5007 flux ratio. We then apply a small ionization correction factor (ICF) to account for the fact that the fraction of carbon that is in the doubly-ionized state is not necessarily the same as that of oxygen. Following Mainali2020 , we estimate the ICFs using scaling relations derived in Berg2019 . If we conservatively adopt a metallicity range between 0.05 \ Z_ and 0.2 \ Z_ , we find ICF values of 1.0 for COSMOS-11530 and 1.4 for COSMOS-22402. Applying these values, we compute C/O ratios of C/O =-0.95 0.11 ( 0.21 0.05 C/O _ ) for COSMOS-11530 and C/O =-0.48 0.13 ( 0.60 0.18 C/O _ ) for COSMOS-22402. Both values are sub-solar, consistent with the range implied by the BEAGLE models.

gmw_00012242.name: GOODS-S-46692
gmw_00012242.overview: GOODS-S-46692 is a high-redshift galaxy located at a spectroscopic redshift of z = 4.811 [arXiv:2007.12197]. It exhibits weak Lyman-alpha emission, a blue IRAC color that suggests strong H-alpha emission, and no detected carbon III] emission lines [arXiv:2007.12197].

sources.summary: The paper describes GOODS-S-46692 as a z=4.811 Ly-emitting bright galaxy with blue IRAC color, inferred strong H emission, and no detected C III] in the Y-band spectrum.

quotes.quote: GOODS-S-46692 is the only galaxy in our sample with a spectroscopic redshift from earlier efforts Vanzella2008 , with weak Ly emission (Ly EW =7.7 1.8 ) indicating a redshift of z_ Ly =4.811 . The IRAC color is blue, suggesting the likely presence of strong H emission. The BEAGLE SED modeling suggests the IRAC color can be reproduced with H EW =693 86 .

In this redshift range, the Y-band spectrum covers the C III ] doublet. Assuming that the velocity offset of Ly with respect to C III ] is between 200 and 1000 km s ^ 1 e.g. Erb2014,Stark2017 , the [ C III ] 1907 emission line will be located between 11043.5 and 11087.3 , and the C III ] 1909 line will be located between 11055.4 and 11099.3 . No emission lines are seen in either wavelength range.

gmw_00012243.name: GOODS-S-41253
gmw_00012243.overview: GOODS-S-41253 is a high-redshift emission line galaxy with a photometric redshift of 4.28 [arXiv:2007.12197]. It exhibits the bluest [3.6] - [4.5] IRAC color on the GOODS-S mask, which is consistent with a large H-alpha equivalent width [arXiv:2007.12197].

sources.summary: The paper characterizes GOODS-S-41253 as a photometric-redshift z=4.28 source with very blue IRAC color, large inferred H equivalent width, and no significant C III] detection.

quotes.quote: GOODS-S-41253 ( z_ phot =4.28 ) has the bluest IRAC [ 3.6 ] [ 4.5 ] color on the GOODS-S mask, consistent with a large H EW ( 829 182 ). Given its photometric redshift, we expect the C III ] 1907,1909 emission line to lie in the Y band. No significant feature (S/N >5 ) is seen in the spectrum. Using the clean regions between OH skylines, we estimated the 5 upper limits of line flux and EW for GOODS-S-41253 are 2.4 10^ 18 erg s ^ 1 cm ^ 2 and 11 . These limits correspond to individual doublet components.

gmw_00012244.name: COSMOS-18502
gmw_00012244.overview: COSMOS-18502 is a high-redshift galaxy located at a redshift of approximately 4 [arXiv:2007.12197]. Its Spitzer Infrared Array Camera colors indicate the presence of strong hydrogen-alpha emission, although near-infrared spectroscopy shows no convincing emission line features [arXiv:2007.12197].

sources.summary: The paper reports COSMOS-18502 as a z~4 COSMOS target with IRAC colors indicating strong H emission, but no convincing MOSFIRE line feature and strict C III] upper limits.

quotes.quote: The COSMOS mask contains five z 4 galaxies, two of which (COSMOS-18502 and COSMOS-19732) have IRAC colors indicative of strong H emission ( 400 24 \ and 1000 101 , respectively). Visual inspection of the MOSFIRE spectra reveals no convincing line features. The 5 upper limit on the line flux between OH skylines is 3.1 10^ 18 erg s ^ 1 cm ^ 2 for COSMOS-18502 and 3.0 10^ 18 erg s ^ 1 cm ^ 2 for COSMOS-19732. Using the continuum from the best-fit SED models, we compute a 5 EW upper limit (for individual doublet components) of 4.0 \ for COSMOS-18502 and 2.5 \ for COSMOS-19732.

gmw_00012245.name: COSMOS-19732
gmw_00012245.overview: COSMOS-19732 is a high-redshift galaxy located at a redshift of approximately 4 [arXiv:2007.12197]. Its Infrared Array Camera colors indicate very strong hydrogen-alpha emission, although near-infrared spectroscopy shows no convincing emission line features [arXiv:2007.12197].

sources.summary: The paper reports COSMOS-19732 as a z~4 COSMOS target with IRAC colors indicating very strong H emission, but no convincing MOSFIRE line feature and C III] upper limits.

quotes.quote: The COSMOS mask contains five z 4 galaxies, two of which (COSMOS-18502 and COSMOS-19732) have IRAC colors indicative of strong H emission ( 400 24 \ and 1000 101 , respectively). Visual inspection of the MOSFIRE spectra reveals no convincing line features. The 5 upper limit on the line flux between OH skylines is 3.1 10^ 18 erg s ^ 1 cm ^ 2 for COSMOS-18502 and 3.0 10^ 18 erg s ^ 1 cm ^ 2 for COSMOS-19732. Using the continuum from the best-fit SED models, we compute a 5 EW upper limit (for individual doublet components) of 4.0 \ for COSMOS-18502 and 2.5 \ for COSMOS-19732.

gmw_00012246.name: COSMOS-11116
gmw_00012246.overview: COSMOS-11116 is a high-redshift galaxy with a photometric redshift of approximately 5.62 [arXiv:2007.12197]. Spectroscopic observations tentatively identify an O III] 1666 emission line, which suggests a redshift of 5.719 [arXiv:2007.12197].

sources.summary: The paper tentatively identifies a 6.2-sigma line in COSMOS-11116 as O III] 1666, giving z=5.719 and an 8.8 Angstrom rest-frame equivalent width.

quotes.quote: The first of these sources, COSMOS-11116, has a photometric redshift of z_ phot =5.62 . We detect a 6.2 emission line feature centered at 11194.3 \ with flux of 3.1 0.5 10^ 18 erg s ^ 1 cm ^ 2 (Figure fig:COS11116_spec ), which is at the expected spatial position of COSMOS-11116. The emission line is unresolved, with FWHM similar to the spectral resolution ( 3.3 ). It is shown that the emission line has the standard negative-positive-negative pattern resulting from the subtraction of AB dither pattern, indicating that the emission line is present in both dither positions.

Given the photometric redshift, we conclude that the line is most likely either O III ] 1666 or O III ] 1661 . The separation between O III ] doublets is 35 \ at the expected redshift, which is more than 10 the FWHM of the emission line, indicating that the O III ] doublets must be resolved in the spectrum. If the emission line feature is O III ] 1666 (O III ] 1661 ), the systematic redshift of COSMOS-11116 would be z=5.719 ( z=5.740 ), which is consistent with the photometric redshift. Using the spectroscopic redshift derived from O III ] 1666 (O III ] 1661 ), we search for O III ] 1661 (O III ] 1666 ) which should be located at 11158.4 ( 11230.3 ) . No convincing ( >5 ) emission line feature is detected at the expected position. Theoretical transition probabilities imply a O III ] 1666 /O III ] 1661 ratio =2.5 FroeseFischer1985 , and the observed doublet ratio found in high-redshift star-forming galaxies is 1666:1661>1 e.g. Stark2014,Mainali2020 . Therefore, we expect the O III ] 1666 emission line to be stronger than O III ] 1661 . As a result, we tentatively identify this emission line as O III ] 1666 , implying a spectroscopic redshift of z=5.719 . Using the far-UV continuum flux density derived from the best-fitting BEAGLE model, we compute the rest-frame O III ] 1666 EW ( =8.8 1.7 ).

gmw_00012247.name: 4C 12.02
gmw_00012247.overview: 4C 12.02 is a giant Fanaroff-Riley II radio galaxy located at a redshift of z = 0.143 [arXiv:2105.11695]. It features a central radio core, two lobes, and hot spots with a steep spectrum that suggests they are no longer replenished by fresh electrons [arXiv:2105.11695].

sources.summary: The paper images 4C 12.02 as a giant FR II radio galaxy with resolved multi-peak hot spots, lobe substructure, and a steep lobe/hot-spot spectrum suggesting depleted fresh electrons.

quotes.quote: It is an FR II radio galaxy whose optical counterpart (a galaxy) is located at redshift z = 0.143. 1975MmRAS..79....1S first noted it as a triple source, with a radio core, and two radio lobes located on opposite sides along the east-west direction. Recently, Xiaolongetal reported it as a representative giant X-shaped radio source based on the GMRT TGSS ADR1 Intemaetal . Our uGMRT and MeerKAT images are shown in Fig. sample-NVSS (top-left panel) and Fig. Meer-uGMRT (top-left and top-right panels respectively). The angular resolution of the uGMRT provides important insight into the morphology of the hot spots, while the MeerKAT image is better suited to highlight the details and extent of the lobes.
In particular, the uniform steep spectrum in the lobes and hot-spots of 4C 12.02 suggests that the hot-spots are no longer replenished by fresh electrons.

gmw_00012248.name: CGCG 044--046
gmw_00012248.overview: CGCG 044--046 is a cD radio galaxy located at the center of the Zwicky cluster 1313.7+0721 [arXiv:2105.11695]. It belongs to the wide-angle tail class, featuring bent radio tails shaped by its motion through the intracluster medium [arXiv:2105.11695].

sources.summary: The paper describes CGCG 044--046 as a cD wide-angle-tail radio galaxy in a cluster, with bent extended tails, sharp spectral transitions, and polarization asymmetry shaped by the environment.

quotes.quote: The source is identified as a m = 14.2 cD galaxy and is associated with the Zwicky cluster 1313.7 + 0721 at z = 0.050145 ZwickyHerzog . Indeed the bent radio morphology is typical of radio galaxies at the centre of galaxy clusters and groups. Some notable examples are ESO 137--006 Ramatsokuetal and 3C 465 Eileketal , but see also ODonoghueetal1990 , and Garonetal for a recent analysis. The radio source was first studied in detail by Patnaik1984 and Patnaik1986 , who noticed the asymmetry between the sharp bend of about 90 ^ in the eastern tail and the more gentle bend in the western one, with diffuse emission further out in both. This asymmetry is confirmed in both our images (see Fig. sample-NVSS , upper-right panel, and Fig. Meer-uGMRT , third row, left and right panels for uGMRT and MeerKAT respectively), whose sensitivity allows the detection of emission from the radio tails to much larger distances than previously detected at these frequencies (see also Sec. cgcg044 ). The morphology suggests that it possibly belongs to the wide-angle tail class of radio galaxies.

CGCG 044--046 The overall observational properties of CGCG 044--046 source suggest that the local environment plays a major role. More specifically, the physical mechanism giving rise to bent tails is possibly due to the motion of the host galaxy through the ICM. The source extends 414 kpc to the west and 207 kpc to the south-east, i.e. well into the ICM.

gmw_00012249.name: CGCG 021--063
gmw_00012249.overview: CGCG 021--063 is a Fanaroff-Riley II radio galaxy with a projected linear size of 280 kpc [arXiv:2105.11695]. The galaxy features a single visible jet, two hot spots, and filamentary lobe structures embedded within a low-surface-brightness cocoon of radio emission [arXiv:2105.11695].

sources.summary: The paper classifies CGCG 021--063 as an FR II radio galaxy embedded in a low-surface-brightness cocoon, with one visible jet, hot spots, filamentary lobes, and probable back-flow emission.

quotes.quote: Very little information is reported in the literature on the arcsecond-scale properties of this radio galaxy, characterised by a compact, i.e. < 0.2 milli-arcsec in size Dodsonetal , radio core. Using the FIRST survey image, reported that this source has a resolved compact component, with triple extended structure and possibly a S or Z shaped structure. Our uGMRT and MeerKAT images (Fig. sample-NVSS , bottom-right panel, and bottom row in Fig. Meer-uGMRT , left panel and right panel respectively) are dynamic range limited, most likely because of the strong compact core. However, they clearly show that the radio emission of CGCG 021--063 has two components: a radio galaxy, whose projected linear size is 280 kpc that is embedded in a low-surface brightness cocoon of radio emission. The inner radio galaxy and the lobes have an FR II morphology, with only one visible jet and two hot spots of moderate brightness.

It is interesting to note that the west jet seems to propagate beyond the hot spot or working surface of the jet. There is filamentary structure in the east lobe which is well reproduced in both images and there are also clearly beads in the west jet.
