gmw_00012305.overview: System 1 is a triply lensed spiral galaxy that is the largest multiply-lensed system in its field [arXiv:1406.2702]. Its photometric redshift is estimated at z=1.80 after spectroscopy ruled out lower-redshift emission lines [arXiv:1406.2702].

sources.summary: System 1 is the largest multiply-lensed source, a triply lensed spiral galaxy whose photometric redshift favored z=1.80 after spectroscopy ruled out lower-redshift emission lines.

quotes.quote: System 1. This is a triply lensed spiral galaxy, which is the largest multiply-lensed system. We compute the photometric redshift of image 1.3, which is the one that is least contaminated by cluster light, thus presumably the one that yields the most accurate photometric redshift. The probability distribution peaks at z 1.5 with a secondary peak at z 1.8. A spectroscopic study by johnson14 , however, failed to detect any emission lines on this image between 440nm and 980nm, thus placing a lower limit of z>1.6. As a result, we chose the second likely photometric redshift z=1.80 as input.

gmw_00012306.name: System 2
gmw_00012306.overview: System 2 is a four-image gravitationally lensed system [arXiv:1406.2702]. It features two long radial arcs focused on the central brightest cluster galaxy and two outer tangential arcs [arXiv:1406.2702].

sources.summary: System 2 is modeled as four related lensed images, with radial and tangential arcs whose relensed predictions mostly match observed centroids but reveal central cD-galaxy complexity.

quotes.quote: System 2. This is a four-image system where two long radial arcs are focused on the central BCG and two tangential arcs lie further out in radius, outside the tangential critical curve. As seen in figure system2 we securely demonstrate that these four images are related by successively delensing and relensing to generate predictions for the other three. We can see that the agreement between the predicted images and the observations is in general very good in terms of the predicted centroids. There is a small difference in the orientation of the central radially directed images, indicating the DM distribution of the central cD galaxy may be more complex than the circular symmetry assumed in our model.

gmw_00012307.name: System 4
gmw_00012307.overview: System 4 is a high-redshift galaxy system with a spectroscopic redshift of z=3.580 [arXiv:1406.2702]. It is a strongly gravitationally lensed source consisting of a double-image pair, a faint counter-image, and a long radial arc [arXiv:1406.2702].

sources.summary: System 4 consists of multiple images from one source, including a double image pair and radial arc, with an SED redshift consistent with spectroscopy.

quotes.quote: System 4. This system comprises a double-image pair straddling the critical curve, along with a fainter counter image at a large radius from the BCG. In addition, a long radial arc was identified crossing a radial critical curve centred on a bright cluster member to the South. We show that all these images belong to the same source by successively delensing and relensing each image, selected examples of which are shown in figure system4 . A number of re-lensed cases are not useful to show where the observed image is of low contrast against the extended light of a member galaxy. The SED of 4.3 is compatible with a photometric redshift of z=3.47 0.44, which is consistent with the spectroscopic redshift of z=3.580 richard14, johnson14 .

gmw_00012308.name: System 6
gmw_00012308.overview: System 6 is a high-surface-brightness triple system [arXiv:1406.2702]. It has a spectroscopic redshift of 2.019, which is in good agreement with its photometric and geometric redshifts [arXiv:1406.2702].

sources.summary: System 6 is a high-surface-brightness triple system whose photometric, geometric, and spectroscopic redshifts agree, so it is used to normalize the lens model.

quotes.quote: System 6. A high surface brightness triple system. We determine a photometric redshift of z=2.34 0.33 and geometric redshift of z=2.16 0.10, both in good agreement with the spectroscopic redshift of z=2.019 richard14, johnson14 . This object accurately falls on our distance-redshift relation as its spectroscopic redshift was used as input for our lens model reconstruction. We use this system as our model normalization as described in section sec:geoz .

gmw_00012309.name: System 7
gmw_00012309.overview: System 7 is a high-redshift galaxy system identified through strong gravitational lensing [arXiv:1406.2702]. It features a triple-image configuration and has a derived photometric redshift of approximately 3.00 [arXiv:1406.2702].

sources.summary: System 7 has a bimodal photometric-redshift uncertainty, but its triple-image configuration resembles System 6 and argues against a low-redshift interloper interpretation.

quotes.quote: System 7. The derived photometric redshift of 7.3 is z=3.00^ +0.39 _ 2.80 . The large uncertainty towards the low redshift is due to a bimodal probability distribution. Since the spatial configuration of this triple system is similar to system 6, they should have comparable redshifts, thus ruling out the possibility of a being a low-redshift interloper.

gmw_00012310.name: System 9
gmw_00012310.overview: System 9 is a faint gravitationally lensed image pair that may include an unidentified third image at a large radius [arXiv:1406.2702]. It has a photometric redshift of 1.74, though its broad and complex probability distribution results in a large uncertainty [arXiv:1406.2702].

sources.summary: System 9 is described as a faint image pair with a possible third image; its broad redshift uncertainty prevents use in the lens model.

quotes.quote: System 9. A pair of faint images and a unidentified third image possibly at a large radius. We measure the photometric redshift of 9.2 to be z=1.74^ +1.34 _ 1.62 . The large uncertainty reflects a broad and complex probability distribution in redshift space. As we cannot confidently measure its redshift, system 9 is not included into constructing our model.

gmw_00012311.name: System 11
gmw_00012311.overview: System 11 is a faint, high-redshift galaxy with a bimodal, double structure [arXiv:1406.2702]. The system is strongly gravitationally lensed into four multiple images with a wide angular separation [arXiv:1406.2702].

sources.summary: System 11 is a faint bimodal four-image object whose geometric and photometric redshifts agree; its wide image separation disfavors a low-redshift interloper.

quotes.quote: System 11. This is a faint bimodal object with four images. We infer a geometric redshift of z=2.76 0.02, which is in good agreement with the photometric redshift of z=2.88^ +0.64 _ 2.67 . Again, the large uncertainty towards the low-redshifts is due to the bimodal probability distribution. The fact that its multiple images are separated by large angles imply that it being a low-redshift interloper is improbable. We also relens the system as it is extended and has a double structure.

gmw_00012312.name: System 12
gmw_00012312.overview: System 12 is a high-redshift galaxy with a measured photometric redshift of approximately 2.77 [arXiv:1406.2702]. It is observed as a compact three-image strong gravitationally lensed configuration located near a Y-shaped junction of critical curves [arXiv:1406.2702].

sources.summary: System 12 is a compact three-image lensed configuration near a Y-shaped critical-curve junction, with a possible dim fourth image at larger radius.

quotes.quote: System 12. It consists of 3 multiple images each located not far away from one another. This less common configuration arises because they are lensed near a Y-shaped junction of critical curves. Its measured photometric redshift of z=2.77^ +0.69 _ 2.58 . We rule out the possibility of it being a low-redshift interloper with the same argument supplied for system 11. A fourth image is expected at large radus but is not confidently identified due to its expected dimness.

gmw_00012313.name: System 14
gmw_00012313.overview: System 14 is a high-redshift galaxy system that is subject to strong gravitational lensing [arXiv:1406.2702]. The system has a photometric redshift of z=5.61 and contains three identified lensed images, one of which is buried in the light of a group of member galaxies [arXiv:1406.2702].

sources.summary: System 14 is revised from an earlier two-image identification; the paper proposes a better second image, gives z=5.61, and identifies a buried third image.

quotes.quote: System 14. It was first identified by atek14 as their system 1, which comprises two images. We propose an alternative, which has a more plausible surface brightness and colour, to their second image. It is designated as 14.2 and is the most magnified image of system 14. We calculated for this image a photometric redshift of z=5.61 0.65. We also identified a third image, 14.3, which is badly buried in the light of a group of member galaxies but clearly identified at the expected location given by our model.

gmw_00012314.name: System 16
gmw_00012314.overview: System 16 is a high-redshift galaxy system that is strongly gravitationally lensed [arXiv:1406.2702]. The system consists of three lensed images, with the brightest image yielding a photometric redshift of z = 4.89 [arXiv:1406.2702].

sources.summary: System 16 is a three-image lensed system whose model-preferred third image has consistent colour, and whose brightest image gives a photometric redshift of z=4.89.

quotes.quote: System 16. It was first identified by atek14 as having three images. Our image 16.3 is firmly preferred by our lens model to the identification 4.1 proposed by , and with a colour consistent with 16.1 and 16.2. We derive a photometric redshift of z=4.89 0.58 from the brightest image 16.1.

gmw_00012315.name: System 17
gmw_00012315.overview: System 17 is a galaxy located at a very high redshift, with a measured photometric redshift of 6.65 [arXiv:1406.2702]. The system features two central images situated close to the brightest cluster galaxy [arXiv:1406.2702].

sources.summary: System 17 is a very high-redshift system with central images near the BCG; the paper rejects one claimed faint counterimage and secures a nearby alternative.

quotes.quote: System 17. It is at very high redshift with a measured photometric redshift of z=6.65 0.85. Two central images lie close to the BCG. In this region a faint counter image claimed by atek14 (as their image 5.4) does not seem to correspond to a faint red source, but rather to noise. We do find the anticipated image relatively nearby, 17.4, and with consistent colours which we take to be a very secure identification.

gmw_00012316.name: System 18
gmw_00012316.overview: System 18 is a high-redshift galaxy with a photometric redshift of approximately z = 7.01 [arXiv:1406.2702]. It is observed as a triple-image system due to strong gravitational lensing and is detected exclusively in near-infrared bands [arXiv:1406.2702].

sources.summary: System 18 is a z=7.01 triple-image high-redshift source, unique in color, detected only in NIR bands, and well reproduced by the lens model.

quotes.quote: We identified system 18, which is included in zheng14 prior to this work.

System 18. Triple image at photometric redshift of z=7.01 0.78, consistent with the finding of zheng14 . This high redshift system is unique in colour and detected only in the NIR bands. As shown in figure system18 , we find good agreement between the observed images and those we predict.

gmw_00012317.name: System 19
gmw_00012317.overview: System 19 is a multiply-lensed galaxy located in the cluster A2744 [arXiv:1406.2702]. It is identified as a double-image pair with a photometric redshift of approximately 9.83, though its predicted third image remains unobserved or obscured by the light of neighboring galaxies [arXiv:1406.2702].

sources.summary: System 19 is a double-image pair and the highest-redshift multiply-lensed galaxy in A2744; its missing third image may be too faint or buried in nearby light.

quotes.quote: System 19. This is a double image pair identified by zitrin14 , and is the multiply-lensed galaxy with the highest redshift in A2744. We adopt the photometric redshift of z=9.83^ +0.22 _ 0.44 from zitrin14 . The unidentified third image may be too dim to be observed, or it is too heavily buried in the light of nearby galaxies.

gmw_00012318.name: System 21
gmw_00012318.overview: System 21 is a high-redshift galaxy with a photometric redshift of approximately 6.97 [arXiv:1406.2702]. It is a triply-lensed system featuring two clearly visible central images and a third predicted image that is too faint to be detected [arXiv:1406.2702].

sources.summary: System 21 is identified as a high-redshift triply-lensed galaxy at z=6.97, with two visible central images and a predicted faint image at larger radius.

quotes.quote: System 21. Also, identified in the complete HFF data, it is a high-redshift triply-lensed galaxy at a photometric redshift of z=6.97 0.78. 20.1 can be clearly seen in the Western side of the central BCG, while 20.2 appears close to one of the member galaxy, but is still visible. 20.3 is predicted to appear at a large radius near the position of 17.1, but the expected flux is too low for it to be detected.

gmw_00012319.name: System 22
gmw_00012319.overview: System 22 is a high-redshift galaxy system [arXiv:1406.2702] that is green in color [arXiv:1406.2702]. It features three images, two of which straddle the critical curve near the central brightest cluster galaxy [arXiv:1406.2702].

sources.summary: System 22 is a green three-image system straddling the critical curve; delensing and relensing gives the largest positional offset, likely from a poor SED fit.

quotes.quote: System 22. It is green in colour, with two images straddling the critical curve near the central BCG, and a third image at large radius. Delensing and relensing this system yields the largest positional offset. We suspect this may be due to the relatively poor fit of the SED, which yields a photometric redshift of z-4.64 0.58.

gmw_00012320.name: System 23
gmw_00012320.overview: System 23 is a blue galaxy characterized by a bimodal internal structure [arXiv:1406.2702]. Strong gravitational lensing reveals a double pair near its primary images, and the galaxy has a photometric redshift of 1.81 [arXiv:1406.2702].

sources.summary: System 23 is a blue galaxy with bimodal internal structure; the paper identifies a double pair but finds the predicted third image too faint to match.

quotes.quote: System 23. A blue galaxy with bimodal internal structure. A double pair can be clearly seen near images 1.1 and 1.2, but the 3rd image is predicted to have a small flux which is difficult for us to match. We derive a photometric redshift of z=1.81 0.28.

gmw_00012321.name: HIPASS J1123+13
gmw_00012321.overview: HIPASS J1123+13, also known as Leib, is a dwarf galaxy characterized by an extremely low surface brightness and a high gas content [arXiv:0902.0256]. An H I filament, which is barely visible optically, extends from this source toward the neighboring bright galaxy NGC 3628 [arXiv:0902.0256].

sources.summary: The paper identifies HIPASS J1123+13, dubbed Leib, as an extremely low-surface-brightness unusual dwarf system with high gas content, apparent optical dimensions, and an H I filament toward NGC 3628.

quotes.quote: Based on the results of our work, we found ten more galaxies with a high gas content per unit luminosity: J0718-09 (6.6), J0727+04 (7.4), J1007-66 (12.4), Leib (19.1), KKSG 47 (7.8), J1549+16 (11.0), J1739-51 (10.4), J2011-09 (5.4), J2015+12 (6.6), and ESO 403--036 (5.1). Among them, only one galaxy, J1739-51, exceeds significantly the critical condition (2). However, its visual B_T estimate needs a photometric improvement. Note that our sample includes the unusual dwarf system HIPASS J1123+13 in the nearby group around NGC 3628. We identified this HIPASS source with an object of an extremely low surface brightness with angular sizes of 1.4 ^ 0.8^ and a total apparent magnitude B_T =17.5^m . According to Haynes et al. (1979), an HI filament, which is also barely seen optically, extends from this source to the neighboring bright galaxy NGC 3628. Obviously, this exotic object, whichwe called ``Leib'', deserves a more detailed study at various wavelengths.

gmw_00012322.name: source A
gmw_00012322.overview: Source A is a candidate high-redshift radio galaxy [arXiv:1109.5906]. It is characterized by a straight, steep power-law spectrum between 1.4 GHz and 153 MHz [arXiv:1109.5906].

sources.summary: The paper revisits Croft et al.'s candidate high-redshift radio galaxies, adds 153 MHz spectral-index measurements, and finds source A retains a straight steep spectrum meeting the USS HzRG criterion.

quotes.quote: croft2008 examine 4 candidate HzRGs (which they labelled A, B, C and E) based on their steep ( 0.87 ) spectral index between 1.4 GHz and 325 MHz. As their candidate sources are also present in the 153 MHz catalog, we complement their data with our new spectral index measurements in Table tab:bootes_croft . Both sources A and E appear to have fairly straight power-law spectra down to 153 MHz, while sources B and C appear to undergo considerable spectral flattening. Based on our selection criteria for USS sources, only source A would be considered a candidate HzRG.

gmw_00012323.name: BD 34
gmw_00012323.overview: The galaxy BD 34 is identified as an outlier with an especially steep metallicity gradient [arXiv:1505.02797]. However, this gradient is poorly constrained and carries very large errors because it is measured from only four valid data points [arXiv:1505.02797].

sources.summary: The paper identifies BD 34 as an especially steep-gradient outlier, but notes its metallicity gradient has large errors because only four valid data points constrain it.

quotes.quote: We highlight two galaxies with especially steep metallicity gradients. Firstly, BD 34 which shows very large errors in its measured metallicity gradient. This gradient is measured from only four valid data points and is poorly constrained.

gmw_00012324.name: BD 45
gmw_00012324.overview: The galaxy BD 45 is characterized by an especially steep metallicity gradient [arXiv:1505.02797]. Additionally, its metallicity profile displays some hints of asymmetry [arXiv:1505.02797].

sources.summary: BD 45 is highlighted as an outlier with an especially steep metallicity profile that hints at asymmetry, although the paper gives no clear explanation for the extreme inferred gradient.

quotes.quote: We highlight two galaxies with especially steep metallicity gradients.

Secondly, BD 45 whose metallicity profile shows some hints of asymmetry, but otherwise offers no explanation for the excessively steep inferred metallicity gradient. Regardless, we retain both these outlying galaxies in our analysis.

gmw_00012325.name: Ly 1
gmw_00012325.overview: Ly 1 is a Lyman-alpha emitting galaxy that exhibits optical continuum emission and has a rest-frame B magnitude comparable to an L* galaxy [arXiv:1607.06755]. Its gas heating is driven by local star formation, and the galaxy may reside within a tentative atomic gas bridge resulting from a gravitational interaction [arXiv:1607.06755].

sources.summary: Ly 1 is presented as a Ly-emitting galaxy north of the QSO with optical continuum, L*-like B magnitude, star-formation-powered line ratios, and possible placement in a tentative atomic-gas bridge.

quotes.quote: The Ly- emission in the BRI1202--0725 field shows a broad line from the QSO, and two additional sources: a detected extension 2.3 '' to the north of the QSO peti96,hu96,will14 , called Ly 1, and emission from a galaxy 3'' to the southwest of the QSO, called Ly 2 hu97,will14 . Optical continuum emission from both Ly 1 ( font96,hu96,font98 ) and Ly 2 ( will14 ) is also detected. Ly 1 shows a rest frame B magnitude comparable to a L* galaxy ( hu96 ). A study of the permitted UV atomic emission lines and the forbidden FIR fine structure lines from Ly 1 suggest that the QSO is unlikely to be the heating source for the ionized gas, and the line ratios are consistent with heating by local star formation will14 .

The [ CII ] emission shows Ly 1 may be in a complete bridge of atomic gas between the QSO and SMG cari13 . Such a bridge would be the natural consequence of strong gravitational interaction during the merging of two gas rich galaxies, although the detection of the bridge remains tentative.

gmw_00012326.name: Ly 2
gmw_00012326.overview: Ly 2 is a Lyman-alpha emitting galaxy located southwest of the quasar BRI1202-0725 [arXiv:1607.06755]. It has a star formation rate of 170 solar masses per year and a gas depletion timescale comparable to main-sequence galaxies [arXiv:1607.06755].

sources.summary: The paper identifies Ly 2 as a Ly-emitting galaxy southwest of the QSO, associated with outflow-direction emission, detected in CO, with substantial gas mass and a main-sequence-like depletion time.

quotes.quote: The Ly- emission in the BRI1202--0725 field shows a broad line from the QSO, and two additional sources: a detected extension 2.3 '' to the north of the QSO peti96,hu96,will14 , called Ly 1, and emission from a galaxy 3'' to the southwest of the QSO, called Ly 2 hu97,will14 . Optical continuum emission from both Ly 1 ( font96,hu96,font98 ) and Ly 2 ( will14 ) is also detected.

Evidence suggests that Ly 2 may be somehow associated with an outflow from the QSO. In particular, an extension from the QSO towards Ly 2 has been seen in CO( J=5 4 ) salo12 , and emission between the QSO and Ly 2 appears in the [ CII ] channel maps of cari13 . The star formation rate, estimated from the dust continuum emission, is 170 M _ year ^ 1 cari13 .

We also detect CO( J=2 1 ) emission from Ly 2 in the BRI1202--0725 system. The total gas mass is (3.2 0.5) ( _ CO /0.8) 10^ 10 M _ , and the gas depletion timescale is 2 10^8 ( _ CO /0.8) yr, where _ CO has units of M _ K ^ 1 km ^ 1 s pc ^ 2 . Even assuming a low value of _ CO (compared to _ CO 4 for normal galaxies; bola13 ), this galaxy has a gas depletion timescale comparable to main sequence galaxies at low and high redshift (e.g., 7 10^8 years for z=1-3 MS SFGs, tacc13 ), and not the extremely short timescales applicable to starbursts.

gmw_00012327.name: NVSS J104938+274212
gmw_00012327.overview: NVSS J104938+274212 is a galaxy located at a redshift of z = 0.144 [arXiv:2203.13268]. It features a resolved radio structure on arcsecond scales and contains a compact radio component on parsec scales [arXiv:2203.13268].

sources.summary: The paper identifies NVSS J104938+274212 as a possible radio counterpart, a z=0.144 galaxy with arcsecond-scale radio structure, a compact parsec-scale component, and measured 1.5 GHz luminosity.

quotes.quote: Lying within the 50 uncertainty region, 4FGL was the first identified candidate source associated with the neutrino event. Its possible radio counterpart is NVSS J104938+274212 (SDSS J104938.80+274213.0, a galaxy at z = 0.144, 2015ApJS..219...12A ). Parameters of VLBI observation are reported in Table tab:obs_res_appendix and spectral indices deduced from these data are in Table tab:ind-spec_app . The FIRST 1.4 GHz peak flux density is 9.2 mJy, and the integrated flux density is 15.6 mJy, suggesting that contains a resolved radio structure on the arcsecond-scales (see Table tab:nvss-first ). Our VLBI observations reveal a compact component on parsec-scales, with a lower flux density than what is observed in the VLA surveys.
The luminosity of the source at 1.5 GHz is (2.1 0.2) 10^ 23 W Hz^ 1 , assuming a spectral index _ NVSS ^ VLASS =-0.3 0.2 .

gmw_00012328.name: J1725.5+1312
gmw_00012328.overview: J1725.5+1312 is a faint gamma-ray source that exhibits both high and low levels of activity [arXiv:2203.13268]. Its weak radio counterpart has a structure consisting of a main core and a southeastern blob component, making it a strong blazar candidate [arXiv:2203.13268].

sources.summary: The paper describes J1725.5+1312 as a faint gamma-ray source with high and low activity levels whose weak radio counterpart appears to be a compact blazar candidate.

quotes.quote: is the possible counterpart for the newly detected ray source, J1725.5+1312, reported in 2020ATel14111....1B . This ray source was significantly detected in the ray band only performing the integration of the data taken over the period of 10 years 2020ATel14111....1B . This suggests the idea that J1725.5+1312 is a faint source in the ray band, experiencing high and low level of activity. Also, the association with a radio (although weak) counterpart, identified in the VLASS data, indicates it represents a good blazar candidate.

is clearly detected in our e-MERLIN images. Its radio structure consists of a main component with a flux density of about 0.96 mJy, and a blob component 90 mas south-east from the core, with a flux density of about 160 Jy. The source is not detected in the NVSS, allowing us to set an upper limit of about 0.6 mJy (i.e., 3 times the noise measured on the image plane).

gmw_00012329.name: host galaxy of GRB 020405
gmw_00012329.overview: The host galaxy of GRB 020405 is a member of an interacting group of galaxies [arXiv:astro-ph/0208008] at a heliocentric redshift of 0.68986 [arXiv:astro-ph/0208008]. It exhibits bright emission lines [arXiv:astro-ph/0208008] and has a lower-limit star formation rate of 4 solar masses per year [arXiv:astro-ph/0208008].

sources.summary: The paper identifies the GRB 020405 host in an interacting group and measures host emission lines, a heliocentric redshift, and a lower-limit star-formation rate from [O II] and H lines.

quotes.quote: In particular, observations with the VLT rapidly identified the host galaxy to be a member of a group of interacting galaxies mpm+02 at a redshift of z = 0.695 0.005 mpp+02 .

We detect several bright emission lines which we attribute to [ O II ] , H and [ O III ] at a mean Heliocentric redshift of 0.68986 0.00004, consistent with the measurement first reported by mpp+02 . We list these emission lines in Table  tab:spec . Using the observed [ O II ] and H line fluxes and assuming a flat Lambda cosmology with H_0 = 65 km s ^ 1 Mpc ^ 1 and _ M = 0.3, we calculate ken98 a star formation rate of 4 M _ yr ^ 1 . This star-formation rate is uncorrected for extinction or stellar absorption, and is therefore a lower limit.

gmw_00012330.name: EES-Cetus 1
gmw_00012330.overview: EES-Cetus 1 is a radio-detected extremely red object with a secure radio counterpart [arXiv:astro-ph/0112342]. Depending on the model interpretation, the galaxy is either a star-forming system at a redshift of z < 1.5 or an old elliptical galaxy at z > 1.3 [arXiv:astro-ph/0112342].

sources.summary: EES-Cetus 1 is a radio-detected ERO with a secure counterpart; its data allow either star-forming or old-elliptical interpretations with z<1.5 or z>1.3 constraints.

quotes.quote: We have detected 1.4 GHz radio continuum emission from the NIR positions of two EROs : EES-Cetus 1 and EES-Cetus 2 (hereafter, EESC1 and EESC2). Though there appears to be some extended emission in the image of EESC1, given the signal-to-noise ratios of the two detections, the two sources are essentially unresolved. This implies that the a posteriori probabilities of the radio detections being actual counterparts are 99.92 and 97.73 for EESC1 and EESC2 respectively (assuming that the a priori probability that the ERO does have a radio counterpart is 50 ). Hence the radio counterpart to EESC1 seems real but the identification of the counterpart of EESC2 is slightly less secure.

The limiting redshifts of EESC1 and EESC2, derived as described above, are z< 1.5 and z< 2.0 respectively. If the two galaxies are assumed to be old ellipticals instead, then using the pure luminosity evolution models, the extreme R-K colours of the two galaxies ( R-K> 6.1) imply that they lie at redshifts greater than 1.3 (Daddi et al. 2000b) dcr00 . For z between 1.3 and 5, the 1.4 GHz rest-frame luminosities of EESC1 and EESC2 correspond to the luminosity range of FR I galaxies or radio-quiet ellipticals.

gmw_00012331.name: EES-Cetus 2
gmw_00012331.overview: EES-Cetus 2 is a radio-detected extremely red object [arXiv:astro-ph/0112342]. Depending on whether it is a star-forming galaxy or an old elliptical galaxy, its redshift is constrained to z < 2.0 or z > 1.3 respectively [arXiv:astro-ph/0112342].

sources.summary: EES-Cetus 2 is a radio-detected ERO with a less secure counterpart; its data allow either star-forming or old-elliptical interpretations with z<2.0 or z>1.3 constraints.

quotes.quote: We have detected 1.4 GHz radio continuum emission from the NIR positions of two EROs : EES-Cetus 1 and EES-Cetus 2 (hereafter, EESC1 and EESC2). Though there appears to be some extended emission in the image of EESC1, given the signal-to-noise ratios of the two detections, the two sources are essentially unresolved. This implies that the a posteriori probabilities of the radio detections being actual counterparts are 99.92 and 97.73 for EESC1 and EESC2 respectively (assuming that the a priori probability that the ERO does have a radio counterpart is 50 ). Hence the radio counterpart to EESC1 seems real but the identification of the counterpart of EESC2 is slightly less secure.

The limiting redshifts of EESC1 and EESC2, derived as described above, are z< 1.5 and z< 2.0 respectively. If the two galaxies are assumed to be old ellipticals instead, then using the pure luminosity evolution models, the extreme R-K colours of the two galaxies ( R-K> 6.1) imply that they lie at redshifts greater than 1.3 (Daddi et al. 2000b) dcr00 . For z between 1.3 and 5, the 1.4 GHz rest-frame luminosities of EESC1 and EESC2 correspond to the luminosity range of FR I galaxies or radio-quiet ellipticals.

gmw_00012332.name: SA57-1
gmw_00012332.overview: SA57-1 is an extremely red object detected at 1.25 mm with a redshift estimated to be between 0.9 and 3.5 [arXiv:astro-ph/0112342]. Its physical nature remains ambiguous, as it may be either a pure starburst galaxy or a host to an active galactic nucleus [arXiv:astro-ph/0112342].

sources.summary: SA57-1 is a tentative 1.25 mm ERO detection whose nature is ambiguous between starburst and AGN; the paper derives 0.9<z<3.5 plus SFR and FIR limits.

quotes.quote: SA57-1 is detected at 1.25 mm with the IRAM 30m telescope at the 3 level: S _ 1.25 mm =1.45 0.45 mJy. Assuming that this detection is real, it is not obvious whether this source is a pure starburst or whether it also harbours an AGN. From the 1.25 mm measurement and the upper limit to the radio emission, a lower limit of 0.9 can be derived for the redshift of the object (using the z^+ curve in Carilli Yun 2000) cy00 , as described in section 5.1. This is also the lower limit to z if SA57-1 is an elliptical galaxy.

Using the K-z relation for radio-loud galaxies, the derived upper limit to the redshift is 3.5. If this object were a pure starburst, the implied SFR is between 45--800 for _ =0 (and 70--1300 for _ =0.7). The corresponding L _ FIR is greater than 4.5 10 ^ 10 .

gmw_00012333.name: S82-DG-1
gmw_00012333.overview: S82-DG-1 is a red ultra-diffuse galaxy located in a nearby cosmic void [arXiv:1903.08168]. Situated at a distance of approximately 28.7 Mpc, it is highly likely associated with the isolated galaxy NGC 1211 [arXiv:1903.08168].

sources.summary: The paper reports S82-DG-1 as a newly named red ultra-diffuse galaxy in a cosmic void, derives a 28.7 Mpc globular-cluster distance, and links it to NGC 1211.

quotes.quote: Here we report the discovery of a red ultra-diffuse galaxy (S82-DG-1: r_ eff = 1.6 kpc; < _ g > = 25.7 mag arcsec ^ 2 ; g-i = 0.78 mag) located in a nearby cosmic void. We used multi-band luminosity functions of its globular clusters to obtain the distance to S82-DG-1, at 28.7 _ 3.6 ^ +4.2 Mpc. Follow-up deep spectroscopy with the GTC telescope yields a redshift of 3353 29 km s ^ 1 , making its association with the NGC 1211 galaxy (one of the most isolated galaxies known) highly likely. Both galaxies have compatible distances and redshifts, share a high peculiar velocity ( 1000 km s ^ 1 ) and lie within a void of radius 7 Mpc .

Using data from the IAC Stripe82 Legacy Survey 2016MNRAS.456.1359F,2018RNAAS...2c.144R we serendipitously discovered a galaxy with very low surface brightness and quite extended size at coordinates R.A. = +03 ^ h 07 ^ m 18.0 ^ s , Dec. = 00 47' 34.7''. There is no previous detection of this galaxy according to NASA/IPAC Extragalactic Database (NED). We name the object as S82-DG-1.

gmw_00012334.overview: The high-redshift galaxy candidate 4162 has divergent spectral energy distribution interpretations [arXiv:2210.01777]. One model fits the galaxy as an older population with a Balmer break, while another model interprets it as an extremely young galaxy with strong emission lines [arXiv:2210.01777].

sources.summary: The paper highlights candidate 4162 as having divergent SED interpretations: one fit gives the sample's largest stellar mass and a Balmer break, while another gives a very young, high-sSFR galaxy.

quotes.quote: The \ SED fits of 4162 have the largest stellar mass with = 10.19^ +0.04 _ 0.04 coupled with the lowest star formation rate of 0.08^ +0.32 _ 0.07 . This is a result of \ fitting the red F410M -- F444W = 0.6 color as a Balmer break (see Figure fig:candidates_b ; top), with a relatively old mass-weighted age of 227^ +41 _ 50 Myr. On the other hand, \ fits this galaxy as an extremely young ( 3^ +1 _ 1 Myr) galaxy with a high sSFR rate of 250 Gyr ^ 1 and strong inferred + \ emission (rest-frame equivalent width of 2000 ). The \ fit yields a more typical mass of = 8.42^ +0.05 _ 0.03 .

gmw_00012335.overview: The galaxy 2796 is a high-redshift candidate that is strongly gravitationally lensed [arXiv:2210.01777]. It has a mean magnification of 6.1 and spectral energy distribution fits indicating strong optical emission lines and a very young stellar age [arXiv:2210.01777].

sources.summary: The paper identifies 2796 as one of three strongly lensed WHL0137 high-redshift candidates, estimating mean magnification 6.1 and SED fits with strong optical emission lines and very young ages.

quotes.quote: The three lensed candidates are 2796 , 7009 , and 9356 . Using the four lens models described above, 2796 has a magnification in the range from = 1.3 - 9.6 , with a mean value of = 6.1 .

2796 and 9356 also have relatively red F410M -- F444W colors of 0.6 . For these two candidates, both \ and \ fit these galaxies with a SED template containing strong + \ optical emission lines. \ gives very young ages for both of these galaxies of <5 Myr, while \ yields ages of 32 and 14 Myr for 2796 and 9356 , respectively.

gmw_00012336.overview: 9356 is a strongly lensed galaxy at a photometric redshift of approximately 8.9 [arXiv:2210.01777]. Stretched into an arc by gravitational lensing, the galaxy contains at least two bright knots of unevenly distributed star formation [arXiv:2210.01777].

sources.summary: The paper describes 9356 as a strongly lensed z~9 arc with two star-forming knots, mean magnification near 8, young stellar properties, and the best spatial resolution yet for a z~9 galaxy.

quotes.quote: One of our lensed high-redshift candidates, 9356 , is stretched into an arc 2 6 long by the effects of strong gravitational lensing. The arc has at least two bright knots of unevenly distributed star formation (see Figure fig:z9arc ). This candidate has a lensed F200W AB magnitude of 26 0.1 . Assuming a magnification of = 7.9 ( = 2.4 - 20.2 ; see Section sec:magnifications ), its intrinsic AB magnitude is 28.2 0.1 . After correcting for magnification, the \ results yield a stellar mass of = 7.71^ +0.49 _ 0.34 and a star formation rate of 2.6^ +3.3 _ 0.9 .

With a photometric redshift of = 8.9^ +0.5 _ 0.4 , 9356 is the most spatially-resolved galaxy at z 9 known to date. Assuming a primarily linear and tangential magnification of 8 , the intrinsic size of the arc is 1.5 kpc at z = 9 . Given the F150W point-spread function full width at half maximum of 0.05 \ ( 230 pc at z = 9 ), we can resolve 30 pc scales in this z 9 galaxy.

gmw_00012337.name: ESO443-09
gmw_00012337.overview: ESO443-09 is an isolated transition-type dwarf galaxy located approximately 5.97 Mpc away [arXiv:1103.3707]. It is characterized by a low density, a small amount of HI gas, and no evidence of ongoing star formation [arXiv:1103.3707].

sources.summary: The paper describes ESO443-09 as the faintest, least dense, most isolated target, with little HI, only 1900 CMD stars, no current star formation, and transition-type dwarf properties.

quotes.quote: The faintest and least dense galaxy in our sample is ESO443-09. This object also contains the smallest amount of HI gas ( M_ HI 10^ 7 M _ ), and it is the most isolated one, having a tidal index of 0.9 . Its distance from us is 5.97 0.46 Mpc, and its deprojected distance from M83 is 1.2 0.4 Mpc (computed using the radial distance and the coordinates from kara07 ). ESO443-09 has not yet been studied in H . We detect only 1900 stars in its CMD (Fig. cmds ).

ESO443-09 indeed contains very few massive MS stars and no evidence of currently ongoing star formation as judged from its CMD. There is, however, a considerable presence of BL stars, on top of the old component seen in the RGB; the RSG and an intermediate-age population (luminous AGB) are almost absent. Hence, overall this galaxy exhibits the properties of a typical transition-type dwarf e.g. grebel03 .

gmw_00012338.name: ESO381-18
gmw_00012338.overview: ESO381-18 is an isolated dwarf galaxy with a high central stellar density [arXiv:1103.3707]. Although it contains neutral gas, the galaxy currently shows no ongoing star formation, despite experiencing two recent starbursts [arXiv:1103.3707].

sources.summary: The paper describes ESO381-18 as an isolated, gas-bearing dwarf with no ongoing star formation, high central stellar density, mostly old stars, and two recent star-formation bursts.

quotes.quote: ESO381-18 is another rather isolated galaxy ( =-0.6 ), found at a deprojected distance of 1.2 0.1 Mpc from M83 (computed using the radial distance from kara07 ). As for ESO443-09, neutral gas has been detected ( M_ HI 3 10^ 7 M _ ), but there is no evidence of ongoing star formation (poorly populated upper MS, not observed in H ). Also the BL and RSG phases are very sparsely populated, but a clump of AGB stars is visible in the CMD in Fig. cmds , the latter containing a total of 7300 stars. ESO381-18 has a particularly high stellar density, with a peak stellar density of 909 stars per 0.1 kpc ^ 2 in its central regions. This galaxy formed 55 3 of its stars prior to 8 Gyr ago, while 72 3 were at place by 5 Gyr ago.

The SFR during the past 100 Myr and the past 1 Gyr (see b_ 100 and b_ 1G in Tab. ressfh ) was higher than the average SFR value, which is 0.0008 0.0007 M _ yr ^ 1 . The derived SFH in Fig. sfhsnew suggests the occurence of two strong recent bursts, the first from 600 Myr to 1 Gyr ago and the second one, shorter, from 40 to 60 Myr ago.

gmw_00012339.name: ESO444-78
gmw_00012339.overview: ESO444-78 is a dwarf irregular galaxy located in a dense environment close to M83 [arXiv:1103.3707]. The galaxy contains little neutral gas and is dominated by an old stellar population, with 82 percent of its stars having formed more than 5 Gyr ago [arXiv:1103.3707].

sources.summary: The paper presents ESO444-78 as the sample dwarf closest to M83, in the densest environment, with little neutral gas, a predominantly old CMD, and low recent activity.

quotes.quote: The dwarf irregular ESO444-78 is the closest one to M83 within our sample (only 110 500 kpc deprojected distance, again considering the values reported kara07 ), and is also currently located in the densest environment, with respect to the other targets of our study ( =2.1 ). According to LEDA, also in this case there is a high internal extinction in the B band due to the inclination (0.88 mag). As the previous two galaxies, ESO444-78 contains little neutral gas (see Tab. infogen ), but it was detected in H cote09 . From the CMD alone ( 11400 stars), the old stellar component seems predominant, while there are few, smoothly distributed stars in the young and intermediate-age phases, with a smaller concentration in the luminous AGB. Indeed, ESO444-78 formed already 65 5 of its stars more than 8 Gyr ago, and 82 5 more than 5 Gyr ago.

The fraction of the galaxy's total stellar content formed prior to 8 Gyr is the largest for this galaxy, compared to the others in the sample. The average SFR for ESO444-78 is 0.0063 0.0036 M _ yr ^ 1 ). For ages younger than 8 Gyr, ESO444-78 seems to have experienced an almost constant and low level of activity (see Tab. ressfh ), with only 3 of its stars born in the last Gyr.

gmw_00012340.name: (RA, Dec) = (11:00:06.30, +44:21:43.4)
gmw_00012340.overview: The galaxy (RA, Dec) = (11:00:06.30, +44:21:43.4) is a faint companion candidate located 2.7 arcseconds east of the active galaxy J1100+4421 [arXiv:1707.05416]. Optical spectroscopy of this companion candidate shows no continuum or emission lines at the redshift of J1100+4421, leaving it unclear if the two systems are physically interacting [arXiv:1707.05416].

sources.summary: The paper identifies a faint galaxy 2.7 arcsec east of J1100+4421, gives its g and z magnitudes, and finds no spectral evidence that it shares J1100+4421's redshift.

quotes.quote: We identified a galaxy at (RA, Dec) = (11:00:06.30, +44:21:43.4), to the east of J1100+4421 by 2.7 arcsec (21 kpc at this redshift, z=0.840 ) in our deep HSC imaging data. The magnitudes of this galaxy are g=25.32 0.36  mag and z=22.15 0.09  mag in Novermber 2014, a factor of 18.4 and 3.0 fainter than the faintest phases of J1100+4421, respectively. We took a GMOS optical spectrum of this galaxy with a 1.0 arcsec-width slit along J1100+4421 as described in Section  sec:sec_followup_optspec . The spectrum does not show any significant continuum because of the faintness of the galaxy and the short exposure, and does not show any emission lines at the same redshift as that of J1100+4421. Hence it is unclear whether or not this galaxy interacts with J1100+4421.

gmw_00012341.name: HDFS-1269
gmw_00012341.overview: HDFS-1269 is a compact red galaxy at a photometric redshift of approximately 2.4 [arXiv:astro-ph/0310131]. It has a high K-band luminosity and an estimated stellar mass of around 10^11 solar masses, with models indicating an old stellar population [arXiv:astro-ph/0310131].

sources.summary: The paper fits HDFS-1269 as a compact red high-redshift galaxy at zphot about 2.4, with B-band flux, high K-band luminosity, old stellar-population models, and stellar mass around 10^11 solar masses.

quotes.quote: HDFS 1269 This is the sole object for which Sextractor has reliably detected flux in the B _ 450 band. The best-fitting value to the redshift of HDFS-1269 is z_ phot =2.4^ +0.3 _ 0.2 . The quoted errors are the formal uncertainties of the fitting procedure (68 confidence level). The rest-frame (k-corrected) absolute magnitude of this galaxy, as resulting from its distant modulus (46.44) is M _K 25.1 , i.e. L > 3L ^* , where we considered M ^*_K 24.2 for local galaxies (Cole et al. 2001; Huang et al. 2002).

For this galaxy 61 exponentially 0.3 Gyr decaying models fit the data ( 0 A_V 2 ), providing Age in the range 1-4 Gyr and redshifts 1.9<z<2.7 . The fitting models provide stellar masses of the order of 10^ 11 M _ independently on the IMF assumed, as shown in Tab. 4.

gmw_00012342.name: HDFS-822
gmw_00012342.overview: HDFS-822 is a high-redshift galaxy with an estimated photometric redshift of approximately 3.0 [arXiv:astro-ph/0310131]. It is characterized by a very high K-band luminosity and a stellar mass exceeding 10^11 solar masses, regardless of the assumed initial mass function [arXiv:astro-ph/0310131].

sources.summary: The paper estimates HDFS-822 at zphot about 3.0, with very high K-band luminosity, a best-fit old reddened stellar population, and stellar masses above 10^11 solar masses across IMF choices.

quotes.quote: HDFS 822 The best-fitting value to the redshift is z_ phot =3.0^ +0.3 _ 0.2 and the resulting absolute magnitude is M _K 26.0 (L > 5L ^* ). The best fit is formally given by a 1.7 Gyr old, =0.3 Gyr model with an extinction A _V= 0.9 and Z=0.2Z _ obtained with a MS IMF and it is shown in Fig. 3 (middle panel). For this galaxy, 93 templates fit the data (0.4 A _V 2) providing Age in the range 0.2-2.5 Gyr and redshifts 2.4 <z<3.2 The stellar mass derived in the case of Sal IMF is M _ stars =3.3 10^ 11 M _ . For this galaxy the models provide a stellar mass always larger than 10^ 11 M _ independently on the IMF assumed.

gmw_00012343.name: HDFS-850
gmw_00012343.overview: HDFS-850 is a high-redshift galaxy with a photometric redshift estimated at approximately 2.9 [arXiv:astro-ph/0310131]. It is characterized as a dusty, red galaxy with an estimated stellar mass of 1.4 x 10^11 solar masses [arXiv:astro-ph/0310131].

sources.summary: The paper treats HDFS-850 as the faintest and reddest selected galaxy, fitting it at zphot about 2.9 with dusty young models and a stellar mass of 1.4 x 10^11 solar masses.

quotes.quote: HDFS 850 This is the faintest and the reddest of the three galaxies selected. In this case the best-fitting template is given by a 0.5 Gyr old, =0.1 Gyr model with an extinction A _V= 1.9 and Z=Z _ obtained with a Kro IMF. This model provides a redshift z_ phot =2.9^ +0.2 _ 0.3 . For this ERO, 58 templates (all the 2 Gyr models and SSP model) fit well the data with extinction 1 A _V 2, Age in the range 0.25-1.7 Gyr and redshifts 2.6 <z<3.1 . The stellar mass derived is M _ stars =1.4 10^ 11 M _ (Sal IMF).

gmw_00012344.name: source number 20
gmw_00012344.overview: Source number 20 is a nearby dwarf galaxy located in the Sculptor filament [arXiv:1709.00780]. Observations confirm that its neutral hydrogen emission is spatially coincident with its clumpy optical emission, which shares a similar major axis orientation [arXiv:1709.00780].

sources.summary: The paper identifies source number 20 as one of five H I detections likely to be nearby Sculptor dwarf galaxies, with ATCA data confirming coincident H I and optical emission.

quotes.quote: Assuming that the detected H I emission is indeed associated with those optical counterparts, this would confirm for the first time that they are nearby dwarf galaxies in the Sculptor filament rather than distant background galaxies at higher redshift.

Due to a fortunate coincidence, one of the five objects without optical redshift information, source number 20 in our catalogue, happened to be covered by the ATCA observations of NGC 55 presented by Westmeier2013 . ATCA H I column density contours of this source on top of an optical image from the Digitized Sky Survey are presented in Fig. fig_20_atca . At a much higher angular resolution of 154 93 arcsec , the ATCA data confirm that the H I emission indeed is spatially coincident with the optical source , and a physical association appears highly likely even without optical redshift confirmation. Additional evidence stems from the fact that the H I emission appears marginally extended in the same direction as the major axis of the clumpy optical emission.

gmw_00012345.name: 2dFGRS S491Z111
gmw_00012345.overview: The galaxy 2dFGRS S491Z111 exhibits a discrepancy of approximately 50 km/s between its optical and H I redshift measurements [arXiv:1709.00780]. This difference is attributed to large uncertainties in the optical data, making the H I redshift measurement far more precise [arXiv:1709.00780].

sources.summary: The paper reports that 2dFGRS S491Z111 has an optical-H I redshift discrepancy of about 50 km/s, attributes it to optical-redshift uncertainty, and presents the H I redshift as more precise.

quotes.quote: While optical and H I redshifts agree within a few km s ^ 1 for most of our detections, there are two cases, (14) 2dFGRS S491Z111 and (29) LEDA 198121, where we find a discrepancy between the optical and H I redshift measurements of about 50 km s ^ 1 . In both cases, the discrepancy is caused by large uncertainties in the optical redshift, and both values are in excellent agreement within those uncertainties. Consequently, our new H I redshifts for those two sources are far more accurate and precise than the existing optical redshifts.

gmw_00012346.name: LEDA 198121
gmw_00012346.overview: LEDA 198121 is a galaxy that exhibits a discrepancy of approximately 50 km/s between its optical and neutral hydrogen redshift measurements [arXiv:1709.00780]. This difference is attributed to large uncertainties in the optical data, making the neutral hydrogen redshift measurement far more precise [arXiv:1709.00780].

sources.summary: The paper reports that LEDA 198121 has an optical-H I redshift discrepancy of about 50 km/s, attributes it to optical-redshift uncertainty, and presents the H I redshift as more precise.

quotes.quote: While optical and H I redshifts agree within a few km s ^ 1 for most of our detections, there are two cases, (14) 2dFGRS S491Z111 and (29) LEDA 198121, where we find a discrepancy between the optical and H I redshift measurements of about 50 km s ^ 1 . In both cases, the discrepancy is caused by large uncertainties in the optical redshift, and both values are in excellent agreement within those uncertainties. Consequently, our new H I redshifts for those two sources are far more accurate and precise than the existing optical redshifts.

gmw_00012347.name: J1009 + 26
gmw_00012347.overview: J1009 + 26 is classified as an early-type galaxy that shows no signs of spiral arms [arXiv:2402.11665]. Its spectrum features a largely featureless continuum with relatively weak, mildly blueshifted absorption lines [arXiv:2402.11665].

sources.summary: The paper describes J1009 + 26 as early-type, with modest blueshifted absorption, weak constrained features, and a continuum/emission-line fit adapted for its spectrum.

quotes.quote: While J0906 + 56 is likely a late-type galaxy with possible spiral arms, J0954 + 47 and J1009 + 26 show no sign of spiral arms and are more consistent with early-type galaxies.

In J1009 + 26, the overall featureless continuum is fit with a linear function. The unabsorbed \ emission line profile is modeled in the same way as that for J0954 + 47, except that the line width of \ is not fixed to that of \ but set as a free parameter instead. The recovered \ absorption features are relatively weak and poorly constrained due to the large uncertainties of the unabsorbed \ emission line profile. We then examine the normalized absorption features closely and find that the blueshift of absorption features is in general modest (Fig. fig:J1009fit4line and Appendix A2 ). \ and \ absorption features are mildly blueshifted with \ 20 \ when each feature is fit individually with a single Voigt profile (Here \ is the velocity at the location that accumulates 50 of the total equivalent width (EW) of the entire absorption feature).

gmw_00012348.name: NGC 6028
gmw_00012348.overview: NGC 6028 is a Hoag-type comparison galaxy [arXiv:1102.3184]. Its core structure likely consists of a weak bar embedded within a lens system [arXiv:1102.3184].

sources.summary: The paper discusses NGC 6028 as a Hoag-type comparison object, citing a weak-bar interpretation, producing an SDSS residual image, and finding an inner structure unlike UGC 4599.

quotes.quote: In particular, Wakamatsu focused on NGC 6028 ( cz=4475 km s ^ 1 ) as ``the best object for a detailed study of its structure'' and concluded that the core probably consists of a weak bar embedded in the lens system. Unfortunately, the figures presented by Wakamatsu do not allow a detailed comparison of the cores of UGC 4599 and NGC 6028. Based on archival SDSS images and following the technique described in Section S:photometry we produce a residual image of NGC 6028 and present it in Fig. \ fig:NGC6028 along with its derived geometrical properties. Unlike UGC 4599, the residual image of NGC 6028 clearly illustrates the presence of an inner structure.

gmw_00012349.name: NGC 0812
gmw_00012349.overview: NGC 0812 is a spiral galaxy located at a distance of approximately 58.3 Mpc [arXiv:2304.12361]. It hosted the supernova SN 2020udy, which occurred in a young, low-metallicity stellar environment offset by 15 kpc from the galactic center [arXiv:2304.12361].

sources.summary: The paper identifies NGC 0812 as the spiral host of SN 2020udy at z = 0.01722, places the supernova 15 kpc from its center, and describes a low-metallicity young environment.

quotes.quote: As part of the ZTF public survey Bellm2019, fremling_bts , a new transient ZTF20acdqjeq (hereafter SN 2020udy) was identified by an automated detection algorithm implemented in AMPEL NordinAMPEL on 2020-09-24.35 UT (MJD 59116.35) at 19.63 mag in the band, coincident with the galaxy NGC 0812 at a spectroscopic redshift of z = 0.01722 0.00003 1999PASP..111..438F .

The host of SN 2020udy is a spiral galaxy, NGC 0812, and SN 2020udy is offset from the centre of the galaxy by 15 kpc at the measured redshift. The distance modulus of the galaxy has been estimated using the Tully-Fisher method to be 33.83 0.43 mag 2014MNRAS.444..527S , corresponding to a distance of 58.3 Mpc.
The host galaxy environment is low metallicity and is consistent with a young stellar population.

gmw_00012350.name: J082930.60+272822.7
gmw_00012350.overview: J082930.60+272822.7 is a quasar and active galactic nucleus [arXiv:1312.6694]. It is classified as a binary black hole candidate, exhibiting consistent broad-line velocity shifts and a measured radial acceleration over a 6.2-year period [arXiv:1312.6694].

sources.summary: The paper classifies this object as a BBH candidate, reporting consistent broad-line velocity shifts over 6.2 yr, a measured radial acceleration, and prior identification without second-epoch spectra.

quotes.quote: J082930.60+272822.7 . We detect consistent velocity shifts over 6.2 yr in broad \ and broad \ with no significant line profile changes. The radial acceleration measured from broad \ is [ 54 , 13 ] km s ^ 1 yr ^ 1 (2.5 ). This object was also noted by eracleous11 and by tsalmantza11 , but no second-epoch spectrum was available.

gmw_00012351.name: J110051.02+170934.3
gmw_00012351.overview: J110051.02+170934.3 is a quasar and active galactic nucleus that is considered a candidate binary supermassive black hole [arXiv:1312.6694]. Its broad emission lines exhibit consistent velocity shifts over a 3.2-year period, indicating a measurable radial acceleration [arXiv:1312.6694].

sources.summary: The paper treats this object as a BBH candidate, finding consistent broad-line velocity shifts over 3.2 yr, a broad-line radial acceleration, and previous noting without follow-up spectra.

quotes.quote: J110051.02+170934.3 . Broad \ and \ show consistent velocity shifts over 3.2 yr with no significant line profile change. The radial acceleration measured from broad \ is [ 113 , 55 ] km s ^ 1 yr ^ 1 (2.5 ). This object was also noted by eracleous11 , but no second-epoch spectrum was available.

gmw_00012352.name: J130534.49+181932.9
gmw_00012352.overview: J130534.49+181932.9 is a quasar and active galactic nucleus [arXiv:1312.6694]. It is classified as a binary black hole candidate, exhibiting consistent broad-line velocity shifts over a two-year period and a measurable radial acceleration [arXiv:1312.6694].

sources.summary: The paper classifies this object as a BBH candidate with consistent two-year broad-line shifts and a radial acceleration; earlier work found no significant shift in a shorter-baseline spectrum.

quotes.quote: J130534.49+181932.9 . Consistent velocity shifts over 2.0 yr are detected in broad \ and \ with no significant line profile change. The radial acceleration measured from broad \ is [ 80, 158 ] km s ^ 1 yr ^ 1 (2.5 ). This object was also noted by eracleous11 . The authors found no significant shift in the \ region based on a spectrum taken 1.9 yr after the original SDSS observation; no second-epoch spectrum was available for the \ region.

gmw_00012353.name: J134548.50+114443.5
gmw_00012353.overview: J134548.50+114443.5 is a quasar and active galactic nucleus [arXiv:1312.6694]. It is a candidate binary black hole system that exhibits consistent broad-line velocity shifts over a 7.2-year period [arXiv:1312.6694].

sources.summary: The paper identifies this as the strongest BBH-candidate velocity-shift case, with the largest detected shift, consistent broad-line changes over 7.2 yr, and measured acceleration.

quotes.quote: J134548.50+114443.5 . This object has the largest velocity shift detected among the BBH candidates in our sample. Consistent velocity shifts over 7.2 yr are detected in broad \ and \ with no significant line profile change. The radial acceleration measured from broad \ is [ 44, 73 ] km s ^ 1 yr ^ 1 (2.5 ).

gmw_00012354.name: NGC 2577
gmw_00012354.overview: NGC 2577 is an early-type galaxy [arXiv:2001.08087]. Although it was previously associated with a high dust mass, this measurement is likely contaminated by a nearby spiral galaxy located six arcminutes to the northwest [arXiv:2001.08087].

sources.summary: The paper flags NGC 2577 as a likely contaminated comparison object: its high IRAS dust mass may come from a nearby spiral aligned with the IRAS scan track.

quotes.quote: In Table table:aver_c , the reason for the small difference between gas-rich and gas-poor galaxies in 2019A&A...622A..87K is that two galaxies are contaminated or miss-classified: NGC 2577 and NGC 3301. As can be seen in Fig. fig:MdvsMd , these galaxies have the highest estimated dust mass.

However, the data of NGC 2577 comes from IRAS observations, and there is a nearby large spiral galaxy 6 arcmin north-west. Moreover, these two galaxies are located in a similar direction with the IRAS scanning track. Therefore, the high dust mass of this galaxy is likely due to the contamination from the nearby source.

gmw_00012355.name: NGC 3301
gmw_00012355.overview: NGC 3301 is an early-type galaxy that has been treated as a gas-poor system [arXiv:2001.08087]. Although single-dish observations previously detected neutral hydrogen, the galaxy was not detected in later observations and is noted for its high estimated dust mass [arXiv:2001.08087].

sources.summary: The paper treats NGC 3301 as a possibly misclassified comparison object, noting its high estimated dust mass, single-dish H I detection, and adopted gas-poor status after non-detection elsewhere.

quotes.quote: In Table table:aver_c , the reason for the small difference between gas-rich and gas-poor galaxies in 2019A&A...622A..87K is that two galaxies are contaminated or miss-classified: NGC 2577 and NGC 3301. As can be seen in Fig. fig:MdvsMd , these galaxies have the highest estimated dust mass.
For NGC 3301, single-dish observations detected \ in this galaxy 2005ApJS..160..149S . However, we consider it as gas-poor since it was not detected in 2012MNRAS.422.1835S .

gmw_00012356.name: NGC 4036
gmw_00012356.overview: NGC 4036 is a gas-rich early-type galaxy characterized by a large outer H I ring or disc structure [arXiv:2001.08087]. This CO-poor galaxy features a red, dusty inner region and displays blue colors in its outer regions where the highest H I column density is located [arXiv:2001.08087].

sources.summary: The paper identifies NGC 4036 among six gas-rich ETGs with enormous H I ring or disc structures, red dusty inner regions, blue outer H I rings, and is described with the group as CO-poor.

quotes.quote: We note that six rich galaxies (NGC 2859, NGC 3941, NGC 3945, NGC 4036, NGC 4278, and NGC 5582) in our sample have enormous \ ring or disc structures, while they have no \ in their very central regions (red circles in Fig. fig:derivedH ). While these galaxies show very red colours in the inner regions due to dust, they show blue colours in the outer regions where the highest \ column density rings are located 2017MNRAS.464..329Y . We would like to point out that these galaxies are also CO-poor with the exception of NGC 4278. Therefore, we conclude that the presence of a large \ disc or ring in an ETG indicates a dust structure in the centre, even if there is no \ in central regions. It would be interesting to continue to study these objects in future studies.

gmw_00012357.name: SDSS J113108.31+202147.3
gmw_00012357.overview: SDSS J113108.31+202147.3 is a bright, extended galaxy located close to a quasar line of sight [arXiv:0912.0736]. Although it has a photometric redshift of approximately 0.65, observations show it is not the source of the emission lines detected in the quasar spectrum [arXiv:0912.0736].

sources.summary: The paper identifies SDSS J113108.31+202147.3 as a bright extended galaxy near the quasar sightline, then shows it is not the source of the detected emission lines.

quotes.quote: An extended galaxy (SDSS J113108.31+202147.3) is clearly visible close to the quasar line of sight in the SDSS image, with its centroid located at about 3.5 from the quasar image and extended towards the quasar image (see Fig. im1131 ). Moreover, the photometric redshift provided by SDSS, z=0.65 0.07 , is close to that of the detected emission and absorption lines. This is therefore a very good case to study the connection between the galaxy and the absorber.

Interestingly, despite of aligning the slit with the galaxy seen at 3.5 from the quasar (see Fig. im1131 ), there is no emission line at the corresponding position (marked by a 'X') in the 2D spectrum. On the contrary, while the second slit angle was chosen to avoid including the galaxy, the [ ] emission is still detected. This demonstrates the galaxy seen on the SDSS image is not responsible for the detected emission lines. Interestingly, this galaxy does not produce any other detectable absorption line system in the QSO spectra over the wavelength range covered by the SDSS and IGO spectra.

gmw_00012358.name: UDF12
gmw_00012358.overview: UDF12 is a high-redshift galaxy located at a redshift of z = 5.0 [arXiv:1606.00227]. Although it has the lowest estimated stellar mass among its local ALMA detections, it is one of the most massive galaxies in the Hubble Ultra Deep Field for its redshift [arXiv:1606.00227].

sources.summary: The paper reports UDF12 as the sole z>3.5 ALMA detection, a z=5 galaxy low in the ALMA stellar-mass ranking yet unusually massive for its HUDF redshift.

quotes.quote: At redshifts z > 3.5 we have detected only one object, UDF12, which lies at z = 5.0 . Among our ALMA detections, this is in fact the galaxy with the lowest estimated stellar mass, but from Fig. 7 it can be seen that it is one of the most massive galaxies in the HUDF for its redshift (i.e. it is one of the very few galaxies in this field at z > 4 which has M_* > 10^ 9.5 M_ ). We can therefore speculate that this detection may reflect a modest increase in typical specific star-formation rate between z 2 and z 5 (e.g. Steinhardt et al. 2014; Marmol-Queralto et al. 2016), combined with the sensitivity of 1.3-mm observations to extreme redshift dusty star-forming galaxies, and that moderately deeper sub-mm/mm observations of the field may yield significantly more detections at z > 3 .

gmw_00012359.name: ESO 209-009
gmw_00012359.overview: ESO 209-009 is a galaxy featuring a disk-side tail and clumpy extraplanar radio emission that extends out of the galactic plane [arXiv:2403.05709]. It hosts a newly detected radio halo on its left side [arXiv:2403.05709] and requires a thick vertical dust component to describe its profile [arXiv:2207.11589].

sources.summary: The paper analyzes ESO 209-G009 despite high rms, finding clumpy extraplanar radio emission, a disk-side tail, and a first-time radio-halo detection only on the left side.

quotes.quote: ESO 209-G009: Despite it having a significantly higher rms value than the other galaxy images, we could still thoroughly analyze this galaxy. To construct the z profile, we had to select a smaller rectangular region than the entire galaxy, excluding the disk's outer regions that appeared as a tail on one side. The radio emission extends through the optical disk and is extended out of the galactic plane in z direction, showing a clumpy structure, more prominent near the tail. The z profile revealed that the galaxy exhibited emission beyond its disk, and a two-component model with a Gaussian disk was required to describe the profile accurately.

However, as the halo scale height on the right side of the profile results in the same order as the disk scale height, we conclude that the radio halo is only detected on the left side. In the past, detected extraplanar ionized gas in this galaxy, while recently, found extraplanar dust. However, the radio halo was studied and detected for the first time in the present work.

sources.summary: Using Herschel profiles, the paper finds that ESO 209-009 requires an additional thicker dust component: the vertical emission dominates above the model at 1-2 kpc, making it an outlier from single-component fits.

quotes.quote: The vertical profiles for 6 galaxies (ESO 209-009, NGC 0891, NGC 3628, NGC 4013, NGC 4437, and NGC 4631) show the presence of another (thicker) dust emission component in all or almost all wavebands. This component clearly dominates over the model at larger vertical distances above the galaxy mid-plane, usually starting at z=1-2 kpc. For these galaxies, the average ^2=19.8 10.1 compared to ^2=1.04 0.45 for the whole sample without these outliers. This proves that another dust component is required to better describe the observed profiles of these six galaxies.

gmw_00012360.name: NGC 3592
gmw_00012360.overview: NGC 3592 is an edge-on spiral galaxy [arXiv:2207.11589]. It exhibits an excess of far-infrared emission at large vertical distances, which indicates the presence of real extraplanar dust starting at heights of 1 to 2 kiloparsecs above the mid-plane [arXiv:2207.11589].

sources.summary: The paper identifies NGC 3592 as an Additional-sample edge-on galaxy with excess FIR emission at large vertical distances, interpreting the extraplanar dust signal as real rather than PSF or inclination driven.

quotes.quote: Six galaxies from the Additional sample (IC 2233, NGC 3592, UGC 07321, UGC 07387, UGC 07522, and UGC 09242) also show an excess of FIR emission compared to our models at large vertical distances. We note that this extraplanar FIR emission, which typically starts to dominate at heights 1--2 kpc above the mid-plane, cannot be explained by the scattered light of the PSF since we accounted for this effect while modelling. Also, variations in the galaxy inclination angle as described in Sect. sec:method cannot explain the observed excess of dust emission (the red spread along the model profile in Fig. fig:profiles shows a one- spread of the model profiles for varied inclination angles, as described in Sect. sec:3Ddisc ). This indicates that the observed extraplanar emission is real and produced by the dust at high distances above the mid-plane.

gmw_00012361.name: UGC 07387
gmw_00012361.overview: UGC 07387 is an edge-on spiral galaxy [arXiv:2207.11589]. It exhibits an excess of far-infrared emission at large vertical distances, indicating the presence of extraplanar dust starting at heights of 1 to 2 kiloparsecs above the mid-plane [arXiv:2207.11589].

sources.summary: The paper identifies UGC 07387 as an Additional-sample edge-on galaxy with excess FIR emission at large vertical distances, interpreting the extraplanar dust signal as real rather than PSF or inclination driven.

quotes.quote: Six galaxies from the Additional sample (IC 2233, NGC 3592, UGC 07321, UGC 07387, UGC 07522, and UGC 09242) also show an excess of FIR emission compared to our models at large vertical distances. We note that this extraplanar FIR emission, which typically starts to dominate at heights 1--2 kpc above the mid-plane, cannot be explained by the scattered light of the PSF since we accounted for this effect while modelling. Also, variations in the galaxy inclination angle as described in Sect. sec:method cannot explain the observed excess of dust emission (the red spread along the model profile in Fig. fig:profiles shows a one- spread of the model profiles for varied inclination angles, as described in Sect. sec:3Ddisc ). This indicates that the observed extraplanar emission is real and produced by the dust at high distances above the mid-plane.

gmw_00012362.name: UGC 09242
gmw_00012362.overview: UGC 09242 is an edge-on spiral galaxy [arXiv:2207.11589]. It exhibits an excess of far-infrared emission at large vertical distances, indicating the presence of real extraplanar dust located one to two kiloparsecs above its mid-plane [arXiv:2207.11589].

sources.summary: The paper identifies UGC 09242 as an Additional-sample edge-on galaxy with excess FIR emission at large vertical distances, interpreting the extraplanar dust signal as real rather than PSF or inclination driven.

quotes.quote: Six galaxies from the Additional sample (IC 2233, NGC 3592, UGC 07321, UGC 07387, UGC 07522, and UGC 09242) also show an excess of FIR emission compared to our models at large vertical distances. We note that this extraplanar FIR emission, which typically starts to dominate at heights 1--2 kpc above the mid-plane, cannot be explained by the scattered light of the PSF since we accounted for this effect while modelling. Also, variations in the galaxy inclination angle as described in Sect. sec:method cannot explain the observed excess of dust emission (the red spread along the model profile in Fig. fig:profiles shows a one- spread of the model profiles for varied inclination angles, as described in Sect. sec:3Ddisc ). This indicates that the observed extraplanar emission is real and produced by the dust at high distances above the mid-plane.

gmw_00012363.name: NGC5011C
gmw_00012363.overview: NGC5011C is a companion satellite galaxy located in close proximity to Centaurus A [arXiv:2301.07113]. Although it visually appears to participate in the Centaurus A satellite plane, its orbit is fundamentally misaligned, making it inconsistent with plane membership [arXiv:2301.07113].

sources.summary: The paper classifies NGC5011C as inconsistent with CASP membership under default criteria, noting it appears close to the Centaurus A plane but has orbits fundamentally misaligned from the satellite plane.

quotes.quote: On the other hand, relaxing the required orbital pole alignment to 45^ results in NGC5011C, dw1341-43, and KK211's removal from the set of Unlikely satellites, instead being classified as Both . These three satellites demonstrate an initial proximity to Centaurus A and may visually appear to participate in the CASP, but have orbits fundamentally misaligned from the satellite plane.

We identified a set of 5 satellites -- NGC5011C, dw1341-43, KK211, ESO269-037, and KK221 -- as being inconsistent with CASP membership, the latter two of which remain robustly so even when relaxing our criteria.

gmw_00012364.name: dw1341-43
gmw_00012364.overview: The galaxy dw1341-43 is a companion satellite that is located in close proximity to Centaurus A [arXiv:2301.07113]. Although it visually appears to participate in the Centaurus A satellite plane, its orbit is fundamentally misaligned, making it inconsistent with plane membership [arXiv:2301.07113].

sources.summary: The paper classifies dw1341-43 as inconsistent with CASP membership under default criteria, noting it appears close to the Centaurus A plane but has orbits fundamentally misaligned from the satellite plane.

quotes.quote: On the other hand, relaxing the required orbital pole alignment to 45^ results in NGC5011C, dw1341-43, and KK211's removal from the set of Unlikely satellites, instead being classified as Both . These three satellites demonstrate an initial proximity to Centaurus A and may visually appear to participate in the CASP, but have orbits fundamentally misaligned from the satellite plane.

We identified a set of 5 satellites -- NGC5011C, dw1341-43, KK211, ESO269-037, and KK221 -- as being inconsistent with CASP membership, the latter two of which remain robustly so even when relaxing our criteria.

gmw_00012365.name: HX2
gmw_00012365.overview: HX2 is a merging galaxy that hosts a Seyfert active galactic nucleus at its center [arXiv:1902.03754]. It is connected to two outlying emitting regions by a bridge structure that displays a velocity gradient around the host [arXiv:1902.03754].

sources.summary: The paper treats HX2 as the Seyfert-AGN host of two connected outlying emitters in a merging two-galaxy system, with emission continuity, bridge structure, and velocity gradients around the host.

quotes.quote: Here we identify the host galaxy not with the galaxy on which the MaNGA IFU is centered (ObJID: 1237655374109802660), but rather with the galaxy adjacent to it (ObJID: 1237655374109802661), due to the continuity of the emission with it. X2.1 has a velocity very near the velocity of the host galaxy and could be a case of extraplanar gas. This merger is caught in the MaNGA IFU field of view. The IFU is centred around the galaxy 8601-12701. X2.1 is at the edge of composite/star forming region, and X2.2 has Seyfert emission line ratios. The host galaxy (HX2) also has Seyfert AGN in the centre.

It is intriguing to see that both X2.1 and X2.2 emitting regions which are on opposite sides of the host galaxy show a connected bridge. Moreover, the velocity map shows a gradient as we go along the connecting bridge around the host galaxy.

gmw_00012366.name: HX4
gmw_00012366.overview: HX4 is a spiral host galaxy that shows no nuclear activity [arXiv:1902.03754]. The galaxy has a close companion, is possibly interacting with an early-type galaxy, and exhibits a misalignment between its gas and stellar rotation [arXiv:1902.03754].

sources.summary: The paper describes HX4 as a spiral host with a nearby companion, possible interaction with an early-type galaxy, misaligned gas and stellar rotation, and no detected nuclear activity.

quotes.quote: X4 is classified as Seyfert and LINER in the [ NII ] / and the [ SII ] / BPT diagram respectively. However, peculiarly the host (HX4) does not show any nuclear activity.

The host galaxy is a spiral galaxy with a close companion nearby (G18) and has a LINER/composite emission line ratios. It is possibly interacting with another early type galaxy. X4 has Seyfert/LINER like emission line ratio. The gas and stellar rotation is in the same direction although there is an misalignment between the two. X4 is also an example of Hanny's Voorwerp like object.

gmw_00012367.name: HX5
gmw_00012367.overview: The host galaxy HX5 is a lenticular galaxy that contains a central Seyfert active galactic nucleus [arXiv:1902.03754]. It features outlying gas moving away from the host at a velocity of at least 300 km/s, which is likely an AGN-driven outflow undergoing in-situ star formation [arXiv:1902.03754].

sources.summary: The paper describes HX5 as an S0 host with a central Seyfert AGN; its connected outlying emission has a large velocity offset, likely AGN-driven outflowing gas with in-situ star formation.

quotes.quote: The host galaxy (HX5) also shows a Seyfert-like line ratios in the centre, and thus could be the source of ionisation.

X5, like X2.1 and X2.2, also shows a continuity with the host galaxy. The host of X5 is an S0 galaxy (G18) which has a Seyfert AGN in the centre. Interestingly, X5 is at the edge of the star forming/Seyfert region. The velocity map shows that the gas has very different velocity from the host galaxy and is moving away from the galaxy with a velocity of at least 300 km/s, which is fairly large. However, given the continuity in the emission with the host galaxy we believe that it is associated with it.
It is possible that this is a event of outflowing gas due to AGN feedback, and this gas is undergoing in-situ star formation.

gmw_00012368.name: SQ B
gmw_00012368.overview: SQ B is a tidal dwarf galaxy candidate located within Stephan's Quintet [arXiv:astro-ph/0208494]. It exhibits in-situ molecular gas formation and a star formation rate of approximately 0.2 solar masses per year, which is typical for dwarf galaxies [arXiv:astro-ph/0208494].

sources.summary: The paper describes SQ B as Stephan's Quintet's strongest tidal dwarf candidate, with CO aligned with star-formation tracers, in-situ molecular gas formation, normal mass-to-light ratio, and dwarf-like star formation rates.

quotes.quote: In SQ B the CO emission coincides with that of tracers of star formation ( , 15 m and radio continuum). The CO peak lies slightly offset from the HI peak towards a steep HI gradient. This is indicating that the molecular gas is forming in-situ, possibly in a region of compressed HI, with subsequent star formation. The star forming region at SQ B is the object in SQ that most resembles a TDG.

Its has a clear tidal origin and we have summarized evidence that the molecular gas is forming in situ. The ratio of molecular gas mass to the luminosity is normal for star forming galaxies. The star formation rates that can be derived from different tracers such as emission, 15 m emission following the estimate in Xu99 or radio continuum (applying eq. 21 in Condon92 , extrapolated with a Salpeter initial mass function down to stars with masses above 0.1 ) give similar values of about 0.2 yr ^ 1 . These values are in the same range as those found for TDGs ( 0.01 - 0.3 yr ^ 1 ) and several nearby dwarf galaxies ( 0.05 - 0.9 yr ^ 1 ) Braine01 showing that the observations of SQ B are consistent with the formation of a dwarf galaxy in the tidal tail.

gmw_00012369.name: NGC 705
gmw_00012369.overview: NGC 705 is an early-type galaxy in the Abell 0262 cluster, located at a projected distance of 1.1 arcmin from the central galaxy NGC 708 [arXiv:0804.4653]. Despite its early-type classification, the galaxy features a major-axis absorption lane and an S-shaped disk twist [arXiv:0804.4653].

sources.summary: NGC 705 is modeled as an early-type Abell 0262 galaxy with an absorption lane, S-shaped disk twist, contaminated eastern photometry, and uncontaminated profiles yielding a disk scalelength.

quotes.quote: NGC 705 NGC 705 is an early type galaxy in Abell 0262, situated just 1.1 arcmin (projected distance: 0.03 Mpc) away from the CD galaxy NGC 708. Its redshift is the same as was assigned to the cluster, z=0.016 . Despite being an early-type galaxy, it has a noticeable absorption lane on the major axis. Its disc also displays an S-shaped twist, which is visible in Figure soiimgs . NGC 705 is close enough to NGC 708 that its surface photometry is contaminated by the cD's light on the eastern side.

The remaining and uncontaminated R profiles are very well fit to a single projected exponential, with no clear truncation or downturn. The inner radii used are again large in order to avoid the bright central bulge. The average scalelength for NGC 705 is R_0 = 4.5 1.3 kpc when all 10 available profiles are used.

gmw_00012370.name: ESO187G19
gmw_00012370.overview: ESO187G19 is a late-type spiral galaxy located in the Abell 3716 cluster [arXiv:0804.4653]. It is viewed edge-on with a strong dust lane cutting through its weak central bulge, and it features a flared outer disk [arXiv:0804.4653].
