sources.summary: ESO187G19 is described as a later-type Abell 3716 spiral with a weak spheroid, strong dust lane, edge-on inclination, and flared outer disk affecting profile fits.

quotes.quote: ESO187G19 ESO187G19 is a member of Abell 3716 and is located some 2 arcmin ( 0.1 Mpc in projected separation) from the cluster centre, although its measured redshift, z=0.047 , is a bit higher than that of the cluster ( z=0.041 ). In many respects it is a very distinct galaxy when compared to ESO243G49. It is a latter type spiral, with a weak spheroid visible only in its very central regions. It displays a strong dust lane, which cuts the central bulge through its centre. Assuming that this dust absorption originates from the edge of an optically thick thin disc, the inclination of ESO187G19 should be i=90^o .

The galaxy disc is clearly flared in its outer areas ( R > 130 pixels = 20.8 arcsec 17 kpc), so our profiles are truncated at this radius, and the fits also avoid these flared regions. On the other hand, the much weaker bulge allows the inner fit region to be R > 40 pix = 6.4 arcsec. The resulting horizontal profiles and fits are shown in Figure eso187r .

gmw_00012371.name: LCSBS0496P
gmw_00012371.overview: LCSBS0496P is a late-type, nearly edge-on spiral galaxy with a very small bulge [arXiv:0804.4653]. It is located in relative isolation at a projected distance of approximately 0.6 Mpc from the center of the Abell 0419 cluster, with which it shares a matching redshift [arXiv:0804.4653].

sources.summary: LCSBS0496P is presented as a relatively isolated Abell 0419 late-type, nearly edge-on spiral whose redshift matches the cluster and whose profiles can be extracted close to center.

quotes.quote: LCSBS0496P In contrast to the other galaxies studied in this paper, LCSBS0496P is not close to the centre of its cluster. Its projected distance from the centre of Abell 0419 is about 13 arcmin ( 0.6 Mpc). It is therefore found in relative isolation and has perhaps not had time to fall into the more crowded regions of Abell 0419. On the other hand, we note that its measured redshift is exactly the same as that attributed to Abell 0419. This galaxy is at a comparable distance to ESO187G19.

It is also a late type, rather bulgeless, spiral at an inclination close to i=90^o . This conclusion again comes from the assumption that the aborption lane is generated at the edge of an optically thick thin disc. As a result of its very late type, profiles could be extracted at R > 20 pix from its centre.

gmw_00012372.name: LEDA 84274
gmw_00012372.overview: LEDA 84274 is a star-forming galaxy at a redshift of z=0.0377 [arXiv:1103.2453]. It contains a luminous, compact hot dust component that is likely heated by a buried active galactic nucleus [arXiv:1103.2453].

sources.summary: LEDA 84274 is identified with IRAS 14416+6618, has star-forming optical lines at z=0.0377, and shows luminous 500-600 K hot dust likely surrounding a buried AGN.

quotes.quote: LEDA 84274 is identified as the FIR source, IRAS 14416+6618. Previous optical spectroscopy of LEDA 84274 kim95,vielleux95 reported a low-ionization spectrum with strong Balmer lines, indicating an HII-region-like or star-forming galaxy at z=0.0377. Thus, the gas emitting these optical lines is photoionized by early-type stars.

As shown in Section sec:multi , both galaxies show near-infrared continuum from hot dust with T_ dust =500-600 K , although they differ in other ways. The origin of the energy source heating this dust, however, remains unclear. Fits indicate that the luminosity of this hot dust component is 2.7 10^ 10 \ L _ and 6.6 10^ 10 \ L _ for LEDA 84274 and IRAS 01250+2832, respectively.

As discussed in Section sec:hot , the compact hot dust component that we found in LEDA 84274 and IRAS 01250+2832 is likely to surround an AGN that is heating it, even though other possibilities cannot be completely ruled out.

gmw_00012373.name: IRAS 01250+2832
gmw_00012373.overview: IRAS 01250+2832 is a low-ionization nuclear emission-line region (LINER) galaxy located at a redshift of z=0.043 [arXiv:1103.2453]. Its spectral energy distribution indicates the presence of both hot and cool dust components [arXiv:1103.2453].

sources.summary: IRAS 01250+2832 is first identified as a z=0.043 galaxy; its SED requires hot and cool dust components, while its optical lines suggest a LINER galaxy.

quotes.quote: For IRAS 01250+2832, this is its first reported identification as a galaxy at z=0.043 .

When we also fit the AKARI NIR spectrum using Equation equ:1 and the elliptical galaxy template, we need to introduce a blackbody component of temperature of 510 20 K in optically thick conditions as shown in Figure fig:sed2 (b). Another blackbody component of 100 K is needed to explain the IRAS 60 flux. The higher fluxes of the model relative to those observed at 9 and 12 m can be attributed to 9.7 silicate absorption, which is not included in this model.

While there are no signs of H and [ OIII ] emission lines in Figure fig:opt_spec (b), H and [ NII ] lines are clearly detected. The [ NII ] 6584 emission line is stronger than H , suggesting that IRAS 01250+2832 is a low-ionization nuclear emission-line region (LINER) galaxy. However, the other indicators of LINERs, [ OI ] 6300 and [ SII ] 6716 +6731 emission lines, are not seen because of the weakness of the lines, relative to the stellar continuum.

gmw_00012374.name: BCG2
gmw_00012374.overview: BCG2 is one of the two brightest cluster galaxies located in the central clump A of the galaxy cluster A2163-A [arXiv:0712.2715]. This giant galaxy is positioned with an offset to the west of clump A2, and its axis is aligned in an east-west direction along the cluster's large-scale density distribution [arXiv:0712.2715].

sources.summary: The paper identifies BCG2 as one of two brightest cluster galaxies in A2163-A, significantly west of clump A2, with the BCG axes aligned east-west with the cluster large-scale structure.

quotes.quote: It is important to stress that the two Brightest Cluster Galaxies (BCG1 and BCG2 in Fig. fig:BCGs and Table Table_BCG ) are both located in the central clump A, but their positions do not coincide with the maxima of the density peaks. However, while BCG1 is near A1 (at bright and faint magnitudes), BCG2 has a significant offset to the west of A2. The axes of these two giant galaxies and the line joining them are aligned along the same direction of the large--scale cluster density distribution, i.e. the E--W direction (see Table Table_BCG ).

gmw_00012375.name: HE 1104 1805
gmw_00012375.overview: HE 1104 1805 is a gravitational lens system that has been studied to analyze the properties of lens galaxies [arXiv:1306.0008]. X-ray observations of the system detected no differential absorption between its lensed images [arXiv:1306.0008].

sources.summary: The paper treats HE 1104 1805 as a gravitational lens in the sample, cites prior observations, and reports no detected absorption in its lens analysis.

quotes.quote: In this paper, we analyze \ observations of 10 gravitational lenses, HE 0047 1756 (Wisotzki et al. \ 2004; Ofek et al. \ 2006), QJ 0158 4325 (Maza et al. \ 1995; Morgan et al. \ 1999; Faure et al. \ 2009), SDSS 0246 0805 (Inada et al. \ 2005; Eigenbrod et al. \ 2006), HE 0435 1223 (Wisotzki et al. \ 2002; Ofek et al. \ 2006), SDSS 0924+0219 (Inada et al. \ 2003a; Ofek et al. \ 2006), SDSS 1004+4112 (Inada et al. \ 2003b), PG 1115+080 (Weymann et al. \ 1980; Kundi et al. \ 1997), Q 1355 2257 (Morgan et al. \ 2003; Eigenbrod et al. \ 2006), HE 1104 1805 (Wisotzki 1993; Lidman et al. \ 2000), and Q 2237+0305 (Huchra et al. \ 1985), to measure the differential X-ray absorption between the lensed images, the metallicity, and the dust-to-gas ratio of lens galaxies.
Observations of QJ 0158 4325, HE 0435 1223, SDSS 0924+0219, SDSS 1004+4112, HE 1104 1805, and Q 2237+0305 were reported in Chen et al. \ (2012).
We detected no absorption in HE 1104 1805 and SDSS 0924 + 0219, and we had to drop QJ 0158 4325 and SDSS 0246 0825 since the differential extinction E(B-V) and absorption N_H are of opposite sign.

gmw_00012376.name: Q 2237+0305
gmw_00012376.overview: Q 2237+0305 is a gravitational lens galaxy [arXiv:1306.0008]. Observations of the system have enabled a direct measurement of its gas-phase metallicity [arXiv:1306.0008].

sources.summary: The paper includes Q 2237+0305 among the lens galaxies and reports that its variable-metallicity absorption fit converges, enabling a direct gas-phase metallicity measurement.

quotes.quote: In this paper, we analyze \ observations of 10 gravitational lenses, HE 0047 1756 (Wisotzki et al. \ 2004; Ofek et al. \ 2006), QJ 0158 4325 (Maza et al. \ 1995; Morgan et al. \ 1999; Faure et al. \ 2009), SDSS 0246 0805 (Inada et al. \ 2005; Eigenbrod et al. \ 2006), HE 0435 1223 (Wisotzki et al. \ 2002; Ofek et al. \ 2006), SDSS 0924+0219 (Inada et al. \ 2003a; Ofek et al. \ 2006), SDSS 1004+4112 (Inada et al. \ 2003b), PG 1115+080 (Weymann et al. \ 1980; Kundi et al. \ 1997), Q 1355 2257 (Morgan et al. \ 2003; Eigenbrod et al. \ 2006), HE 1104 1805 (Wisotzki 1993; Lidman et al. \ 2000), and Q 2237+0305 (Huchra et al. \ 1985), to measure the differential X-ray absorption between the lensed images, the metallicity, and the dust-to-gas ratio of lens galaxies.
Observations of QJ 0158 4325, HE 0435 1223, SDSS 0924+0219, SDSS 1004+4112, HE 1104 1805, and Q 2237+0305 were reported in Chen et al. \ (2012).

The fits allowing varying metallicity converge only for two lenses with long total exposure times, SDSS 1004+4112, and Q 2237+0305. The metallicities of these two lenses (in units of the solar metallicity Z_ ) are shown in Table tab:spectra .

gmw_00012377.name: J105327+205835
gmw_00012377.overview: J105327+205835 is a compact radio source with no previous individual observations in the literature [arXiv:1411.0361]. The host galaxy is located very close to a companion, suggesting a past interaction that may explain its broad, slightly blueshifted H I profile [arXiv:1411.0361].

sources.summary: The paper says J105327+205835 lacks previous individual observations, appears compact in NVSS/FIRST, shows a broad slightly blueshifted H I profile, and may have interacted with a nearby companion.

quotes.quote: J105327+205835 In the literature there are no records of individual observations of this radio source. The NVSS and FIRST images suggest that it is a compact source. In our observations, we detect a broad profile peaked at the systemic velocity and slightly asymmetric towards blueshifted velocities. The SDSS image shows that the host galaxy of this source is very close to a companion. Past interaction with a companion could explain the presence of the \ in the system.

gmw_00012378.name: 2MASX J112030+273610
gmw_00012378.overview: 2MASX J112030+273610 is a compact galaxy with no prior individual radio observations reported in the literature [arXiv:1411.0361]. Its detected neutral hydrogen profile is narrow and blueshifted, which may indicate that the gas is not settled in a rotating disk [arXiv:1411.0361].

sources.summary: The paper describes 2MASX J112030+273610 as a compact source with no prior individual radio observations and a narrow blueshifted H I profile suggesting unsettled neutral gas.

quotes.quote: 2MASX J112030+273610 There are no individual radio observation of this source reported in the literature. According to our classification, it is a compact source. The detected \ profile is narrow and blueshifted with respect to the optical velocity. This may indicate that we are tracing neutral hydrogen which is not settled in a rotating disk.

gmw_00012379.name: B3 1206+469
gmw_00012379.overview: B3 1206+469 is a possible BL-Lacertae object classified as a lobe-dominated, steep-spectrum radio source [arXiv:1411.0361]. It features an extended morphology with a central core, two symmetric radio lobes spanning approximately 550 kpc, and a narrow, shallow absorption line near its systemic velocity [arXiv:1411.0361].

sources.summary: The paper describes B3 1206+469 as a possible BL-Lac source with extended two-lobed radio morphology spanning about 550 kpc and a narrow shallow absorption line near systemic velocity.

quotes.quote: B3 1206+469 This radio source has been selected as part of the Cosmic Lens All Sky Survey (CLASS) as a possible BL-Lac object and then classified as a lobe dominated steep spectrum source. This radio source is extended with a central core and two symmetric lobes oriented in the north-south direction. The distance between the lobes is 4 arcminutes ( 550 kpc at z=0.100 ).
In our observations, we detect a narrow and shallow absorption line close to the systemic velocity.

gmw_00012380.name: B2 1229+33
gmw_00012380.overview: B2 1229+33 is a blue galaxy classified as a Fanaroff-Riley class II, high-excitation radio galaxy [arXiv:1411.0361]. Its neutral hydrogen profile shows a narrow detection at the systemic velocity alongside a redshifted component, which suggests the presence of a disk and infalling gas [arXiv:1411.0361].

sources.summary: The paper describes B2 1229+33 as an FR II/HERG blue galaxy excluded from the overall sample analysis but showing systemic narrow H I and a redshifted component suggesting disk plus infall.

quotes.quote: B2 1229+33 This extended source was classified as an FR II by . Based on the SDSS optical spectrum and image, it appears to be a High Excitation Radio Galaxy (HERG). Optically, the galaxy appears to be blue ( g - = 0.6), hence it has been excluded from the analysis of the overall sample presented in Sec. Discussion and Sec. sec:CompExt . The \ profile shows a narrow detection at the systemic velocity and, a second, redshifted component is also seen. These \ properties suggest the presence of a disk and infalling gas in this object.

gmw_00012381.name: 4C +49.25
gmw_00012381.overview: 4C +49.25 is a compact steep spectrum radio galaxy with a physical size of 6 kiloparsecs [arXiv:1411.0361]. Radio observations of the galaxy reveal a central core and two opposing jets, which may be interacting with surrounding gas clouds to drive a blueshifted outflow [arXiv:1411.0361].

sources.summary: The paper describes 4C +49.25 as a 6 kpc CSS source with VLA core and two jets, possible cloud interactions, and a blueshifted H I component attributed to jet-driven outflow.

quotes.quote: 4C +49.25 The size of this CSS source is 6 kpc Saikia2003, Fanti2000 . The 5 GHz VLA map reveals a core and two jets on the opposite sides Saikia2003 . It was suggested by that the higher degree of polarization asymmetry in CSS objects, including 4C +49.25, could be the result of interactions with clouds of gas which possibly fuel the radio source. Indeed, we find a blueshifted, shallow \ component in this source. This could be the result of outflowing gas, induced by jet-ISM interactions.

gmw_00012382.name: 2MASX J13013264+4634032
gmw_00012382.overview: 2MASX J13013264+4634032 is a point radio source classified as an active galactic nucleus [arXiv:1411.0361]. It is a blazar candidate that exhibits a faint, blueshifted H I profile, which may indicate interactions between the active nucleus and its surrounding gas [arXiv:1411.0361].

sources.summary: The paper describes 2MASX J13013264+4634032 as a point radio source with faint blueshifted H I indicating AGN-gas interaction, a blazar-candidate classification, and continued AGN classification.

quotes.quote: 2MASX J13013264+4634032 According to , this radio source is a point source. We detect a faint, blueshifted \ profile, which can indicate interactions between the AGN and the surrounding gaseous medium. The radio source is a Blazar candidate in the CLASS and CRATES surveys CaccianigaOpt, Healey2007 . However, it remains classified as an AGN by .

gmw_00012383.name: B3 1315+415
gmw_00012383.overview: B3 1315+415 is a compact radio galaxy with a complex radio morphology and a small size of only 5 parsecs [arXiv:1411.0361]. The source has an estimated dynamical age of 130 years and exhibits redshifted hydrogen absorption, which indicates gas is falling toward its nuclear region [arXiv:1411.0361].

sources.summary: The paper describes B3 1315+415 as a compact CORALZ source with complex VLBI morphology, a 5 pc size, a 130 yr dynamical age, redshifted H I indicating infall, and confirmed detection.

quotes.quote: B3 1315+415 VLBI observations of the CORALZ sample  Vries2009 reveal complex radio morphology in this object. The source has a small size of LLS = 5 pc. From the lobe expansion speed analysis  Vries2010 , a dynamical age of 130 yr is estimated in this source.  detected \ absorption redshifted by +77   \ relative to the systemic velocity, indicating in-falling gas towards the nuclear region. Our observations confirm the \ detection.

gmw_00012384.name: SDSS J132513.37+395553.2
gmw_00012384.overview: SDSS J132513.37+395553.2 is classified as a compact symmetric object with a projected linear size of 14 parsecs [arXiv:1411.0361]. It contains both blueshifted and redshifted H I components, which suggest the presence of unsettled gas structures such as infalling clouds or outflows [arXiv:1411.0361].

sources.summary: The paper classifies SDSS J132513.37+395553.2 as a 14 pc CORALZ compact symmetric object with blue- and redshifted H I components indicating unsettled gas such as infall or outflow.

quotes.quote: SDSS J132513.37+395553.2 In the CORALZ sample this source is classified as a compact symmetric object (CSO), with largest (projected) linear size (LLS) of 14 pc Vries2009 . Our observations show two \ components, one blueshifted, and the other redshifted relative to the systemic velocity. The newly detected \ profiles suggest that unsettled gas structures are present in this galaxy, e.g., infalling clouds, outflowing gas.

gmw_00012385.name: IRAS F13384+4503
gmw_00012385.overview: IRAS F13384+4503 is an optically blue Seyfert galaxy with a late-type morphology [arXiv:1411.0361]. It hosts a compact core-jet radio source spanning 4.1 parsecs, with narrow systemic H I absorption indicating the presence of a gas disk [arXiv:1411.0361].

sources.summary: The paper describes IRAS F13384+4503 as a blue late-type Seyfert with a compact core-jet radio source, 4.1 pc size, and narrow systemic H I absorption indicating a gas disk.

quotes.quote: IRAS F13384+4503 This galaxy is optically blue ( g - = 0.6), and the SDSS image revels a Seyfert galaxy with late-type morphology. This object is not included in the analysis presented in Sec. Discussion and Sec. sec:CompExt . In the CORALZ sample, the radio source is classified as a compact core-jet (CJ) source with two components which are significantly different in flux density and/or spectral index Vries2009 . The largest linear size of the source is 4.1 pc. Against the small continuum source, a very narrow \ absorption profile is detected at the systemic velocity, indicative of a gas disk.

gmw_00012386.name: 2MASX J142210+210554
gmw_00012386.overview: 2MASX J142210+210554 is an early-type galaxy that hosts a compact radio source [arXiv:1411.0361]. It exhibits a broad, asymmetric, and blueshifted H I absorption line with a smooth blueshifted edge [arXiv:1411.0361].

sources.summary: The paper describes 2MASX J142210+210554 as a compact radio source hosted by an early-type galaxy, with broad asymmetric blueshifted H I absorption and a smoother blueshifted edge.

quotes.quote: 2MASX J142210+210554 There are no individual observations of this source available in the literature. In our classification, the radio source is compact. The SDSS observations show that it is hosted by an early-type galaxy. We detect an absorption line, blueshifted with respect to the systemic velocity. The line is broad and asymmetric with a smoother blueshifted edge.

gmw_00012387.name: 2MASX J14352162+5051233
gmw_00012387.overview: 2MASX J14352162+5051233 is an unresolved active galactic nucleus with an estimated radio source size of 270 parsecs [arXiv:1411.0361]. Observations of the galaxy reveal a shallow, broad, blueshifted H I profile, which likely indicates gas interacting with the radio source [arXiv:1411.0361].

sources.summary: The paper describes 2MASX J14352162+5051233 as an unresolved CORALZ AGN with a 270 pc radio size, prior nondetection, and new shallow broad blueshifted H I likely interacting with the radio source.

quotes.quote: 2MASX J14352162+5051233 This is an unresolved CORALZ AGN, the size of the radio source is estimated to be 270 pc. This galaxy has been observed in \ by , however no components were detected. Our observations show a shallow, broad, blueshifted \ profile without deep/narrow component at the systemic velocity. Likely we are seeing gas interacting with the radio source.

gmw_00012388.name: 2MASX J150034+364845
gmw_00012388.overview: 2MASX J150034+364845 is a compact radio source with no prior targeted observations in the literature [arXiv:1411.0361]. Its neutral hydrogen traces a regularly rotating disk at the systemic velocity, with a blueshifted asymmetry that suggests non-circular motions [arXiv:1411.0361].

sources.summary: The paper describes 2MASX J150034+364845 as a compact source with no prior targeted observations, deep systemic H I tracing a rotating disk, and blueshifted asymmetry indicating non-circular motions.

quotes.quote: 2MASX J150034+364845 In the literature, there is no record of targeted observations of this radio source. According to our classification it is a compact source. We detect a deep absorption line. The line lies at the systemic velocity of the host galaxy and traces a regularly rotating \ disk. The line is slightly asymmetric in the blueshifted range of velocities.
This asymmetry is not recovered by the BF fit, suggesting that non-circular motions characterize the neutral hydrogen.

gmw_00012389.name: 2MASX J15292250+3621423
gmw_00012389.overview: 2MASX J15292250+3621423 is a radio galaxy classified as a compact source [arXiv:1411.0361]. It exhibits neutral hydrogen absorption near its systemic velocity, displaying a profile that is not entirely smooth [arXiv:1411.0361].

sources.summary: The paper describes 2MASX J15292250+3621423 as a compact source lacking previous individual observations, with H I absorption near systemic velocity and a profile that is not entirely smooth.

quotes.quote: 2MASX J15292250+3621423 We find no individual observations of this source in the literature. In our sample it is classified as a compact source. We detect \ in this object close to the systemic velocity. However, similarly to the case of source 9 the profile is not entirely smooth.

gmw_00012390.name: 4C +52.37
gmw_00012390.overview: 4C +52.37 is classified as a compact symmetric object [arXiv:1411.0361]. High-resolution observations of the galaxy reveal a central core, jet-like emission on opposite sides, and a blueshifted H I absorption wing that indicates a jet-driven outflow [arXiv:1411.0361].

sources.summary: The paper classifies 4C +52.37 as a compact symmetric object with core and two-sided jet-like emission, known main H I absorption, and a newly detected blueshifted wing indicating jet-driven outflow.

quotes.quote: 4C +52.37 This source is classified as a compact symmetric object (CSO) in the CORALZ sample. High-resolution observations reveal a core, and jet-like emission on the opposite sides Vries2009 . The main \ absorption component in 4C +52.37 was detected by , using the Giant Metrewave Radio Telescope (GMRT). Besides the main \ line, we detect a broad, shallow profile of blueshifted \ absorption. The broad component was not detected by , most likely because of the higher noise of the GMRT spectra.
The kinematical properties of the newly detected blueshifted wing are indicative of a jet-driven \ outflow in this compact radio source.

gmw_00012391.name: RA = 00:14:21.8; Dec = -30:23:44.0
gmw_00012391.overview: RA = 00:14:21.8; Dec = -30:23:44.0 is a Lyman-alpha emitting galaxy at a redshift of z=5.66, located in the Abell 2744 gravitational lensing field [arXiv:2312.04151]. It is characterized by a low stellar mass, a low metallicity, and a high specific star formation rate [arXiv:2312.04151].

sources.summary: The paper presents a MUSE-selected z=5.66 Ly-emitting galaxy at the quoted RA/Dec in A2744, confirms it with JWST spectroscopy, measures low mass/high SFR, and highlights a NIRSpec Ly non-detection caused by slit loss.

quotes.quote: In this paper, we present the JWST observations and results from both photometric and spectral analyses of a MUSE-selected strong Ly emitting galaxy (RA = 00:14:21.8; Dec = -30:23:44.0) at z=5.66 in the Abell 2744 (A2744) gravitational lensing field. Using the deep prism spectroscopy from the NIRSpec multiplexed microshutter assembly (MSA) Jakobsen2022 , we confirm the spectroscopic redshift of via the detection of a prominent Lyman break and a series of rest-frame optical strong nebular lines. Utilizing JWST/NIRCam Rieke2005,Rieke2023 and HST/WFC3+ACS imaging across twelve bands spanning the wavelength range of 0.8-5 m , we perform spatially resolved spectral energy distribution (SED) fitting. Surprisingly, the pronounced Ly emission seen in MUSE is not detected in the NIRSpec prism data, resulting in a significant bias in estimating the Ly escape fraction.

In this Letter, we present a z=5.66 LAE confirmed by MUSE IFU data with the absence of Ly emission line in JWST NIRSpec prism spectrum, reiterating the strong bias caused by slit loss. With JWST NIRCam data, we conduct broadband photometry SED fitting, obtaining the stellar mass of 10^ 8.82 M_ , SFR of 8.35 M_ yr^ 1 and further sSFR of 1.26 10^ 8 yr^ 1 . Besides, we use spectroscopic fitting and make emission diagnostics, giving a blue UV slope of 2.574 , low metallicity of 12+log(O/H) 7.88 and instantaneous SFR of 8.23M_ yr^ 1 . These observations are consistent with typical properties of LAEs, including low stellar mass and high sSFR.

gmw_00012392.name: NGC 291
gmw_00012392.overview: NGC 291 is a barred spiral galaxy hosting a Seyfert 2 nucleus [arXiv:1702.06545]. Although a narrow water maser was previously reported, subsequent observations failed to detect it, though they did reveal extended 20 GHz continuum emission [arXiv:1702.06545].

sources.summary: The paper identifies NGC 291 as a barred Seyfert 2 galaxy whose prior narrow water maser was not recovered, while extended 20 GHz continuum emission was detected.

quotes.quote: NGC 291 is a barred spiral galaxy with a Seyfert 2 nucleus kewley2001,nair2010 . The maser was detected as a narrow 60 mJy line in 2006 by GBT program GBT06A-009, footnote:maser_url but we did not detect it (2.6 mJy beam ^ 1 rms per 1.2 km s ^ 1 channel; Table tab:obs and Figure fig:NGC291 ) nor did kondratko2006 in 2002 (15 mJy rms per 1.3 km s ^ 1 channel). We detect extended 20 GHz continuum with 18--22 GHz spectral index 2.0 0.3 (Table tab:continuum ).

gmw_00012393.name: NGC 520b
gmw_00012393.overview: NGC 520b is a galaxy belonging to a colliding galaxy pair [arXiv:1702.06545]. It hosts a detected water maser likely originating from star formation, as well as extended flat-spectrum radio continuum emission [arXiv:1702.06545].

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 520b as part of a colliding pair with a detected water maser, extended flat-spectrum radio continuum, and evidence favoring a star-formation maser origin.

quotes.quote: NGC 520b is one galaxy in the colliding pair in NGC 520 e.g., stanford1990 . castangia2008 detected a maser in 2005, measuring a 40 mJy peak with the VLA and L_ iso 1 \ L_ . Our VLA detection shows a 35 3 mJy peak and L_ iso = 3.05 0.26 \ L_ (Figure fig:NGC520b and Table tab:masers ). Our VLA map shows extended 20 GHz radio continuum emission (Figure fig:NGC520b , bottom) with a flat 18--22 GHz spectral index of 0.23 0.19 (Table tab:continuum ) and a similar east-west morphology to the castangia2008 14.9 GHz map.

castangia2008 favor a star formation origin for the water maser in NGC 520b, but cannot rule out a low luminosity AGN. The preponderance of evidence suggests that NGC 520b is a poor candidate for an inclined maser disk.

gmw_00012394.name: CGCG 120 039
gmw_00012394.overview: CGCG 120 039 is a little-studied galaxy that hosts a multi-component water maser [arXiv:1702.06545]. This maser is spatially coincident with a flat-spectrum 20 GHz radio continuum source, making the galaxy a candidate for hosting an inclined maser disk [arXiv:1702.06545].

sources.summary: The paper presents CGCG 120 039 as a little-studied galaxy whose multi-component maser and flat-spectrum 20 GHz continuum are spatially coincident, making it a good inclined maser disk candidate.

quotes.quote: The water maser in this little-studied galaxy was detected in 2013 in GBT program GBT13A-236. footnote:maser_url It had a peak flux density of 210 mJy, was blueshifted from the systemic velocity, and showed several components. We detect the maser --- now at 82 2 mJy peak, but still showing multiple components (Figure fig:CGCG120-039 ) --- and the 20 GHz continuum at 0.56 0.04 mJy, which are spatially coincident to within a remarkably small 3.5 1.4 pc (Table tab:positions ). The 18--22 GHz spectral index is flat: = -0.27 0.16 (Table tab:continuum ). This object is a good inclined maser disk candidate.

gmw_00012395.name: J0912+2304
gmw_00012395.overview: J0912+2304 is a galaxy that hosts a water megamaser [arXiv:1702.06545]. First detected in 2008, the maser's origin remains ambiguous due to an undetected 20 GHz continuum [arXiv:1702.06545].

sources.summary: The paper reports J0912+2304 as a water-maser galaxy whose VLA maser peak is weaker than the GBT detection and whose undetected 20 GHz continuum leaves the maser origin ambiguous.

quotes.quote: The water maser in the galaxy J0912+2304 was detected in 2008 by GBT program GBT07A-034, footnote:maser_url showing a 30 mJy peak. zhu2011 list this as a water maser nondetection. The VLA observations show a 16 3 mJy peak, but no 20 GHz continuum down to an rms noise of 16 Jy beam ^ 1 (Figure fig:2MASX0912 and Tables tab:obs and tab:masers ). The provenance of the maser remains ambiguous.

gmw_00012396.name: J1011 1926
gmw_00012396.overview: J1011 1926 is a galaxy that hosts a broad water maser detected near its systemic velocity [arXiv:1702.06545]. Observations with the Very Large Array reveal this maser to be a multi-component emission source, though the galaxy lacks a 20 GHz continuum detection [arXiv:1702.06545].

sources.summary: The paper describes J1011 1926 as an almost unknown galaxy with a broad GBT maser and VLA multi-component maser near systemic velocity, but no 20 GHz continuum detection.

quotes.quote: The maser in this almost unknown galaxy was detected in GBT program GBT07A-066 footnote:maser_url in 2008. zhu2011 list this object as a water maser nondetection. The GBT detection shows a broad line with a 80 mJy peak. The VLA detection shows a broad multi-component maser with a 44 4 mJy peak in good agreement with the systemic velocity (Figure fig:2MASX1011 and Table tab:masers ).
We do not detect the 20 GHz continuum. The nature of this maser therefore remains ambiguous.

gmw_00012397.name: UGC 7016
gmw_00012397.overview: UGC 7016 is a barred spiral galaxy [arXiv:1702.06545]. Although a water maser was previously reported in the galaxy, subsequent observations failed to detect either the maser or any 20 GHz continuum emission [arXiv:1702.06545].

sources.summary: The paper describes UGC 7016 as a barred spiral with a previously reported water maser that was not recovered, nor was 20 GHz continuum emission detected.

quotes.quote: UGC 7016 The water maser in this barred spiral RC3 was detected in 2007 by GBT program GBT07A-034, footnote:maser_url but zhu2011 list this as a nondetection. The GBT detection spectrum had a peak flux density of 55 mJy, but we did not detect the maser or the 20 GHz continuum emission. VLA rms noise values were 3.8 mJy beam ^ 1 per 1.2 km s ^ 1 channel in the spectral line cube and 16 Jy beam ^ 1 in the continuum map (Table tab:obs ).

gmw_00012398.name: NGC 5691
gmw_00012398.overview: NGC 5691 is a barred spiral galaxy that does not exhibit Seyfert activity [arXiv:1702.06545]. Although a water maser was previously reported in the galaxy, subsequent observations failed to detect either the maser or any 20 GHz continuum emission [arXiv:1702.06545].

sources.summary: The paper identifies NGC 5691 as a barred non-Seyfert spiral whose previously reported water maser and any 20 GHz continuum emission were not detected in the VLA data.

quotes.quote: NGC 5691 The water maser in NGC 5691, a barred non-Seyfert spiral galaxy mulchaey1997 , was detected in 2009 by GBT program AGBT08C-035 footnote:maser_url with a 45 mJy peak flux density. It was listed by zhu2011 as a maser nondetection. We did not detect the maser or any 20 GHz continuum in this galaxy, with rms noise levels of 3.7 mJy beam ^ 1 per 1.1 km s ^ 1 channel and 17 Jy beam ^ 1 , respectively (Table tab:obs ).

gmw_00012399.name: CGCG 168 018
gmw_00012399.overview: CGCG 168 018 is an active galactic nucleus that hosts a detected water maser [arXiv:1702.06545]. The galaxy's unresolved maser emission and 20 GHz continuum exhibit a relative offset of 21.6 parsecs, and its continuum may arise from a radio jet [arXiv:1702.06545].

sources.summary: The paper describes CGCG 168 018 as a little-studied AGN with detected maser and continuum emission, but their significant offset and possible jet continuum make the maser origin ambiguous.

quotes.quote: CGCG 168 018 CGCG 168 018 is a little-studied galaxy classified as an AGN by schawinski2010 and listed as a water maser nondetection by zhu2011 . Water maser emission was detected by GBT program GBT07A-066 footnote:maser_url in 2008. The GBT water maser spectrum shows a 50 mJy peak, detected by this work at 25 2 mJy (Table tab:masers ). The 0.31 0.03 mJy continuum shows a 18-22 GHz spectral index of 0.95 0.17 (Table tab:continuum ).

The maser emission and 20 GHz continuum are unresolved but show a significant relative offset of 21.6 2.7 pc (Table tab:positions and Figure fig:CGCG168-018 ). This is the only object in the sample that shows a significant offset between the maser and continuum emission (Figure fig:offsets ). While the offset suggests that the maser emission is not deflected from an inclined maser disk, the continuum spectral index suggests that the continuum may arise from a jet, and the radio core is not detected.

gmw_00012400.name: J1939 0124
gmw_00012400.overview: J1939 0124 is a spiral galaxy that hosts a Seyfert 2 active galactic nucleus [arXiv:1702.06545]. The galaxy contains a detected water maser with an ambiguous nature, but it lacks a detectable 22 GHz continuum [arXiv:1702.06545].

sources.summary: The paper reports J1939 0124 as a Seyfert 2 spiral with a detected water maser but no 20 GHz continuum, leaving a changed-profile maser of ambiguous nature.

quotes.quote: J1939 0124 greenhill2003 detected the water maser in this spiral galaxy hosting a Sey 2 nucleus in 2002 using the Tidbinbilla antenna. The maser showed a peak flux density of 28 mJy, and henkel2005 report L_ iso 160 \ L_ . No 22 GHz continuum was detected by greenhill2003 using the VLA ( < 2.8 mJy). We likewise detect no continuum, with rms noise of 16 Jy beam ^ 1 (Table tab:obs ), but we do detect the maser emission with peak 30 2 mJy ( L_ iso = 102 16 \ L_ ) although with a substantially different maser profile (Figure fig:2MASX1939 and Table tab:masers ).

The 6 and 20 cm continua were detected at 5.4 0.4 mJy and 15.5 1.0 mJy, respectively, by vader1993 , and the 6 cm continuum position agrees with the maser position to within 1 ( 430 pc). The nature of this maser remains ambiguous.

gmw_00012401.name: HerBS-69B
gmw_00012401.overview: HerBS-69B is a galaxy located in a field with multiple sources [arXiv:2303.04830]. It is the fainter companion component in its system, situated near a slightly brighter primary source that has a different redshift [arXiv:2303.04830].

sources.summary: HerBS-69B is identified as one of four sources with CO line ratios above thermalized expectations; the authors report r_4,3 about 2.53 while emphasizing large ratio uncertainties.

quotes.quote: While the majority of sources show sub-thermalized profiles (i.e., below their respective dashed lines ), several sources (HerBS-69B, -120A, -120B, -159B) have SLEDs above the typically-assumed theoretical maximum excitation, based on the equipartition distribution of the CO excitation states; this will be discussed separately in Section sec:ch5 .

In Subsection sec:COSLEDs , we identified four sources with line ratios in excess of the thermalized profile ( L'_ CO,a /L'_ CO,b = 1 ; eq. eq:JaJb ), namely HerBS-69B, -120A, -120B, and -159B. Interestingly, these are found only in fields with multiple sources where the companion galaxy has a slightly different redshift. The large angular separation between the components ( 7 - 10 arcsecs ) rules out galaxy-galaxy lensing, and the slightly different redshifts suggest they are not one source multiply imaged. In three out of four cases, they are the fainter component, i.e., B sources For HerBS-69, the A source is 1.2 times brighter than the B source; for HerBS-120, the A source has a similar brightness as the B source; but for HerBS-159B, the A source is 3 times brighter than the B source. .

We measure the following luminosity ratios for these four sources: r_ 4,3 = 2.53 0.89 for HerBS-69B; r_ 5,4 = 1.44 0.46 for HerBS-120A; r_ 5,4 = 2.70 0.74 for HerBS-120B; and r_ 4,3 = 1.69 0.44 for HerBS-159B. As you can see, although these are consistent with thermalized profiles by 1 - 2.3 , we note a fundamental limitation in assessing the super-thermalized line profiles using our data.

gmw_00012402.name: HerBS-120A
gmw_00012402.overview: HerBS-120A is a galaxy that exhibits a CO line ratio, specifically r_5,4, of approximately 1.44 [arXiv:2303.04830]. This ratio is above thermalized expectations, although it is subject to large measurement uncertainties [arXiv:2303.04830].

sources.summary: HerBS-120A is identified as one of four sources with CO line ratios above thermalized expectations; the authors report r_5,4 about 1.44 while emphasizing large ratio uncertainties.

quotes.quote: While the majority of sources show sub-thermalized profiles (i.e., below their respective dashed lines ), several sources (HerBS-69B, -120A, -120B, -159B) have SLEDs above the typically-assumed theoretical maximum excitation, based on the equipartition distribution of the CO excitation states; this will be discussed separately in Section sec:ch5 .

In Subsection sec:COSLEDs , we identified four sources with line ratios in excess of the thermalized profile ( L'_ CO,a /L'_ CO,b = 1 ; eq. eq:JaJb ), namely HerBS-69B, -120A, -120B, and -159B. In three out of four cases, they are the fainter component, i.e., B sources For HerBS-69, the A source is 1.2 times brighter than the B source; for HerBS-120, the A source has a similar brightness as the B source; but for HerBS-159B, the A source is 3 times brighter than the B source. .

We measure the following luminosity ratios for these four sources: r_ 4,3 = 2.53 0.89 for HerBS-69B; r_ 5,4 = 1.44 0.46 for HerBS-120A; r_ 5,4 = 2.70 0.74 for HerBS-120B; and r_ 4,3 = 1.69 0.44 for HerBS-159B. As you can see, although these are consistent with thermalized profiles by 1 - 2.3 , we note a fundamental limitation in assessing the super-thermalized line profiles using our data.

gmw_00012403.name: HerBS-120B
gmw_00012403.overview: HerBS-120B is a galaxy located in a field with multiple sources, positioned near a companion galaxy with a slightly different redshift [arXiv:2303.04830]. It is one of several sources that exhibit CO line ratios above thermalized expectations, with a measured r_5,4 ratio of approximately 2.70 [arXiv:2303.04830].

sources.summary: HerBS-120B is identified as one of four sources with CO line ratios above thermalized expectations; the authors report r_5,4 about 2.70 while emphasizing large ratio uncertainties.

quotes.quote: While the majority of sources show sub-thermalized profiles (i.e., below their respective dashed lines ), several sources (HerBS-69B, -120A, -120B, -159B) have SLEDs above the typically-assumed theoretical maximum excitation, based on the equipartition distribution of the CO excitation states; this will be discussed separately in Section sec:ch5 .

In Subsection sec:COSLEDs , we identified four sources with line ratios in excess of the thermalized profile ( L'_ CO,a /L'_ CO,b = 1 ; eq. eq:JaJb ), namely HerBS-69B, -120A, -120B, and -159B. In three out of four cases, they are the fainter component, i.e., B sources For HerBS-69, the A source is 1.2 times brighter than the B source; for HerBS-120, the A source has a similar brightness as the B source; but for HerBS-159B, the A source is 3 times brighter than the B source. .

We measure the following luminosity ratios for these four sources: r_ 4,3 = 2.53 0.89 for HerBS-69B; r_ 5,4 = 1.44 0.46 for HerBS-120A; r_ 5,4 = 2.70 0.74 for HerBS-120B; and r_ 4,3 = 1.69 0.44 for HerBS-159B. As you can see, although these are consistent with thermalized profiles by 1 - 2.3 , we note a fundamental limitation in assessing the super-thermalized line profiles using our data.

gmw_00012404.name: HerBS-159B
gmw_00012404.overview: HerBS-159B is a galaxy that exhibits a CO line ratio, specifically r_4,3, of approximately 1.69 [arXiv:2303.04830]. This value is above typically assumed thermalized expectations, though it remains consistent with thermalized profiles within 1 to 2.3 sigma due to large uncertainties [arXiv:2303.04830].

sources.summary: HerBS-159B is identified as one of four sources with CO line ratios above thermalized expectations; the authors report r_4,3 about 1.69 while emphasizing large ratio uncertainties.

quotes.quote: While the majority of sources show sub-thermalized profiles (i.e., below their respective dashed lines ), several sources (HerBS-69B, -120A, -120B, -159B) have SLEDs above the typically-assumed theoretical maximum excitation, based on the equipartition distribution of the CO excitation states; this will be discussed separately in Section sec:ch5 .

In Subsection sec:COSLEDs , we identified four sources with line ratios in excess of the thermalized profile ( L'_ CO,a /L'_ CO,b = 1 ; eq. eq:JaJb ), namely HerBS-69B, -120A, -120B, and -159B. Interestingly, these are found only in fields with multiple sources where the companion galaxy has a slightly different redshift. The large angular separation between the components ( 7 - 10 arcsecs ) rules out galaxy-galaxy lensing, and the slightly different redshifts suggest they are not one source multiply imaged. In three out of four cases, they are the fainter component, i.e., B sources For HerBS-69, the A source is 1.2 times brighter than the B source; for HerBS-120, the A source has a similar brightness as the B source; but for HerBS-159B, the A source is 3 times brighter than the B source. .

We measure the following luminosity ratios for these four sources: r_ 4,3 = 2.53 0.89 for HerBS-69B; r_ 5,4 = 1.44 0.46 for HerBS-120A; r_ 5,4 = 2.70 0.74 for HerBS-120B; and r_ 4,3 = 1.69 0.44 for HerBS-159B. As you can see, although these are consistent with thermalized profiles by 1 - 2.3 , we note a fundamental limitation in assessing the super-thermalized line profiles using our data.

gmw_00012405.name: 7C 1751+6809
gmw_00012405.overview: 7C 1751+6809 is a radio galaxy located at a redshift of z = 1.54 [arXiv:0908.4106]. Observations of its environment reveal no overdensity of red galaxies or excess of BzK-selected galaxies in its immediate surroundings [arXiv:0908.4106].

sources.summary: The paper selects 7C 1751+6809 as a z=1.54 radio galaxy target and finds no red-galaxy overdensity or close BzK-galaxy excess around it.

quotes.quote: From the seven such sources available in the Spring semester of the Northern hemisphere, we selected the two radio galaxies with the most supporting data, 7C 1756+6520 ( z = 1.48 ; R.A.: 17:57:05.44, Dec.: +65:19:53.11) and 7C 1751+6809 ( z = 1.54 ; R.A.: 17:50:49.87, Dec.: +68:08:25.93). These two radio galaxies were first published in Lacy1992 as part of a sample of 57 radio sources selected at 38 MHz. That paper presents high resolution radio maps of both objects.
We develop and apply BzK color criteria to select cluster member candidates around the radio galaxies and find no evidence of an overdensity of red galaxies within 2 Mpc of 7C 1751+6809.
No excess of BzK galaxies is found in the close surroundings of 7C 1751+6809. This region is actually under-dense for elliptical candidates by a factor of two compared to the full field.

gmw_00012406.name: Sharov 21
gmw_00012406.overview: Sharov 21 is a quasar at a redshift of z = 2.109 that is observed through the disk of the Andromeda Galaxy [arXiv:1706.03079]. It exhibited an 800-day ultraviolet flare that may have been caused by a tidal disruption event or gravitational microlensing [arXiv:1706.03079].

sources.summary: The paper describes Sharov 21 as a z=2.109 quasar with an 800-day UV flare, large brightening, asymmetric profile, high bolometric energy, and possible TDE or microlensing interpretation.

quotes.quote: reported a significant UV flare in a quasar, Sharov 21 at z = 2.109 , seen through the disk of M31 (this had previously been misidentified as a nova event). The total outburst lasted 800 days with the source 3.3 mag brighter at maximum. The flare showed an asymmetric profile with a gradual increase followed by an abrupt rise and then a quasi-exponential decline (t^ 5/3 ) and a total bolometric energy release of 2 10^ 54 ergs. It is consistent with a standard TDE scenario involving a 10 M_ star and a 5 10^8 M_ black hole (although microlensing is also considered as an alternate explanation).

gmw_00012407.name: SDSS J102912+404220
gmw_00012407.overview: SDSS J102912+404220 is a narrow-line Seyfert 1 galaxy located at a redshift of z = 0.147 [arXiv:1706.03079]. The galaxy hosted a luminous 400-day nuclear optical transient, which is interpreted as a Type IIn supernova occurring within its active galactic nucleus region [arXiv:1706.03079].

sources.summary: The paper describes SDSS J102912+404220 as a z=0.147 narrow-line Seyfert 1 galaxy whose nuclear optical transient lasted 400 days and was interpreted as a luminous Type IIn supernova.

quotes.quote: discovered an extremely luminous optical transient within 150 pc of the nucleus of a narrow-line Seyfert 1 galaxy, SDSS J102912+404220 at z = 0.147 . The total outburst lasted 400 days with the source 1.2 mag brighter at maximum. It also showed an asymmetric profile with a slow increase and then a longer slow decline that was inconsistent with the expected t^ 5/3 or t^ 5/12 decline expected for TDEs. The proposed interpretation is an extremely luminous Type IIn supernova within the range of the narrow-line region of an AGN.

gmw_00012408.name: NGC 6845A
gmw_00012408.overview: NGC 6845A is the main galaxy in an interacting group of four galaxies [arXiv:2404.11549]. It displays an extended tidal tail with a highly active star-forming hinge clump at its base that is kinematically and chemically compatible with the galactic disk [arXiv:2404.11549].

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 6845A as the main interacting galaxy in NGC 6845, with an extended tidal tail whose hinge clump matches the disk kinematics and metallicity gradient.

quotes.quote: It is composed by 4 interacting galaxies labeled A, B, C and, D (as it is shown in Figure figfov ), being the main galaxy of this group NGC 6845A, which displays the most prominent and extended tidal tail of the system. At the outskirts of its disk, hinted in a blue square box on Figure figfov , there is a extremely bright source located at the base of the northern tidal tail , which is the most intense source of all regions visible in the disk and tidal tails of this group.

In fact, this clump represents 15 of total SFR of NGC 6845A. Large-scale modeling of the observed velocity field of NGC 6845A rules out the scenario on which this "hinge clump" was a satellite galaxy. Indeed, its kinematics is compatible with the galactic disk of NGC 6845A. Its abundance, mean value of 0.4Z , is also consistent with the metallicity gradient of the galaxy.

gmw_00012409.name: SDSS J1354+1327
gmw_00012409.overview: SDSS J1354+1327 is an active galactic nucleus host galaxy at a redshift of z=0.06 [arXiv:1710.00825]. A tidal interaction with a companion galaxy 12.5 kpc away triggered two sequential outbursts, producing a southern relic outflow and a newer northern outflow [arXiv:1710.00825].

sources.summary: The paper presents SDSS J1354+1327 as a z=0.06 AGN host whose nucleus turned off and on again, producing southern relic outflow and northern shocked gas after interaction with a nearby companion.

quotes.quote: We have used Chandra , HST , APO/DIS optical longslit spectroscopy, and Keck/OSIRIS near-infrared AO-assisted IFS to determine the nature of the z=0.06 galaxy SDSS J1354+1327. SDSS J1354+1327 was originally selected from SDSS spectra of active galaxies for its narrow AGN emission lines that are offset in line-of-sight velocity from systemic by 69 km s ^ 1 . We determined that the velocity offset arises from the peak in emission line gas, which is located 0.40 kpc northeast of the galaxy center. We conclude that SDSS J1354+1327 is a galaxy whose AGN has recently turned on and then on again, through two accretion events that led to two sequential AGN outbursts.

The two accretion events arose from clumpy ISM being driven to the center of SDSS J1354+1327 as a result of a tidal interaction with the companion galaxy located 12.5 kpc away. The outflow visible to the south of the galaxy center is remnant emission lingering from a past AGN outburst, because it is photoionized gas with a large spatial extent of the gas ( 10 kpc). The emission north of the galaxy center is a new AGN outburst that launched a new outflow, based on the shocked nature of the gas and the smaller spatial extent of the gas ( 1 kpc).

gmw_00012410.name: SDSS J1354+1328
gmw_00012410.overview: SDSS J1354+1328 is a companion galaxy located 12.5 kpc northeast of SDSS J1354+1327 [arXiv:1710.00825]. The two galaxies are interacting, as evidenced by tidal tails of stars connecting them, and SDSS J1354+1328 likely hosts an obscured active galactic nucleus [arXiv:1710.00825].

sources.summary: The companion SDSS J1354+1328 lies 12.5 kpc from SDSS J1354+1327, shows Seyfert-like optical line ratios, lacks Chandra and WISE detections, and is interpreted as likely hosting an obscured AGN.

quotes.quote: SDSS J1354+1327 has a companion galaxy, SDSS J135429.17+132807.3, that is located 12.5 kpc to the northeast and is redshifted by 76 km s ^ 1 relative to SDSS J1354+1327. Emission line diagnostics of the companion's SDSS spectrum suggest that it hosts an AGN, and also noted that SDSS J1354+1327 and SDSS J1354+1328 are a pair of active galaxies. However, there is no WISE source detected at the location of the companion galaxy. Further, we do not detect an AGN in our Chandra observations of the companion galaxy.

Given that the dust map indicates obscuration at the galaxy center ( V-H=2.4 ), we conclude that the galaxy most likely hosts an obscured AGN. The HST observations show tidal tails of stars connecting the primary and companion galaxies, indicating that they are already interacting (Figure fig:positions , bottom left).

gmw_00012411.name: DELS J1217+0131
gmw_00012411.overview: DELS J1217+0131 is a known high-redshift quasar located at a redshift of z=6.17 [arXiv:2111.03073]. The active galactic nucleus exhibits a strong Lyman-alpha emission line [arXiv:2111.03073].

sources.summary: The paper treats DELS J1217+0131 as a known z=6.17 high-redshift QSO that the classifier fails to recover, attributing the miss to flux-model limitations despite its strong Ly emission.

quotes.quote: Among the classified sources there are eight known high- z QSOs that were found in the VIKING survey area from past works: DELS J1217+0131 ( z=6.17 ; Banados16 ; Wang17 ), ATLAS J025.6821-33.4627 ( z=6.31 , hereafter J0142-3327; Carnall15 ), HSC J1137+0045 ( z=6.4 ; Matsuoka19a ), J0148-2826 ( z=6.54 ; Yang20a ), HSC J0921+0007 ( z=6.56 ; Matsuoka18b ), VIK J0305-3400 ( z=6.604 ; Venemans13 ), DELS J1048-0109 ( z=6.63 ; Wang17 ), HSC J1205-0000 ( z=6.74 ; Matsuoka16 ). These sources and their main properties are listed in Table tab:known_qsos , while their probabilities of being high- z QSOs are pinpointed with red arrows in Fig. fig:probabilities .

In the range 6 z 6.5 , our models are able to correctly classify one known QSO out of three, J0142-3327 ( P_ QSO 99.9 ), but our selection threshold in this redshift range ( P_ th =10 ) does not allow us to recover DELS J1217+0131 ( P_ QSO 0.06 ), and HSC J1137+0045 ( P_ QSO 10^ 7 ). The probability of these three quasars are also reported in Table tab:known_qsos and shown in Fig. fig:probabilities (upper panel). The low probability of the latter one is not surprising, considering that HSC J1137+0045 is a very faint QSO ( J=21.51 and SNR (J)=5.4 ), selected from the Hyper Suprime-Cam (HSC) Subaru Strategic Program (SSP) survey ( Aihara18 ), and that apparently lacks strong Ly in emission ( Matsuoka19a ).

It is apparent that in some sub-plots of Fig. fig:known_6_65 (especially those with f_z and f_Y ), the relative fluxes of both HSC J1137+0045 and DELS J1217+0131 are not consistent with the simulated 6 z 6.5 QSOs relative flux distributions (blue contours), consequently lowering the classification probability of these two objects. Considering that HSC J1137+0045 is a QSO that apparently lacks strong Ly in emission ( Matsuoka19b ), while DELS J1217+0131 exhibits a strong Ly emission line ( Wang17 ), we conclude that the properties of the ``simqso'' simulated high- z QSOs, that have been used for the training of our XDHZQSO QSO models, are too rigid to include these two sources.

gmw_00012412.name: HSC J1137+0045
gmw_00012412.overview: HSC J1137+0045 is a faint quasar located at a redshift of approximately z=6.4 [arXiv:2111.03073]. This high-redshift active galactic nucleus apparently lacks strong Lyman-alpha emission [arXiv:2111.03073].

sources.summary: The paper identifies HSC J1137+0045 as a faint z=6.4 QSO missed by the classifier because its relative fluxes and weak Ly emission do not match the simulated QSO models.

quotes.quote: Among the classified sources there are eight known high- z QSOs that were found in the VIKING survey area from past works: DELS J1217+0131 ( z=6.17 ; Banados16 ; Wang17 ), ATLAS J025.6821-33.4627 ( z=6.31 , hereafter J0142-3327; Carnall15 ), HSC J1137+0045 ( z=6.4 ; Matsuoka19a ), J0148-2826 ( z=6.54 ; Yang20a ), HSC J0921+0007 ( z=6.56 ; Matsuoka18b ), VIK J0305-3400 ( z=6.604 ; Venemans13 ), DELS J1048-0109 ( z=6.63 ; Wang17 ), HSC J1205-0000 ( z=6.74 ; Matsuoka16 ). These sources and their main properties are listed in Table tab:known_qsos , while their probabilities of being high- z QSOs are pinpointed with red arrows in Fig. fig:probabilities .

In the range 6 z 6.5 , our models are able to correctly classify one known QSO out of three, J0142-3327 ( P_ QSO 99.9 ), but our selection threshold in this redshift range ( P_ th =10 ) does not allow us to recover DELS J1217+0131 ( P_ QSO 0.06 ), and HSC J1137+0045 ( P_ QSO 10^ 7 ). The probability of these three quasars are also reported in Table tab:known_qsos and shown in Fig. fig:probabilities (upper panel). The low probability of the latter one is not surprising, considering that HSC J1137+0045 is a very faint QSO ( J=21.51 and SNR (J)=5.4 ), selected from the Hyper Suprime-Cam (HSC) Subaru Strategic Program (SSP) survey ( Aihara18 ), and that apparently lacks strong Ly in emission ( Matsuoka19a ).

It is apparent that in some sub-plots of Fig. fig:known_6_65 (especially those with f_z and f_Y ), the relative fluxes of both HSC J1137+0045 and DELS J1217+0131 are not consistent with the simulated 6 z 6.5 QSOs relative flux distributions (blue contours), consequently lowering the classification probability of these two objects. Considering that HSC J1137+0045 is a QSO that apparently lacks strong Ly in emission ( Matsuoka19b ), while DELS J1217+0131 exhibits a strong Ly emission line ( Wang17 ), we conclude that the properties of the ``simqso'' simulated high- z QSOs, that have been used for the training of our XDHZQSO QSO models, are too rigid to include these two sources.

gmw_00012413.name: NGC 7683
gmw_00012413.overview: NGC 7683 is a lenticular (S0) galaxy located at a redshift of approximately 0.01227 [arXiv:2205.06819]. It exhibits highly regular photometry and kinematics, with evidence of a rotating pseudo-bulge or bar remnant at its center [arXiv:2205.06819].

sources.summary: The paper applies a Bayesian structural decomposition to NGC 7683, identifying the S0 galaxy as a regular CALIFA target with a rotating central pseudo-bulge or bar remnant.

quotes.quote: NGC 7683 NGC 7683 is an S0 galaxy at redshift z=0.01227 \ ( 53 Mpc ) with a mild inclination and a regular velocity pattern that can be considered a representative example of the disc galaxies population in the local Universe. Even though our assumptions of razor-thin disc with a clear bulge to disc decomposition may fit better Sa-Sb galaxies we instead opted for an S0 galaxy. Lenticular galaxies are usually dominated by old stellar populations, affected by little or negligible dust attenuation, thus resulting in homogeneous M/L ratios and prominent absorption features in their spectra, which facilitate the extraction of the kinematics. Among the S0s in the CALIFA survey we selected NGC 7683 by visually inspecting its highly regular photometry and kinematics (no isophotal distortions, rings, spiral structures, counter rotation etc.) resulting in an optimal candidate for a first test of our implementation.

As a first case-study, we applied the algorithm to NGC 7683, an S0 galaxy at redshift z 0.012 , whose kinematics has been obtained as part of the CALIFA survey Sanchez_2012 , while the ugriz band photometry was collected within the Sloan Digital Sky Survey SDSS . The Bayesian analysis is able to: fully constrain the posterior probability distributions of all 17 fitting parameters; detect with high statistical significance the presence of a rotating component in the very center of NGC 7683. This may suggest that NGC 7683 possesses a rotating pseudo-bulge, possibly a bar remnant.

gmw_00012414.name: HST J15433+5352
gmw_00012414.overview: HST J15433+5352 is an optical gravitationally lensed arc discovered in the Hubble Space Telescope Medium Deep Survey [arXiv:astro-ph/0609542]. The system features a lens galaxy and a companion galaxy, with the lensed source likely positioned over a cusp [arXiv:astro-ph/0609542].

sources.summary: The paper models HST J15433+5352 as an optical lensed arc from the HST Medium Deep Survey, using F450W data and finding elliptical lens models cannot distinguish intrinsic ellipticity from external shear.

quotes.quote: The optical lensed arc HST J15433+5352 HST J15433+5352 1999AJ....117.2010R was one of several gravitationally lensed objects found in the HST Medium Deep Survey. The data are available (in several filters) as a WFPC2 association from the HST archive including variance files. The pixel scale of the data is 0.1 arcsec pixel ^ 1 . In this analysis we use only the F450W image which has the best signal in the arcs and the faintest lens galaxy. A section of the F450W image containing the lens galaxy (L) and companion galaxy (G) is shown in Fig. fig:j15433_gals .

Our models show that for HST J15433+5352, the data are unable to distinguish between an elliptical model and an elliptical model with external shear. We speculate that this because the source is lying over a cusp, therefore has a very generic image configuration.

gmw_00012415.name: C-4636
gmw_00012415.overview: C-4636 is a high-redshift Lyman-break galaxy candidate with a photometric redshift of approximately 7.69 [arXiv:2404.02960]. It exhibits a compact morphology and is located at a projected distance of 223 physical kiloparsecs from the quasar ULAS J1342+0928 [arXiv:2404.02960].

sources.summary: The paper identifies C-4636 as a zphot=7.69 LBG candidate 223 pkpc from ULAS J1342+0928, with photometry, compact morphology, and IRAC non-detections supporting a high-redshift interpretation.

quotes.quote: We find a new LBG candidate, C-4636, at z_ phot =7.69 (see Figure cand1 ). This photometric redshift is slightly higher than z=7.5 that of the quasar given the very flat redshift probability distribution between z=7.5-8 , but the solution dominates among the other redshift distributions with P(7<z<8)= 80 , constraining its association with the environment of the quasar. Moreover, C-4636 is at a projected distance of 223 pkpc from the quasar. 1 This distance is similar to that of galaxies found around other high-z quasars in the literature, which showed strong quasar-galaxy clustering e.g., morselli_primordial_2014,farina_mapping_2017,mignoli_web_2020,meyer_constraining_2022 .

The color of candidate C-4636 in Y_ 105 J_ 125 = 0.7 and its compact morphology with a 1 half-light radius r_ 0.5 = 0 10 is comparable with the average radius of stars in this field 1 of r_ 0.5 = 0 13 0 01 , make it a possible MLT-dwarf contaminant (see Figure color-color ). However, the ratio of the flux in the total Kron to the 0 4 aperture 1 of 1.45 0.07 , and the Source Extractor stellarity parameter of CLASS STAR =0.08 do not classify this source as a star. Additionally, galaxies at this redshift would show a distinct SED from MLT contaminants at >2 m, which can be determined even with shallow IRAC imaging e.g finkelstein_census_2022, bagley_bright_2024 . The non-detection in our IRAC/3.6 m and 4.5 m imaging support the high-redshift nature of this candidate.

gmw_00012416.name: C-4966
gmw_00012416.overview: C-4966 is a high-redshift galaxy candidate with a photometric redshift of approximately 6.91 [arXiv:2404.02960]. It is located at an impact parameter of 290 pkpc from a metal absorption system detected at redshift 6.8 [arXiv:2404.02960].

sources.summary: The paper identifies C-4966 as a zphot=6.91 galaxy candidate near a z=6.84 absorber; its 290 pkpc impact parameter may fit high-redshift group absorption, pending spectroscopy.

quotes.quote: We begin by selecting all sources with P(6<z<12) > 60 , and a P(6<z<7) higher than neighboring distributions in z=1 bins. After visually inspecting the candidates and evaluating the best fits for the high and lower-redshift solutions ^2_ l-h , as we did for similar to the process used for the z 7.5 galaxy candidates search, we identify two galaxy candidates. C-4966 has a photometric redshift of z_ phot =6.91 and a probability distribution P(6<z<7)=55 and P(6.5<z<7.5)=71 . C-5764 is found at z_ phot =6.89 and has a P(6<z<7)=68 and P(6.5<z<7.5)=79 (see Figures rgb , cand1 , and Table table-pz ). Both candidates favor a redshift solution closer to z=6.5-7.5 .

If this behavior holds at high redshift see for implication on the end of the reionization on absorption systems doughty_environments_2023 , this can explain the relatively large impact parameters observed for our two candidates C-4966 and C-5764 (290 pkpc, 476 pkpc, assuming z=6.9 , respectively). The two z 6.9 galaxy candidates need to be spectroscopically confirmed to establish the physical link with the metal absorption system detected at z 6.8 by simcoe_interstellar_2020 .

gmw_00012417.name: C-5764
gmw_00012417.overview: C-5764 is a high-redshift galaxy candidate with a photometric redshift of approximately 6.89 [arXiv:2404.02960]. It is located near a quasar sightline absorber with an estimated impact parameter of 476 pkpc [arXiv:2404.02960].

sources.summary: The paper identifies C-5764 as a zphot=6.89 galaxy candidate near the quasar sightline absorber, with a 476 pkpc impact parameter and a need for spectroscopic confirmation.

quotes.quote: We begin by selecting all sources with P(6<z<12) > 60 , and a P(6<z<7) higher than neighboring distributions in z=1 bins. After visually inspecting the candidates and evaluating the best fits for the high and lower-redshift solutions ^2_ l-h , as we did for similar to the process used for the z 7.5 galaxy candidates search, we identify two galaxy candidates. C-4966 has a photometric redshift of z_ phot =6.91 and a probability distribution P(6<z<7)=55 and P(6.5<z<7.5)=71 . C-5764 is found at z_ phot =6.89 and has a P(6<z<7)=68 and P(6.5<z<7.5)=79 (see Figures rgb , cand1 , and Table table-pz ). Both candidates favor a redshift solution closer to z=6.5-7.5 .

If this behavior holds at high redshift see for implication on the end of the reionization on absorption systems doughty_environments_2023 , this can explain the relatively large impact parameters observed for our two candidates C-4966 and C-5764 (290 pkpc, 476 pkpc, assuming z=6.9 , respectively). The two z 6.9 galaxy candidates need to be spectroscopically confirmed to establish the physical link with the metal absorption system detected at z 6.8 by simcoe_interstellar_2020 .

gmw_00012418.name: dw0137+1541
gmw_00012418.overview: The dwarf companion galaxy dw0137+1541 is located in close proximity to UGC 1171 [arXiv:astro-ph/0607452]. Data suggest that this pair of galaxies may be embedded within a shared neutral-gas envelope that extends from the northeast to the southwest [arXiv:astro-ph/0607452].

sources.summary: The dwarf companion dw0137+1541 is detected in AGES near UGC 1171, where the data suggest the pair may sit in a northeast-southwest neutral-gas envelope.

quotes.quote: The improvement in spatial resolution is also highlighted by the detection of dw0137+1541. This dwarf is so close to UGC 1171 that it is undetected in the HIPASS data but is clearly visible in the AGES data (Fig. fig9 b) at position ( , ) = 01h 40m 03s , +15 56 00 . Fig. fig9 (b) suggests that UGC 1171 dw0137+1541 are embedded in a neutral gas envelope extending from northeast to southwest. This could be an effect of beam smearing since there is no hint of extended emission in Briggs (1986), but it is possible that it is real and has been resolved out by the synthesised beam of the VLA.

gmw_00012419.name: SDSS J013149.81+152326.5
gmw_00012419.overview: SDSS J013149.81+152326.5 is an isolated spiral galaxy [arXiv:astro-ph/0607452]. Its spiral morphology is reflected in its neutral hydrogen profile, and it has a recessional velocity of approximately 10,700 km/s [arXiv:astro-ph/0607452].

sources.summary: The AGES signal coincides with this isolated SDSS spiral galaxy; optical and radio redshifts agree closely, and its spiral morphology is reflected in the H I profile.

quotes.quote: AGES J013149+152353 This signal coincides with the SDSS galaxy, SDSS J013149.81+152326.5. There is an optical redshift available for this galaxy placing it at cz= 10717 . The radio data place the galaxy at a recessional velocity of cz= 10705 . SDSS J013149.81+152326.5 is a spiral galaxy and this morphology is reflected in the profile. This galaxy appears to be somewhat isolated since there are no galaxies reported in this region within 1700 .

gmw_00012420.name: SDSS J013204.6+153001.2
gmw_00012420.overview: SDSS J013204.6+153001.2 is an early-type galaxy with a red optical color [arXiv:astro-ph/0607452]. Its nuclear spectrum displays a red continuum and shows no signs of recent star formation [arXiv:astro-ph/0607452].

sources.summary: The SDSS galaxy lies near the beam center, has matching optical and radio velocities, and appears red and early-type with no recent star-formation signatures.

quotes.quote: AGES J013204+152947 The SDSS galaxy, SDSS J013204.6+153001.2, falls almost in the centre of the Arecibo beam. It has an optically measured redshift of cz= 13195 from the SDSS. Our radio measurement of the recessional velocity is cz= 13196 .

The good agreement between the positional and redshift data make it almost certain that this is the source of the . This galaxy has a red optical colour ( g- 0.75 mag) typically observed in early type galaxies (Bernardi 2003). Moreover the SDSS nuclear spectrum shows a red continuum and no sign of recent star formation (i.e. no emission lines).

gmw_00012421.name: AGES J013538+154850
gmw_00012421.overview: AGES J013538+154850 is a neutral hydrogen source detected in the radio spectrum [arXiv:astro-ph/0607452]. The emission is likely associated with a central spiral galaxy, a classification supported by its typical spiral profile [arXiv:astro-ph/0607452].

sources.summary: The H I source is likely associated with a central spiral galaxy whose profile supports that interpretation, though optical redshift data are needed for confirmation.

quotes.quote: AGES J013538+154850 The position is likely to be coming from the spiral located near the centre of the image. This is supported by the profile which is a typical spiral, but only optical redshift data would really tie down the emission to this galaxy.

gmw_00012422.name: AGES J013718+153635
gmw_00012422.overview: AGES J013718+153635 is an object with a neutral hydrogen profile typical of a spiral galaxy [arXiv:astro-ph/0607452]. Identifying its single optical counterpart is difficult because it lies in a densely populated field containing at least five galaxies [arXiv:astro-ph/0607452].

sources.summary: The detection has a spiral-like H I profile but an uncertain optical counterpart in a crowded frame, with possible associations to nearby galaxies or a continuum source.

quotes.quote: AGES J013718+153635 The profile for this object is typical of a spiral galaxy. Tying it down to a single galaxy is difficult though.

There is a continuum source (NVSS J013714+153722) located at a distance of 1.2 . This frame is rather densely populated with at least five galaxies present, two of which fall within the Arecibo beam. Neither of these sources have any redshift information listed in NED for comparison. The continuum source could be associated with the smudge at 01 37 17.7 , 15 38 00 and this just falls within the edge of the beam. The signal could also be coming from the object at 01 37 17.0 , 15 39 50 .

gmw_00012423.name: AGES J013807+154328
gmw_00012423.overview: AGES J013807+154328 is a neutral hydrogen detection with an estimated mass of 4.5 billion solar masses [arXiv:astro-ph/0607452]. Its profile and a hint of optical structure suggest that the galaxy is a spiral [arXiv:astro-ph/0607452].

sources.summary: The paper describes this H I detection as a likely spiral galaxy of about 4.5 x 10^9 solar masses, with a probable uncatalogued optical counterpart.

quotes.quote: AGES J013807+154328 The profile of this galaxy suggests a spiral galaxy of mass 4.5 10^ 9 M _ . The optical counterpart is probably the object located near the centre of the POSS II frame (Fig. fig11 ) but this object is not listed in NED. There is a hint of spiral structure in the optical which does lend support to this being the counterpart. Optical spectroscopy would be needed to help confirm this.

gmw_00012424.name: AGES J013827+154728
gmw_00012424.overview: AGES J013827+154728 is a large spiral galaxy with a double-horn H I profile [arXiv:astro-ph/0607452]. Its optical counterpart is well aligned with the radio position and exhibits a clumpy structure [arXiv:astro-ph/0607452].

sources.summary: The detection has a double-horn H I profile indicating a large spiral galaxy and a likely clumpy optical counterpart well aligned with the radio position.

quotes.quote: AGES J013827+154728 While the bright galaxy to the south of the frame in Fig. fig13 is identified by NED as 2MASX J01383039+1545455, the object in the centre of the frame is not listed at all. The double horn profile indicates a large, spiral galaxy. The optical image, however, shows a galaxy with a rather clumpy structure, although it may be exaggerated by the presence of foreground stars. Without optical redshift data one can't say for certain that this galaxy is the source of the emission. However the correlation between the positions of the radio and optical data is very good.

gmw_00012425.name: AGES J013917+154613
gmw_00012425.overview: AGES J013917+154613 is a faint galaxy located at a distance of approximately 230 Mpc [arXiv:astro-ph/0607452]. Despite its dim optical appearance, it contains a substantial neutral gas mass of 3.3 billion solar masses, suggesting it is a Milky Way-sized system [arXiv:astro-ph/0607452].

sources.summary: The paper identifies a faint galaxy with substantial neutral gas, arguing it is probably a distant Milky-Way-sized system despite its dim optical appearance.

quotes.quote: AGES J013917+154613 There is a faint galaxy that can been seen nestling amongst the cluster of stars just south of the centre of the image. Although quite dim compared with the surrounding stars, the galaxy's neutral gas content (3.3 10^ 9 M _ ) suggests we are looking at a Milky Way sized galaxy that is merely far away ( 230 Mpc). This is unusual since this galaxy also has approximately the same mass as object 2 and is at the same distance, yet nothing as obvious as 2MASX J01463012+1543250 lies within the Arecibo beam.

gmw_00012426.name: AGES J013953+151955
gmw_00012426.overview: AGES J013953+151955 is an H I source with a narrow, fairly Gaussian spectrum [arXiv:astro-ph/0607452]. It is likely associated with an edge-on SDSS galaxy, although its narrow profile makes this counterpart identification somewhat uncertain [arXiv:astro-ph/0607452].

sources.summary: This H I source is probably an SDSS edge-on galaxy with a comparable optical redshift, but its narrow Gaussian profile leaves the counterpart identification uncertain.

quotes.quote: AGES J013953+151955 This galaxy is probably the edge-on galaxy located in Fig. fig11 at 01 39 55.0 , 15 20 34 . This galaxy has also been identified by SDSS with an optical recessional velocity of cz= 16693 . Our redshift of cz =16806 compares reasonably well with this.

The shape of the spectrum is fairly Gaussian and has a narrow width (W _ 50 = 49 ). It would be highly unusual for a galaxy as massive as this one (5.1 10^ 9 M _ ) to have such a narrow profile unless we were viewing it face-on. SDSS J013955.02+152036.7 looks more like an edge-on galaxy, so either some of the flux is hidden in the noise or this galaxy is not responsible for the emission.

gmw_00012427.name: AGES J013956+153135
gmw_00012427.overview: AGES J013956+153135 is a neutral hydrogen detection with an estimated mass of 5 billion solar masses [arXiv:astro-ph/0607452]. It has no obvious optical counterpart on shallow imaging, suggesting it may be a low-surface-brightness galaxy [arXiv:astro-ph/0607452].

sources.summary: This interesting H I detection has no obvious optical counterpart; its mass suggests a substantial galaxy, possibly with a low-surface-brightness counterpart requiring deep follow-up.

quotes.quote: AGES J013956+153135 The signal is about a 5- detection with a peak of around 5.5 mJy and W _ 50 =105 . The optical counterpart is a little more tricky. There is a small smudge in Fig. fig10 (object 4) at 01 39 55.0 , 15 30 22 .

However, the mass is 5 10^ 9 H _ , which is similar to object 2. Therefore we might expect to see a galaxy of a similar size to 2MASX J01463012+1543250. This is a very interesting detection. There are no obvious optical counterparts but the POSS II image is relatively shallow so it is possible this detection has a low surface brightness optical counterpart. Deep optical follow-up observations are essential to try and determine whether this object has an optical association.

gmw_00012428.name: AGES J014013+153319
gmw_00012428.overview: AGES J014013+153319 is a source where two candidate galaxies have been identified within the beam [arXiv:astro-ph/0607452]. Its profile shows an excess of neutral gas in the approaching half of the spectrum, which may indicate a superposition of profiles or an asymmetric gas distribution [arXiv:astro-ph/0607452].

sources.summary: The detection may blend emission from two candidate galaxies or reflect an asymmetric gas distribution in one galaxy, as suggested by excess neutral gas in the profile.

quotes.quote: AGES J014013+153319 Two objects have been identified within the beam that could be contributing to the emission. The profile shows an excess of neutral gas in the approaching half of the spectrum. This could be due to superposition of individual profiles or due to asymmetry in the gas distribution of one of the galaxies, if these galaxies are indeed responsible for the emission.

gmw_00012429.name: AGES J014025+151903
gmw_00012429.overview: AGES J014025+151903 is a galaxy detected via a narrow neutral hydrogen emission line [arXiv:astro-ph/0607452]. The source 2MASX J01402402+1518240 lies within its radio beam and appears as a faint smudge in optical imaging [arXiv:astro-ph/0607452].

sources.summary: The narrow but significant H I detection has a possible 2MASX optical source in the beam, while follow-up spectroscopy and interferometry are needed to test the association.

quotes.quote: AGES J014025+151903 This object has a narrow velocity width (W _ 50 = 44 ) but is a 6- detection. 2 objects exist in NED in this area, but one of them is a distant quasar (SDSS J014026.6+151813.5). 2MASX J01402402+1518240 falls within the Arecibo beam and shows up in the POSS II image as a faint smudge (Fig. fig11 ). Follow-up optical spectroscopy and radio interferometry would enable us to determine if 2MASX J01402402+1518240 is the source of the emission.

gmw_00012430.name: AGES J014033+160513
gmw_00012430.overview: AGES J014033+160513 is an astronomical object associated with a neutral hydrogen detection [arXiv:astro-ph/0607452]. Its optical counterpart resembles a spiral galaxy, but closer inspection suggests a potential merger of multiple objects connected by a tidal stream [arXiv:astro-ph/0607452].

sources.summary: The optical candidate near this H I detection appears spiral-like but may resolve into multiple objects connected by a tidal stream, suggesting a possible merger.

quotes.quote: AGES J014033+160513 The POSS II plate presents a particularly interesting object, 2MASXi J0140360+160414, which lies just on the edge of the beam. At first glance the optical image looks like a spiral galaxy with a spiral arm extending to the south. A closer inspection of the POSS II images has revealed that the central part of the galaxy can almost be resolved into two objects and the trailing spiral arm looks like a tidal stream linking a third object to the south. It looks as if there is an interesting merger going on here. Further investigation would be required to ascertain the nature of this merger, and to determine whether or not these objects are responsible for the emission.

gmw_00012431.name: AGES J01430+161502
gmw_00012431.overview: AGES J01430+161502 is a source with an unmistakable neutral hydrogen signal [arXiv:astro-ph/0607452]. It features an elongated central optical structure that points toward the galaxy 2MASX J01443758+1615501, which may indicate an ongoing interaction between them [arXiv:astro-ph/0607452].

sources.summary: The optical source of this strong H I signal is uncertain; the central smudge points toward a 2MASX galaxy, possibly indicating an interaction.

quotes.quote: AGES J01430+161502 The signal is unmistakable but locating the optical source is not as straightforward. 2MASX J01443758+1615501 is present in the optical frame in Fig. fig15 but its position would place it at the very edge of the beam.
