The position is more likely linked to the smudge nearer the centre of the beam at 01 44 33.5 , 16 14 48 . Its elongated structure is pointing towards 2MASX J01443758+1615501 which may be the result of an interaction with this galaxy.

gmw_00012432.name: AGES 014630+154332
gmw_00012432.overview: AGES 014630+154332 is a galaxy with a double-horned neutral hydrogen profile that suggests it is a spiral galaxy [arXiv:astro-ph/0607452]. However, its optical counterpart is highly circular, which instead suggests a face-on orientation or a very bright central bulge [arXiv:astro-ph/0607452].

sources.summary: The double-horned H I profile suggests a spiral galaxy, but the circular optical candidate looks face-on and does not match the expected profile shape.

quotes.quote: AGES 014630+154332 The profile is double-horned with a width of 132 , suggesting this object is a spiral galaxy but the galaxy located at the centre of optical image does not reflect this. It is a very circular object, so if this object is the optical counterpart to the it must have a very bright bulge or very diffuse arms. It's circular nature also suggests we are looking at the galaxy face-on, but if this was the case the profile would look more Gaussian.

gmw_00012433.name: AGES J014644+155622
gmw_00012433.overview: AGES J014644+155622 is a galaxy detected via a very narrow, persistent H I signal [arXiv:astro-ph/0607452]. Its optical counterpart remains unresolved, with two nearby candidate objects located on the edge of the beam [arXiv:astro-ph/0607452].

sources.summary: This narrow, persistent H I signal is likely real, but two possible optical objects near the beam edge leave the counterpart identification unresolved.

quotes.quote: AGES J014644+155622 Although the signal of this object is very narrow (26 ) it persists over several channels suggesting that it is indeed real even though the peak is only a 4- detection. There are two objects visible on the optical image (Fig. fig13 ) that are on the edge of the beam. The first object, 2MASX J01465179+1557192, is very bright and large although it is 2.1 away from the centre point of the emission. The second object is only visible as a faint smudge in the B band and R band images but is closer in separation ( 1.5 ). The second object is not listed in NED.

gmw_00012434.name: AGES J014719+155603
gmw_00012434.overview: AGES J014719+155603 is an astronomical source with only one possible optical counterpart visible in its optical frame [arXiv:astro-ph/0607452]. The radio source NVSS J014722+155737 is located less than 30 arcseconds away from this optical detection and is the likely origin of the radio emission [arXiv:astro-ph/0607452].

sources.summary: The field has one possible optical counterpart and a likely NVSS radio source, but the positional mismatch means spectroscopy is needed to identify the H I source.

quotes.quote: AGES J014719+155603 The optical frame shows only one possible optical counterpart. The object is located at 01 47 21.5 , 15 57 00 . NVSS J014722+155737 is listed in NED less than 30 away from the optical detection and is probably the source of the radio emission. Whether or not it is the source of the emission is difficult to say. The positions do not match that well but optical spectra would help make this distinction.

gmw_00012435.name: AGES J014742+161317
gmw_00012435.overview: AGES J014742+161317 is a galaxy characterized by a fairly Gaussian neutral hydrogen profile [arXiv:astro-ph/0607452]. This profile exhibits a velocity width of 138 kilometers per second, and optical imaging reveals two possible counterparts for the emission source [arXiv:astro-ph/0607452].

sources.summary: The H I profile is fairly Gaussian and two possible optical counterparts are visible, with further investigation needed to determine the emission source.

quotes.quote: AGES J014742+161317 The profile is fairly Gaussian with a velocity width of W _ 50 = 138 . The optical image shows two possible counterparts at 01 47 39.0 , 16 13 56 (most likely) and 01 47 46.0 , 16 13 18 (2MASXi 0147459+161318). Further investigation will reveal the true source of the emission.

gmw_00012436.name: AGES J014834+152756
gmw_00012436.overview: AGES J014834+152756 is a spiral galaxy that exhibits a double-horned velocity profile [arXiv:astro-ph/0607452]. Optical data suggest it may be a perturbed, one-armed spiral, making it a candidate for higher-resolution radio observations to search for associated tidal debris [arXiv:astro-ph/0607452].

sources.summary: The optical image and double-horned profile indicate a one-armed spiral galaxy, motivating higher-resolution radio data to search for tidal debris.

quotes.quote: AGES J014834+152756 The double-horned velocity profile and the optical image both indicate the this galaxy is a spiral. On closer inspection of the optical data it seems that the galaxy may be a one-armed spiral. Again this object is not listed in NED. It would be interesting to acquire higher resolution radio data of this object to see if there is tidal debris linked to this perturbed spiral.

gmw_00012437.name: SDSSJ112518.89-014532.4
gmw_00012437.overview: SDSSJ112518.89-014532.4 is an extreme post-starburst galaxy with a very young mean stellar age of 30 Myr [arXiv:1309.2948]. Hubble Space Telescope imaging reveals a complex structure combining compact and extended components, alongside unmodeled spiral features [arXiv:1309.2948].

sources.summary: This compact galaxy is presented as an extremely young post-starburst system with weak [O II], high velocity dispersion, bulge-dominated morphology, and complex HST structure.

quotes.quote: In one case (SDSSJ112518.89-014532.4, Table tab1 and Figure fig1 ) we identify an ``extreme post-starburst galaxy'' which we apparently observe immediately after the cessation of a strong and short star formation episode: this object has no prominent emission lines. However, the mean stellar age is 30 Myr, close to the lowest limit covered by our SSP models. This object is considerably younger than typical E+A galaxies with luminosity weighted ages of 500--800 Myr CdRB09,Du+10 .

SDSSJ112518.89-014532.4 is a peculiar young object with only a weak [ O II ] line (section sec:spectralfit ) observed with the HST/WFC3 program targeting recently quenched galaxies with high-velocity gas outflows (Proposal ID 12272: C.Tremonti). The best-fit model surface brightness profile combines two functions: a compact and an extended S ersic profile. The largest half-light radius exceeds the one for the best-fit single S ersic model by a factor of nine (R _ e, ^ multi =6.54 kpc vs. R _ e, ^ single =0.74 kpc, Table tab2 ). The residual map in Fig. fig1 shows spiral structure that we do not try to model.

gmw_00012438.name: SDSSJ123657.44+631115.4
gmw_00012438.overview: SDSSJ123657.44+631115.4 is an old, compact galaxy located within an X-ray luminous galaxy cluster [arXiv:1309.2948]. Multi-band Hubble Space Telescope imaging reveals that its light profile is well-fit by a two-component model with consistent structural parameters across different filters [arXiv:1309.2948].

sources.summary: This old compact galaxy lies in an X-ray luminous cluster; multi-band HST imaging shows similar Sersic parameters and a satisfactory two-component surface-brightness fit.

quotes.quote: SDSSJ123657.44+631115.4 resides in one of the X-ray luminous clusters targeted by multi-wavelength HST ACS/WFC3 snapshot survey (Proposal ID 12166: H. Ebeling). The abundant HST imaging allows construction of the best-fit 2D models in three filters (F606W, F814W and F110W). The light profiles in all three bands have very similar S ersic parameters (R _ e, ^ multi 3 kpc, R _ e, ^ single 1.7 kpc). We obtain a good fit with only two S ersic profiles (Table tab2 ).

gmw_00012439.name: 3C 215
gmw_00012439.overview: 3C 215 is a lobe-dominated quasar [arXiv:astro-ph/9907034]. It resides in a rich cluster environment and exhibits a distorted radio structure, likely influenced by hot cluster gas [arXiv:astro-ph/9907034].

sources.summary: The lobe-dominated quasar 3C 215 has a significant extended X-ray component, likely hot cluster gas associated with its rich environment and distorted radio source.

quotes.quote: 3C 215 This object is a well-studied lobe-dominated quasar. Both the co-added HRI image used in our analysis and, to a lesser extent, the individual datasets from which we generated it show elongation of the X-rays in a NNW-SSE direction; the weak nearby point sources in the field also show elongation roughly in this direction, so we attribute the elongation to aspect-smearing problems. The long observation contains many different OBIs and satellite roll angles, and to apply dewobbling it was necessary to drop some of the shorter intervals with insufficient counts for good centroiding, leaving 65 ks, or 75 per cent of the available data. The restacked image is indeed narrower and without significant elongation. Radial profile fits, using a PRF based on the inner regions of the source, show the dewobbled source to have a significant extended component (Table profile ).

Galaxy counts around 3C 215 also suggest a rich cluster environment (Ellingson, Yee Green 1991) and some extended line emission is seen (Crawford Fabian 1989) so it seems likely that we are seeing emission from hot cluster gas, perhaps influencing the distorted structure of the radio source (Bridle \ 1994). The core radius corresponds to 140 kpc and the cooling time of the gas will be less than 10^ 10 years for kT 4 keV.

gmw_00012440.name: 3C 263.1
gmw_00012440.overview: 3C 263.1 is an FRII radio galaxy with a compact nuclear structure [arXiv:astro-ph/9907034]. It is surprisingly bright in X-rays relative to the weak flux of its radio core [arXiv:astro-ph/9907034].

sources.summary: The FRII radio galaxy 3C 263.1 is detected close to its optical position, appears compact, and is surprisingly X-ray bright relative to its weak radio core.

quotes.quote: 3C 263.1 This FRII radio galaxy was not detected by Crawford Fabian (1996a) in a far-off-axis PSPC observation. However, an HRI observation detects a source within 6 arcsec of the optical position of 3C 263.1, with a count rate consistent with the upper limit of Crawford Fabian. There is no obvious background source on the optical plates of Laing \ (1978) that might be responsible for the X-ray emission. The HRI emission is compact and adequately fitted by a broadened PRF. The source is surprisingly bright if it is due only to nuclear X-ray emission, given the weak flux of the radio core (Liu, Pooley Riley 1992).

gmw_00012441.name: 3C 288
gmw_00012441.overview: The radio galaxy 3C 288 is the optically dominant member of a galaxy cluster [arXiv:astro-ph/9907034]. It exhibits a distorted double radio structure with strong jets and weak hotspots, which is likely influenced by interactions with the surrounding cluster gas [arXiv:astro-ph/9907034].

sources.summary: The radio galaxy 3C 288 is at least partly extended in X-rays, optically dominant in a cluster, and has unusual radio structure likely influenced by cluster gas.

quotes.quote: 3C 288 Because this radio galaxy is 21 arcmin off-axis in the PSPC observation, uncertainties in the PSPC PRF at this radius mean that we have not been able to separate extended and compact emission, although the source is at least partially extended. 3C 288 appears in the optical to be the dominant member of a cluster (Wyndham 1966) and so it is not surprising that extended X-ray structure is seen. Interactions with the cluster gas may be the reason for its unusual structure in the radio (Bridle \ 1989); it is one of a small class of reasonably powerful, distorted double sources with strong jets but weak hotspots, which often have a low-excitation optical spectrum and an association with a cluster [ other good examples are 3C 401 and 3C 438, Hardcastle \ (1997); the class is similar in radio properties to the `jetted doubles' of Law-Green \ (1995) ] . The available X-ray data are unfortunately not good enough to test whether 3C 288 lies in a cooling flow, a suggested origin for this type of source.

gmw_00012442.name: 3C 442A
gmw_00012442.overview: 3C 442A is a peculiar radio galaxy associated with NGC 7237 in the merging galaxy pair NGC 7236/7237 [arXiv:astro-ph/9907034]. It exhibits a possible double X-ray core and a large-scale bar of X-ray emission aligned with the pair's common optical envelope [arXiv:astro-ph/9907034].

sources.summary: 3C 442A is associated with NGC 7237 in a merging pair and shows complex X-ray structure, including a possible double core and large-scale bar-like emission.

quotes.quote: 3C 442A This peculiar radio galaxy (Comins Owen 1991) is associated with NGC 7237 in the merging galaxy pair NGC 7236/7237, which lies in a poor cluster. The small-scale structure in the HRI image seems to suggest a double X-ray core with separation roughly 20 arcsec in an east-west direction. Such structure is unreliable because of aspect problems, but the large extension makes an aspect-related explanation unlikely. Other weak sources nearby are not doubled up in the same way, including one apparently coincident with the small associated galaxy to the SE. No compact source is found associated with NGC 7236.

On larger scales, the map smoothed with a Gaussian of =16 arcsec seems to show a bar of X-ray emission, oriented roughly NNW-SSE (see Fig. \ 442a-overlay ); this lies in approximately the same position angle as the common optical envelope of the galaxy pair (e.g. \ Borne Hoessel 1988) and approximately perpendicular to the position angle of the radio source, contrary to the prediction of Comins Owen (1991) who expected the hot gas to be aligned with the radio source.

gmw_00012443.name: G1
gmw_00012443.overview: G1 is a cluster member galaxy located near the southern giant arc of the galaxy cluster Abell 383 [arXiv:1412.0023]. It has a redshift of 0.191, a measured velocity dispersion of 255 km/s, and a dark matter halo truncation radius of 50.5 kpc [arXiv:1412.0023].

sources.summary: The paper identifies G1 as a cluster member near A383's southern giant arc, using velocity dispersion and surface-brightness reconstruction to constrain its halo size, core radius, and truncation radius.

quotes.quote: These galaxies have measured _ sp , which, combined with the strong lensing constraints from their nearby arcs, allow to directly measure their halo sizes. We call these two galaxies G1 and G2 (see Fig.  fig:a383rgb ). Their redshifts and measured velocity dispersions are z_ G1 =0.191 , z_ G2 =0.195 and _ G1 =255 13 km/s, _ G2 =202 15 km/s, respectively (see Tab. tab:vel_gal ).

G1 gets a velocity dispersion of =239 2 km/s, which is consistent with previous results within the 1 uncertainties. The core radius is 1.3 0.1 kpc and the truncation radius is 50.5^ +3.6 _ 4.6 kpc. For G2 we get =186^ +4 _ 7 km/s, r_ =0.3_ 0.2 ^ +0.3 kpc (consistent with zero) and r_ tr =68.8_ 10.9 ^ +10.0 kpc. In Fig.  fig:scaling_law we plot the results for G1 and G2 to compare them with the prediction from the scaling relations obtained from the pointlike modelling. In the previous models these two galaxies get truncation radii which are several times larger than the predictions from the respective luminosity scaling laws in Eq.  eq:scal_law_nosigma and eq:scal_law_wsigma .

gmw_00012444.name: G2
gmw_00012444.overview: G2 is a galaxy located near the southern giant arc of the Abell 383 cluster [arXiv:1412.0023]. It has a redshift of 0.195, a measured velocity dispersion of 202 km/s, and a dark matter halo truncation radius of approximately 68.8 kpc [arXiv:1412.0023].

sources.summary: The paper identifies G2 as a cluster member near A383's southern giant arc, using velocity dispersion and surface-brightness reconstruction to constrain its halo size and truncation radius.

quotes.quote: These galaxies have measured _ sp , which, combined with the strong lensing constraints from their nearby arcs, allow to directly measure their halo sizes. We call these two galaxies G1 and G2 (see Fig.  fig:a383rgb ). Their redshifts and measured velocity dispersions are z_ G1 =0.191 , z_ G2 =0.195 and _ G1 =255 13 km/s, _ G2 =202 15 km/s, respectively (see Tab. tab:vel_gal ).

For G2 we get =186^ +4 _ 7 km/s, r_ =0.3_ 0.2 ^ +0.3 kpc (consistent with zero) and r_ tr =68.8_ 10.9 ^ +10.0 kpc. In Fig.  fig:scaling_law we plot the results for G1 and G2 to compare them with the prediction from the scaling relations obtained from the pointlike modelling. In the previous models these two galaxies get truncation radii which are several times larger than the predictions from the respective luminosity scaling laws in Eq.  eq:scal_law_nosigma and eq:scal_law_wsigma .

gmw_00012445.name: GR
gmw_00012445.overview: GR is a galaxy located within the Abell 383 cluster [arXiv:1412.0023]. It has a measured velocity dispersion of 238 +/− 15 km/s and a predicted truncation radius of approximately 13.2 kpc [arXiv:1412.0023].

sources.summary: The paper treats GR as the reference galaxy for A383 scaling relations, optimizing its velocity dispersion and truncation radius and comparing predicted radii between lensing models with measured dispersions.

quotes.quote: In the first model we scale all the galaxies with respect to GR using Eq.  eq:F_J and the left side of Eq.  eq:r_tr . We individually optimise only the BCG, the reference galaxy GR and the two galaxies G1 and G2 close to the lensed system 3-4. Their velocity dispersions and truncation radii are optimised with flat priors in the range of [ 100,500 ] km/s and [ 1,100 ] kpc.

The predicted velocity dispersion and radii for GR, G1 and G2 are _ GR =214_ 32 ^ +40 km/s, _ G1 =253 23 km/s, _ G2 =194_ 45 ^ +54 km/s and r_ tr,GR =23.1_ 12.8 ^ +29.4 kpc, r_ tr,G1 =47.8 20.9 kpc, r_ tr,G2 =32.2_ 23.4 ^ +31.5 kpc. The total mass of the cluster within the Einstein radius _E = 11.6_ 1.9 ^ +2.3 arcsec is M_ tot =9.72 0.23 10^ 12 M_ .

The measured velocity dispersions of GR, BCG, G1 and G2 are optimised within their uncertainties. The final values for these parameters are _ GR =238 15 km/s, _ BCG =379 21 km/s, _ G1 =252 14 km/s and _ G2 =201 20 km/s. The predicted radii for GR, BCG, G1 and G2 are r_ tr,GR =13.2_ 4.3 ^ +6.2 kpc, r_ tr,BCG =58.4_ 33.2 ^ +24.9 kpc, r_ tr,G1 =73.1_ 35.5 ^ +38.7 kpc, r_ tr,G2 =53.2_ 36.3 ^ +49.2 kpc.

gmw_00012446.name: SPT0532-50
gmw_00012446.overview: SPT0532-50 is a gravitationally lensed galaxy located at a redshift of z = 3.399 [arXiv:1906.05469]. Observations of the system have detected both water line emission and carbon monoxide CO (6-5) transitions [arXiv:1906.05469].

sources.summary: The paper observes SPT0532-50's water line with ALMA, treats it as one of three similar lensed SPT targets, uses ALMA lensing and CO data, and observes it in Cycles 3 and 4.

quotes.quote: We observed the \ 987.927 GHz line in SPT0529-54 ( z = 3.369), SPT0532-50 ( z = 3.399) and SPT0538-50 ( z = 2.782) with ALMA.

Sample Selection The three SPT targets were selected such that they are at a similar redshift and within 10 ^ of each other on the sky. This selection was chosen to observe the same line transition in the three galaxies and to make observations efficient for resolved ALMA Band 9 continuum observations, which where A-rated in Cycle 5, but not yet observed. All three sources have ALMA 870 m imaging and lens models spilker16 . SPT0538-50 is a possible ongoing major merger as seen from dust continuum models bothwell13 and has resolved CO (1-0) and CO (3-2) ATCA observations aravena13,spilker15 . SPT0529-54 and SPT0532-50 have resolved CO (6-5) observations from ALMA dong19 which we make use of in this work.
Data Reduction and Imaging For SPT0532-50 and SPT0538-50, the observations are taken with ALMA in Cycle 3 (2015.1.01578.S) and Cycle 4 (2016.1.01554.S).

gmw_00012447.name: VLA J123644+621133
gmw_00012447.overview: VLA J123644+621133 is a very red elliptical galaxy located at a redshift of z=1.050 [arXiv:1301.1253]. It is classified as a Fanaroff-Riley class I radio galaxy that hosts an active galactic nucleus and displays a compact radio core [arXiv:1301.1253].

sources.summary: The source is identified as a very red z=1.050 elliptical FR-I radio galaxy with AGN evidence; earlier EVN data showed a compact core and adjacent component.

quotes.quote: VLA J123644+621133 is optically identified with an I=20.9 , very red elliptical galaxy at z=1.050 cohen00 . Its large-scale radio structure and luminosity indicate a classical FR-I radio galaxy R98, muxlow05 . The AGN nature also follows from its negative q_ 24 upper limit. The previous EVN 1.6 GHz observations garrett01 revealed a compact core component and an additional 5 component located 60mas south of the core component, of which the direction coincides with the extension of the VLA-only R98 and MERLIN-VLA radio morphology muxlow05 .
The WSRT and more recent M10 VLA flux densities indicate substantial resolution effects. Our high resolution VLBI observations merely detect the core.

gmw_00012448.name: VLA J123646+621404
gmw_00012448.overview: VLA J123646+621404 is a nearly face-on spiral galaxy located at a redshift of 0.961 [arXiv:1301.1253]. It hosts an active galactic nucleus characterized by broad emission lines, a compact two-sided radio structure, and radio variability [arXiv:1301.1253].

sources.summary: The paper associates VLA J123646+621404 with a nearly face-on z=0.961 spiral galaxy, compact two-sided radio structure, variability, radio excess, and broad-line AGN evidence.

quotes.quote: VLA J123646+621404 has a slightly inverted radio spectrum ( = -0.04 ) and is associated with a nearly face-on spiral galaxy ( I=20.7 ), at a redshift of 0.961 cowie04 . The MERLIN-VLA image shows a compact component and two-sided extended emission, and EVN 1.6 GHz observations detect a 4 radio source at this position garrett01,muxlow05 . Its radio variability is evident from R98 , from the present data, and from recent VLA data M10 . Those VLA data in fact imply q_ 24 = -0.18 . Taken together with the broad emission line character phillips97, brandt01 , there is no doubt about the presence of an AGN.

gmw_00012449.name: VLA J123652+621444
gmw_00012449.overview: VLA J123652+621444 is an inverted-spectrum radio source located in an elliptical galaxy at a redshift of z=0.321 [arXiv:1301.1253]. It hosts an active galactic nucleus with a compact radio core and a one-sided jet-like structure [arXiv:1301.1253].

sources.summary: The source is described as an inverted-spectrum, variable radio object with AGN indicators, an elliptical host at z=0.321, a compact core, and a one-sided jet-like feature.

quotes.quote: VLA J123652+621444 is an inverted spectrum radio source ( =-0.12 ) located in an adjacent HFF and observed to vary in intensity on the timescale of months, suggesting the presence of an AGN R98,R00,M10 . The object displays radio excess, as judged from its q_ 24 value. MERLIN-VLA observations reveal a compact core, which overlies the nucleus of an I=18.5 elliptical galaxy at z=0.321 , and a one-sided jet-like feature to the east cowie04,muxlow05 . The high resolution VLBI observations merely detect the 0.08 mJy radio core.

gmw_00012450.name: VLA J123608+621035
gmw_00012450.overview: VLA J123608+621035 is a flat-spectrum radio source hosted by an I=20.6 galaxy at a redshift of z=0.681 [arXiv:1301.1253]. The host galaxy is bright in the infrared, follows the radio-far-infrared correlation, and contains a slightly extended compact radio core [arXiv:1301.1253].

sources.summary: The paper describes VLA J123608+621035 as a flat-spectrum radio source hosted by a z=0.681 galaxy, infrared-bright, radio-FIR correlated, and containing a slightly extended VLBI core.

quotes.quote: VLA J123608+621035 is a relatively flat-spectrum radio source ( =0.36 ) hosted by an I=20.6 galaxy at z=0.681 R00,cowie04 . The object is bright in the infrared, and falls on the radio-FIR correlation. Its compact radio core is straddled by two-sided radio emission extending over 1 muxlow05 ; our VLBI detects a slightly extended 0.140mJy radio core.

gmw_00012451.name: VLA J123642+621545
gmw_00012451.overview: VLA J123642+621545 is an infrared-luminous galaxy located at a redshift of 0.857 [arXiv:1301.1253]. It contains a compact, variable radio core and exhibits a definite active galactic nucleus contribution [arXiv:1301.1253].

sources.summary: The paper describes VLA J123642+621545 as a z=0.857 infrared-luminous galaxy detected by ISO and Chandra, with a compact variable VLBI component and definite AGN contribution.

quotes.quote: VLA J123642+621545 is an intermediate radio spectrum source ( =0.50 ), associated with an I=20.7 galaxy at a redshift of 0.857 R00,hornschemeier01 . The source was also detected by ISO and Chandra . muxlow05 suggest that the compact component could be an AGN, but hornschemeier01 interpreted the X-ray's as originating from a starburst system. The VLBI observations uncover a compact component of variable flux density in this infrared-luminous object. The object falls on the radio-infrared correlation using the low-state VLA flux density, but definitely also contains an AGN.

gmw_00012452.name: VLA J123700+620909
gmw_00012452.overview: VLA J123700+620909 is an optically faint starburst galaxy located at a photometric redshift of approximately 1.68 [arXiv:1301.1253]. It exhibits a steep radio spectrum and contains a compact VLBI radio core with one-sided emission extending to the northeast [arXiv:1301.1253].

sources.summary: The paper describes VLA J123700+620909, also GN 16, as an optically faint steep-spectrum source considered a z≈1.68 starburst galaxy with a compact VLBI radio core.

quotes.quote: VLA J123700+620909 represents similar properties to VLA J123642+621331. It has relatively strong radio emission and a steep radio spectrum ( =0.89 ). There is no optical counterpart to R = 27 R98 . The MERLIN-VLA observations detected the source, finding a compact radio component and one-sided emission to the north-east. At the same time, deep Spitzer IRAC and MIPS imaging as well as SCUBA 850 m observations pope06 also detected this source, GN 16.

Given its ERO-character as well as the results of the multi-wavelength studies in sub-mm, IR, and radio, this source is considered to be a starburst galaxy (SFR 1000 M_ /yr ), at z_ photom. = 1.68 pope05,pope06 . The VLBI observations find a 0.15 mJy compact radio core in this mild radio-excess, q_ 24 = -0.08 object.

gmw_00012453.name: VLA J123714+620823
gmw_00012453.overview: VLA J123714+620823 is associated with a faint, red optical object [arXiv:1301.1253] that has a tentative redshift of z=0.847 [arXiv:1301.1253]. The source exhibits a moderate radio excess [arXiv:1301.1253] and displays extended radio emission on both arcsecond and subarcsecond scales [arXiv:1301.1253].

sources.summary: The source is associated with a faint red object at tentative z=0.847, has moderate radio excess and extended VLBI/VLA emission, while a nearby 8.4 GHz source remains ambiguous.

quotes.quote: VLA J123714+620823 is associated with a faint, R=24.25 capak04 red object. Cowie (2010, priv. communication) provided a tentative redshift z=0.847 . With a total 1.4 GHz flux density of well over a mJy, the object has a moderate radio excess, as noted also by donley05 . The VLBI radio source is somewhat extended, and the WSRT and VLA measurements indicate extended emission on the arcsec and subarcsec scale. The 21 Jy 8.4 GHz VLA source J123714+620822 R98 is 0.3 arcsec away and its relation to J123714+620823 is unclear.

gmw_00012454.name: VLA J123716+621512
gmw_00012454.overview: VLA J123716+621512 is an early-type galaxy [arXiv:1207.2157] located at a redshift of z=0.561 [arXiv:1301.1253]. It hosts a compact VLBI radio core [arXiv:1301.1253], exhibits a flat radio spectrum with one-sided emission [arXiv:1301.1253], and shows evidence of radio variability [arXiv:1301.1253].

sources.summary: The paper describes VLA J123716+621512 as a flat-spectrum source hosted by a z=0.561 galaxy, with compact-plus-one-sided radio structure, a compact VLBI core, and variability.

quotes.quote: VLA J123716+621512 has a relatively flat radio spectrum ( =0.41 ) R00 . The radio structure consists of a compact component and one-sided emission extending 0.4 arcsec to the south-west muxlow05 ; the radio structure is hosted by an I=19.8 galaxy at z=0.561 (Cowie 2012, priv.comm.). The VLBI observations detect a 0.1 mJy compact radio core in this q_ 24 = -0.06 object. Recent VLA observations M10 indicate radio variability.

sources.summary: The paper notes that 123716.3+621512 was absent from the GOODS catalog, classifies it from HST imaging as an early-type galaxy, and excludes it from the analysis.

quotes.quote: The remaining galaxy, 123716.3+621512, was not present in the GOODS catalog. We constructed the HST image cut-out as described above and visually classified the source as an early-type galaxy. Correspondingly, we did not consider it further in our analyses.

gmw_00012455.name: VLA J123721+621129
gmw_00012455.overview: VLA J123721+621129 is a radio source at a redshift of z=1.604 [arXiv:1301.1253]. It is associated with a very red object that lacks an optical counterpart and is dominated by a compact, slightly extended radio component [arXiv:1301.1253].

sources.summary: The paper presents VLA J123721+621129 as an optically undetected inverted-spectrum source associated with a very red z=1.604 object and dominated by a compact radio component.

quotes.quote: VLA J123721+621129 has no optical counterpart to I = 25 and shows an inverted radio spectrum ( = -0.28 ) with a dominant compact radio component and one-sided emission to the north muxlow05 . Using deep imaging data from barger99 , richards99 report that this radio source is associated with a very red object having I-K > 5.2 . barger08 list z=1.604 . The compact radio component is slightly extended at 25 mas resolution, and must be responsible for most if not all of the radio emission in this moderate radio-excess ( q_ 24 = -0.26 ) object.

gmw_00012456.name: NGC 2784
gmw_00012456.overview: NGC 2784 is a nearby, fast-rotating early-type galaxy [arXiv:1902.10175]. Dynamical modeling of the galaxy indicates that it hosts a central supermassive black hole with a mass of approximately 1.0 × 10^8 solar masses [arXiv:1902.10175].

sources.summary: The paper treats NGC 2784 as one of six nearby fast-rotating early-type galaxies with new SINFONI-based dynamical modeling and reports a Schwarzschild black-hole mass of (1.0 0.6) 10^8 M_.

quotes.quote: We add six new black hole mass (M _ BH ) measurements of nearby fast rotating early-type galaxies to the known black hole mass sample, namely NGC 584, NGC 2784, NGC 3640, NGC 4570, NGC 4281 and NGC 7049. Our target galaxies have effective velocity dispersions ( _ e ) between 170 and 245 km s ^ 1 , and thus this work provides additional insight into the black hole properties of intermediate-mass early-type galaxies. We combine high-resolution adaptive-optics SINFONI data with large-scale MUSE, VIMOS and SAURON data from ATLAS ^ 3D to derive two-dimensional stellar kinematics maps. We then build both Jeans Anisotropic Models and axisymmetric Schwarzschild models to measure the central black hole masses. Our Schwarzschild models provide black hole masses of (1.3 0.5) 10^8 M_ for NGC 584, (1.0 0.6) 10^8 M_ for NGC 2784, (7.7 5) 10^7 M_ for NGC 3640, (5.4 0.8) 10^8 M_ for NGC 4281, (6.8 2.0) 10^7 M_ for NGC 4570 and (3.2 0.8) 10^8 M_ for NGC 7049 at 3 confidence level, which are consistent with recent M _ BH _ e scaling relations.

gmw_00012457.name: NGC 3640
gmw_00012457.overview: NGC 3640 is a nearby, fast-rotating early-type galaxy [arXiv:1902.10175]. Axisymmetric Schwarzschild dynamical modeling of the galaxy indicates that it hosts a central supermassive black hole with a mass of approximately 7.7 × 10^7 solar masses [arXiv:1902.10175].

sources.summary: The paper treats NGC 3640 as one of six nearby fast-rotating early-type galaxies with new SINFONI-based dynamical modeling and reports a Schwarzschild black-hole mass of (7.7 5) 10^7 M_.

quotes.quote: We add six new black hole mass (M _ BH ) measurements of nearby fast rotating early-type galaxies to the known black hole mass sample, namely NGC 584, NGC 2784, NGC 3640, NGC 4570, NGC 4281 and NGC 7049. Our target galaxies have effective velocity dispersions ( _ e ) between 170 and 245 km s ^ 1 , and thus this work provides additional insight into the black hole properties of intermediate-mass early-type galaxies. We combine high-resolution adaptive-optics SINFONI data with large-scale MUSE, VIMOS and SAURON data from ATLAS ^ 3D to derive two-dimensional stellar kinematics maps. We then build both Jeans Anisotropic Models and axisymmetric Schwarzschild models to measure the central black hole masses. Our Schwarzschild models provide black hole masses of (1.3 0.5) 10^8 M_ for NGC 584, (1.0 0.6) 10^8 M_ for NGC 2784, (7.7 5) 10^7 M_ for NGC 3640, (5.4 0.8) 10^8 M_ for NGC 4281, (6.8 2.0) 10^7 M_ for NGC 4570 and (3.2 0.8) 10^8 M_ for NGC 7049 at 3 confidence level, which are consistent with recent M _ BH _ e scaling relations.

gmw_00012458.name: MCG+05-10-007
gmw_00012458.overview: MCG+05-10-007 is a massive central elliptical galaxy located at a spectroscopic redshift of approximately 0.01795 [arXiv:2110.09189]. It hosts a recurrent active galactic nucleus that powers two symmetric, 25-kiloparsec-long radio jets and multiple pairs of outer remnant lobes [arXiv:2110.09189].

sources.summary: The paper identifies MCG+05-10-007 as the massive central elliptical in Nest200047 and describes its recurrent AGN activity, compact radio jets, and outer remnant lobes traced by LOFAR.

quotes.quote: At its center lies the massive elliptical galaxy MCG+05-10-007, with spectroscopic redshift z=0.01795 0.00015 (see Supplementary Figure 2).

In particular, the central galaxy shows AGN activity in the form of two nearly-symmetric radio jets about 25-kpc long. These jets have a radio luminosity equal to L_ 144MHz =1.7 10^ 23 W Hz^ 1 (marked as A in Figure 1, top panels) and a spectral index typical for freshly injected plasma ( _ 53MHz ^ 144MHz (A) 0.6 0.18 , S ^ , where S is the flux density and is the frequency, see Figure 3). As shown in Figure 1, top-right panel, in the innermost regions of the jets we observe two symmetric very compact components, suggesting that the AGN is recurrently inflating new bubbles of radio-emitting plasma. This scenario is also supported by the presence of two extra pairs of low surface brightness lobes located beyond the inner jets (marked as B and C in Figure 1, top panels). These can be interpreted as remnant lobes of past AGN-jet outbursts or, as a series of bubbles of plasma, which regularly detach from a continuously operating jet.

gmw_00012459.name: Ursa Minor
gmw_00012459.overview: Ursa Minor is a nearby Local Group dwarf spheroidal galaxy that has been observed by the XMM-Newton archive [arXiv:0805.1054]. Analysis of these observations reveals no diffuse X-ray emission above the level expected from the X-ray background [arXiv:0805.1054].

sources.summary: The paper analyzes Ursa Minor as an XMM-Newton-observed local dwarf spheroidal dark-matter target, finding no significant diffuse X-ray excess after matched blank-field background processing.

quotes.quote: Of the nearby Local Group dwarf spheroidal (dSph) galaxies, three have observations in the XMM-Newton archive: Ursa Minor observed for 52 ks between August and October 2005 (ObsIDs 0301690201, 0301690301, 0301690401, 0301690501), Fornax observed for 104 ks in August 2005 (ObsID 0302500101), and Carina observed for 42 ks in May 2004 (ObsID 0200500201).
All of the XMM observations were taken in Full Frame mode; for Ursa Minor and Carina the thin optical blocking filter was used, while for Fornax the medium filter was used.

Finally, we re-project the appropriate blank-field event files (medium filter for Fornax and thin filter for Ursa Minor and Carina) to match the sky position of the dwarf galaxy observations using the routine skycast. Renormalizing the blank-field count rate in the source region (i.e. within =6^ excluding point source regions) using the ratio of the count rate in the blank-fields to that in the dwarf galaxy observations in an outer region of the detector (where the source flux is expected to be much lower), we find that none of the three dwarfs show significant diffuse X-ray emission above what is expected from the X-ray background.

gmw_00012460.name: A0355 465
gmw_00012460.overview: A0355 465 is a dwarf irregular galaxy with an estimated heliocentric velocity of 1168 km/s [arXiv:astro-ph/0301492]. Based on this velocity, the galaxy is located at an estimated distance of 15.6 Mpc [arXiv:astro-ph/0301492].

sources.summary: The paper estimates A0355 465 velocity, distance, luminosity, and absolute magnitude from emission lines and compares the distance scale with nearby clusters.

quotes.quote: There is currently no velocity listed for this galaxy in the NED database. From the emission lines in the spectrum, the heliocentric velocity was estimated to be 1168 km s ^ 1 . Assuming that A0355 465 is isolated and that the Hubble constant has a value of 75 km s ^ 1 Mpc ^ 1 , the estimated distance is 15.6 Mpc and the distance modulus is 31.0. This distance is comparable to that of the Virgo Cluster and the Fornax Cluster. The estimated absolute magnitude in B is 14.7, which is similar to that of the Local Group dI NGC 6822.

gmw_00012461.name: A1243 335
gmw_00012461.overview: A1243 335 is a dwarf irregular galaxy located in the Centaurus A group [arXiv:astro-ph/0301492]. The galaxy contains three identified H II regions, with the brightest region located in its southeast corner [arXiv:astro-ph/0301492].

sources.summary: The paper describes three H II regions in A1243 335, reports spectroscopy of region A, and derives a lower bright-line oxygen abundance and nitrogen-to-oxygen ratio.

quotes.quote: A1243 335 (ESO381 G020) identified three H II regions in this Cen A group dwarf irregular galaxy. The brightest (labelled A) was found in the southeast corner of the galaxy; H II regions B and C were found in the northwest and southwest part of the galaxy, respectively. They obtained spectroscopy for all three H II regions and detected \ only in H II A.

Our spectrum of H II region A revealed the bright oxygen lines, but no . The derived bright-line oxygen abundance is 12 + log(O/H) = 7.78, which is almost 0.3 dex lower than the value. The adopted nitrogen-to-oxygen abundance ratio is log(N/O) = 1.62 .

gmw_00012462.name: A1334 277
gmw_00012462.overview: A1334 277 is a dwarf irregular galaxy located in the Centaurus A group [arXiv:astro-ph/0301492]. It is a metal-poor galaxy situated approximately 380 kpc away from the spiral galaxy M 83 [arXiv:astro-ph/0301492].

sources.summary: The paper reports the first oxygen abundance for A1334 277, classifies it among the most metal-poor sample galaxies, and places it 380 kpc from M 83.

quotes.quote: A1334 277 (ESO444 G084) The oxygen abundance reported here (12 + log(O/H) = 7.40) is the first ever published for this Cen A group member and is the third most metal-poor galaxy in the present study. While the upper limit to the \ intensity ratio may indicate an upper branch abundance for this Cen A group dwarf, a low metallicity is assumed, because the I ( )/ I ( ) and I ( )/ I ( ) ratios are small, i.e., less than 3 and 2 , respectively. Indeed, bright-line calibrations using I ( )/ I ( ) vanzee98sp,denicolo02 yield lower branch abundances. While this dwarf galaxy is relatively metal-poor, it is not particularly isolated. The TRGB distance of this dI is 4.61 Mpc, which puts this galaxy 380 kpc from the spiral galaxy M 83 kara02b .

gmw_00012463.name: A1346 358
gmw_00012463.overview: A1346 358 is a dwarf irregular galaxy with an oxygen abundance of approximately one-fifth of the solar value [arXiv:astro-ph/0301492]. Its proximity to the giant galaxies Centaurus A and M83 may explain its unusually low neutral hydrogen-to-luminosity ratio [arXiv:astro-ph/0301492].

sources.summary: The paper measures A1346 358 oxygen, nitrogen, and neon abundances from H II-region spectroscopy and suggests nearby giant galaxies may explain its low H I ratio.

quotes.quote: A1346 358 (ESO383 G087) obtained spectroscopy of three H II regions (A, B, and C), but did not detect \ at all. They estimated the oxygen abundance to be 12 + log(O/H) 8.3. In our spectrum of H II region A, \ was detected and the subsequent oxygen abundance is 12 + log(O/H) = 8.19 0.06 , or about one-fifth of the solar value. The nitrogen- and neon-to-oxygen ratios are: log(N/O) = 1.37 0.08 and log(Ne/O) = 0.46 0.05 .

This dwarf lies (in projection) approximately between the elliptical Cen A and the spiral M 83. The proximity to these two giant galaxies may explain why the M_ HI /L_B value for the dwarf is several times lower than typical values for dIs at comparable luminosities (e.g., sdk92 ).

gmw_00012464.name: ESO347 G017
gmw_00012464.overview: ESO347 G017 is a dwarf galaxy located in the Sculptor group [arXiv:astro-ph/0301492]. It has an oxygen abundance of 12 + log(O/H) = 7.84, a nitrogen-to-oxygen ratio of log(N/O) = -1.49, and a neon-to-oxygen ratio of log(Ne/O) = -0.53 [arXiv:astro-ph/0301492].

sources.summary: The paper says ESO347 G017 has little prior literature beyond tabulated and H I properties, and reports oxygen, nitrogen-to-oxygen, and neon-to-oxygen abundances.

quotes.quote: ESO347 G017, ESO348 G009 Aside from the properties listed in Table table_gxylist , and the inclusion of this galaxy in the H I catalog by , there is little in the literature describing further these two dwarf galaxies in the Sculptor group. The adopted (bright-line) oxygen abundances, respectively, are: 12 + log(O/H) = 7.84, and 7.98. The nitrogen-to-oxygen ratios, respectively, are: log(N/O) = 1.49 , and 1.60 . The neon-to-oxygen ratio for ESO347 G017 is log(Ne/O) = 0.53 .

gmw_00012465.name: ESO348 G009
gmw_00012465.overview: ESO348 G009 is a dwarf galaxy located in the Sculptor group [arXiv:astro-ph/0301492]. It has an adopted oxygen abundance of 7.98 and a nitrogen-to-oxygen ratio of 1.60 [arXiv:astro-ph/0301492].

sources.summary: The paper says ESO348 G009 has little prior literature beyond tabulated and H I properties, and reports its adopted oxygen and nitrogen-to-oxygen abundances.

quotes.quote: ESO347 G017, ESO348 G009 Aside from the properties listed in Table table_gxylist , and the inclusion of this galaxy in the H I catalog by , there is little in the literature describing further these two dwarf galaxies in the Sculptor group. The adopted (bright-line) oxygen abundances, respectively, are: 12 + log(O/H) = 7.84, and 7.98. The nitrogen-to-oxygen ratios, respectively, are: log(N/O) = 1.49 , and 1.60 .

gmw_00012466.name: ESO358 G060
gmw_00012466.overview: ESO358 G060 is a dwarf galaxy confirmed as a member of the Fornax Cluster [arXiv:astro-ph/0301492]. It is characterized by a high neutral hydrogen mass-to-light ratio and a particularly low oxygen abundance, making it a highly metal-poor dwarf [arXiv:astro-ph/0301492].

sources.summary: The paper identifies ESO358 G060 as a Fornax Cluster dwarf with the sample largest H I mass-to-light ratio and the lowest oxygen abundance.

quotes.quote: ESO358 G060 (FCC 302) This dwarf is a confirmed member of the Fornax Cluster FCC 302; drinkwater01,schroeder01 . With an assumed distance modulus of 31.5 mould00 , the absolute magnitude is M_B 15.7 . The derived H I mass to blue luminosity ratio is 4 , which is the largest in the present sample. However, the true distance is uncertain, which is a source of large uncertainty in M_ HI /L_B . The derived oxygen abundance (12 + log(O/H) = 7.32; 2.8 of solar) makes this galaxy the most metal-poor dwarf in the present study.

The abundance is comparable to the second most metal-poor galaxy known, i.e., the blue compact dwarf galaxy SBS 0335 052 with 12 + log(O/H) = 7.33 melnick92,izotov97 . If the abundance is truly low, additional spectroscopy of this dI with a larger telescope should reveal , which should still be observable even at the distance of 20 Mpc; see for \ detections in dwarf galaxies at the distance of the Virgo Cluster. The adopted nitrogen-to-oxygen ratio is log(N/O) = 1.24 .

gmw_00012467.name: IC 2032
gmw_00012467.overview: IC 2032 is a little-studied dwarf galaxy that may be a member of the Dorado group [arXiv:astro-ph/0301492]. The galaxy features a bright shell of star formation, which gives it a cometary appearance [arXiv:astro-ph/0301492].

sources.summary: The paper describes IC 2032 as a little-studied possible Dorado group dwarf with a cometary star-forming shell and its first published oxygen abundance.

quotes.quote: IC 2032 Not much is known about this dwarf, although it may be a member of the Dorado group; see and references therein. The H I properties of the galaxy have been studied and listed in the survey by . There is a bright ``shell'' of star formation, giving the galaxy a ``cometary'' appearance. The spectrum presented here was taken with the east-west slit placed along the northern part of the shell, but \ was not detected. The resulting (bright-line) oxygen abundance (12 + log(O/H) = 7.97) is the first ever published for this dwarf.

gmw_00012468.name: RAD-7
gmw_00012468.overview: RAD-7 is a candidate episodic radio galaxy with an extremely elongated host that is possibly a disk [arXiv:1402.3674]. This host galaxy is located asymmetrically relative to its bright radio lobes, and faint, S-shaped radio emission extends beyond these lobes [arXiv:1402.3674].

sources.summary: The paper describes RAD-7 as a candidate episodic radio galaxy with an elongated possible disk host, bright lobes, and faint S-shaped radio emission requiring follow-up.

quotes.quote: RAD-7 (Fig. 1) is an intriguing case where the possible host is extremely elongated, possibly a disk but asymmetrically located to the bright lobes seen to VLA FIRST image. Beyond these two lobes faint and S-shaped radio emission extends like the case of Hydra-A. It is a case of precession, galaxy merger or a distant cluster is unclear with the available data. Its similarity with Speca and unknown redshift require radio-optical follow ups which are is in progress.

gmw_00012469.name: ESO482-G027
gmw_00012469.overview: ESO482-G027 is a galaxy in the Eridanus group that exhibits an asymmetric neutral hydrogen morphology [arXiv:2108.04414]. It features an extended, tail-like gas structure towards the southeast, with compressed contours on its northwest side pointing away from the central galaxy NGC 1395 [arXiv:2108.04414].

sources.summary: The paper describes ESO482-G027 as having asymmetric H I, with a southeast tail and compressed northwest contours pointing away from central galaxy NGC 1395.

quotes.quote: A few galaxies in the Eridanus group show asymmetric 1 morphologies (for example ESO482-G027, IC 1952, ESO482-G013) and/or exhibit truncated 1 discs as in the case of NGC 1422, ESO548-G070 and IC 1953, without exhibiting any visible perturbations in their stellar discs.

In Fig. Fig.5 we show the 1 contours of NGC 1422 and ESO482-G027 overlaid on top of their composite DSS optical images. It is clearly seen that NGC 1422 exhibits a truncated 1 distribution compared to its stellar disc. ESO482-G027 is observed to have an extended tail-like feature towards the south-east end, with the 1 contours compressed towards the north-west end, and the tail pointing away from the large group central galaxy NGC 1395. This suggests that processes other than tidal interactions such as ram-pressure stripping may be prevalent in the Eridanus sub-group.

gmw_00012470.name: SDSS J083917.83 014158.1
gmw_00012470.overview: SDSS J083917.83 014158.1 is a radio galaxy that is identified as a brightest cluster galaxy [arXiv:2402.06192]. It may be a wide-angle tailed radio galaxy in projection and exhibits a diffuse westward radio extension of unclear origin [arXiv:2402.06192].

sources.summary: The paper identifies SDSS J083917.83 014158.1 as the optical counterpart of a possible WAT/BCG radio galaxy with diffuse westward extension of unclear nature.

quotes.quote: A brighter extended source SW of the cluster centre is prominent in the source-subtracted images and is a radio galaxy but also has diffuse emission extending towards the W. The radio galaxy, which may be a wide-angle tailed radio galaxy (WAT) in projection, has an optical ID, (SDSS J083917.83 014158.1), considered the BCG position in the maxBCG cluster catalogue Koester2007 . This would suggest an offset of 2.5 arcmin from the SZ coordinates and the BCG. The nature of the diffuse extension of this radio galaxy is unclear.

gmw_00012471.name: UGC 2526
gmw_00012471.overview: UGC 2526 is an edge-on spiral galaxy classified as morphology Sb [arXiv:1306.2337]. It exhibits a modest star-formation rate of 2 to 5 solar masses per year [arXiv:1306.2337], a low radio luminosity [arXiv:1306.2337], and solar nuclear metallicity [arXiv:1306.2337].

sources.summary: The paper describes UGC 2526 as an edge-on Sb host with an optical-IR SED, modest star formation, low radio luminosity, solar nuclear metallicity, and a far-out supernova site.

quotes.quote: SN 2005ek exploded in the outskirts of UGC 2526, an edge-on spiral galaxy of morphology Sb. In Figure fig:hostsed we show the SED of UGC 2526, which was compiled from the SDSS Ahn2012 and IRAS MD2005 catalogs and supplemented with upper limits from our radio observations described in sec:Obs . Also shown is an Sb model template from the SWIRE database Silva1998 . Using this template, we derive a star-formation rate (SFR) of 2 -5 M _ yr ^ 1 Yun2002,Kennicutt1998 .

UGC 2526 has a low radio luminosity when compared to the Sb template which provides a best fit at other wavelengths. According to Chakraborti2012 this can be explained if electrons responsible for producing the radio synchrotron emission undergo significant inverse-Compton losses.

We measure the metallicity of UGC 2526 using our host-galaxy spectrum centered on the galaxy nucleus ( sec:Obs ). H and [ N II ] line fluxes were measured using the Markov Chain Monte Carlo (MCMC) technique described by Sanders2012a and, using the relations of Pettini2004 , we find the metallicity of the UGC 2526 to be 12 + (O/H) _ PP04N2 = 8.79 0.06 .

gmw_00012472.overview: 0202 + 319 is a radio-loud quasar located at a redshift of z = 1.466 [arXiv:1712.06314]. It features a compact core detected by the Fermi Gamma-ray Space Telescope, alongside a parsec-scale radio jet that exhibits a sharp, nearly 90-degree bend [arXiv:1712.06314].

sources.summary: The paper describes 0202 + 319/J0205 + 3212 as a z=1.466 Fermi-detected radio quasar with a compact core, northern extension, and a nearly 90-degree bent pc-scale jet.

quotes.quote: 0202 + 319 (J0205 + 3212) The radio quasar J0205 + 3212 is at a redshift of z = 1.466 1970ApJ...160L..33B . It was detected with the Large Area Telescope (LAT) onboard the Fermi Gamma-ray Space Telescope ( Fermi ) in the 100 MeV--300 GeV energy range 2010ApJS..188..405A,2012ApJS..199...31N,2015ApJS..218...23A . In the radio, the source shows a core and an extended component 15 arcsec to the north of the core in the 1.4-GHz Very Large Array (VLA) A-array configuration image. The flux densities of the core and the extended component are 652 mJy and 13 mJy, respectively 2007ApJS..171..376C . The extended feature was not detected in the higher-resolution 8.4-GHz VLA image, and the core was detected with a flux density of 765 mJy 2007ApJS..171...61H .

On mas scales, the source is dominated by a compact core with a curved jet extending to the north and then to the northwest, as was seen previously at 1.4-GHz Observed in the VLBA project BG196 (PI: D. Gabuzda), calibrated data downloaded from the Astrogeo database (http://astrogeo.org/) , 5-GHz 2012ApJ...744..177L , 2.3- and 8.4-GHz 2002ApJS..141...13B and 15-GHz VLBA images 2009AJ....137.3718L . The intriguing feature of this source is the large jet bending from the north to the west with a position angle changing nearly 90 (Fig. fig:0202+319 a-b). The jet first points to the north until 7 mas (a projected distance of 55 pc north of the core), where it bends with a gradually changing position angle from 0 to 64 at 35 mas (a projected distance of 280 pc away from the core).

gmw_00012473.overview: 1128+385 is an optically violent quasar located at a redshift of z = 1.733 [arXiv:1712.06314]. Radio observations of the source reveal a compact core with a weak extended jet structure to the west [arXiv:1712.06314].

sources.summary: The paper describes 1128+385/B2 1128 + 38 as an optically violent z=1.733 quasar with compact radio core emission and weak west/southwest jet structure.

quotes.quote: 1128+385 (B2 1128 + 38) is a low-spectral peaked source at a redshift of z = 1.733 2007AJ....134..102S and an optically violent quasar 2010A&A...518A..10V . It was not detected by the Energetic Gamma Ray Experiment Telescope (EGRET) on board the Compton Gamma Ray Observatory (CGRO) satellite, but was detected by Fermi /LAT at a level of 5.5 2015ApJS..218...23A . The 1.6-GHz VLA image in the combined A+B-array configuration shows a compact core and a hint of a weak feature at 15 ( 130 kpc) southeast of the core 1993MNRAS.264..298M . The snapshot VLBI images derived from the VLBI calibrator survey VLBA calibrator survey data base is maintained by Leonid Petrov, http://astrogeo.org/. only show a naked core at 2.3 and 8.4 GHz. In a 5-GHz VLBI observation (Fig. fig:1128+385 a), 1995ApJS...99..297X detected a weak extended jet to the west of the compact core.

gmw_00012474.name: JO69
gmw_00012474.overview: JO69 is a jellyfish galaxy candidate located near a shock region of gas in the galaxy cluster Abell 2399 [arXiv:2009.01400]. This location suggests that enhanced winds and increased ram pressure stripping during a cluster merger may have modified the galaxy's morphology [arXiv:2009.01400].

sources.summary: The paper identifies JO69 as a jellyfish candidate near an A2399 shock region, suggesting enhanced winds and ram pressure during the merger may have modified its morphology.

quotes.quote: We also highlight JO69, a jellyfish candidate from at RA = 329. ^ 36517 , and Dec. = -7. ^ 80358 , with a cyan open circle.

Near this discontinuity, at the northeast, is the jellyfish candidate JO69, shown as a cyan open circle, which probably had its morphology modified by the enhancement of the winds near to this shock region.

We also found that the jellyfish candidate JO69 from Poggianti2016 is near a shock region of the gas. A possible scenario to explain these characteristics is that this subgroup crossed the main cluster, increasing the ram pressure stripping (that may have influenced on the formation of the jellyfish) in that region and also on the release of metals from the galaxies of that subgroup in the western clump, explaining its higher metallicity.

gmw_00012475.overview: The galaxy 22036975 is classified as a star-forming galaxy [arXiv:1612.06866]. It is located in the green valley between the blue cloud and the red sequence, displaying an unusually red color and the highest C III] equivalent width in its sample [arXiv:1612.06866].

sources.summary: The paper identifies 22036975 as the reddest high-EW C iii ] outlier, places it in the green valley, finds no [ Ne v ] AGN evidence, and keeps it as star-forming.

quotes.quote: The only outlier in the color-magnitude diagram is the galaxy 22036975, which has the highest C iii ] EW ( 4.09 ) in the sample of detections and stands out in having an unusual red color ( =1.00 mag) compared to other objects with C iii ] detections. In fact, this object falls in the ``green valley" between the blue cloud and red sequence (Figure fig:galprop , bottom). One possible explanation of strong CIII ] emission accompanied by a red color is the presence of an AGN.

However, we find no evidence of an AGN in 22036975 based on the non-detection of the [ Ne v ] 3425 line. The 3 upper limit on the [ Ne v ] rest-frame EW is 0.72 in 22036975, which is much lower than the typical EW observed in the optically-selected narrow-line AGNs Zakamska2003 . Therefore, although we are not certain about the physical explanation of the redder color shown by this object relative to other C iii ] detections, we still treat it as a star-forming galaxy and retain it in our sample.

gmw_00012476.name: SDSS J022844.09+000217.0
gmw_00012476.overview: SDSS J022844.09+000217.0 is a broad-absorption line quasar located at a redshift of z = 2.719 [arXiv:1509.03694]. Multi-year spectral observations reveal broad-absorption line variability that is strongly correlated with the spectral index, suggesting that dust is intrinsic to the outflows [arXiv:1509.03694].

sources.summary: The paper analyzes 18 SDSS/BOSS spectra of the BAL QSO SDSS J022844.09+000217.0, finding BAL variability linked to spectral index and dust, with little evidence that photoionization dominates.

quotes.quote: The one BAL QSO with the most observations, SDSS J022844.09+000217.0 (z = 2.719), has 18 high signal-to-noise ratio (S/N) SDSS/BOSS spectra, covering 4128 days (about 11 years) in the observed frame (Table 1), i.e., about 3 years in the rest frame.
The strong correlation between the BAL-trough EW and the spectral index for this one QSO suggests that dust is intrinsic to outflows.
The weak correlation between the BAL variability and the continuum luminosity for this one QSO implies that the BAL-trough variation is not dominated by photoionization.

gmw_00012477.name: GIN 049
gmw_00012477.overview: GIN 049 is a dumbbell brightest cluster galaxy in the A160 cluster with four main optical cores [arXiv:2309.02002]. It is classified as a Fanaroff-Riley Class I wide-angle tail radio galaxy, and its physical size of 744 kpc qualifies it as a giant radio galaxy [arXiv:2309.02002].

sources.summary: The paper identifies GIN 049 as A160's dumbbell BCG and FRI wide-angle-tail radio galaxy; new LOFAR images make it a giant radio galaxy whose northern plume was reshaped, not re-accelerated, by JO36.

quotes.quote: GIN 049 GIN 049 (RA 18.2482, DEC 15.4912, a.k.a. B0110+152) is a dumbbell galaxy with 4 main optical cores and is the Brightest Cluster Galaxy (BCG) of A160. At radio wavelengths, it is classified as wide-angle tail O'Donoghue_1990 FRI radio galaxy Fanaroff-Riley_1974 .

Radio images The radio emission of GIN 049 obtained with the new images is more extended than that presented in the literature 8 arcmin Giovannini_1987 . Specifically, in the new LOFAR image (Figure panel ) we observe two new components, both toward the south and the north, that extend the radio source projected angular size at 144 MHz to 15.5 arcmin, corresponding to 744 kpc at the cluster redshift. Having a physical size larger than 700 kpc, GIN 049 can thus be classified as a `giant' radio galaxy Dabhade_2020 .

Concerning GIN 049, the northern and southern lobes show similar surface brightness and spectral index, but they differ in morphology, so we conclude that the JO36 encounter did not result in a detectable re-acceleration of the old radio plasma. However, the galaxy could have re-shaped the northern plume by dragging the lobe magnetic field along its orbit due to the magnetic draping.

gmw_00012478.name: Eri 3
gmw_00012478.overview: Eri 3 is a dwarf galaxy that lacks radial velocity measurements [arXiv:1907.09484]. Based on its on-sky position and distance, there are radial velocities for which the galaxy has a high probability of being a satellite bound to the Large Magellanic Cloud [arXiv:1907.09484].

sources.summary: The paper finds that Eri 3 lacks radial velocity but has broad velocity ranges with significant probability of LMC membership, motivating future reassessment.

quotes.quote: We apply this technique to dwarf galaxies lacking radial velocities and find that Eri 3 has a broad range of radial velocities for which it has a significant chance ( > 0.4 ) of having being bound to the Cloud. We see that there exist radial velocities for which Eri 3 would have a high probability, 0.50, of being bound an LMC satellite. In addition, they found Hor 2, Eri 3, Ret 3, Tuc 4, Tuc 5, and Phe 2 had on-sky positions and distances consistent with a Magellanic origin. We also find that there are radial velocities for which Eri 3 would likely be an LMC satellite. Furthermore, we find that there exist radial velocities for which Eri 3 has a significant probability of being an LMC satellite ( p_ LMC > 0.4 ).

gmw_00012479.name: host galaxy of SN Ia SCP15A03
aliases_local: host galaxy of SN Ia SCP15A03
gmw_00012479.overview: The host galaxy of SN Ia SCP15A03 is located at a redshift of z=1.3395 [arXiv:2005.07112]. Its spectrum displays Ca II and Balmer absorption lines alongside weak evidence of [O II] emission, with a stellar age estimated at 1.0 Gyr [arXiv:2005.07112].

sources.original_name: host galaxy of SN Ia SCP15A03
sources.summary: The paper identifies the SCP15A03 host at z=1.3395 with Ca II and Balmer absorption, weak [O II], discrepant low SED star formation, and a 1.0 Gyr stellar age.

quotes.quote: SCP15A03 The host galaxy of SN Ia SCP15A03 was identified to be at z=1.3395 , with the spectrum showing Ca ii H K and H9/CN absorption lines. The host spectrum, which is shown in Fig. fig:snia , shows weak evidence of [ O ii ] emission. Assuming this emission is associated with star formation, and not contaminated by other sources, implies a SFR of 12^ +7 _ 10 ,M _ yr ^ 1 , whereas the FAST SED fitting finds a SFR of 0.0^ +0.3 _ 0.0 ,M _ yr ^ 1 . Given the large uncertainties in the emission line SFR measurement, this represents only a 1 disagreement. Using FAST , we derive a stellar age of 1.0^ +0.1 _ 0.3 Gyr for this host galaxy.

gmw_00012480.name: SCP15A04/SCP16A04 host galaxy
gmw_00012480.overview: The SCP15A04/SCP16A04 host galaxy is located at a redshift of z=1.3345 [arXiv:2005.07112]. It exhibits optical and near-infrared emission lines, with a line ratio that indicates likely active galactic nucleus activity [arXiv:2005.07112].

sources.summary: The paper says one SPT0205 galaxy hosted SCP15A04 and SCP16A04, shows optical and NIR emission lines, and likely has AGN-contaminated line flux.

quotes.quote: SCP15A04 and SCP16A04 During the See Change survey, one galaxy in SPT0205 produced two individual SNe Ia, SCP15A04 and SCP16A04. The galaxy shows [ O ii ] emission in the optical (seen in both the FORS2 and X-Shooter spectra), along with Ca ii H K at z=1.3345 . The X-Shooter NIR spectrum reveals emission lines from H , and [ N ii ] 6548 and 6583 . The measurement of these emission line fluxes is illustrated in Fig. fig:em , and we find log( [ N ii ] 6583 /H )=0.03^ +0.12 _ 0.17 , which indicates there is likely at least some AGN contribution to the emission line flux (see e.g. \ 2001ApJ...556..121K,2003MNRAS.346.1055K ).

H and [ O iii ] are in regions of very poor S/N, so no meaningful measurement can be made for those. If the H flux is associated with star formation, a SFR of 23^ +11 _ 16 M _ yr ^ 1 would be implied, although given the [ N ii ] 6583 /H ratio, this should be considered an upper limit. The FAST SED fitting indicates a SFR of 0.0^ +12.3 _ 0.0 M _ yr ^ 1 , although this assumes that any AGN activity does not contribute significantly to the continuum. FAST implies a stellar age of 0.2^ +0.5 _ 0.1 Gyr for this galaxy.

gmw_00012481.name: host galaxy of SCP15A05
gmw_00012481.overview: The host galaxy of SCP15A05 is located at a redshift of z=1.3227 [arXiv:2005.07112]. It has an estimated stellar age of 1.0 Gyr and exhibits a low star formation rate of 0.7 solar masses per year [arXiv:2005.07112].

sources.summary: The paper describes the SCP15A05 host at z=1.3227 with Ca II, Mg II, and strong Balmer absorption, a low H-derived SFR, and a 1.0 Gyr stellar age.

quotes.quote: SCP15A05 The host galaxy of SCP15A05 shows Ca ii H K at z=1.3227 , along with several other absorption features at that redshift, including strong Balmer lines. We also detect Mg ii 2800 \ absorption. The FORS2 and X-Shooter spectra of this galaxy are shown in Fig. fig:snia .

After removing the effects of a sky line in part of the expected emission profile as outlined in Section sec:galpar , we measure a H flux of (5.2 3.8) 10^ 18 erg s ^ 1 . The H measurement implies a SFR of 0.7^ +0.9 _ 0.6 M _ yr ^ 1 , consistent with the FAST estimate of 5.1^ +0.0 _ 5.1 M _ yr ^ 1 from the stellar light. For this host galaxy we derive a stellar age of 1.0^ +0.3 _ 0.8 Gyr. The detection of strong Balmer absorption lines indicates that A stars contribute a high fraction of the total optical light emitted by the galaxy, and thus helps to constrain the stellar age significantly.

gmw_00012482.name: host galaxy of SCP15A06
gmw_00012482.overview: The host galaxy of SCP15A06 is a high-redshift galaxy located at a redshift of z=1.3128 [arXiv:2005.07112]. It exhibits strong Ca II absorption lines, a stellar age of approximately 1.3 Gyr, and a very low star formation rate [arXiv:2005.07112].

sources.summary: The paper describes the SCP15A06 host at z=1.3128 with strong Ca II absorption, low star-formation constraints, little SED-derived SFR, and a 1.3 Gyr stellar age.

quotes.quote: SCP15A06 The host galaxy of SCP15A06 shows strong Ca ii H K absorption lines at z=1.3128 0.0005 , similar to that found by z=1.3119 0.0005 2013ApJ...763...93S , who also derived the redshift from the Ca ii lines. Our FORS2 spectrum is shown in Fig. fig:snia . The spectrum taken by 2013ApJ...763...93S can be seen in their fig. 3 (galaxy J020543.00--582936.4), which they used to derive a 3 upper limit on the star formation rate of <0.63 M _ yr ^ 1 from the [ O ii ] flux, although note that this estimate does not include any correction for potential internal extinction (which we constrain to be A_V=0.3^ +0.6 _ 0.1 for the stellar light).

From our FORS2 spectrum we derive SFR <8.4 M _ yr ^ 1 from [ O ii ] constraints and SFR = 0.0^ +0.1 _ 0.0 M _ yr ^ 1 from the FAST SED fitting. Using FAST we derive a stellar age of 1.3^ +1.9 _ 0.3 Gyr for the galaxy.

gmw_00012483.name: host galaxy of SCP15C01
gmw_00012483.overview: The host galaxy of SCP15C01 is an emission-line galaxy located at a redshift of z=0.9718 [arXiv:2005.07112]. Spectral energy distribution fitting indicates a stellar age of 0.6 Gyr and a low star formation rate of 0.2 solar masses per year [arXiv:2005.07112].

sources.summary: The paper places the SCP15C01 host in the foreground at z=0.9718, deriving emission-line redshift, poorly constrained SFRs, low SED SFR, and a 0.6 Gyr age.

quotes.quote: SCP15C01 SCP15C01 was a SN Ia in the foreground of the MOO1014 cluster, with the redshift of z=0.9718 . This redshift was derived mainly from [ O ii ] , H and [ O iii ] emission lines, although higher order Balmer line absorption can also be seen in the continuum (see Fig. fig:snia ). A SFR derived from the [ O ii ] line is very poorly constrained, due in large part to the very uncertain extinction internal to the host ( A_V=0.8^ +0.4 _ 0.8 ). For this host galaxy, we can also estimate the SFR from H which has a measured flux of (2.16 0.30) 10^ 17 erg s ^ 1 , after correcting for stellar absorption.

This implies a SFR of 12^ +17 _ 10 M _ yr ^ 1 , which is still poorly constrained, even if better than is possible using [ O ii ] . The FAST SED fitting indicates a low star formation rate of 0.2^ +2.4 _ 0.2 M _ yr ^ 1 , which is still consistent with the poorly constrained emission line SFR. FAST gives a stellar age of 0.6^ +0.7 _ 0.4 Gyr for the host galaxy of SCP15C01.

gmw_00012484.name: SCP15C04 host galaxy
gmw_00012484.overview: The SCP15C04 host galaxy is a high-redshift galaxy located at a redshift of z=1.2305 [arXiv:2005.07112]. Spectral analysis reveals a stellar age of 4.0 Gyr, a small amount of star formation, and very little internal extinction [arXiv:2005.07112].

sources.summary: The paper finds the SCP15C04 host spectrum shows Ca II at z=1.2305, modest SED star formation, no detected [O II], and very little internal extinction.

quotes.quote: SCP15C04 The FORS2 spectrum of the SCP15C04 host galaxy, which is shown in Fig. fig:snia , shows Ca ii H K at z=1.2305 . The FAST analysis indicates a small amount of star formation ( 2.2^ +0.9 _ 1.4 M _ yr ^ 1 ) and a stellar age of 4.0^ +0.0 _ 1.6 Gyr. We do not detect [ O ii ] emission, although the resulting 2 limit on the SFR of <0.9 M _ yr ^ 1 is still broadly consistent with that determined from the SED. The SED indicates that this galaxy has very little internal extinction, with A_V=0.2^ +0.1 _ 0.2 .

gmw_00012485.name: SCP15D01 host galaxy
gmw_00012485.overview: The SCP15D01 host galaxy is a foreground galaxy located at a redshift of z=0.5682 [arXiv:2005.07112]. Spectroscopic observations reveal both emission and absorption lines, indicating that the galaxy has ongoing star formation [arXiv:2005.07112].

sources.summary: The paper identifies the SCP15D01 foreground SN Ia host at z=0.5682, with visible emission and absorption lines and ongoing star formation from both SED and H emission.

quotes.quote: SCP15D01 SCP15D01 was a SN Ia in the foreground of SPT2106. Multiple emission and absorption lines are visible in the FORS2 spectrum, from which we derive a redshift of z=0.5682 for the host galaxy. Both the SED fitting and the emission lines indicate some ongoing star formation. A SFR of 3.5^ +1.6 _ 1.3 M _ yr ^ 1 was derived from the SED fitting, with a SFR of 6^ +4 _ 3 M _ yr ^ 1 calculated from the strength of the H emission line. While SCP15D01 is a SN Ia, it has a substantially lower redshift than our target SN Ia sample of z 1 .

gmw_00012486.name: SCP15D02 host galaxy
gmw_00012486.overview: The SCP15D02 host galaxy is a high-redshift emission line galaxy located at a redshift of z=1.1493 [arXiv:2005.07112]. It exhibits broad [O II] and [O III] emission lines, which suggest a likely active galactic nucleus contribution that heavily contaminates the galaxy's emission line fluxes [arXiv:2005.07112].

sources.summary: The paper describes the SCP15D02 host as a possible cluster SN Ia host with broad [O II] and [O III], likely AGN contamination, and highly uncertain stellar parameters.

quotes.quote: SCP15D02 SCP15D02 was a possible SN Ia in the SPT2106 cluster. The host galaxy shows significant [ O ii ] emission at z=1.1493 . Furthermore, the X-Shooter NIR spectrum shows an emission line consistent with [ O iii ] 5007 \ at z=1.1493 . At this redshift, H is in a region of very high atmospheric absorption, so a measurement of that line is not possible. The FORS2 and X-Shooter spectra of the object can be seen in Fig. fig:snia .

The [ O ii ] and [ O iii ] 5007 \ emission lines are both broad for a galaxy spectrum, with FWHM 700 km s ^ 1 . We measure log( [ O iii ] 5007/H )=0.3^ +0.2 _ 0.6 , which does not tell us a great deal about potential AGN contribution (e.g. \ see Fig. 1 of 2003MNRAS.346.1055K ). Due to this, we use the version of the FAST code that includes an AGN component in the fitting. This indicates that potentially the majority of the continuum flux in our spectra could be from an AGN, which makes the derived stellar parameters very uncertain, with a stellar age of 2.5^ +2.4 _ 2.4 Gyr, log(M/M _ ) of 9.29^ +2.17 _ 0.60 and SFR of 0^ +513 _ 0 M _ yr ^ 1 . Due the likelihood of heavy AGN contamination of the emission lines, we do not calculate SFRs from the measured emission line fluxes.

gmw_00012487.name: host galaxy of SN Ia SCP15D03
aliases_local: host galaxy of SN Ia SCP15D03
gmw_00012487.overview: The host galaxy of SN Ia SCP15D03 is located at a redshift of z=1.1130 [arXiv:2005.07112]. Its spectrum shows strong Balmer absorption and potential weak [O II] emission, with a stellar age estimated at 0.8 Gyr and little to no ongoing star formation [arXiv:2005.07112].

sources.original_name: host galaxy of SN Ia SCP15D03
sources.summary: The paper describes the SCP15D03 host at z=1.1130 with strong Balmer absorption, weak Ca II K, little SED star formation, possible [O II], and young stellar age.

quotes.quote: SCP15D03 The host galaxy of SN Ia SCP15D03 shows several absorption lines at z=1.1130 , including strong Balmer absorption. The spectrum has strong H absorption and is consistent with a relatively strong Balmer break. From visual inspection of Fig. fig:snia , it appears that Ca ii K does not align with the redshift. However, the redshift does agree with the other Balmer lines and potential [ O ii ] emission. In galaxies with strong Balmer lines, Ca ii K can be relatively weak (with Ca ii H blended with H ), due to the domination of A stars over later type stars.

The SED of the SCP15D03 host is consistent with no ongoing star formation ( 0.0^ +0.1 _ 0.0 M _ yr ^ 1 ). However there is a weak detection of [ O ii ] emission from the spectrum, which if all associated with star formation would imply a SFR of 4^ +4 _ 2 M _ yr ^ 1 . The FAST analysis indicates a stellar age of 0.8^ +0.2 _ 0.1 Gyr.

gmw_00012488.name: host galaxy of SCP16D01
gmw_00012488.overview: The host galaxy of SCP16D01 is located at a redshift of z=1.1289 [arXiv:2005.07112]. Its spectrum displays a clear 4000 Angstrom break with strong absorption features [arXiv:2005.07112], indicating an older stellar population with low star formation [arXiv:2005.07112].

sources.summary: The paper finds the SCP16D01 host has strong absorption features, a clear 4000 Angstrom break, older stellar population than SCP15D03, and low SED-derived star formation.

quotes.quote: SCP16D01 The host galaxy of SCP16D01 shows strong Ca ii H K and G-band absorption, along with other absorption features. We find a redshift of z=1.1289 and it has a very strong cross-correlation peak, as would be expected given the strength and number of features. The spectrum of the host galaxy is shown in Fig. fig:snia , which shows a clear 4000 \ break. The stellar age of this galaxy derived from the SED fitting is 2.5^ +0.8 _ 0.3 Gyr.

This is significantly older than the stellar age of the SCP15D03 host in the same cluster. This can be seen in the spectra, where the SCP15D03 host shows strong Balmer absorption, rather than the strong 4000 \ break seen in the SCP16D01 host, due to the light of the former having a much stronger contribution from A stars. The SED fitting of the SCP16D01 host galaxy indicates a SFR of 0.0^ +0.3 _ 0.0 M _ yr ^ 1 , with a SFR of 4^ +4 _ 3 M _ yr ^ 1 derived from the weak [ O ii ] detection.

gmw_00012489.name: SCP15E06 host galaxy
gmw_00012489.overview: The SCP15E06 host galaxy is a high-redshift galaxy located at a redshift of 1.484 [arXiv:2005.07112]. Its spectrum exhibits a red continuum with Ca II H K absorption lines and possible Mg II absorption [arXiv:2005.07112].

sources.summary: The paper derives the SCP15E06 host redshift from Ca II H K, notes a red continuum and Mg II absorption, and finds uncertain mass and age.

quotes.quote: SCP15E06 The FORS2 data of the SCP15E06 host galaxy shows a red continuum with Ca ii H K absorption lines, along with possible absorption from Mg ii 2800 \ also seen in the spectrum, which is shown in Fig. fig:snia . We adopt a redshift of 1.484 from the Ca ii H K lines. The cross-correlation prefers a slightly lower redshift of 1.481, however as this conflicts with Ca ii H K (the main features that can be seen by visual inspection), we use the redshift derived from Ca ii H K alone. Compared to most of the other objects, this has significant uncertainty in the host mass, with log _ 10 (M/M _ )=10.94^ +0.29 _ 0.14 , which in part is due to the uncertainty in the F814W HST photometry. The stellar age of this host is also relatively uncertain at 1.6^ +2.4 _ 0.5 Gyr, noting the age of the Universe at z=1.48 is only 4.3 Gyr.

gmw_00012490.name: SCP15E07 host galaxy
gmw_00012490.overview: The SCP15E07 host galaxy is a high-redshift galaxy located at a redshift of z=1.4858 [arXiv:2005.07112]. Spectral analysis indicates the galaxy has a stellar age of approximately 1.6 Gyr and exhibits a low star formation rate [arXiv:2005.07112].

sources.summary: The paper uses cross-correlation and optical/NIR features to identify the SCP15E07 host at z=1.4858, deriving a 1.6 Gyr age and low star-formation rate.

quotes.quote: SCP15E07 We obtained both FORS2 and X-Shooter observations for the SCP15E07 host galaxy. A redshift is not immediately obvious by visual inspection. However, the cross-correlation yields a peak at z=1.4858 . The Ca ii NIR triplet appears to be detected in the NIR data, as well as some optical features such as Ca ii H K. The combined spectrum of the SCP15E07 host are shown in Fig. xcorr , with zoomed-in sections of the spectrum shown in Fig. fig:snia .

We used FAST to derive a stellar age of 1.6^ +0.5 _ 0.7 Gyr. Both the H emission measurement and the FAST SED fitting indicate a low SFR for the SCP15E07 host galaxy, deriving 0.0^ +0.1 _ 0.0 and 0.6^ +0.4 _ 0.4 M _ yr ^ 1 respectively for the two methods.

gmw_00012491.name: SCP15E08
gmw_00012491.overview: SCP15E08 is an active galactic nucleus host galaxy with a likely redshift of z=2.02 [arXiv:2005.07112]. The galaxy exhibits a red continuum likely subject to internal reddening, and it hosted a transient offset from the center of the active galactic nucleus [arXiv:2005.07112].

sources.summary: The paper describes SCP15E08 as a broad-lined, internally reddened AGN host at likely z=2.02 whose transient is offset from the central source.

quotes.quote: SCP15E08 The source near SCP15E08 has a bright continuum and a single emission line at 8460 . The emission line is very broad, with FWHM 6500 km s ^ 1 . The source was detected as an X-ray source by XMM in 2013 2016A&A...590A...1R . Taken together, this implies that this galaxy is an AGN. As only one line is identified, the redshift is not completely secure, but the line is most likely to be Mg ii 2800 \ at z=2.02 , which is consistent with some weaker features expected in a quasar spectrum.

The continuum appears red and is likely subject to significant internal reddening. The photometry of SCP15E08 is consistent with a SN Ia, likely at a higher redshift than the cluster ( z>1.6 ), and is indeed consistent with a SN Ia at z=2.02 . The transient is not coincident with the centre of the AGN, so the new object is not due to variability in the central source itself.

gmw_00012492.name: SCP15G01 host galaxy
gmw_00012492.overview: The SCP15G01 host galaxy is a massive galaxy located at a redshift of z=1.5817 [arXiv:2005.07112]. Spectral analysis reveals possible absorption from Na I D and Mg b, an old stellar age of approximately 4.0 Gyr, and no obvious evidence of H-alpha emission [arXiv:2005.07112].

sources.summary: The paper reports the SCP15G01 host redshift, possible absorption features, high stellar mass, no obvious H emission, low forced H SFR, and old stellar age.

quotes.quote: SCP15G01 A redshift of z=1.5817 was obtained for the SCP15G01 host galaxy, mainly from Keck data (Hayden et al. in prep). Using this redshift, we can put limits on the H emission with our VLT data. At this redshift, the region of the Ca ii H K lines has very poor sensitivity in X-Shooter. However, using this redshift, there does appear to be possible absorption from Na i D and Mg b . The FAST SED analysis indicates that this is a massive galaxy, with log _ 10 (M/M _ ) = 11.44 ^ +0.03 _ 0.15 .
