The spectrum shows no obvious evidence of H emission. A forced measurement of the excess H flux over the fitted continuum yields 5.5 3.8 10^ 18 erg s ^ 1 , implying SFR of 1.6^ +2.7 _ 1.3 M _ yr ^ 1 , consistent with the FAST rate of 0.0^ +17.4 _ 0.0 M _ yr ^ 1 .
The SED indicates a stellar age of 4.0^ +0.0 _ 2.0 Gyr, where the age of the Universe at z=1.5817 sets the upper limit on the stellar age.

gmw_00012493.name: SCP16H01 host galaxy
gmw_00012493.overview: The SCP16H01 host galaxy spectrum reveals two distinct spatial components, designated as component A and component B [arXiv:2005.07112]. Component B is a foreground emission-line galaxy at a redshift of z=1.5388, while the supernova-associated component A has an inconclusive spectroscopic redshift [arXiv:2005.07112].

sources.summary: The paper describes the SCP16H01 host candidate as two components: emission lines from foreground component B and a likely SN-associated continuum component A without a secure redshift.

quotes.quote: SCP16H01 The X-Shooter spectrum of the SCP16H01 host galaxy shows two components; the main continuum and emission lines slightly offset (in the spatial direction) from the continuum. The structure can be seen in Fig. 16h01 , with the position of the SN also indicated. The continuum is from component `A' and the H and [ O iii ] 5007 \ emission lines are from component `B'. The accompanying [ O iii ] 4959 \ line is possibly present as well, but the larger errors and weakness of the line make this only tentative. This gives a redshift for component B of z=1.5388 , which places the galaxy in the foregound of the cluster. These emission lines from component B are very narrow, with the strongest line, H , measured at FWHM 100 km s ^ 1 after correcting for spectral resolution.

Any possible contamination from the emission line object was subtracted from the main trace using the half of the emission line trace furthest spatially from the main trace. This may also subtract a small percentage of the main trace flux ( 5 ). The errors are fully propagated through this subtraction, but do not increase significantly due to the optimal extractions employed; i.e. \ where the flux from the emission-line object is strongest spatially has a reasonable offset from the centre of the main trace and thus the flux/error at this pixel has a low weighting in the optimal combining. The spectrum of component A, after removal of the contamination, shows no evidence of features at the same redshift as component B. In conclusion, from the imaging we can see the SN is likely associated with component A, but the spectroscopic redshift of that component remains inconclusive.

gmw_00012494.name: host galaxy of possible SN Ia SCP15I02
aliases_local: host galaxy of possible SN Ia SCP15I02
gmw_00012494.overview: The host galaxy of the possible Type Ia supernova SCP15I02 has a detected continuum [arXiv:2005.07112]. Its spectrum shows possible evidence of sodium I D absorption near the cluster redshift of 1.63, though a secure redshift has not been obtained [arXiv:2005.07112].

sources.original_name: host galaxy of possible SN Ia SCP15I02
sources.summary: The paper detects continuum for the possible SCP15I02 host but cannot secure a redshift, noting possible Na I D absorption near the cluster redshift.

quotes.quote: SCP15I02 The host galaxy of possible SN Ia SCP15I02 has a detected continuum. However, we have not been able to obtain a secure redshift from the data. The spectrum is shown in Fig. fig:snia and shows possible evidence of Na i D absorption near the cluster redshift of 1.63. At this redshift, X-Shooter has low sensitivity in the region of Ca ii H K.

gmw_00012495.name: SDSS J114013.23-002442.2
gmw_00012495.overview: SDSS J114013.23-002442.2 is a blue compact dwarf galaxy characterized by a regular, elliptical-like morphology [arXiv:0906.2055]. Despite this structure, the galaxy exhibits a blue optical color and the largest H-alpha flux, which led to its exclusion from the star-forming elliptical galaxy sample [arXiv:0906.2055].

sources.summary: The paper identifies SDSS J114013.23-002442.2/Mrk 1303 as a previously classified blue compact dwarf with elliptical-like morphology, unusual blue color, strong H flux, and exclusion from the star-forming elliptical sample.

quotes.quote: One galaxy in the SFEGs sample (SDSS J114013.23-002442.2 or Mrk 1303) is picked out because it is classified as a BCD (Blue Compact Dwarf) galaxy from previous observation (Gondhalekar et.al. 1998). Though its morphology is regular and elliptical like, this galaxy has unusual blue optical color and the largest H flux. Due to the difference between EGs and BCD galaxies in the evolution perspective, it is excluded from the SFEGs sample.

gmw_00012496.name: SDSS J114031.65+005111.4
gmw_00012496.overview: SDSS J114031.65+005111.4 is an elliptical-like galaxy located approximately 305.6 Mpc away [arXiv:0906.2055]. Although its optical color resembles a quiescent elliptical galaxy, it exhibits strong emission lines indicating active star formation and belongs to a small four-member galaxy group [arXiv:0906.2055].

sources.summary: The paper presents SDSS J114031.65+005111.4 as an elliptical-like star-forming galaxy with strong SDSS emission lines, group membership, 305.6 Mpc distance, and 2MASS/GALEX observations.

quotes.quote: SDSS J114031.65+005111.4 (PGC1177304) The morphology of this galaxy is similar to a typical elliptical galaxy, the optical color is also close to the quiescent EGs (g- =0.79 ), but strong emission lines are detected in the SDSS spectrum. This galaxy is 305.6 21.4 Mpc away, the -band absolute magnitude M_r=-21.40 . Based on Tago et al. (2008), it belongs to a small galaxy group (Group944) which has 4 members. This galaxy has 2MASS (Skrutskie et al. 2006) (J=14.826, H=14.112, K=13.596) and GALEX (Martin et al. 2005) (FUV=20.14) observation. The image and spectrum are shown in Fig.10.

gmw_00012497.name: SDSS J150712.72-005735.2
gmw_00012497.overview: SDSS J150712.72-005735.2 is an isolated emission-line galaxy located approximately 181.2 Mpc away [arXiv:0906.2055]. Although it exhibits an elliptical morphology, it features a very blue optical color, a bright nucleus, and a spectrum characteristic of active star formation [arXiv:0906.2055].

sources.summary: The paper describes SDSS J150712.72-005735.2 as an elliptical-looking emission-line galaxy with very blue color, bright nucleus, strong star-forming spectrum, 181.2 Mpc distance, and no group or cluster membership.

quotes.quote: SDSS J150712.72-005735.2 (PGC1130399) It is also a typical elliptical galaxy in morphology, but has very blue (g-=0.50) optical color and a very bright nucleus. The spectrum is very similar to star-forming galaxies with flat continua and strong emission lines. This galaxy is classified as emission line galaxy (ELG) in NED. It is 181.2 12.7 Mpc away and bright absolute magnitude ( M_r =-23.05). This galaxy does not belong to any galaxy group or cluster.

gmw_00012498.name: SDSS J221130.31+291949.0
gmw_00012498.overview: SDSS J221130.31+291949.0 is identified as a potential dual quasar candidate [arXiv:2306.08376]. This classification is supported by two Gaia detections and a broad H-alpha emission line that is offset from the narrow [O III] line by approximately 870 km/s [arXiv:2306.08376].

sources.summary: The paper identifies SDSS J221130.31+291949.0 as a potential dual quasar because its broad H line is offset from [O III] and two Gaia detections are present.

quotes.quote: We also find one target, SDSS J221130.31+291949.0, with a distinct line offset of the broad H_ emission line with respect to the narrow [ O III ] 5007 emission line. We use one Gaussian to fit both the broad and narrow emission lines and obtain the positions of the peaks for the emission lines. The best-fitting results and the optical confirmation are shown in the bottom left and right panels of Figure fig:f4 , respectively. Via the fitting results, we estimate the velocity offset to be about 870 km s ^ 1 . Considering the line offset property as well as the two detections from Gaia , this target is a potential DQ candidate.

For our selected dual quasar candidate SDSS J221130.31+291949.0, we also find that one Gaia detection was observed with large proper motion. However, we still propose it as a dual quasar candidate in this work considering its line offset properties as well as the two detections from Gaia . We are carrying up follow-up spectroscopic confirmation to pin it down from alternatives.

gmw_00012499.name: Sextans II
gmw_00012499.overview: Sextans II is a candidate satellite galaxy of the Milky Way [arXiv:2311.05439]. It is classified as an ultra-faint dwarf galaxy due to its old, spatially extended stellar population, which includes red giant branch, main sequence turn-off, and blue horizontal-branch stars [arXiv:2311.05439].

sources.summary: The paper names Sextans II as a new Milky Way satellite candidate; its CMD shows RGB, turn-off, and BHB stars, and its old, spatially extended population leads the authors to classify it as a UFD.

quotes.quote: Here, we identify two new candidate MW satellites, which we name Sextans II (Sext II) and Virgo III (Vir III) at (RA, DEC) =(156^ .4, -0^ .6) and (186^ .3, 4^ .4) , respectively. In Figures fig: SextansII_cmd_new and fig: VirgoIII_cmd_new , we show the space and CMD distribution of stars in these candidates, demonstrating that they are true stellar systems: RGBs, main sequence turn-off stars (MSTOs), and blue horizontal-branch stars (BHBs) are clearly visible in the CMD. Vir III is found to have the smaller number of each of RGBs, MSTOs, and BHBs than does Sext II. These are considered to be UFDs because (1) their distributions in CMDs are qualitatively consistent with old stellar populations and (2) they are spatially extended compared to globular clusters with similar brightness, as we describe in Section 4.1.

gmw_00012500.name: Virgo III
gmw_00012500.overview: Virgo III is a candidate Milky Way satellite galaxy located at right ascension 186.3 degrees and declination 4.4 degrees [arXiv:2311.05439]. It is classified as an ultra-faint dwarf galaxy containing an old, spatially extended stellar population that includes red giant branch, main sequence turn-off, and blue horizontal-branch stars [arXiv:2311.05439].

sources.summary: The paper names Virgo III as a new Milky Way satellite candidate; its CMD shows RGB, turn-off, and BHB stars, fewer than Sextans II, and its old, extended population leads to a UFD classification.

quotes.quote: Here, we identify two new candidate MW satellites, which we name Sextans II (Sext II) and Virgo III (Vir III) at (RA, DEC) =(156^ .4, -0^ .6) and (186^ .3, 4^ .4) , respectively. In Figures fig: SextansII_cmd_new and fig: VirgoIII_cmd_new , we show the space and CMD distribution of stars in these candidates, demonstrating that they are true stellar systems: RGBs, main sequence turn-off stars (MSTOs), and blue horizontal-branch stars (BHBs) are clearly visible in the CMD. Vir III is found to have the smaller number of each of RGBs, MSTOs, and BHBs than does Sext II. These are considered to be UFDs because (1) their distributions in CMDs are qualitatively consistent with old stellar populations and (2) they are spatially extended compared to globular clusters with similar brightness, as we describe in Section 4.1.

gmw_00012501.name: HRS 25
gmw_00012501.overview: The galaxy HRS 25 is identified as a radio-slope outlier [arXiv:2204.00831]. It exhibits a star formation rate similar to that of the Milky Way and has a critical frequency between MWA frequencies and 1500 MHz [arXiv:2204.00831].

sources.summary: The paper identifies HRS 25 as a radio-slope outlier with Milky-Way-like SFR and a critical frequency between MWA and 1500 MHz, though the flatter slope may be statistical.

quotes.quote: We clearly see that two galaxies (HRS 25 and 144) are outliers in the left panel of Fig. fig:comparehist , while they do not show any deviation from the bulk of the population when the shorter frequency data are included.

However, HRS 25 and 144 have a SFR similar to the Milky Way Boselli2015 and a critical frequency between MWA frequencies and 1500 [ MHz ] . This would be difficult to explain by the conjecture of Schober2017 . Possible reasons for this are 1) poor statistics, i.e., fewer data can constrain the fitted slope only weakly, and 2) physical peculiarity of the galaxy. As for HRS 25 might really have a flatter slope, but very possibly it is due to the statistical uncertainty.

gmw_00012502.name: RR 216
gmw_00012502.overview: RR 216 is an early-type and spiral galaxy pair located within a very poor, evolving group [arXiv:0905.1264]. The system exhibits an optically distorted morphology and features relatively faint, compact X-ray emission likely originating from an evolved stellar population [arXiv:0905.1264].

sources.summary: The paper describes RR 216 as an X-ray-faint E+S pair with three new faint members, six associated galaxies, compact X-ray emission, and an optically distorted morphology in a very poor evolving group.

quotes.quote: We found two and ten new members likely to be associated with the X-ray bright systems RR 143 and RR 242, respectively. The X-ray faint RR 210 and RR 216, which were only partially covered by the VIMOS observations, have two and three new faint members, respectively. The new members increase the number of associated galaxies to 4, 7, 6, and 16 for RR 143, RR 210, RR 216, and RR 242, respectively, down to M_R 12 + 5 h_ 100 .

RR 210 and RR 216 show relatively faint and compact (i.e., within the optical galaxy) X-ray emission, consistent with an origin in an evolved stellar population. The emission in RR 143 and RR 242 can be argued to be related to a group potential (as in CPG127) rather than to an individual galaxy. In such a scenario, RR 210 and RR 216 could represent the active part of very poor and loose evolving groups see e.g., Rampazzo06 . The activity is reflected by the optically extended and distorted morphologies (see Paper III).

gmw_00012503.name: And X
gmw_00012503.overview: Andromeda X is a faint dwarf spheroidal galaxy located approximately 621 kiloparsecs away [arXiv:1101.2201]. It is a highly elongated, metal-poor system situated about 174 kiloparsecs from the Andromeda Galaxy [arXiv:1101.2201].

sources.summary: And X is analyzed with deep LBC photometry; the paper derives a 621 kpc distance, finds it faint and highly elongated 174 kpc from Andromeda, and measures low metallicity with intrinsic spread.

quotes.quote: We use deep wide-field photometry from the Large Binocular Camera to study the stellar and structural properties of the recently discovered Andromeda X and Andromeda XVII (And X and And XVII) dwarf galaxies. Using the mean apparent magnitude of the horizontal branch (HB), we derive distances of 621 20 kpc to And X and 734 23 kpc to And XVII, closer by > 60 kpc than the previous estimates which were based on red giant branch (RGB) observations. Thus our results warrant against the use of the RGB tip method for determining distances to systems with sparsely populated RGBs, and show how crucial HB observations are in obtaining accurate distances in systems such as these.

We find that And X is a relatively faint (M _V = 7.36), highly elongated ( =0.48 ) system at a distance of 174 62 kpc from Andromeda. And XVII is brighter (M _V = 8.61) with an M31-centric distance of 73 kpc which makes it one of the closest satellites to Andromeda. Both galaxies are metal-poor: we derive Fe/H = 2.2 for And X, while And XVII shows Fe/H = -2.0 , consistent with the relation of higher luminosity dwarfs being more metal-rich. Additionally, both galaxies show considerable intrinsic spreads in metallicity (0.2 and 0.3 dex for And X and And XVII respectively), consistent with multiple stellar populations.

gmw_00012504.name: And XVII
gmw_00012504.overview: And XVII is a dwarf spheroidal galaxy located approximately 734 kpc away [arXiv:1101.2201]. It is one of the closest satellite galaxies to Andromeda, situated at an M31-centric distance of 73 kpc, and is characterized as a metal-poor system with an intrinsic metallicity spread [arXiv:1101.2201].

sources.summary: And XVII is analyzed with deep LBC photometry; the paper derives a 734 kpc distance, finds it brighter and among Andromeda's closest satellites, and measures low metallicity with intrinsic spread.

quotes.quote: We use deep wide-field photometry from the Large Binocular Camera to study the stellar and structural properties of the recently discovered Andromeda X and Andromeda XVII (And X and And XVII) dwarf galaxies. Using the mean apparent magnitude of the horizontal branch (HB), we derive distances of 621 20 kpc to And X and 734 23 kpc to And XVII, closer by > 60 kpc than the previous estimates which were based on red giant branch (RGB) observations. Thus our results warrant against the use of the RGB tip method for determining distances to systems with sparsely populated RGBs, and show how crucial HB observations are in obtaining accurate distances in systems such as these.

We find that And X is a relatively faint (M _V = 7.36), highly elongated ( =0.48 ) system at a distance of 174 62 kpc from Andromeda. And XVII is brighter (M _V = 8.61) with an M31-centric distance of 73 kpc which makes it one of the closest satellites to Andromeda. Both galaxies are metal-poor: we derive Fe/H = 2.2 for And X, while And XVII shows Fe/H = -2.0 , consistent with the relation of higher luminosity dwarfs being more metal-rich. Additionally, both galaxies show considerable intrinsic spreads in metallicity (0.2 and 0.3 dex for And X and And XVII respectively), consistent with multiple stellar populations.

gmw_00012505.name: NGC 3811
gmw_00012505.overview: NGC 3811 is a galaxy featuring a disk populated by H II regions, active galactic nuclei (AGN) candidates, and intermediate-type objects [arXiv:1607.07190]. These distinct emission-line regions have been mapped to trace the radial distribution of oxygen abundance across the galaxy's disk [arXiv:1607.07190].

sources.summary: The paper uses NGC 3811 spaxels to illustrate BPT classification, mapping AGN-like, HII-region-like, and intermediate objects in the disk and deriving their radial oxygen-abundance distribution.

quotes.quote: Panel a of Fig. figure:f3811 shows the [ N ii ] 6584/H vs. \ [ O iii ] 5007/H diagram for the spectra of individual spaxels in the galaxy NGC 3811. Solid and dashed curves mark the boundary between AGNs and H ii regions defined by Kauffmann2003 and Kewley2001 , respectively. The light-blue filled circles show the large sample of emission-line SDSS galaxies studied by Thuan2010 . The red points indicate AGN-like objects in NGC3811 according to the dividing line of Kewley2001 . The black points represent H ii region-like objects in NGC3811 according to the dividing line of Kauffmann2003 . The dark-blue points are ``intermediate'' objects in NGC 3811 located between the dividing lines of Kauffmann2003 and Kewley2001 .

Panel b shows the locations of the AGN-like, H ii region-like, and ``intermediate'' objects in NGC 3811 in the deprojected face-on image of the galaxy. Panel shows the radial distribution of oxygen abundances in the disk of the galaxy NGC 3811 traced by the H ii region-like and ``intermediate'' objects.

gmw_00012506.name: NGC 5520
gmw_00012506.overview: NGC 5520 is a galaxy classified under galaxy disks [arXiv:1607.07190]. Its abundance map consists of 2,316 spaxels and has a global azimuthal asymmetry of 0.011 dex [arXiv:1607.07190].

sources.summary: The paper uses NGC 5520 to test spaxel-number effects, reporting 2316 spaxels, a 0.011 dex global azimuthal asymmetry, and 1000 reduced-spaxel abundance-map realizations.

quotes.quote: We consider the galaxy NGC 5520 to examine this problem. The abundance map of NGC 5520 consists of 2316 spaxels and has a global azimuthal asymmetry of 0.011 dex in case A . We construct a set of 1000 abundance maps of the galaxy NGC 5520 with a reduced number of spaxels, randomly selected from the full map of 2316 spaxels.

gmw_00012507.name: NGC 6132
gmw_00012507.overview: NGC 6132 is a spiral Sab galaxy located at a distance of 76.5 Mpc [arXiv:1607.07190]. It has a linear optical radius of 12.46 kpc and exhibits a possible azimuthal asymmetry in its disk oxygen abundance [arXiv:1607.07190].

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 6132 as a spiral Sab galaxy at 76.5 Mpc, derives photometric and abundance-map geometry, and flags possible azimuthal oxygen-abundance asymmetry in its disk.

quotes.quote: The galaxy NGC 6132 is a spiral Sab galaxy (morphological type T = 2.0 ) at a distance of 76.5 Mpc ( leda , ned ). Our analysis of the band SDSS map results in an inclination angle i = 68 with a position angle of the major axis of PA = 125 . The angular optical radius of the galaxy is 0.56 arcmin. This results in a linear optical radius R_ 25 = 12.46 kpc for the adopted distance.

The left-column panels of Fig. figure:n6132ac show the properties of the abundance distribution across the disk of NGC 6132 where the deprojected radii of the H ii regions (spaxels) were computed using the geometric parameters of the galaxy obtained from the photometric map, case A . From the analysis of the abundance map, we obtained the position of the ``chemical center'' of the galaxy at X_0 = 34.3 spaxels, Y_0 = 34.3 spaxels, a position angle of the major axis of PA = 126 , and an inclination angle i = 69 . The panels in the right column of Fig. figure:n6132ac show the properties of the abundance distribution across the disk of NGC 6132 where the deprojected radii of the H ii regions were computed using the geometric parameters of case C .
Azimuthal asymmetry of the abundances in the disk Fig. figure:n6132ac shows the galaxy NGC 6132, which is a galaxy with possible azimuthal asymmetry in the oxygen abundance in the disk.

gmw_00012508.name: NGC 2693
gmw_00012508.overview: NGC 2693 is a massive, fast-rotating elliptical galaxy [arXiv:2111.13699]. Triaxial orbit modeling reveals that the galaxy has a triaxial intrinsic shape and hosts a central supermassive black hole with a mass of approximately 1.7 billion solar masses [arXiv:2111.13699].

sources.summary: The paper measures a newly detected central SMBH in NGC 2693, modeling stellar kinematics with triaxial Schwarzschild orbits to derive black-hole mass, dark matter content, mass-to-light ratio, and intrinsic shape.

quotes.quote: We present a stellar dynamical mass measurement of a newly detected supermassive black hole (SMBH) at the center of the fast-rotating, massive elliptical galaxy NGC 2693 as part of the MASSIVE survey. We combine high signal-to-noise integral field spectroscopy (IFS) from the Gemini Multi-Object Spectrograph (GMOS) with wide-field data from the Mitchell Spectrograph at McDonald Observatory to extract and model stellar kinematics of NGC 2693 from the central 150 pc out to 2.5 effective radii. Observations from Hubble Space Telescope ( HST ) WFC3 are used to determine the stellar light distribution.

We perform fully triaxial Schwarzschild orbit modeling using the latest TriOS code and a Bayesian search in 6-D galaxy model parameter space to determine NGC 2693's SMBH mass ( ), stellar mass-to-light ratio, dark matter content, and intrinsic shape. We find = 1.7 0.4 10^ 9 \ and a triaxial intrinsic shape with axis ratios p=b/a=0.902 0.009 and q=/a=0.721^ +0.011 _ 0.010 , triaxiality parameter T = 0.39 0.04 . In comparison, the best-fit orbit model in the axisymmetric limit and (cylindrical) Jeans anisotropic model of NGC 2693 prefer = 2.4 0.6 10^ 9 \ and = 2.9 0.3 10^ 9 \ , respectively. Neither model can account for the non-axisymmetric stellar velocity features present in the IFS data.

gmw_00012509.name: NGC 2719
gmw_00012509.overview: NGC 2719 is an interacting galaxy that forms the pre-merger pair Arp 202 alongside NGC 2719A [arXiv:1407.7643]. The galaxy features a regularly rotating edge-on disk with a warp, which is strongly perturbed at its southeastern end by the ongoing interaction [arXiv:1407.7643].

sources.summary: NGC 2719 is treated as one member of the Arp 202 pre-merger pair, with a regular but perturbed H i disk and stellar mass shaped by interaction with NGC 2719A.

quotes.quote: The contours between 3050 \ to 3200 \ indicate the \ in the central and NW parts of the NGC 2719 disk are in a regularly rotating edge on disk, although with indications of a warp. This rotation pattern is broken at south-eastern end of the NGC 2719 \ disk at velocities below 3050 . The abrupt change of rotation pattern in the contours together with the tidal tail morphology at velocities below 3050 \ indicates the \ there has been strongly perturbed by the interaction between NGC 2719 and NGC 2719A. The narrow range of \ velocities, 60 , in the tidal tail running westward from NGC 2719A to and including the TDG candidate provide clear evidence linking the both the tidal tail and TDG candidate to the interaction between NGC 2719 and NGC 2719A.

Arp 202 -- major interactions The pre-merger interacting pair black , NGC 2719 and NGC 2719A, have an optical radial velocity separation of 40 \ and D _ 25 of 1.3 arcsec (17.5 kpc) and 0.9 arcsec (12 kpc) respectively. We estimate the stellar mass for NGC 2719 at 3.4 x 10 ^9 \ and NGC 2719A at 5.0 x 10 ^9 \ (a stellar mass ratio of 1:1.4), based on the parameters from and for SDSS g -band magnitudes and g-- colours.

gmw_00012510.name: NGC 2724
gmw_00012510.overview: NGC 2724 is a large SABc spiral galaxy located near the Arp 202 system [arXiv:1407.7643]. It features an H I-rich, relatively undisturbed disk, which suggests that no major gas exchange has occurred with its neighboring system [arXiv:1407.7643].

sources.summary: NGC 2724 is discussed as a large SABc spiral near Arp 202 with an H i-rich, relatively undisturbed disk, making major gas exchange with Arp 202 unlikely.

quotes.quote: A 60 ^ radius (720 kpc at that distance) search around the Arp 202 system, within a velocity range of 2000 km s ^ 1 to 4000 km s ^ 1 , yields only one similar sized neighbour (NGC 2724) and two small dwarfs (SDSS J090028.22+354009.8 and SDSS J090029.37+354840.6). The projected distance between Arp 202 and NGC 2724 is 9.6 ^ (115 kpc). Assuming black galaxies are moving at 150 km s ^ 1 , a typical number for velocity dispersion in loose groups, we estimate that if they have crossed paths, then the last interaction could have black occurred about 3.6 10 ^ 8 years ago, less than the dynamical time scales of NGC 2719 and NGC 2724, which are estimated to be 4.2 10 ^ 8 years and 5.2 10 ^ 8 years respectively.

Had there been an interaction involving a massive transfer of \ between these two galaxies, extended tidal features would have remained in the \ disks for at least one dynamical timescale. While the \ in Arp 202 is heavily perturbed, the NGC 2724 H i disk is relatively undisturbed and shows no significant \ deficiency. The expected H i mass of NGC 2724 is 2.9 10 ^ 9 M _ 1984AJ.....89..758H compared to the observed mass of 5.6 0.4 10 ^ 9 M _ , making it an H i rich spiral.

gmw_00012511.name: SDSS J090028.22+354009.8
gmw_00012511.overview: SDSS J090028.22+354009.8 is a nearby dwarf galaxy located southeast of the Arp 202 system [arXiv:1407.7643]. It shows signs of recent interaction with Arp 202, including a connecting neutral hydrogen bridge and active star formation [arXiv:1407.7643].

sources.summary: The paper identifies SDSS J090028.22+354009.8 as a nearby dwarf associated with Arp 202, showing H i interaction signatures, a faint bridge, star formation, and old-dwarf properties.

quotes.quote: SDSS J090028.22+354009.8, to the south-east is 4.1 ^ (49 kpc) away and SDSS J090029.37+354840.6 to the north east is 5.8 ^ (69 kpc) away. Their stellar masses were estimated from the SDSS photometric measurements quoted in NED and using the mass to light ratio to colour relation prescribed in . The value for solar absolute magnitude in g - band was taken from . The stellar masses for SDSS J090028.22+354009.8 and SDSS J090029.37+354840.6 are estimated as 3.9 10 ^ 9 M _ and 1.1 10 ^ 8 M _ respectively.

Both the galaxies are black beyond the fields imaged by Spitzer and only SDSS J090028.22+354009.8 is within the GALEX Medium Imaging Survey (MIS) field. The galaxy is bright in the GALEX map indicating star formation activity within the last 10 ^8 yr. This is not unexpected as SDSS J090028.22+354009.8 shows signs of recent interaction with the Arp 202 system. We have detected fragments of an \ bridge connecting Arp 202 and SDSS J090028.22+354009.8.

gmw_00012512.name: SDSS J090029.37+354840.6
gmw_00012512.overview: SDSS J090029.37+354840.6 is a blue dwarf galaxy with optical colors similar to blue compact dwarf galaxies [arXiv:1407.7643]. It is located approximately 69 kiloparsecs northeast of the galaxy Arp 202, suggesting it is an old satellite companion [arXiv:1407.7643].

sources.summary: The paper treats SDSS J090029.37+354840.6 as a nearby blue dwarf associated with Arp 202, measuring H i properties and arguing it is an old satellite rather than a detached TDG.

quotes.quote: SDSS J090028.22+354009.8, to the south-east is 4.1 ^ (49 kpc) away and SDSS J090029.37+354840.6 to the north east is 5.8 ^ (69 kpc) away. Their stellar masses were estimated from the SDSS photometric measurements quoted in NED and using the mass to light ratio to colour relation prescribed in . The value for solar absolute magnitude in g - band was taken from . The stellar masses for SDSS J090028.22+354009.8 and SDSS J090029.37+354840.6 are estimated as 3.9 10 ^ 9 M _ and 1.1 10 ^ 8 M _ respectively.

SDSS J090029.37+354840.6 on the other hand is a bluer dwarf, with g- value as 0.19, which is considered low in dwarf galaxy samples barazza and similar to blue compact dwarf galaxies (BCDG) noah . In terms of stellar populations, tidal dwarf galaxies are known to be of two types, one with extremely new stars, with optical colours similar to BCDGs and the other dominated by an older population, mostly derived from the fragments of the parent galaxies ducmirabel1999 . However the dynamical mass to gas mass ratio of SDSS J090029.37+354840.6 is 8.

gmw_00012513.name: MCG+08-19-017
gmw_00012513.overview: MCG+08-19-017 is a companion galaxy to NGC 3191, located at a projected distance of 45 kpc [arXiv:1708.03856]. The galaxy exhibits a low star formation rate, hosts an older stellar population, and previously hosted the Type Ic supernova SN 2003ds [arXiv:1708.03856].

sources.summary: MCG+08-19-017 is discussed as NGC 3191’s companion, with low star formation, older stellar population, similar metallicity, and a Type Ic supernova in the same active pair.

quotes.quote: It has a companion, MCG+08-19-017, at a projected distance of 45 kpc (between the galaxy centres) and a radial velocity difference of only 200 km/s. A colour picture of the two galaxies is show in Fig. fig:no10 . From the analysis of the SDSS spectra described in Section 2, we find that MCG+08-19-017 has a low SFR of 0.15 M _ yr ^ 1 and an older stellar population as shown by a low H EW of only 21 and a significant stellar absorption component in the H line (EW of 6.3 ).

The galaxy pair NGC 3191 and MCG+08-19-017 have hosted not only SN 2017egm but another three SNe in the past two decades. This underlines the increased SF and SN activity due to mutual interaction in both galaxies as concluded in the previous section.
NGC 3191 has previously hosted two other SNe, a Type-Ia (SN 1988B) and a Type-II SLSN (PTF10bgl), while the neighboring galaxy MCG+08-19-017 hosted another Type-Ic SN (SN 2003ds).

gmw_00012514.name: CEERS-80374
gmw_00012514.overview: CEERS-80374 is a faint high-redshift galaxy located at z = 7.17 [arXiv:2311.13683]. It is identified as a Lyman-alpha emitter with a detected ultraviolet continuum and a large implied escape fraction [arXiv:2311.13683].

sources.summary: The paper presents CEERS-80374 as a faint z>7 Lyman-alpha emitter with large equivalent width, UV continuum detection, and evidence that it lies in an ionized overdense region.

quotes.quote: The data reveal two likely detections, CEERS-80374 at z = 7.17, and CEERS-80239 at z = 7.48. Both emitting galaxies are identified in the CEERS epoch 3 observations taken in February 2023. The spectra show multiple rest-optical emission lines (i.e., and [ ] ) as well as an emission line feature at the position of at SNR = 7.8 and 8.2 that supports our identification (see Figure fig:spec ). Both emitting galaxies show the detection of UV continuum in the spectra.

The derived values are 205_ 27 ^ +48 for CEERS-80374 and 334_ 62 ^ +109 for CEERS-80239, among the highest EWs detected at z>7 e.g., Jung2020,Bunker2023,Saxena2023,Tang2023 . We measure = 18.5 for CEERS-80374 and 18.2 for CEERS-80239 as above, which are among the faintest UV continuum magnitudes in our sample (see Figure fig:ew-muv ).

In the other galaxy, CEERS-80374, we do not detect given its faintness (see Figure fig:spec ). Nonetheless, a large escape fraction ( = 0.56_ 0.17 ^ 0.31 ) is implied by comparing the observed EW with the value derived from photoionization modeling ( sec:sed ) .

gmw_00012515.name: CEERS-80239
gmw_00012515.overview: CEERS-80239 is a faint Lyman-alpha emitting galaxy located at a high redshift of z = 7.48 [arXiv:2311.13683]. It exhibits a very high Lyman-alpha equivalent width and has a measured Lyman-alpha escape fraction of approximately 0.472 [arXiv:2311.13683].

sources.summary: The paper presents CEERS-80239 as a faint z>7 Lyman-alpha emitter with very large equivalent width, measured escape fraction, and likely association with an overdense ionized structure.

quotes.quote: The data reveal two likely detections, CEERS-80374 at z = 7.17, and CEERS-80239 at z = 7.48. Both emitting galaxies are identified in the CEERS epoch 3 observations taken in February 2023. The spectra show multiple rest-optical emission lines (i.e., and [ ] ) as well as an emission line feature at the position of at SNR = 7.8 and 8.2 that supports our identification (see Figure fig:spec ). Both emitting galaxies show the detection of UV continuum in the spectra.

The derived values are 205_ 27 ^ +48 for CEERS-80374 and 334_ 62 ^ +109 for CEERS-80239, among the highest EWs detected at z>7 e.g., Jung2020,Bunker2023,Saxena2023,Tang2023 . We measure = 18.5 for CEERS-80374 and 18.2 for CEERS-80239 as above, which are among the faintest UV continuum magnitudes in our sample (see Figure fig:ew-muv ).

In one of these systems (CEERS-80239), we are able to measure the escape fraction ( = 0.472_ 0.048 ^ +0.066 ) via detection of , indicating that at least 50 of the photons are transmitted through the IGM (with a larger IGM transmission implied if some of the attenuation occurs in the ISM and CGM of the galaxy).

gmw_00012516.name: CEERS-82069
gmw_00012516.overview: CEERS-82069 is an emission line galaxy characterized by an extremely blue UV continuum slope [arXiv:2311.13683]. It hosts an extremely young stellar population and exhibits a potentially high escape fraction of Lyman-continuum photons [arXiv:2311.13683].

sources.summary: CEERS-82069 is singled out because Lyman-continuum escape models better fit its SED, implying a blue UV slope, possible high escape fraction, and extremely young stellar population.

quotes.quote: However, for CEERS-82069, where the observed SEDs cannot be fitted well with the fiducial BEAGLE models (best ^2 = 36.0), the f_ esc ^ LyC models improve the fitted SED significantly (best ^2 = 1.71, see its SED in Figure fig:beagle ). Its potential high escape fraction of LyC photons is also consistent with the extremely blue UV continuum slope ( = -2.8 ). Therefore, the SED fitting results derived from the f_ esc ^ LyC models are preferred over those from the default ionization-bounded models for CEERS-82069. In the following analysis, we will primarily focus on the results from the f_ esc ^ LyC models for CEERS-82069, and the results from our default models for the remaining 9 galaxies, which are reported in Table tb:lya .

Two of these systems (CEERS-82069 and 476) have escape fractions (and EWs) that are similar to the z=7.3 galaxy in Saxena2023 . Each of these has prominent coupled with weak lines in the rest-optical (Figure fig:spec ), implying / ratios approaching the theoretical limit.

There is one source in our sample (CEERS-82069) that appears to have extremely young stellar populations (CSFH age = 6_ 4 ^ +48 Myr) coupled with a much lower [ ] + EW. As we discussed in sec:sed , this source has a very blue UV slope that may suggest the emission lines may be weakened by the leakage of ionizing photons.

gmw_00012517.name: CEERS-1334
gmw_00012517.overview: CEERS-1334 is an emission line galaxy that exhibits an anomalously high line flux [arXiv:2311.13683]. Its observed Balmer ratios are consistent with zero dust attenuation, corresponding to an optical depth of zero in the V band [arXiv:2311.13683].

sources.summary: The paper flags CEERS-1334 for anomalously high line flux, uses an alternate Balmer ratio, and finds its ratios consistent with zero dust attenuation.

quotes.quote: In one case (CEERS-1334), we use the / ratio ( detected at SNR = 45) owing to an anomalously high flux. Assuming the case B recombination with the gas temperature T_e=10^4 K and density n_e=250 cm ^ 3 , we expect an intrinsic / ( / ) = 2.86 (0.47; Draine2011 ). Comparing this with the observed ratios (median / = 3.20, and / consistent with zero dust for CEERS-1334), we derive the corresponding optical depth in the V band ( _ V ), assuming the SMC attenuation curve Pei1992 . We find that inferred _ V range from 0.0 (CEERS-1334 and CEERS-80916) to 0.56 (CEERS-1561), corresponding to small correction factors to the flux of 1.00--1.55 (median 1.24) to the six galaxies.

gmw_00012518.name: ZD4
gmw_00012518.overview: ZD4 is a high-redshift galaxy confirmed to lie at a redshift of z=7.88 [arXiv:2311.13683]. It is part of a larger galaxy overdensity and exhibits strong rest-optical emission lines alongside Lyman-alpha emission [arXiv:2311.13683].

sources.summary: The paper confirms ZD4 as a z=7.88 overdensity member with Lyman-alpha emission, rest-optical lines, and likely damped absorption rather than nebular-continuum-dominated far-UV light.

quotes.quote: The photometric candidate ZD4 is confirmed to lie at z=7.88 , placing it as part of the same overdensity. In addition to strong rest-optical emission lines ( [ OIII ] +H EW = 794_ 220 ^ +310 ), we identify an emission line feature (SNR = 5.9) at a wavelength consistent with in the galaxy. We measure a relatively large EW ( 68_ 9 ^ +10 ), suggesting the overdensity may indeed have carved out an ionized region.

However, the weak rest-optical emission lines in ZD4 (H EW = 217_ 85 ^ +122 ) suggest that such strong nebular emission is not likely present in this system. The ionizing photon production efficiency implied by the H detection in ZD4 (log ( _ ion / erg ^ 1 Hz) = 25.5_ 0.2 ^ +0.1 ) is well below that required for the nebular continuum to dominate the far-UV continuum e.g., Topping2022_ceers . Also, given the presence of such apparent absorption features redwards of wavelength in all three galaxies observed with UNCOVER in this z=7.88 overdensity, we suggest that the damped absorption remains a very likely explanation for ZD4 and the other two systems.

gmw_00012519.name: galaxy 2592
gmw_00012519.overview: Galaxy 2592 is a nearby lens galaxy located directly adjacent to the straight arc of the galaxy cluster A2390 [arXiv:1008.1963]. It dominates the local gravitational lensing properties in the vicinity of the arc and can be modeled with a spherical density distribution [arXiv:1008.1963].

sources.summary: The paper models galaxy 2592 as the nearby lens galaxy adjacent to A2390's straight arc, finding that it dominates the local lensing map yet bends images too strongly in quasiequilibrium configurations.

quotes.quote: We note that the lensing properties in the arc's vicinity are almost entirely dominated by the closest galaxy. The structure of the critical curves (right panel) already reveals that such models will produce strongly bent images with respect to the galaxy 2592 at the position of interest.

This problem further deteriorates if one tries to avoid the issues related to (a) and (b) by lowering the total mass of the nearby (lens) galaxy 2592 . Observations further indicate the presence of several faint elongated objects whose orientation is approximately the same as that of the arc, with a scatter of only a few degrees .

While this is not necessarily true for the galaxy 2592 (see Fig. figure0 ) which resides directly adjacent to the arc, a spherical density model provides a good description, which is indicated by the rather mild ellipticity seen in the optical HST image. As can be seen in Sec. section52 , this approximation does not affect the basic results of our analysis - at least in the case of quasiequilibrium configurations. Furthermore, we assume that all considered galaxies can be modeled by a matter distribution of the form () = Mr_ H 2 (+ )(+r_ H )^ 3 , where (0)=M/(2 r_ H ^ 2 ) is the central matter density, and the profile's core radius is universally set to r_ H =3 kpc.

gmw_00012520.name: KK 135
gmw_00012520.overview: KK 135 is an extremely isolated dwarf irregular galaxy located in the Local Volume [arXiv:1508.02591]. The galaxy features a compact H II region situated on its southern side [arXiv:1508.02591].

sources.summary: The paper confirms KK 135 as a nearby isolated irregular dwarf, reporting its first radial velocity, ultraviolet and SDSS measurements, a southern compact HII region, and no physical coupling to DDO 123.

quotes.quote: KK 135=PGC 166130=SDSS J121934.68+580234.4. This irregular dwarf galaxy with an angular size of 0.68^ 0.35^ was reported in Ref. 17, but not included in the UNGC catalog because the radial velocity had not been measured. In the ultraviolet sky survey GALEX [ 18 ] it has apparent magnitudes of m(FUV ) = 18.70 0.12 and m(NUV ) = 18.60 0.04 . An image of this galaxy from the Sloan Digital Sky Survey [ 19 ] is shown in Fig. 1. A reproduction of the spectrum of KK 135 obtained by us in the 4000-7000 range is shown in Fig. 2.

Judging from the measured radial velocity V_h = 215 40 km/s, this galaxy certainly belongs to the Local volume sample. A compact HII region lies on the southern side of KK 135. KK 135 is an extremely isolated galaxy.

gmw_00012521.name: UGCA 127 sat
gmw_00012521.overview: UGCA 127 sat is a dwarf irregular companion galaxy located south of the spiral galaxy UGCA 127 [arXiv:1508.02591]. The physical coupling of the two galaxies is confirmed by their close radial velocities, and UGCA 127 sat features an emission node to its east [arXiv:1508.02591].

sources.summary: The paper identifies UGCA 127 sat as a dwarf irregular companion south of UGCA 127, measures bright-line radial velocity near the spiral's velocity, and notes an eastern emission node.

quotes.quote: UGCA 127 sat=LV J0620-0839. This dIr galaxy lies in a zone of strong galactic absorption ( A_b = 3.53^m ) 10 ^ to the south of the spiral galaxy UGCA 127. Bright emission lines can be seen in the spectrum of this presumed dwarf companion (Fig. 3); from these we measured a radial velocity for the companion of V_h = 708 26 km/s, which is close to the velocity of the neighboring spiral galaxy, V_h = 732 km/s, and confirms the physical coupling of the two. To the east of UGCA 127 sat, there is an emission node [ 20 ] with a radial velocity 80 km/s higher than that of the central part of the galaxy.

gmw_00012522.name: UGC 1703
gmw_00012522.overview: UGC 1703 is an isolated dwarf galaxy classified as a spheroidal or transition type [arXiv:1508.02591]. Located at an estimated distance of 4.19 Mpc, it hosts an old stellar population and shows no signs of H-alpha emission [arXiv:1508.02591].

sources.summary: The paper classifies UGC 1703 as an isolated dSph/dTr dwarf, reporting its surface-brightness distance, lack of H-alpha emission, absorption-line velocity, absolute magnitude, and nearest massive neighbor Maffei 2.

quotes.quote: UGC 1703 = KKH 9 = PGC 08484. This is a dwarf galaxy of spheroidal (dSph) or transition (dTr) type. The distance D = 4.19 Mpc to it has been estimated from the fluctuations in its surface brightness [ 21 ] . In an H_ line trace [ 22 ] it shows no signs of emission. Absorption H_ , H_ , and H_ Balmer lines and the CaII (H, K) doublet with an average radial velocity of +40 km/s can be seen in its spectrum (Fig. 4).
For this distance the absolute magnitude of UGC 1703 is M_B = -11.5^m . This galaxy belongs to a rare class of isolated dwarf systems with an old star population.

gmw_00012523.name: HS117
gmw_00012523.overview: HS117 is a dwarf galaxy characterized by having little recent star formation [arXiv:1202.4783]. The galaxy contains several bright red helium burning stars, though a portion of these are likely foreground contaminants rather than members of the galaxy itself [arXiv:1202.4783].

sources.summary: HS117 is treated as a deviant stellar-population case whose bright RHeB stars likely include foreground contaminants; after foreground correction it no longer remains anomalous.

quotes.quote: Two galaxies, ESO540-030 and HS117, in particular appear discrepant in the left-hand panel of Figure fig:agbfrac , showing less TP-AGB light than their SFH might imply.

In the case of HS117 a handful of extremely bright RHeB stars may also be lowering the AGB contribution to the total. As we discuss in Section 3.6, these are likely foreground stars, further complicating the interpretation of these results.

For instance, HS117, which is thought to have little recent star formation, is found to have 8 RHeB stars 4 of which are very luminous and make up the over 90 of the flux. However, the TRILEGAL model predicts that at least three of those luminous stars are foreground. The one remaining RHeB star could also well be foreground given the small number statistics. Removing the foreground stars from the RHeB sequences actually improves the correlation between the flux contribution of RHeBs and the star formation histories of the galaxies as shown in the right hand panel of Figure fig:RHeBfrac . After foreground correction HS117 is no longer a deviant point in the plot.

gmw_00012524.name: UGC4305-1
aliases_external: UGC 4305-1 HST/ANGST field in Ho II
gmw_00012524.overview: UGC4305-1 is a galaxy utilized in stellar population synthesis modeling to analyze stellar content [arXiv:1202.4783]. These models tend to overpredict the galaxy's population of thermally pulsing asymptotic giant branch stars while underpredicting its red helium burning stars [arXiv:1202.4783].

sources.summary: UGC4305-1 is used as a worked example; its SPS models overpredict TP-AGB stars, underpredict RHeB contributions, and scatter some likely AGB stars into the RHeB region.

quotes.quote: To give an example of the type of calculation we will be making in the following sections, Figure fig:cum shows the stellar (model + data) cumulative F160W flux fraction as a function of stellar magnitude for program galaxy UGC4305-1.

Figure fig:modeldatacomp shows an example model/data comparison for UGC 4305-1. The four panels plot the observed numbers (top) and flux contributions (bottom) of TP-AGB and RHeB stars for actual data (filled circles), and the best-fit SPS models from the 2008 (open circles) and 2010 (diamonds) Padova isochrones. Also shown are the results for 100 MC simulations based on the 2010 models (histograms) that span the full range of acceptable SFHs. For UGC4305-1 the SPS models tend to over-predict the observed numbers and fluxes of the TP-AGB stars and under-predict the observed numbers and flux contributions of RHeB stars, although the uncertainties on the model results (i.e. the widths of the histograms) are large.
In galaxies IC2754-SGS and UGC4305-1, a significant number of stars in the 0.3 - 1 Gyr time bin scatter into the RHeB selection region, even though they are likely to be AGB stars.

gmw_00012525.name: VHSJ2100-5820
gmw_00012525.overview: VHSJ2100-5820 is a high-redshift quasar that is well-characterized by a reddened-quasar template [arXiv:1801.02635]. The source does not exhibit an excess of rest-frame ultraviolet emission relative to what is expected from a reddened quasar [arXiv:1801.02635].

sources.summary: VHSJ2100-5820 is well fit by a reddened-quasar template alone; despite classifier hints, the paper finds no rest-frame UV excess for host-SFR analysis.

quotes.quote: Four quasars are found to be well-fit by this model - VHSJ2024-5623, VHSJ2100-5820, VHSJ2257-4700 and ULASJ0016-0038, returning a ^ 2 _ red,QSO < 2.5 and residuals ( F_ mod F_ phot ) <2 across all bands.

Despite the modest classifier characterising two of these sources (VHSJ2024-5623 and VHSJ2100-5820) as high-confidence galaxies in the DES g and bands, we find that a reddened quasar SED is sufficient to fit the photometric data in both cases. Any extended emission in these galaxies is therefore faint and difficult to disentangle from the quasar emission via SED modelling. As such, we find these four quasars to be well characterised by a reddened-quasar template alone and therefore exclude them from the remainder of our analysis.

In our analysis, one of these nine quasars - VHSJ2100-5820 - does not show an excess of rest-frame UV emission relative to that expected from a reddened quasar and so is not considered further in our comparisons to H derived SFRs.

gmw_00012526.name: VHSJ2028-5740
gmw_00012526.overview: VHSJ2028-5740 is a high-redshift quasar [arXiv:1801.02635]. Its rest-frame ultraviolet spectral energy distribution is poorly fit by standard host galaxy templates, but is significantly improved by adopting a dustier galaxy model [arXiv:1801.02635].

sources.summary: VHSJ2028-5740 is one of the quasars poorly fit by scattering or the default host model; a dustier galaxy template improves its rest-frame UV SED fit.

quotes.quote: This model generally appears to provide a good fit to the photometry, finding a scattering fraction 0.2 per cent across all sources and returning a ^ 2 _ red,cont < 2.5 in 10 of the 13 quasars, with only three quasars - VHSJ2028-5740, VHSJ2227-5203 and VHSJ2256-4800 - returning ^ 2 _ red,cont > 2.5.

Four quasars however - VHSJ2028-5740, VHSJ2227-5203, VHSJ2256-4800 and VHSJ2355-0011 - return ^ 2 _ red,GAL+QSO > 2.5 when this default host galaxy model is assumed. We consider VHSJ2227-5203 further in Section sec:qso_variability , but for the remaining three objects we find the g band photometry to lie below the fitted SED in VHSJ2028-5740 (Fig. fig:sed2 ) and above the fitted SED in VHSJ2256-4800 (Fig. fig:sed9 ) and VHSJ2355-0011 (Fig. fig:sed13 ), indicating that a galaxy model with _ V = 1.0 cannot reproduce the slope of the rest-frame UV flux in these systems. We find that significantly improved ^ 2 _ red,GAL+QSO values ( 1-2) can be obtained by adopting a galaxy model with _ V = 5.0 for VHSJ2028-5740 and _ V = 0.2 for VHSJ2256-4800 and VHSJ2355-0011.

gmw_00012527.name: VHSJ2227-5203
gmw_00012527.overview: VHSJ2227-5203 is a high-redshift quasar [arXiv:1801.02635]. Its JHK band photometry is faint compared to its grizY photometry, which cannot be explained by quasar variability alone [arXiv:1801.02635].

sources.summary: VHSJ2227-5203 has poor scattered-continuum and default host-galaxy fits; its JHK photometry is faint, and a variability correction still fails to explain the mismatch.

quotes.quote: This model generally appears to provide a good fit to the photometry, finding a scattering fraction 0.2 per cent across all sources and returning a ^ 2 _ red,cont < 2.5 in 10 of the 13 quasars, with only three quasars - VHSJ2028-5740, VHSJ2227-5203 and VHSJ2256-4800 - returning ^ 2 _ red,cont > 2.5.
Similarly, the J band photometry in VHSJ2227-5203 lies below our fitted SED model (Fig. fig:sed7 ), indicating that a higher reddening would be derived without the inclusion of the grizY photometry.

We consider the possibility however, that individual quasars in our sample may exhibit variability with larger amplitudes and for VHSJ2227-5203 ( ^ 2 _ red,GAL+QSO > 2.5), we find the JHK band photometry to appear several tenths of a magnitude fainter than that extrapolated from the grizY photometry. To test whether variability could explain the poor fit of VHSJ2227-5203, the fitting routine is re-run, having increased the JHK magnitudes by 0.2 mag - the magnitude offset required to align the DES and VHS photometry. However, the fit of this adjusted photometry returned ^ 2 _ red,GAL+QSO 5 , meaning that an offset between the DES and VHS photometry due to quasar variability, cannot account for the large ^ 2 _ red,GAL+QSO in VHSJ2227-5203.

gmw_00012528.name: ULASJ2224-0015
gmw_00012528.overview: ULASJ2224-0015 is a high-redshift quasar that lacks spatially extended emission [arXiv:1801.02635]. Its rest-frame ultraviolet emission is consistent with either star formation or scattered light [arXiv:1801.02635].

sources.summary: ULASJ2224-0015 lacks spatially extended emission in the classifier output, leaving scattered light possible; its UV emission is consistent with either star formation or scattering.

quotes.quote: The modest classifier show evidence for spatially extended emission in all but two (ULASJ2200+0056 and ULASJ2224-0015) of the quasars well-fit by this model (Section sec:data ).

Although we cannot rule out the scattered light scenario in explaining the UV excess in ULASJ2200+0056 or ULASJ2224-0015, where we see no evidence for extended emission, we likely require an alternative explanation for the remainder of our quasar sample.

Furthermore, we have found two quasars - VHSJ2306-5447 and ULASJ2224-0015 - to exhibit rest-frame UV emission consistent with either star formation or scattered light and have derived instantaneous SFRs across the entire quasar sample, based upon their rest-frame UV flux.

gmw_00012529.name: VHSJ2256-4800
gmw_00012529.overview: VHSJ2256-4800 is a high-redshift quasar [arXiv:1801.02635]. Its spectral energy distribution is poorly fit by default host galaxy models, but fits are improved by adopting a lower-extinction galaxy model to account for its elevated g-band flux [arXiv:1801.02635].

sources.summary: VHSJ2256-4800 is poorly fit by the scattering and default host models; its g-band flux lies above the fit, improved by a lower-extinction galaxy model.

quotes.quote: This model generally appears to provide a good fit to the photometry, finding a scattering fraction 0.2 per cent across all sources and returning a ^ 2 _ red,cont < 2.5 in 10 of the 13 quasars, with only three quasars - VHSJ2028-5740, VHSJ2227-5203 and VHSJ2256-4800 - returning ^ 2 _ red,cont > 2.5.

Four quasars however - VHSJ2028-5740, VHSJ2227-5203, VHSJ2256-4800 and VHSJ2355-0011 - return ^ 2 _ red,GAL+QSO > 2.5 when this default host galaxy model is assumed. We consider VHSJ2227-5203 further in Section sec:qso_variability , but for the remaining three objects we find the g band photometry to lie below the fitted SED in VHSJ2028-5740 (Fig. fig:sed2 ) and above the fitted SED in VHSJ2256-4800 (Fig. fig:sed9 ) and VHSJ2355-0011 (Fig. fig:sed13 ), indicating that a galaxy model with _ V = 1.0 cannot reproduce the slope of the rest-frame UV flux in these systems. We find that significantly improved ^ 2 _ red,GAL+QSO values ( 1-2) can be obtained by adopting a galaxy model with _ V = 5.0 for VHSJ2028-5740 and _ V = 0.2 for VHSJ2256-4800 and VHSJ2355-0011.

gmw_00012530.name: VHSJ2306-5447
gmw_00012530.overview: VHSJ2306-5447 is a high-redshift quasar modeled with a reddened quasar template and a Lyman-alpha emission line [arXiv:1801.02635]. Its rest-frame ultraviolet flux may arise solely from Lyman-alpha emission, or alternatively, from star formation or scattered light [arXiv:1801.02635].

sources.summary: VHSJ2306-5447 is the sole quasar whose rest-frame UV flux may arise only from Lyman emission, though the paper also allows star formation or scattering.

quotes.quote: We model the remaining six quasars - ULASJ2200+0056, VHSJ2227-5203, VHSJ2306-5447, ULASJ2315+0143, VHSJ2332-5240 and VHSJ2355-0011 - with a two-component model consisting of a reddened quasar template and a Lyman- emission line.

Although both ULASJ2200+0056 and VHSJ2306-5447 return ^ 2 _ red,ly 2, we note that excess rest-frame UV emission relative to the reddened quasar model is detected in multiple bands in ULASJ2200+0056, further ruling out the scattered Lyman- scenario for this quasar. From this, we conclude VHSJ2306-5447 to be the only quasar for which the rest-frame UV flux may arise solely from Lyman- emission. The best fit SED of this scenario for VHSJ2306-5447 in shown in Fig. fig:fig_lyman .

Furthermore, we have found two quasars - VHSJ2306-5447 and ULASJ2224-0015 - to exhibit rest-frame UV emission consistent with either star formation or scattered light and have derived instantaneous SFRs across the entire quasar sample, based upon their rest-frame UV flux.

gmw_00012531.name: VHSJ2355-0011
gmw_00012531.overview: VHSJ2355-0011 is a high-redshift quasar host galaxy [arXiv:1801.02635]. Its rest-frame ultraviolet flux slope is poorly fit by default models, but is better reproduced by adopting a low-extinction galaxy template [arXiv:1801.02635].

sources.summary: VHSJ2355-0011 is poorly fit by the default host-galaxy model; its g-band flux is above the fit, improved with a lower-extinction galaxy template.

quotes.quote: Four quasars however - VHSJ2028-5740, VHSJ2227-5203, VHSJ2256-4800 and VHSJ2355-0011 - return ^ 2 _ red,GAL+QSO > 2.5 when this default host galaxy model is assumed. We consider VHSJ2227-5203 further in Section sec:qso_variability , but for the remaining three objects we find the g band photometry to lie below the fitted SED in VHSJ2028-5740 (Fig. fig:sed2 ) and above the fitted SED in VHSJ2256-4800 (Fig. fig:sed9 ) and VHSJ2355-0011 (Fig. fig:sed13 ), indicating that a galaxy model with _ V = 1.0 cannot reproduce the slope of the rest-frame UV flux in these systems. We find that significantly improved ^ 2 _ red,GAL+QSO values ( 1-2) can be obtained by adopting a galaxy model with _ V = 5.0 for VHSJ2028-5740 and _ V = 0.2 for VHSJ2256-4800 and VHSJ2355-0011.

gmw_00012532.name: host of GRB 160410A
gmw_00012532.overview: The host galaxy of GRB 160410A is a high-redshift galaxy that remains undetected in deep imaging down to a limiting magnitude of 27.17 [arXiv:2109.13838]. Analysis of the GRB afterglow reveals a low-metallicity environment with minimal dust extinction and a low degree of ionization [arXiv:2109.13838].

sources.summary: The paper infers a faint GRB 160410A host from DLA and metal absorption, finding low metallicity, little extinction, low ionisation, and no detected galaxy in deep imaging.

quotes.quote: The spectrum shows \ in absorption with a column density of N(HI) = 21.3 0.3 cm ^ 2 which, together with , , \ and , allow us to perform the first study of chemical abundances in a SGRB host galaxy. We determine a metallicity of [ Fe/H ] = 2.7 0.3 , significantly lower than observed for any long GRB host. We find no evidence for extinction in the afterglow spectral energy distribution (SED). The environment has a low degree of ionisation and the and lines are completely absent. We do not detect an underlying host galaxy down to deep limits.

We do not find any source at the GRB 160410A position in our late GTC/OSIRIS image (right panel of Fig. fig:FindingChart ). We obtain a limiting magnitude of ^ >27.17 mag (AB, corrected for Galactic extinction), which corresponds to an absolute magnitude limit of M _ ^ > -18.44 mag. None of the nearby sources in the field are likely to be the possible host of GRB 160410A.

gmw_00012533.name: host candidate for GRB 201221D
gmw_00012533.overview: The host candidate for GRB 201221D is a faint galaxy identified at the same position as the gamma-ray burst [arXiv:2109.13838]. Spectral energy distribution modeling indicates that it is an intermediate-mass galaxy with a moderate star-formation rate and a low specific star-formation rate [arXiv:2109.13838].

sources.summary: The paper identifies a faint GRB 201221D host candidate and models its SED as an intermediate-mass, moderately star-forming galaxy with low specific SFR.

quotes.quote: The Large Binocular Telescope (LBT) observed the underlying host galaxy of GRB 201221D at two epochs. A first observation was obtained on the 24^ th of December, 2020 when we observed in the near-infrarred J and K_s filters with the LBT Utility Camera in the Infrared LUCI, 2003SPIE.4841..962S imager and spectrograph under good seeing with ( 1 0 on average, first reported in Rossi2021GCN29311 ). The second observation was obtained the 10^ th of January, 2021 in g^ ^ i^ z^ bands with the Large Binocular Camera LBC, 2008A&A...482..349G under moderate seeing conditions ( 1 6 on average).

In contrast to GRB 160410A we do find a faint host candidate for GRB 201221D at the same position as the GRB. The photometry of the host candidate is shown in Tab. tab:201221D_host for all the bands observed with the LBT telescope. We analyse the Spectral Energy Distribution (SED) of the host galaxy with the available photometry using CIGALE https://cigale.lam.fr/ 2005MNRAS.360.1413B,2009A&A...507.1793N,2019A&A...622A.103B in its most recent version.

We find a best fit to the SED using a model galaxy spectrum with an intermediate mass and a moderate star-formation rate (SFR), as well as a low specific star-formation rate (sSFR). However, our modelling shows some degree of over-fitting, which might be due to the low number of data points.

gmw_00012534.name: 2MASX J140541
gmw_00012534.overview: The galaxy 2MASX J140541 exhibits a compact [O III] emission morphology [arXiv:1802.06184]. Its nuclear region shows slightly blueshifted gas outflows extending out to approximately 300 parsecs, while kinematically disturbed gas is present throughout the wider measured area [arXiv:1802.06184].

sources.summary: 2MASX J140541 has compact [O III] emission, nuclear outflow signatures to about 300 pc, and disturbed single-component gas extending across the measured region.

quotes.quote: 2MASX J140541 This source has a compact [ O III ] morphology. The measured R _ max is inside the observed [ O III ] morphology as it is artificially extended by diffraction spikes. Kinematics show the gas to be near systemic, with slightly blueshifted outflows over the nucleus possessing FWHMs of 800 km s ^ 1 out to an R _ out 300 pc. FWHM measurements remain near 400 km s ^ 1 for single-component emission lines at the furthest extent of our measurements, suggesting kinematically disturbed gas exist throughout (R _ dist 850 pc).

gmw_00012535.name: 2MASX J171350
gmw_00012535.overview: The galaxy 2MASX J171350 features a compact [O III] morphology [arXiv:1802.06184]. Its kinematics follow a rotation pattern with high peak rotation velocities and multi-component emission lines extending out to 490 pc [arXiv:1802.06184].

sources.summary: 2MASX J171350 has compact [O III] morphology, rotational kinematics with broad emission-line widths, high peak rotation velocities, and multicomponent emission to 490 pc.

quotes.quote: 2MASX J171350 Similar to 2MASX J140541, this source has a compact [ O III ] morphology and the measured R _ max is inside the observed [ O III ] morphology as it is artificially extended by diffraction spikes. Kinematics in this system follow a rotation pattern, redshifted to the northeast and blueshifted to the southwest, with broad emission-line FWHMs frequently around 1500 km s ^ 1 . High peak rotation velocities ( 200 km s ^ 1 ) are consistent with the alignment between the modeled host major axis and STIS long-slit position angles (PA _ diff = 16.3 ^ ). Multi-component emission lines are observed out to 490 pc, which we define as R _ out .

gmw_00012536.name: MIDAS J225522-341807
gmw_00012536.overview: MIDAS J225522-341807 is classified as an aged remnant radio galaxy candidate [arXiv:2101.09761]. It exhibits an amorphous radio morphology with extremely relaxed lobes, no hotspots, and an ultra-steep curved spectrum [arXiv:2101.09761].

sources.summary: MIDAS J225522-341807 is classified as an aged remnant candidate with relaxed amorphous lobes, no hotspots, low surface brightness, non-detections at higher frequencies, and an ultra-steep curved spectrum.

quotes.quote: Remnant candidates without hotspots MIDAS J225522-341807 Radio properties. Figure fig:MIDAS_J225522-341807 shows extremely relaxed lobes and an amorphous radio morphology. No compact structures that would indicate hotspots are observed. The average 154 MHz surface brightness is 32 mJy arcmin ^ 2 , satisfying the low SB criterion (SB < 50 mJy arcmin ^ 2 ) employed by 2017A&A...606A..98B .

The diffuse radio emission is undetected by the uGMRT observations, NVSS, GLASS, as well as the 2.1 GHz and 9 GHz ATCA follow-up observations. Unsurprisingly, we find that the source spectrum appears ultra-steep at low frequencies, and demonstrates a curvature ( q=-0.11 ) across the observed range of frequencies. The radio properties point towards an aged remnant.

gmw_00012537.name: MIDAS J225607-343212
gmw_00012537.overview: MIDAS J225607-343212 is a low surface brightness radio source featuring a pair of relaxed radio lobes connected by a diffuse bridge of emission [arXiv:2101.09761]. The lobes contain edge-brightened regions that likely represent expanded, previously active hotspots, and the source exhibits spectral curvature above 1.4 GHz [arXiv:2101.09761].

sources.summary: MIDAS J225607-343212 has relaxed lobes linked by a diffuse bridge, low surface brightness, likely expanded old hotspots, high-frequency non-detections, and spectral curvature above 1.4 GHz.

quotes.quote: MIDAS J225607-343212 Radio properties. Figure fig:MIDAS_J225607-343212 shows a pair of relaxed radio lobes, with a diffuse bridge of emission connecting each lobe along the jet axis. The 154 MHz average surface brightness is calculated as 26 mJy arc-minute ^ 2 , satisfying the low surface brightness criterion. The edge brightened regions likely represent the expanded hotspots of the previously active jet, similar to what is observed in B2 0924+30 2017A&A...600A..65S . The source is undetected by the uGMRT observations, GLASS, as well as the ATCA follow-up at 2.1 GHz and 9 GHz. Curvature is evident in the spectrum, which becomes ultra-steep above 1.4 GHz.

gmw_00012538.name: MIDAS J225608-341858
gmw_00012538.overview: MIDAS J225608-341858 is a radio source characterized by two relaxed, asymmetric, low-surface-brightness lobes [arXiv:2101.09761]. Its radio spectrum appears consistent with a single power law, and the source went undetected in follow-up observations at 2.1 GHz and 9 GHz [arXiv:2101.09761].

sources.summary: MIDAS J225608-341858 shows two relaxed, asymmetric, low-surface-brightness lobes; it is undetected in ATCA follow-up and has a spectrum consistent with a single power law.

quotes.quote: MIDAS J225608-341858 Radio properties. Figure fig:MIDAS_J225608-341858 shows two relaxed, low surface brightness lobes that are asymmetrical in shape. The flattened `pancake'-like morphology of the Northern lobe can be explained by the buoyant rising of the lobes 2001ApJ...554..261C . The surface brightness of each lobes is approximately 43 mJy arcmin ^ 2 , satisfying the low SB criterion employed by 2017A&A...606A..98B . The source is undetected by the follow-up 2.1 GHz and 9 GHz ATCA observations. The spectrum seems consistent with a single power law ( =-0.86 ), and the detection at 5 GHz is too weak to determine whether spectral curvature is evident at higher frequencies.

gmw_00012539.name: MIDAS J225337-344745
gmw_00012539.overview: MIDAS J225337-344745 is a radio galaxy with an edge-brightened Fanaroff-Riley II morphology [arXiv:2101.09761]. Its highly curved spectrum suggests that its lobes are remnants, although compact emitting regions at the lobe edges may indicate recent energy injection [arXiv:2101.09761].

sources.summary: MIDAS J225337-344745 is an FR-II-like radio galaxy with a strongly curved spectrum suggesting remnant lobes, although compact 5.5 GHz edge features may indicate recent energy injection.

quotes.quote: MIDAS J225337-344745 Radio properties. Figure fig:MIDAS_J225337-344745 shows a typical low-resolution FR-II radio galaxy as evidenced by the edge-brightened morphology. The average 154 MHz surface brightness is 160 mJy arcmin ^ 2 . The source is firmly detected by the ATCA follow-up at all frequencies, revealing that the spectrum is highly curved ( q=-0.12 ) over the observed frequency range and only becomes ultra-steep at 2 GHz.

An evaluation of its spectral curvature reveals SPC = 0.78 0.17 , suggesting the lobes are remnant. The properties of the spectrum strongly suggest a lack of energy supply to the lobes, however, GLASS 5.5 reveals compact emitting regions at the edges of each lobe that may suggest recent energy injection. We divert a detailed analysis of this source to Sect. sec:evolutionary .

gmw_00012540.name: MIDAS J225543-344047
gmw_00012540.overview: MIDAS J225543-344047 is an elongated, pencil-thin radio galaxy [arXiv:2101.09761]. It exhibits an edge-brightened Fanaroff-Riley class II morphology, with its brightest and most compact emitting regions detected by the GLASS survey [arXiv:2101.09761].

sources.summary: MIDAS J225543-344047 is described as an elongated, pencil-thin FR-II radio galaxy whose compact bright regions are detected by GLASS while lower-surface-brightness emission is missed.

quotes.quote: MIDAS J225543-344047 Radio properties. Figure fig:MIDAS_J225543-344047 demonstrates an elongated, `pencil-thin', radio galaxy with an edge-brightened FR-II morphology. GLASS detects only the brightest and most compact emitting regions, and misses the lower surface brightness emission seen at 887 MHz. The radio source is detected in all but the 2.1 GHz ATCA follow-up observations. The radio spectrum is well modelled by a power-law ( =-0.87 ), and shows no evidence of a curvature up to 9 GHz.

gmw_00012541.name: MIDAS J225919-331159
gmw_00012541.overview: MIDAS J225919-331159 is a radio source featuring a pair of lobes with compact emitting regions [arXiv:2101.09761]. Its radio spectrum is well approximated as a power-law with no evidence of spectral curvature [arXiv:2101.09761].

sources.summary: MIDAS J225919-331159 has paired lobes with GLASS-detected compact emitting regions and a power-law radio spectrum lacking evidence for spectral curvature.

quotes.quote: MIDAS J225919-331159 Radio properties. Figure fig:MIDAS_J225919-331159 demonstrates a pair of lobes with compact emitting regions seen by GLASS. The radio spectrum is well approximated as a power-law ( =-0.86 ) with no evidence of a spectral curvature over the observed range of frequencies. Host galaxy. G1 ( z_p=0.504 ) is taken as the likely host galaxy due to its central position between each lobe.

gmw_00012542.name: MIDAS J230054-340118
gmw_00012542.overview: MIDAS J230054-340118 is a radio source that exhibits a peculiar, asymmetric lobe morphology [arXiv:2101.09761]. While its western lobe contains bright emitting regions, the counterlobe is completely diffuse and lacks a hotspot [arXiv:2101.09761].

sources.summary: MIDAS J230054-340118 has a peculiar asymmetric morphology: one lobe has bright GLASS regions while the counterlobe is diffuse, with uncertain host candidates between the lobes.

quotes.quote: MIDAS J230054-340118 Radio properties. Figure fig:MIDAS_J230054-340118 shows a peculiar radio morphology; while the western lobe shows bright emitting regions in GLASS, the counter lobe is completely diffuse and does not show a hotspot. It is unclear what is causing this.

Host galaxy. The galaxies G1 ( z_p=0.32 ) and G2 ( z_s=0.306 ) are considered as host candidates, given their position between each lobe. We find that G2 is not associated with any galaxy group; if the lobe asymmetry is due to an environmental effect, we would not expect a field galaxy host. We can not comment on whether G1 is associated with a group, given the lack of a spectroscopic confirmation.

gmw_00012543.name: MIDAS J230104-334939
gmw_00012543.overview: MIDAS J230104-334939 is a Fanaroff-Riley class II radio galaxy with an edge-brightened morphology [arXiv:2101.09761]. The galaxy exhibits clear radio hotspots in each lobe and is unambiguously associated with a central host galaxy at a redshift of 0.312 [arXiv:2101.09761].

sources.summary: MIDAS J230104-334939 is a typical edge-brightened FR-II radio galaxy with clear hotspots, broad frequency detections, no significant energy losses, and an unambiguous central host.

quotes.quote: MIDAS J230104-334939 Radio properties. Figure fig:MIDAS_J230104-334939 shows a typical FR-II radio galaxy, as implied by the edge-brightened morphology. The source exhibits clear hotspots in each lobe, as seen by GLASS 5.5 and GLASS 9.5. The source is detected in all but the 2.1 GHz ATCA follow-up.

Modelling the radio continuum spectrum gives a spectral index of approximately =-0.7 , revealing no significant energy losses. The BIC offers tentative evidence for some spectral curvature, however this may just be a result of a poorly constrained spectrum at low ( 215 MHz) frequencies. Host galaxy. The radio source is unambiguously associated with G1 (z_s=0.312) , which almost perfectly aligns with the projected centre of the source.

gmw_00012544.name: MIDAS J230321-325356
gmw_00012544.overview: MIDAS J230321-325356 is a radio source featuring two distinct radio lobes with compact emitting regions observed at high frequencies [arXiv:2101.09761]. The source has a spectral index of approximately -0.86, showing only marginal evidence of spectral curvature [arXiv:2101.09761].

sources.summary: MIDAS J230321-325356 has two lobes with compact high-frequency emitting regions and a roughly -0.86 spectral index, with only marginal evidence for curvature.

quotes.quote: MIDAS J230321-325356 Radio properties. Figure fig:MIDAS_J230321-325356 shows two distinct radio lobes with compact emitting regions observed at 5.5 GHz and 9.5 GHz. The spectral index is approximately =-0.86 showing no significant evidence of spectral ageing. The BIC does indicate a preference towards the curved power law model, however any real curvature in the spectrum is marginal ( q 0.02 ) and does not necessarily require an absence of energy injection. Host galaxy. Three likely host galaxies are identified by their alignment along the projected jet axis; G1 ( z_p=1.327 ), G2 ( z_p=0.622 ) and G3 ( z_p=0.775 ). We assume G1 is the true host, due to its smaller separation from the projected radio center.

gmw_00012545.name: MIDAS J230442-341344
gmw_00012545.overview: MIDAS J230442-341344 is a radio source featuring two detected radio lobes [arXiv:2101.09761]. While both lobes are visible at 5.5 GHz, only the southern lobe is detected at 9.5 GHz, and the system is likely hosted by the galaxy G1 [arXiv:2101.09761].

sources.summary: MIDAS J230442-341344 has both lobes detected at 5.5 GHz, only the southern lobe at 9.5 GHz, a power-law spectrum, and likely host G1 near the lobe center.

quotes.quote: MIDAS J230442-341344 Radio properties. Figure fig:MIDAS_J230442-341344 . Both lobes are detected in GLASS 5.5, however, only the southern lobe shows emission in GLASS 9.5. It is unclear what is causing this; both components are resolved by GLASS and show similar morphologies, so it is unlikely they are unrelated. The radio spectrum is well approximated by a power-law ( =-1 ), with no evidence of a spectral curvature.

Host galaxy. G1 ( z_p=0.9 ) is a favourable host galaxy candidate, due to its small angular separation from the projected centre between the lobes. G2 ( z_p=0.8075 ) is located further off centre towards the South, at a similar implied redshift.

gmw_00012546.name: BLAST 4
gmw_00012546.overview: BLAST 4 is a low-redshift, edge-on disk galaxy located in the GOODS-South field [arXiv:0910.3642]. It is the brightest 250-micrometer source in its field and exhibits patchy dust obscuration alongside extended radio emission [arXiv:0910.3642].

sources.summary: BLAST 4 is the brightest 250 m source, identified with the brightest GOODS-South optical/near-infrared galaxy: a low-redshift dusty edge-on disc with extended radio emission.

quotes.quote: BLAST 4: z_ spec = 0.038 The brightest 250 m source in the field is unambiguously identified with the brightest optical/near-infrared galaxy in GOODS-South. This is a very low-redshift, edge-on disc galaxy in which the effect of patchy dust obscuration can clearly be seen in the HST ACS colour composite image shown in the first panel of Fig. 7. Comparison with the VLA imaging apparently reveals 5 statistically secure alternative radio counterparts (VLA sources 499, 524, 572, 701, 986, all of which have P < 0.05 ), but in fact all of these lie near to the centroid of the optical galaxy, and it is clear that the VLA imaging has fragmented a more extended radio source. We have adopted VLA source 499 as the formal radio ID (position listed in Table 2); this is both the closest candidate and has the smallest value of P (and is the same radio ID adopted by Dye et al. 2009). A comparison of the sum of the flux densities of the 5 VLA counterparts ( S_ 1.4 = 202 mJy) with the flux density derived from the lower resolution (16 arsec FWHM) ACTA imaging of the field (ACTA S_ 1.4 = 310 48 Jy ; Norris et al. 2006) confirms that much of the radio emission from the galaxy may have been resolved out by the VLA imaging.

gmw_00012547.name: BLAST 6
gmw_00012547.overview: BLAST 6 is associated with an interacting galaxy pair at a spectroscopic redshift of 0.076 [arXiv:0910.3642, arXiv:1207.4478]. Both galaxies in this pair are radio-quiet, extended spiral galaxies that feature central active galactic nuclei and radio detections [arXiv:1207.4478].

sources.summary: BLAST 6 is associated with an interacting galaxy pair at z=0.076; the BLAST emission likely includes both galaxies or dust between them.

quotes.quote: BLAST 6: z_ spec = 0.076 The second brightest 250 m source in the field is clearly associated with an interacting galaxy pair. Both the 250 m and 350 m positions appear to lie almost equi-distant between two comparably bright, apparently interacting galaxies at the same redshift (see Fig. 1 and Fig. 3). The result is two statistically significant alternative radio IDs (VLA sources 72 and 100), and we have adopted the southern source (VLA 72) as the chosen ID because it has a marginally smaller value of P (the 500 m position also favours the southern galaxy). It is this galaxy which is shown in Fig. 7, with the alternative galaxy identification shown in Fig. 8. Because both objects lie at the same redshift, the precise choice obviously does not actually affect our final redshift distribution at all.

sources.summary: The paper treats J033229.88-274424.4 as a very low-redshift, clearly extended spiral with a central point source, radio detection, small apparent radius, and low overall AGN flux contribution.

quotes.quote: Two of our objects (J033229.88-274424.4 and J033229.99-274404.8 ) are clearly extended spiral galaxies at very low redshift (both objects have a spectroscopic redshift of 0.076). They show clear extended spiral galaxy patterns as well as clear central point sources. Both objects have radii of about 2 arcseconds. Both objects are detected in radio but are radio-quiet ( R 2-3 ).
