We obtain perfect fits with residuals < 5 , which is below the RMS of the background.

gmw_00012717.name: SDSS J2343-0030
gmw_00012717.overview: SDSS J2343-0030 is a quadruply imaged lensed source in a cusp-caustic configuration that forms a partial Einstein ring [arXiv:1512.00462]. The lensing galaxy belongs to the GMB11 galaxy cluster and has an edge-on, late-type companion galaxy located seven arcseconds to the southwest [arXiv:1512.00462].

sources.summary: SDSS J2343-0030 is a cusp-caustic quad lens in a cluster, with a nearby edge-on late-type companion and V-band-only modelling of its partial Einstein ring.

quotes.quote: SDSS J2343-0030 is a quadruply imaged source in cusp caustic configuration which forms a partial Einstein ring. The lens galaxy belongs to a galaxy cluster GMB11 and has one nearby companion only 7 arcsec towards the South-West, a late-type galaxy seen almost edge-on. Within a radius of 30 arcsec, we can identify another galaxy that seems to be late-type, whose colour suggests that it is also part of the cluster. This lens has only been modelled in the V-band. No NICMOS data were available from the archive.

gmw_00012718.name: SDSS J2347-0005
gmw_00012718.overview: SDSS J2347-0005 is a doubly imaged gravitational lens configuration [arXiv:1512.00462]. Its complex line of sight features a nearby galaxy cluster, a neighboring galaxy to the west, and multiple late-type galaxies [arXiv:1512.00462].

sources.summary: SDSS J2347-0005 is a doubly imaged lens in a complex line-of-sight field with clusters, nearby galaxies, and strong masking needed for noisy V-band data.

quotes.quote: SDSS J2347-0005 is a doubly imaged lens configuration. A galaxy cluster at a lower redshift (z=0.2637) is located about 100 ^ South-West of the lens. Another galaxy with a similar redshift to the lens appears 75 arcsec towards the West WiggleZ10 . V ^ band data reveal a multitude of sources on the line of sight, with different sizes and morphologies. About 15 ^ South of the lens galaxy we find two late-type galaxies that are about to merge.
At a distance of 16.3 arcsec to the North, we find another late-type galaxy. The H-band photometry gives a good fit, yielding residuals 7 in the region of interest.

gmw_00012719.name: Anon J071031--5615
gmw_00012719.overview: Anon J071031--5615 is a host galaxy located at a spectroscopic redshift of approximately 0.059 [arXiv:1110.4109]. This redshift corresponds to a luminosity distance of 240 Mpc under standard cosmological parameters [arXiv:1110.4109].

sources.summary: The paper identifies Anon J071031--5615 as the host galaxy of XRF 100316D, gives its center coordinates, uses deep imaging for host subtraction, and adopts z=0.059 with a 240 Mpc luminosity distance.

quotes.quote: About 15 h thereafter, a spectroscopic redshift of 0.059 was published for the host galaxy Vergani10a,Vergani10b .

A deep host-galaxy observation was carried out on November 5, 2010. This observation resulted in images with mean seeing of 0 74 and was used as a reference image for the subtraction of the host contribution from the early epochs. To develop a notion of the host contamination, we refer to Fig. \ fFC , where the position of the transient relative to the host galaxy is shown along with an F814W image of the Wield Field Camera 3 (WFC3) on board the Hubble Space Telescope (HST) as reference. In addition, the coordinates of the centre of the host galaxy, catalogue name Anon J071031--5615, were obtained from the last GROND observation: R.A.(J2000) =7^ h 10^ m 30^ s .37( 0^ s .07) , Dec.(J2000) =-56^ 15'20 2( 0 3) .

A luminosity distance of 240 17 Mpc to the host galaxy of XRF 100316D is employed as computed by the NED database http://nedwww.ipac.caltech.edu/ http://nedwww.ipac.caltech.edu/ following the standard CDM model, using the redshift measured by Chornock10b , a Hubble constant of 74.2 \ Riess09 , and the model of the local velocity field by Mould00 .

gmw_00012720.name: J123649.2+621539
gmw_00012720.overview: J123649.2+621539 is a Lyman-alpha emitting galaxy located at a redshift of z=5.190 [arXiv:astro-ph/0306409]. Its Lyman-alpha emission-line profile suggests the presence of a superwind outflow with a velocity of 300 km/s [arXiv:astro-ph/0306409].

sources.summary: The review highlights J123649.2+621539 as a z=5.190 LAE found serendipitously in HDF-N, with a Ly-alpha line profile interpreted as a 300 km/s superwind.

quotes.quote: J123649.2+621539 at z=5.19 One interesting object among the LAEs given in Table 1 is J123649.2+621539 at z=5.190 which was found serendipitously in the HDF-North flanking fields (Dawson et al. 2002). Its Ly emission-line profile shows a sharp blue cutoff and broad red wing emission, both of which are often observed in star-forming systems with prominent wind outflows. These features are also expected from radiative transfer in an expanding envelope. Therefore, Dawson et al. (2002) suggested that the Ly profile of J123649.2+621539 is consistent with a superwind with a velocity of 300 km s ^ 1 ; the Ly line can be decomposed to the following three components, (1) a narrow central emission component with FWHM = 280 km s ^ 1 , (2) a broad emission component with FWHM = 560 km s ^ 1 redshifted by 320 km s ^ 1 from the central component, and (3) a broad absorption component with FWHM = 800 km s ^ 1 blueshifted by 360 km s ^ 1 from the central one.

gmw_00012721.name: LAE J1044-0130
gmw_00012721.overview: LAE J1044-0130 is a Lyman-alpha emitting galaxy located at a redshift of z = 5.69 [arXiv:astro-ph/0306409]. It is identified as a candidate superwind galaxy with a broad Lyman-alpha emission line that may be partially absorbed by intergalactic atomic hydrogen [arXiv:astro-ph/0306409].

sources.summary: The review presents LAE J1044-0130 as a z=5.69 candidate superwind galaxy whose Ly-alpha profile has a sharp blue cutoff, red-wing excess, broad width, and possible intergalactic absorption.

quotes.quote: During the course of a new search for Ly emitters at z 5.7 using Subaru Telescope, Ajiki et al. (2002) found a candidate superwind galaxy at z = 5.69 .

Its emission-line shape shows the sharp cutoff at wavelengths shortward of the line peak and the presence of the excess red-wing emission seems secure (Fig. 3). The FWHM of the Ly emission is measured to be 340 110 km s ^ 1 and the full width at zero intensity (FWZI) is estimated to be 890 110 km s ^ 1 . These properties are similar to those of the Ly emitter at z=5.190 , J123649.2+621539, found by Dawson et al. (2002). We note that approximately half of the intrinsic Ly emission from LAE J1044-0130 could be absorbed by intergalactic atomic hydrogen (e.g., Dawson et al. 2002).

gmw_00012722.name: eBCG
gmw_00012722.overview: The eBCG is the eastern brightest cluster galaxy in the RX J1347.5-1145 cluster [arXiv:1812.01034]. It is associated with an infalling subhalo that has had its low-entropy gas stripped away during a merger [arXiv:1812.01034].

sources.summary: The paper defines eBCG as the eastern brightest cluster galaxy in RX J1347.5-1145, near a secondary lensing peak, and links nearby stripped, low-entropy gas to its subhalo.

quotes.quote: This is also consistent with the results of the weak- and strong-lensing analyses of optical data Bradac2008,Koehlinger2014,Zitrin2015,Ueda2018 , which show that the projected mass density has a primary peak centred near the AGN embedded in the cool core, and an additional component elongated towards a secondary peak at or near the brightest cluster galaxy (BCG) to the east of the X-ray peak (hereafter `eBCG' to distinguish it from that coincident with the cluster core, which we refer to as `wBCG'; see the top panel of Fig. fig:figure01 , which includes the lensing contours from Zitrin2015 ). Furthermore, optical spectroscopic analysis constrains the dynamics of the merger to take place mainly in the plane of the sky Miranda2008,Lu2010 . This is corroborated by the small difference in the redshifts of the two dominant BCGs, measured to be of the order of 100 km s^ 1 Cohen2002 .

The top right-hand panel shows the map from which adiabatic perturbations have been removed, revealing a prominent elongated structure to the south-west of eBCG. This coincides with the most prominent asymmetric excess seen in the X-ray image and, more clearly, in that divided by a spherically symmetric model centred at the X-ray peak (see top left- and bottom left-hand panels in the same figure). When calculating the best-fitting symmetric model, a 90 wedge to the south-east from wBCG was excluded from the analysis. The isobaric nature of this excess confirms the interpretation of this structure as low-entropy gas stripped from the subhalo associated with the eBCG and embedded in higher entropy ambient gas.

Indeed, the cooler structures to the north of the wBCG and to the south-west of the eBCG could be associated with the low entropy gas initially bound to infalling subhaloes and now trailing them. For the eBCG, the cooler gas has apparently already been stripped away.

gmw_00012723.name: Arp 238-W
gmw_00012723.overview: Arp 238-W is an interacting galaxy that contains a compact central carbon monoxide source [arXiv:2106.15041]. The galaxy exhibits a star formation efficiency enhancement of 0.7 dex, with diffuse gas likely distributed in its disk or tidal tails [arXiv:2106.15041].

sources.summary: Arp 238-W is described as one of Arp 238's two compact central CO sources, with a small deconvolved size, 0.7 dex enhanced SFE, and missing diffuse emission likely in disks or tails.

quotes.quote: Compared to the control sample, Arp 238-E shows an SFE enhancement of more than 1 dex whereas Arp 238-W has an enhancement of 0.7 dex.

For Arp 238, CO is detected only in two compact sources at the centers of the two galaxies. Both sources can be fitted well with elliptical 2-D Gaussian functions of FWHM = 4.4 3.8 with P.A. = 80 (Arp 238-E) and and FWHM = 4.4 3.3 with P.A. = 90 (Arp 238-W), respectively. Neglecting the small difference between the P.A. of each source and that of the beam ( P.A. = 88 , Table tab:overview_obs_data ), the intrinsic sizes of these sources after the deconvolution are 2.8 2.3 (Arp 238-E) and 2.8 1.3 (Arp 238-W), respectively. These are smaller than the NOEMA beam size (see Table tab:overview_obs_data ), and slightly larger than the sizes of radio continuum sources ( 1 - 2 ) detected in the high resolution VLA maps of Condon1991 .

Compared to their CO fluxes measured by IRAM-30m ( 51.8 3.4 Jy km s^ 1 and 37.0 3.3 Jy km s^ 1 ), the NOEMA observations missed 9 and 26 of the CO emissions of Arp 238-E and Arp 238-W, respectively. The missed emissions are most likely associated with diffuse gas in the disks and/or tidal tails.

gmw_00012724.name: MATLAS-2019
gmw_00012724.overview: MATLAS-2019 is a confirmed ultra-diffuse dwarf galaxy located within the NGC 5846 group [arXiv:2109.13173]. It hosts a large population of globular clusters with normal bright-end luminosities and a high specific frequency [arXiv:2109.13173].

sources.summary: The paper treats MATLAS-2019 as a robust NGC 5846 group UDG whose globular clusters have normal bright-end luminosities but a large population and high specific frequency.

quotes.quote: The recent analysis of the GC population in the UDG MATLAS-2019, in the NGC 5846 group, using deep --- sampling most of the globular cluster luminosity function (GCLF) --- high resolution Hubble Space Telescope (HST) data Mueller2021 has revealed that the brightest GCs in this UDG are consistent with the normal bright end of the GCLF at the group distance, indicating there are no over-luminous GCs present in this galaxy, contrary to what is found for NGC 1052-DF2 and -DF4 Danieli2019,Shen2021 . Rather, the GCLF is best explained by a large population of GCs for a galaxy of this luminosity.
One UDG candidate, MATLAS-2019, has been confirmed as a group member, and hence is a robust UDG, in individual case studies Mueller2020,Mueller2021,Forbes2019,Forbes2021 .

For MATLAS-2019, we obtain a value for N_ GC and S_N of 26 and 63, respectively, in agreement with the value of 26 6 and 58 14 determined from the deep (sampling most of the GCLF) high resolution HST data Mueller2021 .

gmw_00012725.name: MATLAS-1824
gmw_00012725.overview: MATLAS-1824 is a spectroscopically confirmed tidal dwarf galaxy with ultra-diffuse properties [arXiv:2109.13173]. It is detected in neutral hydrogen and lies along a tidal tail of the massive galaxy NGC 5557 [arXiv:2109.13173].

sources.summary: The paper identifies MATLAS-1824 as a spectroscopically confirmed tidal dwarf UDG, HI detected, lying along NGC 5557's prominent tidal tail and connected to MATLAS-1830 by another feature.

quotes.quote: MATLAS-1824 is a spectroscopically confirmed TDG Duc2014 .

One of the three HI UDGs, MATLAS-1824, is located along a prominent tidal tail of the massive galaxy NGC 5557, already mentioned in Section section:tidalfeatures . This UDG is also part of the six TDGs around ATLAS ^ 3D ETGs showing fine structures identified by Duc2014 , two of them having UDG properties based on their light profile fitting (see their Table 3). Another elongated tidal feature, perpendicular to the shell, passes through MATLAS-1824 and connects to another HI UDG, MATLAS-1830, which is not in our UDG sample due to the absence of successful two-dimensional surface brightness modeling caused by its extremely low surface brightness.

gmw_00012726.name: MATLAS-262
gmw_00012726.overview: MATLAS-262 is a low surface brightness dwarf galaxy classified as an ultra-diffuse galaxy [arXiv:2109.13173]. It shows signs of tidal disruption, appearing as a condensation within a long, east-west oriented stream near a putative lower-mass edge-on host galaxy [arXiv:2109.13173].

sources.summary: The paper classifies MATLAS-262 among tidally disrupted MATLAS UDGs, describing it as a condensation in a long stream near a putative lower-mass edge-on host.

quotes.quote: We visually inspected both the g band and color images of the MATLAS UDGs and found that five out of the 59 MATLAS UDGs (MATLAS-262, MATLAS-951, MATLAS-1059, MATLAS-1779 and MATLAS-1615) show signs of tidal disruptions.

In particular, 1) MATLAS-262 is observed as a condensation within a long stream, oriented in the east-west direction, and appears to be close to perpendicular to a putative host edge-on galaxy; the other filamentary structure close to the UDG with a NW-SE orientation is likely a foreground cirrus as several other such structures with the same orientation are seen in the field; 2) MATLAS-951 has streams with a clear S-shape; 3) MATLAS-1059 shows small tidal extensions; 4) MATLAS-1779 has streams with a possible S-shape, however, there is contamination by a ghost stellar halo; and 5) MATLAS-1615 is asymmetric, elongated toward a massive spiral galaxy, with extended emission also associated with the spiral.

It is worth noting, however, that there is one system (MATLAS-262) where every potential host has M_*/M_ 10^ 10.5 ; the galaxy that we assumed is the host has the highest mass (10.47) of all the potential hosts as well as the smallest physical separation, suggesting that UDGs may be found around some lower mass hosts.

gmw_00012727.name: VGS 07
gmw_00012727.overview: VGS 07 is an isolated void galaxy with a clumpy, blue morphology and a faint, non-interacting companion [arXiv:1008.4616]. The galaxy exhibits regular disk-like gas kinematics and a high star formation rate that suggests a possible starburst [arXiv:1008.4616].

sources.summary: The paper describes VGS 07 as an isolated void galaxy with a faint noninteracting companion, blue clumpy morphology, high H equivalent width, and regular gas kinematics.

quotes.quote: VGS 07 has a faint companion which does not appear to be interacting. It exhibits a clumpy, knotty, blue morphology similar to that seen in chain-galaxies forming at high redshift, or like edge-on, low surface-brightness galaxies. It has an enormous 200 H equivalent width, suggesting a high star formation rate per unit stellar mass, and possible starburst. We see fairly regular disk-like kinematics within the gas distribution. It sits near the center of the void and not near a wall-like configuration.

gmw_00012728.name: VGS 30
gmw_00012728.overview: VGS 30 is an H I-rich void galaxy located in a void wall between two large voids [arXiv:1008.4616]. It is accompanied by a similar-sized H I-rich companion, and both galaxies are embedded in a common H I envelope during a presumed first-time interaction [arXiv:1008.4616].

sources.summary: The paper describes VGS 30 as an H i rich interacting void galaxy paired with a similar-sized companion inside a common H i envelope in a void wall.

quotes.quote: VGS 30 is accompanied by a similar sized H i rich companion. The two are seen to be embedded in a common H i envelope, and due to the thinness of the target galaxy disk, presumably they are interacting for the first time. They are cosmologically situated in a void wall between two large voids.

gmw_00012729.name: VGS 32
gmw_00012729.overview: VGS 32 is a nearby, well-resolved void galaxy that is isolated in the middle of a void [arXiv:1008.4616]. The galaxy displays a flocculent spiral pattern and a flattened rotation curve [arXiv:1008.4616].

sources.summary: The paper describes VGS 32 as a nearby, well-resolved isolated void galaxy with optical and H i imaging, a flocculent spiral pattern, and a flattened rotation curve.

quotes.quote: VGS 32 is particularly close by, and thus significantly better resolved in optical and H i imaging. It displays a quite beautiful flocculent spiral pattern, and a textbook flattened rotation curve. It sits nicely isolated in the middle of a void.

gmw_00012730.name: VGS 35
gmw_00012730.overview: VGS 35 is a galaxy located near the transition zone between a small void and a larger one [arXiv:1008.4616]. It exhibits a mostly regular neutral hydrogen appearance, though it may possess a strong warp or a misaligned gas disk [arXiv:1008.4616].

sources.summary: The paper describes VGS 35 as mostly regular in H i but with a possible warp or misaligned H i disk, complicated by the elongated beam orientation.

quotes.quote: VGS 35 presents a fairly regular appearance in H i , except in its orientation. We see it exhibiting a strong warp, or perhaps a mis-aligned H i disk as we see in VGS 12. This is complicated by the elongated beam, which is suspiciously aligned with the H i in the disk. Possibly the elongation of the beam is exaggerating a slight warp in the H i disk, as the velocity contours in the center appear aligned with the optical disk, and bend away only in the outer parts. It is located near the transition of a small void into a larger one.

gmw_00012731.name: COSMOS-7549
gmw_00012731.overview: COSMOS-7549 is a high-redshift quiescent galaxy observed at z=2.63 [arXiv:2404.17945]. Following a rapid early formation phase, the galaxy quenched at z~9.7 and now exhibits neutral gas absorption alongside very low-level star formation [arXiv:2404.17945].

sources.summary: The paper identifies COSMOS-7549 as the highest-redshift quiescent sample galaxy, with early rapid formation, early quenching, weak later star formation, and neutral gas absorption.

quotes.quote: COSMOS-7549: This galaxy is observed at the highest redshifts in our quiescent sample, at z=2.63 . The Prospector fit is shown in Fig. fig:prospector_7549 , including the results using both the continuity and bursty priors. The stellar mass is M_ 6.3 10^ 10 M_ , and it formed half of its mass at z_ form 10 with high SFR 300 M_ / yr . After this rapid formation, it was quenched at early epoch z_ quench 9.7 .

The two SFH priors show different levels of SF after quenching. When the continuity SFH prior is used, which favors constant SFRs across the time bins, the galaxy continuously forms stars at 10 M_ / yr until recently and still shows very low-level SF at the observed epoch. In contrast, when the bursty prior is used, the SFR drops significantly down to <1 M_ / yr , and then, the galaxy shows a slight hint of recent rejuvenation. The weak H emission (in Fig. fig:neutral_ionized_gas ) may also be due to the low-level SF. The galaxy shows the Na I D excess absorption by neutral gas, with the centroid velocity consistent with the systemic velocity of the galaxy, likely tracing the ISM in the neutral phase.

gmw_00012732.name: COSMOS-16419
gmw_00012732.overview: COSMOS-16419 is a massive quiescent galaxy observed at a redshift of 1.925 [arXiv:2404.17945]. It formed most of its stellar mass extremely early before rapidly quenching, and it exhibits neutral gas absorption along with two small companion galaxies [arXiv:2404.17945].

sources.summary: The paper describes COSMOS-16419 as a very massive old quiescent galaxy that formed and quenched extremely early, with possible rejuvenation and neutral gas.

quotes.quote: COSMOS-16419: This galaxy is the second most massive galaxy in our quiescent sample: M_ =3.3 10^ 11 M_ observed at z=1.925 . The Prospector fitting result of this galaxy is shown in Fig. fig:prospector_16419 . It formed most of its stellar mass at z_ form 10 with extremely high SFR 1000 M_ / yr , and only after 300 Myr , it was rapidly quenched at z_ quench =7-13 . This extremely early formation and quenching is confirmed even when using the continuity prior, which disfavors rapid changes in SFH.

When the bursty prior is used, however, the galaxy appears to go through repeated bursts and quenching until recently. The color-composite image in Fig. fig:prospector_16419 () shows two small companions, which could potentially have contributed to the recent rejuvenation. This galaxy also shows Na I D absorption by neutral gas, with the centroid velocity consistent with systemic velocity. The neutral gas might originate from the cold gas reservoir in the galaxy.

gmw_00012733.name: PKS 2130-538
gmw_00012733.overview: PKS 2130-538 is a complex bent-tail radio source nicknamed "the dancing ghosts" due to its mirrored, ghost-like shape [arXiv:2302.14149]. It features a primary active galactic nucleus with arching jets bridging two radio lobes, alongside a secondary active galactic nucleus that produces a curling, bent-tail jet [arXiv:2302.14149].

sources.summary: The paper describes PKS 2130-538, the dancing ghosts, as a complex bent-tail radio source with primary and secondary AGN jets; AIRI imaging resolves its lobes, bridge, and jet separation differently from uSARA.

quotes.quote: This final selected field is centred on the complex radio source PKS 2130-538, nicknamed ``the dancing ghosts,'' owing to its peculiar and mirrored ghost-like shape. Two radio lobes, bridged by arching jets from the primary AGN host, extend southwards and blend into each other. A secondary AGN in the south-east produces a similar arched jet with a bent-tail that curls back around to the eastern primary lobe. With the original images generated from the ASKAP Evolutionary Map of the Universe Survey (EMU; 2011PASA...28..215N ), 2021PASA...38...46N mention that the interaction between the primary and secondary AGN is unclear. With our super-resolved uSARA images, presented in Part I, we have been able to distinguish a clear physical separation between the secondary curling jet and the primary western lobe.

The separation between the curling secondary jet and the eastern lobe of the primary AGN in PKS 2130-538 is less distinct in our AIRI image when compared to the uSARA image from Part I. However, there is a more drastic difference in the improvement of resolution when moving to the full-band with AIRI. Our AIRI sub-band image shows a much smoother structure than the full-band image, particularly noticeable when focusing on the sharpness of the jet bridge linking the two lobes from the primary AGN.

gmw_00012734.name: 1EX030
gmw_00012734.overview: 1EX030 is a far-infrared quasar located at a redshift of z = 1.11 [arXiv:astro-ph/0507240]. The object exhibits broad optical emission lines and is detected in the X-ray band [arXiv:astro-ph/0507240].

sources.summary: The paper identifies 1EX030 as a far-infrared quasar at z = 1.11 with broad emission lines, and reports it as the only X-ray detection among the sample's AGN galaxies.

quotes.quote: All of the optical counterparts are galaxies except for 1EX030, which is a point-like source showing broad emission lines characteristic of a quasar.
One object (1EX030) is a quasar with L_ FIR = 10^ 13.1 L_ at z = 1.11 .
Out of our seven AGN galaxies, only one source, 1EX030, identified as a quasar, has been detected in the X-ray.

gmw_00012735.name: 2EX015
gmw_00012735.overview: 2EX015 is a radio galaxy located at a redshift of z = 0.710 [arXiv:astro-ph/0507240]. It was not detected in X-ray observations, which may be due to the presence of a Compton-thick absorber [arXiv:astro-ph/0507240].

sources.summary: The paper identifies 2EX015 as a powerful z = 0.710 radio galaxy with the sample's highest radio-to-far-infrared ratio; its XMM non-detection may indicate Compton-thick absorption.

quotes.quote: The source 2EX015, which has the highest L(1.4 GHz)/L(90 m ) , is identified with a powerful radio galaxy at z = 0.710 .

The powerful radio galaxy 2EX015 at z = 0.710 and another AGN candidate 1EX110 (which has no optical spectrum yet) are found within the XMM-Newton survey area with a limiting flux of 1.4 10^ 15 erg cm ^ 2 s ^ 1 in the 2-10 keV energy bands. The X-ray non-detection of these two objects may be attributed to a Compton thick absorber with a column density of 3 10^ 24 cm ^ 2 , which is equivalent to A_V 1200 mag.

gmw_00012736.name: J1918+4937
gmw_00012736.overview: The active galactic nucleus J1918+4937 is a quasar that hosts a supermassive black hole binary system [arXiv:2112.05828, arXiv:2006.08441]. High-resolution radio imaging reveals a wiggled, one-sided jet structure that is consistent with this binary scenario [arXiv:2006.08441].

sources.summary: The paper treats KIC-11606854 (Spikey) as a Kepler source with an hour-scale flare, argues the duration disfavors accretion-induced explanations, and applies self-lensing MBHB models relevant to its light curve.

quotes.quote: More recently, with large optical time-domain surveys, several active galactic nuclei (AGN) have been identified that show quasi-periodic behavior in their light curves graham2015,charisi2016,liu2019,liu+2020,chen+2020 . This periodicity can be attributed either to periodic hydrodynamical modulations of the accretion flow artymowicz1994,macfadyen2008,dorazio2013,shi2015,bowen2019 , or to relativistic Doppler effects dorazio2015,hu2020 . One Kepler source KIC-11606854 hu2020 , also known as Spikey, shows, in addition, a short duration flare, lasting for a time scale of an hour. This short duration makes an accretion-induced flare scenario unlikely, since the viscous and the orbital timescales in the disk regions believed to be responsible for optical emission are both too long compared to the flaring time. An alternative mechanism for these flares is self lensing of the MBHB dorazio2018,hu2020,ingram2021 .

Previous work by showed that point source models of self lensing binaries could explain the observed flare for the source KIC-11606854 , which they dubbed as Spikey. Compared to their work, we included GR effects and finite source sizes. We find that the source size alters the width of the self lensing flare; the flare width, therefore, gives information on the emission morphology of the lensed source. Additionally, Kerr-Doppler boosting is shown to increase the overall Doppler modulation of individual BH light curves, leading to inaccurate model parameter predictions if only relativistic Doppler boosting is included. In future work, we aim to fit our presented model to Spikey's light curve in different bands.

sources.summary: The paper describes J1918+4937 as a quasar hosting a close binary SMBH, whose VLBI wiggled one-sided jet, flux evolution, and possible neutrino output fit the binary-jet scenario.

quotes.quote: The quasar J1918+4937 was recently suggested to harbour a milliparsec-separation binary supermassive black hole (SMBH), based upon modeling the narrow spike in its high-cadence Kepler optical light curve. Known binary SMBHs are extremely rare, and the tight constraints on the physical and geometric parameters of this object are unique. The high-resolution radio images of J1918+4937 obtained with very long baseline interferometry (VLBI) indicate a rich one-sided jet structure extending to 80 milliarcseconds. Here we analyse simultaneously-made sensitive 1.7- and 5-GHz archive VLBI images as well as snapshot 8.4/8.7-GHz VLBI images of J1918+4937, and show that the appearance of the wiggled jet is consistent with the binary scenario.

We find the jet morphological model is consistent with the optical and radio data, and that the secondary SMBH is most likely the jetted one in the system. Furthermore, the decade-long 15-GHz radio flux density monitoring data available for J1918+4937 are compatible with a gradual overall decrease in the the total flux density caused by a slow secular change of the jet inclination due to the spin--orbit precession. J1918+4937 could be an efficient high-energy neutrino source if the horizon of the secondary SMBH is rapidly rotating.

gmw_00012737.name: GRB 051022 host galaxy
gmw_00012737.overview: The GRB 051022 host galaxy is a luminous, compact, star-forming galaxy located at a redshift of z = 0.809 [arXiv:0708.3043]. Its optical spectrum displays emission lines including [O II], [O III], and H-beta [arXiv:0708.3043].

sources.summary: The paper identifies the GRB 051022 host as a luminous, compact, star-forming galaxy at z=0.809, with near-solar metallicity, high SFR, and dust/gas conditions insufficient to explain the dark afterglow alone.

quotes.quote: The nature of the host galaxy The optical spectrum of the GRB 051022 host galaxy (Fig. 6) shows one prominent emission line at 6731 which we interpreted as [ O II ] 3727 at a redshift z = 0.806 0.001. This is confirmed by the additional detection of [ O III ] 5007 at 9071 and H at 8806 consistent with z = 0.811. The [ O III ] 4946 falls within prominent sky lines and could not be accurately determined. The emission lines strengths were measured using a Gaussian fit in IRAF splot .

The spectral energy distribution of the GRB 051022 host galaxy is shown in Fig. 7. The restframe colours (and therefore the associated K-corrections) have been obtained based on the HyperZ code (Bolzonella et al. 2000), fitting synthetic SED templates to our UBVRIJK_ S band magnitudes derived from our own data (see Table 3 and correcting all for the Galactic reddening E(B-V)= 0.06, Schlegel et al. 1998). At z = 0.809, this is a rather luminous galaxy, with M _ B = -21.8 (or 3 L ^ adopting M _ B = -20.6 for a L ^ galaxy from Schechter (1976)).

gmw_00012738.name: HIPASS J1247-77
gmw_00012738.overview: HIPASS J1247-77 is a nearby galaxy located at a distance of approximately 3.2 Mpc [arXiv:1411.3844]. It is identified as an active galactic nucleus candidate based on its very red mid-infrared colors [arXiv:1411.3844].

sources.summary: The paper flags HIPASS J1247-77 as a very red WISE-color nearby AGN candidate, retains it because no optical diagnostics reject it, and places it among five AGN candidates closer than NGC 4395.

quotes.quote: The other two are HIPASS J1247-77 ( W1-W2 2.086 ) and NGC 5253 ( W1-W2 1.757 ), one of the nearest known BCD. For these, we have no optical diagnostic information and we therefore have no reason to remove them from our sample. In total, we therefore are able to identify 313 nearby galaxies with the infrared signature of an active massive black hole.
Our method detected AGN in five galaxies that are closer than NGC 4395: HIPASS J1247-77 (3.2 Mpc), UGC 04459 (3.2 Mpc), NGC 5253 (3.6 Mpc), IC 2574 (3.8 Mpc) and CIRCINUS (4.2 Mpc).

gmw_00012739.name: NGC 0841
gmw_00012739.overview: NGC 0841 is a disky spiral galaxy featuring a nuclear spiral, a dust lane, and a ring-like nuclear structure [arXiv:2201.05080]. It exhibits clumpy disk emission from multiple star-forming regions and a perturbed neutral hydrogen morphology, which indicates an interaction with the neighboring galaxy NGC 0834 [arXiv:2201.05080].

sources.summary: The atlas describes NGC 0841 as having a nuclear spiral, dust lane, ring-like structure, clumpy disk H-alpha emission, and perturbed H I morphology from interaction with NGC 0834.

quotes.quote: NGC 0841 (see Fig. Figure_AB1 ): characterised this galaxy as hosting a nuclear spiral and a dust lane using F547M HST imaging. Our sharp-divided image also suggests a ring-like nuclear structure. The H image shows strong emission in the nucleus and clumpy emission regions along the disc, probably associated to multiple star forming regions. Thus, we classified it as Disky . The HI data of this system show a perturbed morphology indicative of its interaction with NGC 0834 (not in this sample), located south-east of our galaxy, although both systems are optically undisturbed Kuo2008 .

gmw_00012740.name: NGC 3884
gmw_00012740.overview: NGC 3884 is classified as a core-halo galaxy [arXiv:2201.05080]. It features elongated H-alpha emission along its disc, soft X-ray emission, and a blueshifted broad kinematic component that suggests a nuclear outflow [arXiv:2201.05080].

sources.summary: The atlas classifies NGC 3884 as Core-halo, with elongated H-alpha emission, coincident soft X-rays, and two ionized-gas kinematic components whose blueshifted broad component suggests a nuclear outflow.

quotes.quote: NGC 3884 (see Fig. Figure_AB3 ): The H morphology of our image shows a rather elongated structure along the galaxy disc (see last row of Fig. Figure_AB3 ); we classified it as Core-halo . The soft X-ray emission is coincident with the ionised gas (see Fig. Figure_Xrays ). Despite the simple distribution of the ionised gas, in they modelled the emission lines of the ionised gas with two kinematic components. The broadest component is blueshifted and was interpreted as a possible nuclear outflow.

gmw_00012741.name: vc 5100822662
gmw_00012741.overview: The high-redshift galaxy system vc 5100822662 is an observed galaxy merger located at a redshift of z = 4.5210 [arXiv:2401.03020]. This interacting system has a stellar mass ratio of 1.7 and a projected separation of 11 kpc [arXiv:2401.03020].

sources.summary: The paper compares observed merger vc 5100822662 with simulated system H4, noting consistent stellar mass, SFR, mass ratio, redshift, and projected separation for interpreting its merger evolution.

quotes.quote: As an illustrative example, in the next section, we discuss the time evolution of the simulated system H4 at z = 4.495 , which provides the best synthetic analogue of vc 5100822662.

The simulated system H4 at z = 4.495 and the observed system vc 5100822662 (hereafter, vc 662) at z = 4.5210 have stellar mass and SFR which are consistent within the errors: Log( M_ /M_ ) = 10.6 (H4) and Log( M_ /M_ ) = 10.4 ^ +0.1 _ 0.1 (vc 662); Log( SFR / M_ yr^ 1 ) = 2.0 and Log( SFR / M_ yr^ 1 ) = 2.0 ^ +0.2 _ 0.2 , respectively. Moreover, H4 and vc 662 have similar stellar mass ratios _ H4 =1.5 and _ vc 662 =1.7, and projected distance of 16 kpc and 11 kpc respectively.

gmw_00012742.name: 3C 132
gmw_00012742.overview: 3C 132 is a radio galaxy located at a redshift of z=0.214 [arXiv:0712.0595]. It features a smooth, somewhat elongated elliptical host galaxy with several visible companion galaxies and a radio axis oriented nearly perpendicular to the host's major axis [arXiv:0712.0595].

sources.summary: The paper places 3C 132 near the NIC2 image corner, describing its elongated smooth elliptical host, visible companions, and radio axis nearly perpendicular to the host major axis.

quotes.quote: 3C 132; z=0.214 3C 132 appears near the western corner of the NIC2 image, and has a somewhat elongated, smooth elliptical host galaxy. Several companions are visible, including one bright foreground star at the image center. The radio axis of this FR II source is almost perpendicular to the major axis of the host galaxy.

gmw_00012743.name: 3C 153
gmw_00012743.overview: 3C 153 is a radio galaxy with a fairly round host galaxy and an elliptical nucleus [arXiv:0712.0595]. It exhibits a Fanaroff-Riley class II radio morphology, strong emission lines, and a slightly extended [OII] emission line nebula [arXiv:0712.0595].

sources.summary: The atlas identifies 3C 153 as an FR II radio galaxy and CSS candidate with extended [OII] emission, strong emission lines, an elliptical nucleus, and a round NICMOS host.

quotes.quote: 3C 153; z=0.277 3C 153 is a radio galaxy (angular size 6 arcsec=25 kpc) oriented at PA 50^ with an FRII radio morphology and a large arm-length ratio 1.9 laing81 . It has been classified as a CSS source (e.g. akujor+91 ) but it would be at the upper end of the size distribution for such sources. The [ OII ] emission line nebula is slightly extended along the radio axis mccarthy+95 . dekoff+96 note that the galaxy has an elliptical nucleus and strong emission lines. The NICMOS image shows a fairly round host galaxy.

gmw_00012744.name: J0115 0000
gmw_00012744.overview: J0115 0000 is a peculiar radio galaxy with an edge-brightened inner structure [arXiv:1903.11632]. High-resolution observations suggest it is a winged double-double radio galaxy, featuring wings connected to the inner lobes that create an overall Z-shaped symmetry [arXiv:1903.11632].

sources.summary: The paper flags J0115 0000 as a peculiar X-shaped source whose higher-resolution VLA structure suggests a winged double-double radio galaxy with connected wings and Z-shaped symmetry.

quotes.quote: It is also worth mentioning that J0115 0000 is a peculiar source and stands out from the rest of X-shaped radio sources studied. In higher-resolution VLA images 2018ApJ...852...47R , the edge-brightened inner structure becomes more apparent, particularly in the polarization structure, indicating this is a possible `winged double-double' radio galaxy. Interestingly, the wings identified by are apparently connected with the inner lobes in our maps, giving the inner structure an overall `Z'-shaped symmetry gop03 as well.

gmw_00012745.name: RXCJ0439.0+0520
gmw_00012745.overview: RXCJ0439.0+0520 is a brightest cluster galaxy that hosts a massive reservoir of molecular gas [arXiv:2403.03974]. While the bulk of this gas is centered on the radio core, a portion extends along a 10 kpc plume likely shaped by past gravitational disturbances [arXiv:2403.03974].

sources.summary: The paper describes the RXCJ0439.0+0520 BCG as H-alpha luminous with variable radio continuum, massive clumpy molecular gas, a 10 kpc plume, likely past disturbance, and redshifted absorption.

quotes.quote: RXCJ0439.0+0520 Galaxy and environment The brightest cluster galaxy of RXCJ0439.0+0520 is not as well studied as most of this sample, but is known to have a high H luminosity of 6 10^ 40 erg s ^ 1 detected by the VIsible Multi Object Spectrograph (VIMOS) of the Very Large Telescope (VLT) Hamer2016 . The central continuum source's light curve has also shown significant variability in the past, but has been relatively constant between 2010 and 2022 Hogan2015, Rose2022 . Molecular emission In RXCJ0439.0+0520 we find 1.3 0.4 10^ 10 M _ of molecular gas, the bulk of which is in a marginally resolved clump centred on the radio core. The remaining gas lies along a 2 kpc wide and 10 kpc long plume.
As in Abell 2390, ram pressure forces due to a past gravitational disturbance are the most plausible alternative explanation.

gmw_00012746.name: NGC 701
gmw_00012746.overview: NGC 701 is classified as a normal barred spiral galaxy [arXiv:astro-ph/0003298]. Its mid-infrared spectrum, polycyclic aromatic hydrocarbon equivalent width, and continuum emission closely match those of the Seyfert 2 galaxy NGC 5953 [arXiv:astro-ph/0003298].

sources.summary: NGC 701 is used as a normal SB comparison galaxy whose MIR spectrum, PAH equivalent width, PAH luminosity, and continuum closely match the Seyfert 2 NGC 5953.

quotes.quote: n701_n5953 the spectrum of the typical Sf2 spectrum of NGC 5953 on top of the spectrum of the normal SB galaxy NGC 701 (scaled by a factor 0.5). The two spectra are virtually indistinguishable. Moreover, the 7.7 m PAH EW in NGC 701, 5.88 0.05 m as well as the luminosity, L_ 7.7 = 42.048 0.009 erg s^ 1 , are well within the range spanned by Sf2s. This confirms that the PAH emission is not related to the activity in the nucleus. Note also that the continuum in the two objects matches perfectly.

gmw_00012747.name: Mrk 673
gmw_00012747.overview: Mrk 673 is classified as a Seyfert 2 galaxy with a large equivalent width of polycyclic aromatic hydrocarbons [arXiv:astro-ph/0003298]. Its X-ray luminosity and upper limits suggest that its emission is mostly stellar in origin [arXiv:astro-ph/0003298].

sources.summary: Mrk 673 is grouped among large-PAH-equivalent-width Seyfert 2s; its X-ray upper limit and ROSAT luminosity are interpreted as consistent with mostly stellar emission.

quotes.quote: The next three largest PAH EW Sf2s are NGC 1667 , Mrk 673 and NGC 5674 . They have 7.7 m PAH EW of the order of 4.6 m, smaller than that of the normal galaxy NGC 701, but still a factor of 10 larger than Sf1's. NGC 1667 was observed with ASCA by Turner et al. ( ) who report luminosities of 0.8 10^ 40 erg s^ 1 and 2.6 10^ 40 erg s^ 1 over the 2--10 keV and 0.5--2 keV range, respectively, well within the range of inactive galaxies. Furthermore, according to these authors, 68 of the X-ray flux in this galaxy can be accounted for by a thermal component due to the integrated stellar emission from a starburst. Mrk 673 appears in Burstein et al. ( ) with an upper limit L_ x 41.2 to its 0.5--4.5 keV luminosity. Mrk 673 also coincides with the ROSAT PSPC source WGA J1417.3+2651 with a 0.5--2 keV luminosity L_ x = 40.8 , again consistent with a mostly stellar origin. Finally, NGC 5674 may represent the first Sf2 where one starts seeing through the torus, since its X-ray absorbing column could be measured with Ginga, 0.8 10^ 23 cm^ 2 (Smith Done ).

gmw_00012748.name: Gaia16aax
gmw_00012748.overview: Gaia16aax is a luminous nuclear transient located in the nucleus of an active galaxy at a redshift of z=0.25 [arXiv:2001.07446]. The host galaxy contains a supermassive black hole, and the transient exhibited a multi-year optical brightening accompanied by changes in its Balmer-line morphology [arXiv:2001.07446].

sources.summary: Gaia16aax is described as a luminous nuclear transient in a z=0.25 active galaxy, with a massive black hole, multi-year optical brightening, and changed Balmer-line morphology.

quotes.quote: We present the results of a multi-wavelength follow up campaign for the luminous nuclear transient Gaia16aax, which was first identified in January 2016. The transient is spatially consistent with the nucleus of an active galaxy at z=0.25, hosting a black hole of mass 6 10^8M_ . The nucleus brightened by more than 1 magnitude in the Gaia G-band over a timescale of less than one year, before fading back to its pre-outburst state over the following three years. The optical spectra of the source show broad Balmer lines similar to the ones present in a pre-outburst spectrum. During the outburst, the and emission lines develop a secondary peak.

gmw_00012749.name: Gaia16ajq
gmw_00012749.overview: Gaia16ajq is an active galaxy at a redshift of approximately 0.28 that is classified as a QSO and starburst galaxy [arXiv:2001.07446]. In 2016, its nucleus experienced a one-magnitude increase in brightness, followed by a lightcurve decay that featured two bumps at 200 and 400 days after peak [arXiv:2001.07446].

sources.summary: Gaia16ajq is a nuclear brightening in a z~0.28 active galaxy classified as QSO/starburst, with Gaia-like rise behavior, decay bumps, and AGN-like broad-line spectrum.

quotes.quote: In the first months of 2016, other two objects with very similar characteristics to Gaia16aax were discovered by the Gaia Photometric Science Alerts pipeline: Gaia16ajq https://gsaweb.ast.cam.ac.uk/alerts/alert/Gaia16ajq/ (AT2016dvz on the TNS) and Gaia16aka https://gsaweb.ast.cam.ac.uk/alerts/alert/Gaia16aka/ (AT2016dwk on the TNS). Gaia16ajq was discovered on 2016 March 31, as an increase in brightness by 1 mag in the nucleus of an active galaxy at z 0.28. The galaxy is present in SDSS and it is classified as a QSO/starburst galaxy. Gaia16aka was discovered on 2016 April 4 also as an increase in brightness of 1 mag of the nucleus of a galaxy at z 0.31. This galaxy is present in SDSS but an archival spectrum is not available, therefore we were unable to determine if the galaxy hosts an AGN.

All three objects show a similarly shaped rise to peak emission on the same timescale of a few hundreds of days. The lightcurve decay of the three objects is less similar than the rise time. All three objects show bumps in the decay part of the lightcurve, with Gaia16ajq showing two very prominent ones at 200 and 400 days after peak.

For Gaia16ajq and Gaia16aka we only have a classification spectrum, taken with the NOT a few days after discovery. Both spectra and the first spectrum of Gaia16aax, for comparison, are shown in Fig. fig:gaia16_spec . All three objects show a blue bump - with Gaia16aka showing the most prominent one - broad Balmer lines and narrow forbidden lines typical of AGNs.

gmw_00012750.name: Gaia16aka
gmw_00012750.overview: Gaia16aka is a galaxy located at a redshift of approximately 0.31 [arXiv:2001.07446]. In April 2016, its nucleus experienced a one-magnitude brightening event [arXiv:2001.07446], displaying a blue bump and broad Balmer lines characteristic of an active galactic nucleus [arXiv:2001.07446].

sources.summary: Gaia16aka is a nuclear brightening in a z~0.31 galaxy; the paper notes SDSS detection, similar Gaia rise behavior, and an AGN-like blue broad-line spectrum.

quotes.quote: In the first months of 2016, other two objects with very similar characteristics to Gaia16aax were discovered by the Gaia Photometric Science Alerts pipeline: Gaia16ajq https://gsaweb.ast.cam.ac.uk/alerts/alert/Gaia16ajq/ (AT2016dvz on the TNS) and Gaia16aka https://gsaweb.ast.cam.ac.uk/alerts/alert/Gaia16aka/ (AT2016dwk on the TNS). Gaia16ajq was discovered on 2016 March 31, as an increase in brightness by 1 mag in the nucleus of an active galaxy at z 0.28. The galaxy is present in SDSS and it is classified as a QSO/starburst galaxy. Gaia16aka was discovered on 2016 April 4 also as an increase in brightness of 1 mag of the nucleus of a galaxy at z 0.31. This galaxy is present in SDSS but an archival spectrum is not available, therefore we were unable to determine if the galaxy hosts an AGN.

All three objects show a similarly shaped rise to peak emission on the same timescale of a few hundreds of days. The lightcurve decay of the three objects is less similar than the rise time. All three objects show bumps in the decay part of the lightcurve, with Gaia16ajq showing two very prominent ones at 200 and 400 days after peak.

For Gaia16ajq and Gaia16aka we only have a classification spectrum, taken with the NOT a few days after discovery. Both spectra and the first spectrum of Gaia16aax, for comparison, are shown in Fig. fig:gaia16_spec . All three objects show a blue bump - with Gaia16aka showing the most prominent one - broad Balmer lines and narrow forbidden lines typical of AGNs.

gmw_00012751.name: CFHQS J0221 0802
gmw_00012751.overview: CFHQS J0221 0802 is a high-redshift quasar with a broad and asymmetric emission line [arXiv:1710.02212]. Observations with ALMA have detected its dust continuum, which appears significantly elongated, but did not detect the targeted line emission [arXiv:1710.02212].

sources.summary: CFHQS J0221 0802 is a typical CFHQS quasar with broad asymmetric emission lines; ALMA detects dust continuum but no targeted line, likely due to velocity offset.

quotes.quote: CFHQS J0221 0802 This quasar is typical of the luminosity and black hole mass of the CFHQS survey. The \ emission line is broad and asymmetric with a narrow spike at z=6.13 Willott:2010a . The near-IR spectrum shows a relatively broad \ line that has a large uncertainty on both its peak location ( z=6.161 0.014 ) and line width, due to the faintness of the quasar and high sky noise at this wavelength Willott:2010 . Figure fig:contlinemaps shows a clear continuum detection (at 5 ), but no evidence for the \ line is seen in either the line map or extracted spectrum. Despite the low S/N of the continuum detection, the source is significantly elongated at an angle very different from the beam.

Based on the typical \ line to continuum ratios for z>6 quasars with this far-IR luminosity (see Section 4) we would expect to have detected the \ line at >10 significance. Offsets of >1000 km s ^ 1 between the \ and \ redshifts have been found for other high-redshift quasars by . Therefore we make the assumption that the \ redshift is similar to the \ redshift and was therefore missed by these observations.

gmw_00012752.name: CGCG 381-048

quotes.quote: CGCG 381-048- It has a long thin bar which is clearly visible in all J, H and K _s bands. Bulge is small but brighter. Disk is very diffuse and hardly visible for short exposures.

sources.summary: The paper describes UGC 1920 as having an oval bar with a bright classical bulge, very faint disk features, and significant bar-to-bulge color variation indicating an older central bulge.

quotes.quote: UGC 1920- has a oval bar with a bright, classical bulge. Disk features are very faint.

gmw_00012771.name: UGC5205
gmw_00012771.overview: UGC5205 is a gas-depleted companion galaxy in an isolated pair with CGCG007-025 [arXiv:2212.10593]. The two galaxies have a projected separation of 8.3 kpc, and UGC5205 hosts a tidal tail that suggests their interaction drove its current state [arXiv:2212.10593].

sources.summary: The paper treats UGC5205 as CGCG007-025’s gas-depleted companion; their 8.3 kpc projected separation and UGC5205 tidal tail support an interaction-driven contrast in star formation.

quotes.quote: In this manuscript, we present the initial results from an exhaustive analysis of the CGCG007-025 and UGC5205 galaxy pair. As mentioned by van_zee_evolutionary_2001 , this isolated galaxy pair is very interesting. The authors stated that while the projected separation is 8.3 kpc (at a velocity difference of 84 km/s ) there is a stark contrast between them: CGCG007-025 is undergoing a massive star formation phase, while UGC5205 is gas depleted. The observation of a tidal tail in UGC5205 encourages the idea that galactic interaction caused this state.

gmw_00012772.name: ESO 006-001
gmw_00012772.overview: ESO 006-001 is an isolated transition dwarf galaxy located 2.70 Mpc away [arXiv:2301.12789]. Although it contains no detected gas, it features a dominant population of ancient stars alongside recent star formation near its center [arXiv:2301.12789].

sources.summary: HST observations place ESO 006-001 at 2.70 Mpc as an isolated transition dwarf with ancient metal-spread stars, recent central star formation, no detected gas, and a nearby H I cloud.

quotes.quote: Observations with the Hubble Space Telescope unexpectedly revealed that the dwarf galaxy ESO 006-001 is a near neighbor to the Local Group at a distance of 2.70 0.11 Mpc. The stellar population in the galaxy is well resolved into individual stars to a limit of M_I 0.5 mag. The dominant population is older than 12 Gyr yet displays a significant range in metallicity of 2 < [ Fe/H ] < -1 , as evidenced by a Red Giant Branch with substantial width. Superimposed on the dominant population are stars on the Main Sequence with ages less than 100 Myr and Helium burning Blue Loop stars with ages of several hundred Myr.

ESO 006-001 is an example of a transition dwarf; a galaxy dominated by old stars but one that has experienced limited recent star formation in a swath near the center. No \ gas is detected at the location of the optical galaxy in spite of the evidence for young stars. Intriguingly, an \ cloud with a similar redshift is detected 9 kpc away in projection. Otherwise, ESO 006-001 is a galaxy in isolation with its nearest known neighbor IC 3104, itself a dwarf, at a distance of 500 kpc.

gmw_00012773.name: ESO126--G002
gmw_00012773.overview: Mid-infrared observations of the galaxy ESO126--G002 reveal that it lacks a central point source [arXiv:0805.3255]. Instead, the galaxy displays a circumnuclear ring structure with striking emission clumps located northwest and southeast of the center [arXiv:0805.3255].

sources.summary: The paper reports that ESO126--G002 lacks a central VISIR point source but shows circumnuclear MIR ring emission with clumps northwest and southeast of the center.

quotes.quote: ESO126--G002 In the VISIR image (Fig. ima1.ps ) no central point source is detected. The MIR emission displays a circumnuclear ring structure quite similar to that found in NGC 7552 (Siebenmorgen et al. 2004). Striking emission clumps are seen 0.5'' NW and a fainter one 0.8'' SE from the center.

gmw_00012774.name: Mkn 708
gmw_00012774.overview: Mkn 708 features a central point source surrounded by an emission structure that extends five by three arcseconds to the west [arXiv:0805.3255]. Because this extended component contributes 70 percent of the total mid-infrared luminosity, the galaxy's nucleus is classified as starburst dominated [arXiv:0805.3255].

sources.summary: The VISIR image of Mkn 708 shows a central point source plus extended western emission; most MIR luminosity is extended, so the nucleus is classified as starburst dominated.

quotes.quote: Mkn 708 (NGC 2966) The VISIR image (Fig. ima1.ps ) displays a central point source on top of a 5'' 3'' emission structure, extending West. The central core has a flux of 15mJy, consistent with previous upper limits (Siebenmorgen et al. 2004). The extended component gives 70 of the total MIR luminosity and therefore we classify the nucleus as starburst dominated.

gmw_00012775.overview: The galaxy 8082-3704 is classified as an E+A galaxy [arXiv:2106.15613]. Its summed spectra consistently match the characteristic E+A shape across all observed apertures [arXiv:2106.15613].

sources.summary: 8082-3704 is used as an example whose summed MaNGA spectra match the E+A shape in all apertures and satisfy the refined E+A criterion.

quotes.quote: An example of this process with the summed spectra of one galaxy, 8082-3704, is presented in Figure fig:ReSpec . A comparison to the single-fiber spectrum of the galaxy confirms the same characteristic E + A shape in all three apertures. By contrast, the bottom panel of Figure fig:WHANSF shows another of our E+A candidates, 8077-12704, where the spectra of the three apertures clearly differ. The former object fulfilled this criterion of being an E+A galaxy by our refined definition, while the latter object did not.

gmw_00012776.overview: The galaxy 8077-12704 is a rejected E+A candidate that retains post-starburst properties only at its very center [arXiv:2106.15613]. The vast majority of its spaxels are highly star-forming, and its outer regions lose all semblance of an E+A classification [arXiv:2106.15613].

sources.summary: 8077-12704 is a rejected E+A candidate: only the center retains E+A properties, most spaxels are highly star-forming, and outer spectra lose Balmer absorption.

quotes.quote: This is evident in Figure fig:WHANSF , which shows the analysis of the galaxy 8077-12704. While its single-fiber spectrum meets the specified criteria we outlined before, this galaxy only retains its E+A properties at the very center, as shown in its WHAN diagram. Only 3.19 of the spaxels in the MaNGA map are considered "old stars" whereas 93.9 is highly star forming. Preliminarily, using Marvin's interactive "Spectrum in Spaxel" tool, we were able to see that at both two and three R_e , the strong Balmer absorption lines disappear, and are replaced with intense H emission, losing all semblance of E+A classification. The three summed spaxel spectra in the 0-1, 1-2 and 2-3 R_e apertures, as clearly seen in Figure fig:ReSpec , have different continuum shapes from one other.
